license: All hymns included are public domain in the United States.

hymns.title: Abide, O Dearest Jesus
hymns.author: Joshua Stegmann
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Abide, O dearest Jesus, Among us with Thy grace, That Satan may not harm us, Nor we to sin give place.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Abide, O dear Redeemer, Among us with Thy Word, And thus now and hereafter True peace and joy afford.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Abide with heav'nly brightness Among us, precious Light;
Thy truth direct, and keep us From error's gloomy night.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Abide with richest blessings Among us, bounteous Lord;
Let us in grace and wisdom Grow daily through Thy Word.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Abide with Thy protection Among us, Lord, our Strength, Lest world and Satan fell us And overcome at length.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Abide, O faithful Savior, Among us with Thy love;
Grant steadfastness, and help us To reach our home above.

hymns.title: Abide With Me
hymns.author: Henry F. Lyte
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Abide with me; fast falls the eventide;
The darkness deepens;
Lord with me abide.
When other helpers fail and comforts flee, Help of the helpless, O abide with me.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Swift to its close ebbs out life's little day;
Earth's joys grow dim; its glories pass away;
Change and decay in all around I see;
O Thou who changest not, abide with me.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Not a brief glance I beg, a passing word;
But as Thou dwell'st with Thy disciples, Lord, Familiar, condescending, patient, free.
Come not to sojourn, but abide with me.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Come not in terrors, as the King of kings, But kind and good, with healing in Thy wings, Tears for all woes, a heart for every plea?
Come, Friend of sinners, and thus bide with me.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Thou on my head in early youth didst smile;
And, though rebellious and perverse meanwhile, Thou hast not left me, oft as I left Thee, On to the close, O Lord, abide with me.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: I need Thy presence every passing hour.
What but Thy grace can foil the tempter's power?
Who, like Thyself, my guide and stay can be?
Through cloud and sunshine, Lord, abide with me.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: I fear no foe, with Thee at hand to bless;
Ills have no weight, and tears no bitterness.
Where is death's sting?
Where, grave, thy victory?
I triumph still, if Thou abide with me.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: Hold Thou Thy cross before my closing eyes;
Shine through the gloom and point me to the skies.
Heaven's morning breaks, and earth's vain shadows flee;
In life, in death, O Lord, abide with me.

hymns.title: A Child of The King
hymns.author: Harriet E. Buell
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: My Father is rich in houses and lands, He holdeth the wealth of the world in His hands!
Of rubies and diamonds of silver and gold His coffers are full,— He has riches untold.
I'm a child of the King, a child of the King: With Jesus my Savior I'm a child of the King.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: My Father's own Son, the Savior of men, Once wander'd o'er earth as the poorest of men, But now He is reigning forever on high, And will give me a home in heaven by and by.
I'm a child of the King, a child of the King: With Jesus my Savior I'm a child of the King.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: I once was an outcast stranger on earth, A sinner by choice, and an alien by birth!
But I've been adopted, my name's written down,— An heir to a mansion, a robe, and a crown.
I'm a child of the King, a child of the King: With Jesus my Savior I'm a child of the King.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: A tent or a cottage, why should I care?
They're building a palace for me over there!
Tho' exiled from home, yet, still I may sing: All glory to God, I'm the child of a King.
I'm a child of the King, a child of the King: With Jesus my Savior I'm a child of the King.

hymns.title: A Great and Mighty Wonder
hymns.author: Germanus of Constantinople (634-734). Translated by John Mason Neale
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: A great and mighty wonder, a full and holy cure: The virgin bears the Infant with virgin honor pure!
Repeat the hymn again: "To God on high be glory And peace on earth to men!"

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The Word becomes incarnate and yet remains on high, And cherubim sing anthems to shepherds from the sky.
Repeat the hymn again: "To God on high be glory And peace on earth to men!"

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: While thus they sing your Monarch, those bright angelic bands, Rejoice, ye vales and mountains, ye oceans, clap your hands.
Repeat the hymn again: "To God on high be glory And peace on earth to men!"

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Since all He comes to ransom, by all be He adored, The Infant born in Bethl'em, the Savior and the Lord.
Repeat the hymn again: "To God on high be glory And peace on earth to men!"

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: And idol forms shall perish, and error shall decay, And Christ shall wield His scepter, our Lord and God for aye.
Repeat the hymn again: "To God on high be glory And peace on earth to men!"

hymns.title: A Lamb Goes Uncomplaining Forth
hymns.author: Paul Gerhardt
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: A Lamb goes uncomplaining forth, The guilt of all men bearing;
And laden with the sins of earth, None else the burden sharing!
Goes patient on, grow weak and faint, To slaughter led without complaint, That spotless life to offer;
Bears shame and stripes, and wounds and death, Anguish and mockery, and saith, "Willing all this I suffer."

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: This Lamb is Christ, the soul's great Friend, The Lamb of God, our Savior;
Him God the Father chose to send To gain for us His favor.
"Go forth, My Son," the Father saith, "And free men from the fear of death, From guilt and condemnation.
The wrath and stripes are hard to bear, But by Thy Passion men shall share The fruit of Thy salvation."

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: "Yea, Father, yea, most willingly I'll bear what Thou commandest;
My will conforms to Thy decree, I do what Thou demandest." O wondrous Love, what hast Thou done!
The Father offers up His Son!
The Son, content, descendeth!
O Love, how strong Thou art to save!
Thou beddest Him within the grave Whose word the mountains rendeth.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thou lay'st Him, Love, upon the cross, With nails and spear Him bruising;
Thou slay'st Him as a lamb, His loss from soul and body oozing;
From body 'tis the crimson flood Of precious sacrificial blood From soul, the strength of anguish: My gain it is; sweet Lamb to Thee What can I give, whose love to me For me doth make Thee languish?

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Lord, all my life I'll cleave to Thee, Thy love fore'er beholding, Thee ever, as Thou ever me, With loving arms enfolding.
Yea, Thou shalt be my Beaconlight, To guide me safe through death's dark night.
And cheer my heart in sorrow;
Henceforth myself and all that's mine To Thee, my Savior, I consign, From whom all things I borrow.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: From morn till eve my theme shall be Thy mercy's wondrous measure;
To sacrifice myself for Thee Shall be my aim and pleasure.
My stream of life shall ever be A current flowing ceaselessly, Thy constant praise outpouring.
I'll treasure in my memory, O Lord, all Thou hast done for me, Thy gracious love adoring.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Enlarge, my heart's own shrine, and swell, To thee shall now be given A treasure that doth far excel The worth of earth and heaven.
Away with the Arabian gold, With treasures of an earthly mold!
I've found a better jewel.
My priceless treasure, Lord my God, Is Thy most holy, precious blood, Which flowed from wounds so cruel.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: This treasure ever I'll employ, This every aid shall yield me;
In sorrow it shall be my joy, In conflict it shall shield me;
In joy, the music of my feast, And when all else has lost its zest, This manna still shall feed me;
In thirst my drink; in want my food;
My company in solitude, To comfort and to lead me.

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: Of death I am no more afraid, New life from Thee is flowing;
Thy cross affords me cooling shade When noonday's sun is glowing.
When by my grief I am oppressed, On Thee my weary soul shall rest Serenely as on pillows.
Thou art my Anchor when by woe My bark is driven to and fro On trouble's surging billows.

verses.label: Verse 10
lines: And when Thy glory I shall see And taste Thy kingdom's pleasure, Thy blood my royal robe shall be, My joy beyond all measure.
When I appear before Thy throne, Thy righteousness shall be my crown- With these I need not hide me.
And there, in garments richly wrought As Thine own bride, I shall be brought To stand in joy beside Thee.

hymns.title: All Creatures of Our God and King
hymns.author: Francis of Assisi
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: All creatures of our God and King Lift up your voice and with us sing, Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Thou burning sun with golden beam, Thou silver moon with softer gleam!
O praise Him!
O praise Him!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thou rushing wind that art so strong Ye clouds that sail in Heaven along, O praise Him!
Alleluia!
Thou rising moon, in praise rejoice, Ye lights of evening, find a voice!
O praise Him!
O praise Him!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thou flowing water, pure and clear, Make music for thy Lord to hear, O praise Him!
Alleluia!
Thou fire so masterful and bright, That givest man both warmth and light.
O praise Him!
O praise Him!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Dear mother earth, who day by day Unfoldest blessings on our way, O praise Him!
Alleluia!
The flowers and fruits that in thee grow, Let them His glory also show.
O praise Him!
O praise Him!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: And all ye men of tender heart, Forgiving others, take your part, O sing ye!
Alleluia!
Ye who long pain and sorrow bear, Praise God and on Him cast your care!
O praise Him!
O praise Him!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: And thou most kind and gentle Death, Waiting to hush our latest breath, O praise Him!
Alleluia!
Thou leadest home the child of God, And Christ our Lord the way hath trod.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Δ Let all things their Creator bless, And worship Him in humbleness, O praise Him!
Alleluia!
Praise, praise the Father, praise the Son, And praise the Spirit, Three in One!

hymns.title: All Depends On Our Possessing
hymns.author: unknown
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: All depends on our possessing God's abundant grace and blessing, Though all earthly wealth depart.
He who trusts with faith unshaken In His God is not forsaken And e'er keeps a dauntless heart.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: He who hitherto hath fed me And to many joys hath led me, Is and ever shall be mine.
He who did so gently school me, He who still doth guide and rule me, Will remain my Help divine.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Many spend their lives in fretting Over trifles and in getting Things that have no solid ground.
I shall strive to win a treasure That will bring me lasting pleasure And that now is seldom found.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: When with sorrow I am stricken, Hope my heart anew will quicken, All my longing shall be stilled.
To His loving— kindness tender Soul and body I surrender;
For in Him alone I build.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Well He knows what best to grant me;
All the longing hopes that haunt me, Joy and sorrow, have their day.
I shall doubt His wisdom never, As God wills, so be it ever, I to Him commit my way.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: If on earth my days He lengthen, He my weary soul will strengthen;
All my trust in Him I place.
Earthly wealth is not abiding, Like a stream away is gliding;
Safe I anchor in His grace.

hymns.title: Alleluia, Alleluia! Hearts to Heaven and Voices Raise
hymns.author: Christopher Wordsworth
hymns.composer: Ludwig van Beethoven; Adapted by Edward Hodges
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Alleluia, alleluia!
Hearts to Heav'n and voices raise: Sing to God a hymn of gladness, sing to God a hymn of praise.
He, who on the cross a Victim, for the world's salvation bled, Jesus Christ, the King of glory, now is risen from the dead.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Now the iron bars are broken, Christ from death to life is born, Glorious life, and life immortal, on the holy Easter morn.
Christ has triumphed, and we conquer by His mighty enterprise: We with Him to life eternal by His resurrection rise.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Christ is risen, Christ, the first fruits of the holy harvest field, Which will all its full abundance at His second coming yield: Then the golden ears of harvest will their heads before Him wave, Ripened by His glorious sunshine from the furrows of the grave.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Christ is risen, we are risen!
Shed upon us heav'nly grace, Rain and dew and gleams of glory from the brightness of Thy face;
That we, with our hearts in Heaven, here on earth may fruitful be, And by angel hands be gathered, and be ever, Lord, with Thee.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Δ Alleluia, alleluia!
Glory be to God on high;
Alleluia! to the Savior who has gained the victory;
Alleluia! to the Spirit, fount of love and sanctity: Alleluia, alleluia! to the Triune Majesty.

hymns.title: Alleluia, Sing To Jesus!
hymns.author: William Chatterton Dix
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Alleluia! sing to Jesus!
His the scepter, His the throne.
Alleluia!
His the triumph, His the victory alone.
Hark! the songs of peaceful Zion thunder like a mighty flood.
Jesus out of every nation has redeemed us by His blood.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Alleluia! not as orphans are we left in sorrow now;
Alleluia!
He is near us, faith believes, nor questions how;
Though the cloud from sight received Him when the forty days were o'er Shall our hearts forget His promise, "I am with you evermore"?

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Alleluia! bread of angels, Thou on earth our food, our stay;
Alleluia! here the sinful flee to Thee from day to day: Intercessor, Friend of sinners, Earth's Redeemer, plead for me, Where the songs of all the sinless sweep across the crystal sea.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Alleluia!
King eternal, Thee the Lord of lords we own;
Alleluia! born of Mary, Earth Thy footstool, Heaven Thy throne: Thou within the veil hast entered, robed in flesh our great High Priest;
Thou on earth both priest and victim in the Eucharistic feast.

hymns.title: All Hail The Power Of Jesus' Name
hymns.author: Edward Perronet
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: All hail the pow'r of Jesus' Name!
Let angels prostrate fall;
Bring forth the royal diadem, and crown Him Lord of all.
Bring forth the royal diadem, and crown Him Lord of all.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Let highborn seraphs tune the lyre, and as they tune it, fall Before His face Who tunes their choir, and crown Him Lord of all.
Before His face Who tunes their choir, and crown Him Lord of all.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Crown Him, ye morning stars of light, who fixed this floating ball;
Now hail the strength of Israel's might, and crown Him Lord of all.
Now hail the strength of Israel's might, and crown Him Lord of all.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Crown Him, ye martyrs of your God, who from His altar call;
Extol the Stem of Jesse's Rod, and crown Him Lord of all.
Extol the Stem of Jesse's Rod, and crown Him Lord of all.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Ye seed of Israel's chosen race, ye ransomed from the fall, Hail Him Who saves you by His grace, and crown Him Lord of all.
Hail Him Who saves you by His grace, and crown Him Lord of all.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Hail Him, ye heirs of David's line, whom David Lord did call, The God incarnate, Man divine, and crown Him Lord of all, The God incarnate, Man divine, and crown Him Lord of all.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Sinners, whose love can ne'er forget the wormwood and the gall, Go spread your trophies at His feet, and crown Him Lord of all.
Go spread your trophies at His feet, and crown Him Lord of all.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: Let every tribe and every tongue before Him prostrate fall And shout in universal song the crownèd Lord of all.
And shout in universal song the crownèd Lord of all.

hymns.title: All My Heart This Night Rejoices
hymns.author: Paul Gerhardt
hymns.composer: or  or  or  Johann Georg Ebeling
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: All my heart this night rejoices, As I hear, Far and near, Sweetest angel voices;
"Christ is born," their choirs are singing, Till the air Ev'rywhere Now with joy is ringing.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: For it dawns,— the promised morrow Of His birth Who the earth Rescues from her sorrow.
God to wear our form descendeth, Of His grace To our race Here His Son He lendeth:

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Yea, so truly for us careth, That His Son All we've done As our off'ring beareth;
As our Lamb who, dying for us, Bears our load, And to God Doth in peace restore us.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Hark! a voice from yonder manger, Soft and sweet, Doth entreat, "Flee from woe and danger;
Brethren come, from all doth grieve you You are freed, All you need I will surely give you."

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Come then, let us hasten yonder;
Here let all, Great and small, Kneel in awe and wonder.
Love Him who with love is yearning;
Hail the Star That from far Bright with hope is burning!

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Ye who pine in weary sadness, Weep no more, For the door Now is found of gladness.
Cling to Him for He will guide you Where no cross, Pain or loss, Can again betide you.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Hither come, ye heavy— hearted, Who for sin Deep within, Long and sore have smarted;
For the poison'd wounds you're feeling Help is near, One is here Mighty for their healing!

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: Hither come, ye poor and wretched;
Know His will Is to fill Every hand outstretched;
Here are riches without measure, Here forget All regret, Fill your hearts with treasure.

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: Blessed Savior, let me find Thee!
Keep Thou me Close to Thee, Cast me not behind Thee!
Life of life, my heart Thou stillest, Calm I rest On Thy breast, All this void Thou fillest.

verses.label: Verse 10
lines: Thee, dear Lord, with heed I'll cherish, Live to Thee, And with Thee Dying, shall not perish;
But shall dwell with Thee for ever, Far on high In the joy That can alter never.

hymns.title: All People That On Earth Do Dwell
hymns.author: William Kethe
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: All people that on earth do dwell, sing to the Lord with cheerful voice.
Serve him with fear, his praise forth tell, come ye before him and rejoice!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Know that the Lord is God indeed;
Without our aid he did us make;
We are His flock, he doth us feed, And for His sheep He doth us take.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O enter then his gates with praise, Approach with joy his courts unto: Praise, laud, and bless his Name always, For it is seemly so to do.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: For why?
The Lord our God is good, His mercy is forever sure: His truth at all times firmly stood, And shall from age to age endure.

hymns.title: All Praise to Jesus' Hallowed Name
hymns.author: verse 1, ancient German. verses 2-7, Martin Luther
hymns.composer: ancient German found in Walter's Geistliche Gesangbüchlein
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: All praise to Jesus' hallowed Name Who of virgin pure became True man for us!
The angels sing As the glad news to earth they bring.
Hallelujah!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Th'Eternal Father's only Son For a manger leaves His throne Disguised in our poor flesh and blood See now the everlasting Good.
Hallelujah!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: He whom the world could not inwrap Yonder lies in Mary's lap;
He is become an infant small, Who by His might upholdeth all.
Hallelujah!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Th'Eternal Light, come down from heav'n, Hath to us new sunshine giv'n;
It shineth in the midst of night, And maketh us the sons of light.
Hallelujah!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: The Father's Son, God ever— blest, In the world became a guest;
He leads us from this vale of tears, And makes us in his kingdom heirs.
Hallelujah!

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: He came to earth so mean and poor, Man to pity and restore, And make us rich in heaven above, Equal with angels through his love.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: All this He did to show His grace To our poor and sinful race;
For this let Christendom adore And praise His name for evermore.

hymns.title: All Praise To Thee, My God, This Night
hymns.author: Thomas Ken
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: All praise to Thee, my God, this night, For all the blessings of the light!
Keep me, O keep me, King of kings, Beneath Thine own almighty wings.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Forgive me, Lord, for Thy dear Son, The ill that I this day have done, That with the world, myself, and Thee, I, ere I sleep, at peace may be.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Teach me to live, that I may dread The grave as little as my bed.
Teach me to die, that so I may Rise glorious at the judgment day.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O may my soul on Thee repose, And with sweet sleep mine eyelids close, Sleep that may me more vig'rous make To serve my God when I awake.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: When in the night I sleepless lie, My soul with heav'nly thoughts supply;
Let no ill dreams disturb my rest, No pow'rs of darkness me molest.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: O when shall I, in endless day, For ever chase dark sleep away, And hymns divine with angels sing, All praise to thee, eternal King?

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Δ Praise God, from Whom all blessings flow;
Praise Him, all creatures here below;
Praise Him above, ye heavenly host;
Praise Father, Son, and Holy Ghost.

hymns.title: All Who Believe and Are Baptized
hymns.author: Thomas Hansen Kingo
hymns.tune: Es ist das Heil uns Kommen Her
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: All who believe and are baptized shall see the Lord's salvation; baptized into the death of Christ, they are a new creation. through Christ's redemption they shall stand among the glorious heav'nly band of every tribe and nation.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: With one accord, O God, we pray: grant us thy Holy Spirit; look thou on our infirmity through Jesus' blood and merit.
Grant us to grow in grace each day that by this sacrament we may eternal life inherit.

hymns.title: Almighty God Your Word Is Cast
hymns.author: John Cawood
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Almighty God, Your Word is cast Like seed into the ground;
Now let the dew of Heav'n descend, And righteous fruits abound.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Let not the foe of Christ and man This holy seed remove, But give it root in ev'ry heart To bring forth fruits of love.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Let not the world's deceitful cares The rising plant destroy.
But let it yield a hundredfold The fruits of peace and joy.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Nor let Your Word so kindly sent To raise us to Your throne Return to You, and sadly tell That we reject Your Son.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Oft as the precious seed is sown, Your quick'ning grace bestow, That all whose souls the truth receive, Its saving power may know.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Great God, come down and on Your Word Your mighty power bestow, That all who hear the joyful sound, Your saving grace may know.

hymns.title: Amazing Grace
hymns.author: John Newton
hymns.composer: James P. Carrell and David L. Clayton
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Amazing grace!
How sweet the sound That saved a wretch like me!
I once was lost, but now am found;
Was blind, but now I see.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: 'Twas grace that taught my heart to fear, And grace my fears relieved;
How precious did that grace appear The hour I first believed.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Through many dangers, toils and snares, I have already come;
'Tis grace hath brought me safe thus far, And grace will lead me home.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The Lord has promised good to me, His Word my hope secures;
He will my Shield and Portion be, As long as life endures.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Yea, when this flesh and heart shall fail, And mortal life shall cease, I shall possess, with in the veil, A life of joy and peace.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: The earth shall soon dissolve like snow, The sun forbear to shine;
But God, Who called me here below, Shall be forever mine.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: When we've been there ten thousand years, Bright shining as the sun, We've no less days to sing God's praise Than when we'd first begun.

hymns.title: A Mighty Fortress Is Our God
hymns.author: Martin Luther
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: A mighty fortress is our God, a bulwark never failing;
Our helper He, amid the flood of mortal ills prevailing: For still our ancient foe doth seek to work us woe;
His craft and pow'r are great, and, armed with cruel hate, On earth is not his equal.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Did we in our own strength confide, our striving would be losing;
Were not the right Man on our side, the Man of God's own choosing: Dost ask who that may be?
Christ Jesus, it is He;
Lord Sabaoth, His name, from age to age the same, And He must win the battle.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And though this world, with devils filled, should threaten to undo us, We will not fear, for God hath willed His truth to triumph through us: The Prince of Darkness grim, we tremble not for him;
His rage we can endure, for lo, his doom is sure, One little word shall fell him.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: That word above all earthly pow'rs, no thanks to them, abideth;
The Spirit and the gifts are ours through Him who with us sideth: Let goods and kindred go, this mortal life also;
The body they may kill: God's truth abideth still, His kingdom is forever.

hymns.title: And Can It Be
hymns.author: Charles Wesley
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: And can it be that I should gain An interest in the Savior's blood?
Died He for me, who caused His pain?
For me, who Him to death pursued?
Amazing love!
How can it be, That Thou, my God, shouldst die for me?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: 'Tis mystery all: th'Immortal dies: Who can explore His strange design?
In vain the firstborn seraph tries To sound the depths of love divine.
'Tis mercy all!
Let earth adore, Let angel minds inquire no more.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: He left His Father's throne above So free, so infinite His grace?
Emptied Himself of all but love, And bled for Adam's helpless race: 'Tis mercy all, immense and free, For O my God, it found out me!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Long my imprisoned spirit lay, Fast bound in sin and nature's night;
Thine eye diffused a quick'ning ray?
I woke, the dungeon flamed with light;
My chains fell off, my heart was free, I rose, went forth, and followed Thee.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Still the small inward voice I hear, That whispers all my sins forgiv'n;
Still the atoning blood is near, That quenched the wrath of hostile Heav'n.
I feel the life His wounds impart;
I feel the Savior in my heart.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: No condemnation now I dread;
Jesus, and all in Him, is mine;
Alive in Him, my living Head, And clothed in righteousness divine, Bold I approach th'eternal throne, And claim the crown, through Christ my own.

hymns.title: Angels From the Realms of Glory
hymns.author: James Montgomery
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Angels from the realms of glory, Wing your flight o'er all the earth;
Ye who sang creation's story Now proclaim Messiah's birth.
Come and worship, come and worship, Worship Christ, the newborn King.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Shepherds, in the field abiding, Watching o'er your flocks by night, God with us is now residing;
Yonder shines the infant light: Come and worship, come and worship, Worship Christ, the newborn King.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Sages, leave your contemplations, Brighter visions beam afar;
Seek the great Desire of nations;
Ye have seen His natal star.
Come and worship, come and worship, Worship Christ, the newborn King.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Saints, before the altar bending, Watching long in hope and fear;
Suddenly the Lord, descending, In His temple shall appear.
Come and worship, come and worship, Worship Christ, the newborn King.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Sinners, wrung with true repentance, Doomed for guilt to endless pains, Justice now revokes the sentence, Mercy calls you; break your chains.
Come and worship, come and worship, Worship Christ, the newborn King.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Though an Infant now we view Him, He shall fill His Father's throne, Gather all the nations to Him;
Every knee shall then bow down:

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Δ All creation, join in praising God, the Father, Spirit, Son, Evermore your voices raising To th'eternal Three in One.

hymns.title: Angels We Have Heard On High
hymns.author: French Carol; Translated by James Chadwick
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Angels we have heard on high Sweetly singing o'er the plains, And the mountains in reply Echoing their joyous strains.
Gloria, in excelsis Deo!
Gloria, in excelsis Deo!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Shepherds, why this jubilee?
Why your joyous strains prolong?
What the gladsome tidings be Which inspire your heav'nly song?
Gloria, in excelsis Deo!
Gloria, in excelsis Deo!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Come to Bethlehem and see Christ Whose birth the angels sing;
Come, adore on bended knee, Christ the Lord, the newborn King.
Gloria, in excelsis Deo!
Gloria, in excelsis Deo!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: See Him in a manger laid, Whom the choirs of angels praise;
Mary, Joseph, lend your aid, While our hearts in love we raise.
Gloria, in excelsis Deo!
Gloria, in excelsis Deo!

hymns.title: Angel Voices, Ever Singing
hymns.author: Francis Pott
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Angel voices, ever singing, round Thy throne of light, Angel harps, forever ringing, rest not day or night;
Thousands only live to bless Thee, and confess Thee Lord of might.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thou Who art beyond the farthest mortal eye can scan, Can it be that Thou regardest songs of sinful man?
Can we feel that Thou art near us and wilt hear us?
Yea, we can.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Lord, we know Thy love rejoices o'er each work of Thine;
Thou didst ears and hands and voices for Thy praise combine;
Craftsman's art and music's measure for Thy pleasure didst design.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Here, great God, today we offer of Thine own to Thee;
And for Thine acceptance proffer, all unworthily, Hearts and minds, and hands and voices, in our choicest melody.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Δ Honor, glory, might and merit, Thine shall ever be, Father, Son, and Holy Spirit, blessed Trinity;
Of the best that Thou hast given Earth and Heaven render Thee.

hymns.title: A Pilgrim and a Stranger
hymns.author: Paul Gerhardt
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: A Pilgrim and a stranger, I journey here below;
Far distant is my country The home to which I go.
Here I must toil and travel, Oft weary and opprest, But there my God shall lead me To everlasting rest.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: I've met with storm and danger, E'en from my early years, With enemies and conflicts, With fightings and with fears.
There's nothing here that tempts me To wish a longer stay, So I must hasten forwards, No halting or delay.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: It is a well worn pathway, Many have gone before: The holy saints and prophets, The patriarchs of yore.
They trod the toilsome journey In patience and in faith;
And them I fain would follow, Like them in life and death!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Who would share Abr'm's blessing, Must Abr'm's path pursue, A stranger and a pilgrim, Like him, must journey through.
The foes must be encountered, The dangers must be passed;
Only a faithful soldier Receives the crown at last.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: So I must hasten forwards Thank God, the end will come!
This land of my sojourning Is not my destined home.
That ever more abideth, Jerusalem above, The everlasting city, The land of light and love.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: There still my thoughts are dwelling, 'Tis there I long to be!
Come, Lord, and call Thy servant To blessedness with Thee!
Come, bid my toils be ended, Let all my wanderings cease;
Call from the wayside lodging, To the sweet home of peace!

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: There I shall dwell forever, No more a stranger guest, With all Thy blood-bought children In everlasting rest.
The pilgrim toils forgotten, The pilgrim conflicts o'er, All earthly griefs behind us, Eternal joys before!

hymns.title: Ascribe To the Lord Honor
hymns.author: Bola Omodun Ilori, 2012
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Ascribe to the Lord, honor, And glory due His Name Worship in His holiness, Worthy is His pow'r and fame.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The Lord of glory thunders O'er great and mighty seas;
His voice is so majestic, It breaks cedar to pieces.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: His voice like a mighty flash, Shakes the desert of Kadesh, It makes bare the thick forest No other voice is its match.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The Lord sits upon His throne, That's set upon the flood.
He gives strength to His people, Who put their trust in His word.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Ascribe to the Lord, honor, And glory due His Name, Worship Him in holiness Worthy is His might and fame.

hymns.title: As With Gladness Men of Old
hymns.author: William Chatterton Dix
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: As with gladness, men of old Did the guiding star behold As with joy they hailed its light Leading onward, beaming bright So, most glorious Lord, may we Evermore be led to Thee.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: As with joyful steps they sped To that lowly manger bed There to bend the knee before Him whom Heav'n and earth adore;
So may we with willing feet Ever seek Thy mercy seat.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: As they offered gifts most rare At that manger rude and bare;
So may we with holy joy, Pure and free from sin's alloy, All our costliest treasures bring, Christ, to Thee, our heav'nly King.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Holy Jesus, ev'ry day Keep us in the narrow way;
And, when earthly things are past, Bring our ransomed souls at last Where they need no star to guide, Where no clouds Thy glory hide.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: In the heav'nly country bright, Need they no created light;
Thou its Light, its Joy, its Crown, Thou its Sun which goes not down;
There forever may we sing Alleluias to our King!

hymns.title: At The Lamb's High Feast
hymns.author: Latin, circa 6th Century. Translated by Robert Campbell
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: At the Lamb's high feast we sing, Praise to our victorious King, Who has washed us in the tide Flowing from his piercèd side;
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Praise we Him, whose love divine Gives His sacred blood for wine, Gives His body for the feast, Christ the Victim, Christ the Priest.
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Where the Paschal blood is poured, Death's dark angel sheathes his sword;
Israel's hosts triumphant go Through the wave that drowns the foe.
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Praise we Christ, whose blood was shed, Paschal Victim, Paschal Bread;
With sincerity and love Eat we Manna from above.
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Mighty Victim from the sky, Hell's fierce pow'rs beneath Thee lie;
Thou hast conquered in the fight, Thou hast brought us life and light;
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Now no more can death appall, Now no more the grave enthrall;
Thou hast opened Paradise, And in Thee Thy saints shall rise.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Paschal triumph, Easter joy, This alone can sin destroy;
From sin's death do Thou set free Souls reborn, O Lord, in Thee.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: Δ Hymns of glory and of praise, Father, to Thee we raise;
Risen Lord, all praise to Thee, Ever with the Spirit be.

hymns.title: Awake, My Heart, With Gladness
hymns.author: Paul Gerhardt
hymns.tune: Auf, Auf, Mein Herz mit Freuden
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Awake, my heart, with gladness, See what today is done;
How after gloom and sadness, Comes forth the glorious Sun.
My Savior there was laid Where our bed must be made When to the realms of light Our spirit wings its flight.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: They in the grave did sink Him, The foe held jubilee;
Before he can bethink him, Lo!
Christ again is free.
And "Victory" He cries, And waving tow'rds the skies His banner, while the field Is by the Hero held!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Upon the grave is standing The Hero looking round;
The foe, no more withstanding, His weapons on the ground Throws down, his hellish pow'r To Christ must he give o'er, And to the Victor's bands Must yield his feet and hands.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: A sight it is to gladden;
And fill the heart with glee, No more affright or sadden Shall aught, or take from me My trust or fortitude, Or any precious good The Savior bought for me In sov'reign love and free.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Hell and its prince, the devil, Of all their pow'rs are shorn;
Now I am safe from evil, And sin I laugh to scorn.
Grim Death with all his might Cannot my soul affright;
He is a pow'rless form, Howe'er he rage and storm.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: The world against me rageth Its fury I disdain;
Though bitter war it wageth Its work is all in vain.
My heart from care is free, Misfortune now is play, No trouble troubles me, And night is bright as day.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: I cleave now and forever To Christ, a member true, My Head will leave me never, Whate'er He passeth through.
He treads the world beneath His feet, and conquers death And hell, and breaks sin's thrall;
I'm with Him through it all.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: To halls of heavenly splendor With Him I penetrate;
And trouble ne'er may hinder Nor make me hesitate.
No enemy I fear, Because my Head is near;
My Savior is my Shield, By Him all rage is stilled.

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: He brings me to the portal That opens into bliss, Where graved in words immortal This golden scripture is: "Who's there despised with me, Here with me crown'd shall be;
Who there with Me shall die, Here's raised with me on high!"

hymns.title: Awake, My Soul, And With The Sun (see also 'Praise God, From Whom All Blessings Flow')
hymns.author: Thomas Ken
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Awake, my soul, and with the sun Thy daily stage of duty run;
Shake off dull sloth, and joyful rise, To pay thy morning sacrifice.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thy precious time misspent, redeem, Each present day thy last esteem, Improve thy talent with due care;
For the great day thyself prepare.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: By influence of Light divine Let thy own light to others shine.
Reflect all Heaven's propitious ways In ardent love, and cheerful praise.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: In conversation be sincere;
Keep conscience as the noontide clear;
Think how all seeing God thy ways And all thy secret thoughts surveys.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Wake, and lift up thyself, my heart, And with the angels bear thy part, Who all night long unwearied sing High praise to the eternal King.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: All praise to Thee, Who safe has kept And hast refreshed me while I slept Grant, Lord, when I from death shall wake I may of endless light partake.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Heav'n is, dear Lord, where'er Thou art, O never then from me depart;
For to my soul 'tis hell to be But for one moment void of Thee.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: Lord, I my vows to Thee renew;
Disperse my sins as morning dew.
Guard my first springs of thought and will, And with Thyself my spirit fill.

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: Direct, control, suggest, this day, All I design, or do, or say, That all my powers, with all their might, In Thy sole glory may unite.

verses.label: Verse 10
lines: I would not wake nor rise again And Heaven itself I would disdain, Wert Thou not there to be enjoyed, And I in hymns to be employed.

verses.label: Verse 11
lines: Δ Praise God, from Whom all blessings flow;
Praise Him, all creatures here below;
Praise Him above, ye heavenly host;
Praise Father, Son, and Holy Ghost.

hymns.title: Away In A Manger
hymns.author: stanzas 1,2 anonymous published Philadelphia
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Away in a manger, no crib for a bed, The little Lord Jesus laid down His sweet head.
The stars in the sky looked down where He lay, The little Lord Jesus, asleep on the hay.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The cattle are lowing, the Baby awakes, But little Lord Jesus, no crying He makes;
I love Thee, Lord Jesus, look down from the sky And stay by my cradle til morning is nigh.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Be near me, Lord Jesus, I ask Thee to stay Close by me forever, and love me, I pray;
Bless all the dear children in Thy tender care, And fit us for Heaven to live with Thee there.

hymns.title: Baptized Into Thy Name Most Holy
hymns.author: Johann Jakob Rambach
hymns.tune: O Dass Ich Tausend Zungen Hätte (Dretzel)
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Baptized into Thy Name most holy, O Father, Son, and Holy Ghost, I claim a place, though weak and lowly, Among Thy seed, Thy chosen host.
Buried with Christ and dead to sin, Thy Spirit now shall live within.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: My loving Father, here doth take me To be henceforth His child and heir;
My faithful Savior, now doth make me The fruit of all His sorrows share;
My Comforter will comfort me When darkest clouds around I see.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And I have vowed to fear and love Thee And to obey Thee, Lord, alone;
I felt Thy Holy Spirit move me, I dared to pledge myself Thine own, Renouncing sin to keep the faith And war with evil unto death.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: My faithful God, Thou failest never, Thy cov'nant surely will abide;
Oh, cast me not away forever Should I transgress it on my side!
But if I fall hide not Thy face, Restore Thy child, Lord, by Thy Grace.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Yes, all I am and love most dearly, To Thee I offer now the whole;
O let me make my vows sincerely, Take full possession of my soul!
Let naught within me, naught I own, Serve any will save Thine alone.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Hence, Prince of darkness, hence forever, For I belong now to my God!
'Tis true, I sinned; but my dear Savior Hath cleansed me with His holy blood.
Away, vain world, sin, leave me now, I turn from you;
God hears my vow.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: And never let my purpose falter, O Father, Son, and Holy Ghost, But keep me faithful to Thine altar, Till Thou shalt call me from my post;
So unto Thee I live and die, And praise Thee evermore on high.

hymns.title: Beautiful Savior
hymns.author: German Jesuits, published
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Beautiful Savior, King of Creation Son of God and Son of Man!
Truly I'd love Thee, truly I'd serve Thee, Light of my soul, my joy, my crown.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Fair are the meadows, Fair are the woodlands, Robed in the flow'rs of blooming spring;
Jesus is fairer, Jesus is purer, He makes our sorr'wing spirit sing.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Fair is the sunshine, Fair is the moonlight, Bright the sparkling stars on high;
Jesus shines brighter, Jesus shines purer Than all the angels in the sky.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Beautiful Savior, Lord of the nations, Son of God and Son of Man!
Glory and honor, Praise, adoration Now and forevermore be Thine!

hymns.title: Behold, A Host, Arrayed in White
hymns.author: Hans Adolf Brorson
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Behold a host, arrayed in white, Like thousand snow clad mountains bright, With palms they stand.
Who is this band Before the throne of light?
Lo, these are they of glorious fame Who from the great affliction came And in the flood of Jesus' blood Are cleansed from guilt and blame.
Now gathered in the holy place Their voices they in worship raise, Their anthems swell where God doth dwell, Mid angels song of praise.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Despised and scorned, they sojourned here;
But now, how glorious they appear!
Those martyrs stand a priestly band, God's throne forever near.
So oft, in troubled days gone by, In anguish they would weep and sigh.
At home above the God of Love For aye their tears shall dry.
They now enjoy their Sabbath rest, The paschal banquet of the blest;
The Lamb, their Lord, at festal board Himself is Host and Guest.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Then hail, ye mighty legions, yea, All hail!
Now safe and blest for aye, And praise the Lord, who with His Word Sustained you on the way.
Ye did the joys of earth disdain, Ye toiled and sowed in tears and pain.
Farewell, now bring your sheaves and sing Salvation's glad refrain.
Swing high your palms, lift up your song, Yea, make it myriad voices strong.
Eternally shall praise to Thee, God, and the Lamb belong.

hymns.title: Beneath The Cross Of Jesus
hymns.author: Elizabeth C. Clephane
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Beneath the cross of Jesus I fain would take my stand, The shadow of a mighty rock within a weary land;
A home within the wilderness, a rest upon the way, From the burning of the noontide heat, and the burden of the day.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O safe and happy shelter, O refuge tried and sweet, O trysting place where Heaven's love and Heaven's justice meet!
As to the holy patriarch that wondrous dream was giv'n, So seems my Savior's cross to me, a ladder up to heav'n.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: There lies beneath its shadow but on the further side The darkness of an awful grave that gapes both deep and wide And there between us stands the cross two arms outstretched to save A watchman set to guard the way from that eternal grave.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Upon that cross of Jesus mine eye at times can see The very dying form of One Who suffered there for me;
And from my stricken heart with tears two wonders I confess;
The wonders of redeeming love and my unworthiness.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: I take, O cross, thy shadow for my abiding place;
I ask no other sunshine than the sunshine of His face;
Content to let the world go by to know no gain or loss, My sinful self my only shame, my glory all the cross.

hymns.title: Be Still My Soul
hymns.author: Katharina A. von Schlegel
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Be still, my soul: the Lord is on thy side.
Bear patiently the cross of grief or pain.
Leave to thy God to order and provide;
In every change, He faithful will remain.
Be still, my soul: thy best, thy heav'nly Friend Through thorny ways leads to a joyful end.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Be still, my soul: thy God doth undertake To guide the future, as He has the past.
Thy hope, thy confidence let nothing shake;
All now mysterious shall be bright at last.
Be still, my soul: the waves and winds still know His voice Who ruled them while He dwelt below.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Be still, my soul: when dearest friends depart, And all is darkened in the vale of tears, Then shalt thou better know His love, His heart, Who comes to soothe thy sorrow and thy fears.
Be still, my soul: thy Jesus can repay From His own fullness all He takes away.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Be still, my soul: the hour is hast'ning on When we shall be forever with the Lord.
When disappointment, grief and fear are gone, Sorrow forgot, love's purest joys restored.
Be still, my soul: when change and tears are past All safe and blessèd we shall meet at last.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Be still, my soul: begin the song of praise On earth, believing, to Thy Lord on high;
Acknowledge Him in all thy words and ways, So shall He view thee with a well pleased eye.
Be still, my soul: the Sun of life divine Through passing clouds shall but more brightly shine.

hymns.title: Be Thou My Vision
hymns.author: Attr. Dallan Forgaill, 8th Century. Translated by Mary Byrne
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Be Thou my Vision, O Lord of my heart;
Naught be all else to me, save that Thou art.
Thou my best Thought, by day or by night, Waking or sleeping, Thy presence my light.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Be Thou my Wisdom, and Thou my true Word;
I ever with Thee and Thou with me, Lord;
Thou my great Father, and I Thy true son;
Thou in me dwelling, and I with Thee one.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Be Thou my battle Shield, Sword for the fight;
Be Thou my Dignity, Thou my Delight;
Thou my soul's Shelter, Thou my high Tower: Raise Thou me heav'nward, O Pow'r of my pow'r.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Riches I heed not, nor man's empty praise, Thou mine Inheritance, now and always: Thou and Thou only, be first in my heart, High King of Heaven, my Treasure Thou art.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: High King of Heaven, my victory won, May I reach Heav'ns joys, O bright Heaven's Sun!
Heart of my own heart, whatever befall, Still be my Vision, O Ruler of all.

hymns.title: Blessed Assurance
hymns.author: Fanny Crosby
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Blessèd assurance, Jesus is mine!
O what a foretaste of glory divine!
Heir of salvation, purchase of God, Born of His Spirit, washed in His blood.
This is my story, this is my song, Praising my Savior, all the day long;
This is my story, this is my song, Praising my Savior, all the day long.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Perfect submission, perfect delight, Visions of rapture now burst on my sight;
Angels descending bring from above Echoes of mercy, whispers of love.
This is my story, this is my song, Praising my Savior, all the day long;
This is my story, this is my song, Praising my Savior, all the day long.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Perfect submission, all is at rest I in my Savior am happy and blest, Watching and waiting, looking above, Filled with His goodness, lost in His love.
This is my story, this is my song, Praising my Savior, all the day long;
This is my story, this is my song, Praising my Savior, all the day long.

hymns.title: Blessed Jesus at Thy Word
hymns.author: verses 1-3, Tobias Clausnitzer
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Blessed Jesus, at Thy Word We are gathered all to hear Thee;
Let our hearts and souls be stirred Now to seek and love and fear Thee, By Thy teachings sweet and holy, Drawn from earth to love Thee solely.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: All our knowledge, sense, and sight Lie in deepest darkness shrouded, Til Thy Spirit breaks our night With the beams of truth unclouded;
Thou alone to God canst win us;
Thou must work all good within us.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Glorious Lord, Thyself impart!
Light of light, from God proceeding, Open Thou our ears and heart;
Help us by Thy Spirit's pleading;
Hear the cry Thy people raises;
Hear and bless our prayers and praises.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Father, Son, and Holy Ghost, Praise to Thee and adoration!
Grant that we Thy Word may trust And obtain true consolation, While we here below must wander, Till we sing Thy praises yonder.

hymns.title: Blest Be The Tie That Binds
hymns.author: John Fawcett
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Blest be the tie that binds Our hearts in Christian love;
The fellowship of kindred minds Is like to that above.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Before our Father's throne We pour our ardent prayers;
Our fears, our hopes, our aims are one Our comforts and our cares.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: We share each other's woes, Our mutual burdens bear;
And often for each other flows The sympathizing tear.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: When we asunder part, It gives us inward pain;
But we shall still be joined in heart, And hope to meet again.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: This glorious hope revives Our courage by the way;
While each in expectation lives, And longs to see the day.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: From sorrow, toil and pain, And sin, we shall be free, And perfect love and friendship reign Through all eternity.

hymns.title: Blind Bartimaeus
hymns.author: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow, alt
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Blind Bartimaeus at the gates Of Jericho in darkness waits: He hears the crowd; he hears a breath say "It is Christ of Nazareth!" And calls in tones of agony, "O Jesus, Jesus pity me!"

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The thronging multitudes in-crease "Blind Bartimaeus, hold thy peace!" But still, above the noisy crowd, The blind man's cry is shrill and loud: Until they say, "He calleth thee;
Courage! arise!
He calleth thee!"

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Then saith the Christ, as silent stands The crowd, "What wilt thou at My hands?" And he replies, "O give me light!
Rabbi, restore the blind man's sight." And Jesus answers, "Go thy way, Thy faith, thy faith hath savèd thee."

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Ye that hath eyes and cannot see, In darkness and in misery, Recall those mighty voices three— "O Jesus, Jesus pity me!" "Courage! arise!
He calleth thee!" "Thy faith, thy faith hath savèd thee."

hymns.title: Breathe on Me, Breath of God
hymns.author: Edwin Hatch
hymns.composer: or  found in John Chetham's Psalmody
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Breathe on me, breath of God, Fill me with life anew, That I may love what Thou dost love, And do what Thou wouldst do.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Breathe on me, breath of God, Until my heart is pure, Until with Thee I will one will, To do and to endure.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Breathe on me, breath of God, So shall I never die, But live with Thee the perfect life Of Thine eternity.

hymns.title: Built on the Rock
hymns.author: Nikolai F. S. Grundtvig
hymns.tune: Kirken Den Er Et Gammelt Hus
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Built on the Rock the church doth stand, Even when steeples are falling;
Crumbled have spires in every land, Bells still are chiming and calling;
Calling the young and old to rest, But above all the soul distressed, Longing for rest everlasting.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Surely in temples made with hands, God, the Most High, is not dwelling;
High above earth His temple stands, All earthly temples excelling;
Yet He whom heav'ns cannot contain Chose to abide on earth with men, Built in our bodies His temple.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: We are God's house of living stones, Built for His own habitation;
He through baptismal grace us owns, Heirs of His wondrous salvation;
Were we but two His Name to tell, Yet He would deign with us to dwell, With all His grace and His favor.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Now we may gather with our King;
Ev'n in the lowliest dwelling: Praises to Him we there may bring, His wondrous mercy foretelling;
Jesus His grace to us accords, Spirit and life are all His words, His truth doth hallow the temple.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Still we our earthly temples rear, That we may herald His praises;
They are the homes where He draws near And little children embraces, Beautiful things in them are said, God there with us His cov'nant made, Making us heirs of His kingdom.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Here stands the font before our eyes Telling how God did receive us;
The altar recalls Christ's sacrifice And what His table doth give us;
Here sounds the Word that doth proclaim Christ yesterday, today, the same, Yea, and for aye our Redeemer.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Grant then, O God, where'er men roam, That, when the church bells are ringing, Many in saving faith may come Where Christ His message is bringing: "I know Mine own, Mine own know Me;
Ye, not the world, My face shall see.
My peace I leave with you."

hymns.title: By Grace I'm Saved
hymns.author: Christian L. Scheidt
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: By grace I'm savedgrace free and boundless!
My heart, believ'st thou this or not?
Why tremblest thou with terror groundless?
Has ever God a falsehood taught?
His Word is truethen this must be: By grace there is a crown for thee.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: By grace! our works are all rejected, All claims of merit pass for naught;
The mighty Savior, long expected, To us this blissful truth has brought, That He by death redeems our race, And we are saved alone by grace.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: By grace! mark well this phrase's meaning, When sin rolls sorrows o'er thy breast, When Satan threats with pride o'erweening, When troubled conscience sighs for rest: What reason ne'er can comprehend It pleases God by grace to send.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: By grace His Son, on earth appearing, Vouchsafed beneath thy woe to bend;
Hadst thou, damnation justly fearing, Done aught to render Him thy friend?
Was't not that He thy welfare sought, And but by grace deliv'rance wrought?

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: By grace! this ground of our salvation As long as God is true endures: What saints have penned by inspiration, What God to our poor soul secures, What all our faith must rest upon, Is grace, free grace through His dear Son.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: By grace! but think not, thou who livest Securely on in godless ways, That thou, though all are called, receivest The promised rest that wakes our praise: By grace none find in heaven a place Who live in sin in hope of grace.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: By grace!
They who have heard this sentence Must bid hypocrisy farewell;
For only after deep repentance The soul what grace imports can tell;
To sin while grace a trifle seems, To faith it bright with glory beams.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: By grace the timid hearts that languish, Find access to the Father's heart, When conflicts fierce and bitter anguish Bid all their joy and hopes depart.
Where, oftimes, should I strength obtain, Bid grace my anchor not remain!

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: By grace! on this in death I'll rest me, Rejoicing e'en though feeling naught;
I know my sin it oft oppressed me But Him, too, who my soul hath bought: My heart exults grief flees apace Because my soul is saved by grace.

verses.label: Verse 10
lines: By grace!
O sin and Satan hear it!
I bear my flag of faith in hand, And pass, in spite of doubts, nor fear it, The Red Sea to the promised land;
I hold the word my Savior taught As certain, whether felt or not.

hymns.title: Christ Arose
hymns.author: Robert Lowry
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Low in the grave He lay Jesus my Savior!
Waiting the coming day- Jesus my Lord!
Up from the grave He arose, With a mighty triumph o'er His foes;
He arose a Victor from the dark domain, And He lives forever with His saints to reign.
He arose!
He arose!
Hallelujah!
Christ arose!
He arose!
He arose!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Vainly they watch His bed- Jesus my Savior!
Vainly they seal the dead- Jesus my Lord!
Up from the grave He arose, With a mighty triumph o'er His foes;
He arose a Victor from the dark domain, And He lives forever with His saints to reign.
He arose!
He arose!
Hallelujah!
Christ arose!
He arose!
He arose!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Death cannot keep his prey- Jesus my Savior!
He tore the bars away- Jesus my Lord!
Up from the grave He arose, With a mighty triumph o'er His foes;
He arose a Victor from the dark domain, And He lives forever with His saints to reign.
He arose!
He arose!
Hallelujah!
Christ arose!
He arose!
He arose!

hymns.title: Christ Jesus Lay In Death's Strong Bands
hymns.author: Martin Luther
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Christ Jesus lay in death's strong bands, For our offenses given;
But now at God's right hand He stands, And brings us life from Heaven.
Wherefore let us joyful be, And sing to God right thankfully Loud songs of Alleluia!
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: No son of man could conquer Death, Such mischief sin had wrought us, For innocence dwelt not on earth, And therefore Death had brought us Into thralldom from of old And ever grew more strong and bold And kept us in his bondage.
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: But Jesus Christ, God's only Son, To our low state descended, The cause of Death He has undone, His power forever ended, Ruined all his right and claim And left him nothing but the name, His sting is lost forever.
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: It was a strange and dreadful strife When life and death contended;
The victory remained with life;
The reign of death was ended.
Stripped of power, no more it reigns, An empty form alone remains Death's sting is lost forever!
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Here the true Paschal Lamb we see, Whom God so freely gave us;
He died on the accursed tree So strong His love! to save us.
See, His blood doth mark our door;
Faith points to it, Death passes o'er, And Satan cannot harm us.
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: So let us keep the festival Where to the Lord invites us;
Christ is Himself the joy of all, The Sun that warms and lights us.
By His grace He doth impart Eternal sunshine to the heart;
The night of sin is ended!
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Then let us feast this Easter day On the true Bread of Heaven;
The Word of grace hath purged away The old and wicked leaven.
Christ alone our souls will feed;
He is our Meat and Drink indeed;
Faith lives upon no other!
Alleluia!

hymns.title: Christ Returneth
hymns.author: H. L. Turner
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: It may be at morn, when the day is awaking, When sunlight through darkness and shadow is breaking That Jesus will come in the fullness of glory To receive from the world "His own." O Lord Jesus, how long, how long Ere we shout the glad song, Christ returneth!
Hallelujah!
Hallelujah!
Amen.
Hallelujah!
Amen.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: It may be at midday, it may be at twilight, It may be, perchance, that the blackness of midnight Will burst into light in the blaze of His glory, When Jesus receives "His own." O Lord Jesus, how long, how long Ere we shout the glad song, Christ returneth!
Hallelujah!
Hallelujah!
Amen.
Hallelujah!
Amen.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: While hosts cry Hosanna, from heaven descending, With glorified saints and the angels attending, With grace on His brow, like a halo of glory, Will Jesus receive "His own." O Lord Jesus, how long, how long Ere we shout the glad song, Christ returneth!
Hallelujah!
Hallelujah!
Amen.
Hallelujah!
Amen.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Oh, joy! oh, delight! should we go without dying, No sickness, no sadness, no dread and no crying.
Caught up through the clouds with our Lord into glory, When Jesus receives "His own." O Lord Jesus, how long, how long Ere we shout the glad song, Christ returneth!
Hallelujah!
Hallelujah!
Amen.
Hallelujah!
Amen.

hymns.title: Christ The Lord Is Risen Today (Lyra)
hymns.author: Stanzas 1-7, Charles Wesley
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Christ, the Lord, is risen today, Alleluia!
Sons of men and angels say, Alleluia!
Raise your joys and triumphs high, Alleluia!
Sing, ye heavens, and earth, reply, Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Love's redeeming work is done, Alleluia!
Fought the fight, the battle won, Alleluia!
Lo! the Sun's eclipse is o'er, Alleluia!
Lo!
He sets in blood no more, Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Vain the stone, the watch, the seal, Alleluia!
Christ hath burst the gates of hell, Alleluia!
Death in vain forbids His rise, Alleluia!
Christ hath opened paradise, Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Lives again our glorious King, Alleluia!
Where, O death, is now thy sting?
Alleluia!
Once He died our souls to save, Alleluia!
Where thy victory, O grave?
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Soar we now where Christ hath led, Alleluia!
Following our exalted Head, Alleluia!
Made like Him, like Him we rise, Alleluia!
Ours the cross, the grave, the skies, Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Hail, the Lord of earth and Heaven, Alleluia!
Praise to Thee by both be given, Alleluia!
Thee we greet triumphant now, Alleluia!
Hail, the resurrection, thou, Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: King of glory, Soul of bliss, Alleluia!
Everlasting life is this, Alleluia!
Thee to know, Thy power to prove, Alleluia!
Thus to sing and thus to love, Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: Hymns of praise then let us sing, Alleluia!
Unto Christ, our heavenly King, Alleluia!
Who endured the cross and grave, Alleluia!
Sinners to redeem and save.
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: But the pains that He endured, Alleluia!
Our salvation have procured, Alleluia!
Now above the sky He's King, Alleluia!
Where the angels ever sing.
Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 10
lines: Jesus Christ is risen today, Alleluia!
Our triumphant holy day, Alleluia!
Who did once upon the cross, Alleluia!
Suffer to redeem our loss.
Alleluia!

hymns.title: Come Down, O Love Divine
hymns.author: Bianco of Siena d.
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come down, O love divine, seek Thou this soul of mine, And visit it with Thine own ardor glowing.
O Comforter, draw near, within my heart appear, And kindle it, Thy holy flame bestowing.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O let it freely burn, til earthly passions turn To dust and ashes in its heat consuming;
And let Thy glorious light shine ever on my sight, And clothe me round, the while my path illuming.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Let holy charity mine outward vesture be, And lowliness become mine inner clothing;
True lowliness of heart, which takes the humbler part, And o'er its own shortcomings weeps with loathing.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: And so the yearning strong, with which the soul will long, Shall far outpass the pow'r of human telling;
For none can guess its grace, till he become the place Wherein the Holy Spirit makes His dwelling.

hymns.title: Come Forth! Come On, with Solemn Song!
hymns.author: Christian Friedrich Heinrich Sachse
hymns.composer: or  Ludvig Mathias Lindeman, unknown date
hymns.tune: Jeg Har Min Sag Til Gud Hjemstilt
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come forth! come on, with solemn song!
The road is short, the rest is long!
The Lord brought here, He calls away: Make no delay, This home was for a passing day.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Here in an inn a stranger dwelt, Here joy and grief by turns he felt;
Poor dwelling, now we close thy door!
The task is o'er, The sojourner returns no more,

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Now of a lasting home possest, He goes to seek a deeper rest Goodnight! the day was sultry here, In toil and fear;
Goodnight! the night is cool and clear.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Chime on, ye bells! again begin, And ring the Sabbath morning in, The lab'rer's weekday work is done, The rest begun, Which Christ hath for His people won!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Now open to us, gates of peace!
Here let the pilgrim's journey cease.
Ye quiet slumberers, make room In your still home, For the new stranger who has come!

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: How many graves around us lie!
How many homes are in the sky!
Yes, for each saint doth Christ prepare A place with care: Thy home is waiting, brother, there!

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Jesus, Thou reignest, Lord alone, Thou wilt return and claim Thine own, Come quickly, Lord! return again!
Amen!
Amen!
Thine seal us ever, now and then!

hymns.title: Come, God Creator, Holy Ghost
hymns.author: variously ascribed to Ambrose, Gregory the Great, Charlemagne, or Rhabanus Maurus. Translated by Martin Luther
hymns.composer: ancient Latin found in Erfurt Enchiridion
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, God Creator, Holy Ghost, And visit Thou these souls of men;
Fill them with graces, as Thou dost, Thy creatures make pure a gain.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: For Comforter Thy name we call.
Sweet gift of God most high above, A holy unction to us all O Fount of life, Fire of love.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Our minds illumine and refresh, Deep in our hearts let love burn bright;
Thou know'st the weakness of our flesh;
And strengthen us with Thy might.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thou with Thy wondrous sev'nfold gifts The finger art of God's right hand;
The Father's Word Thou sendest swift On tongues of fire to each land.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Drive far from us our wily foe;
Grant us Thy blesséd peace within, That in Thy footsteps we may go, And shun the dark ways of sin.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Teach us the Father well to know, Likewise His only Son our Lord, Thyself to us believing show, Spirit of both, aye adored

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Praise to the Father, and the Son Who from the dead is risen again;
Praise to the Comforter be done Both now and ever.
Amen.

hymns.title: Come Holy Spirit, Lord Our God
hymns.author: v. 1 ancient Latin. v.2-3 Martin Luther
hymns.composer: ancient Latin found in Erfurt Enchiridion
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, Holy Spirit, Lord our God, And pour Thy gifts of grace abroad;
Thy faithful people fill with blessing, Love's fire their hearts possessing.
O Lord, Thou by Thy heav'nly Light Dost gather and in faith unite Through all the world a holy nation To sing to Thee with exultation, Hallelujah!
Hallelujah!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O, holiest Light!
O Rock adored!
Give us Thy light, Thy living Word, To God Himself our spirits leading, With Him as children pleading.
From error, Lord, our souls defend, That they on Christ alone attend;
In Him with faith unfeigned abiding, In Him with all their might confiding.
Hallelujah!
Hallelujah!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O holiest Fire!
O Source of rest!
Grant that with joy and hope possest, And in Thy service kept forever, Naught us from Thee may sever.
Lord, may Thy pow'r prepare each heart;
To our weak nature strength impart, Onward to press, our foes defying, To Thee, through living and through dying.
Hallelujah!
Hallelujah!

hymns.title: Come Thou Fount Of Every Blessing
hymns.author: Robert Robinson
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, Thou Fount of every blessing, Tune my heart to sing Thy grace;
Streams of mercy, never ceasing, Call for songs of loudest praise.
Teach me some melodious sonnet, Sung by flaming tongues above.
Praise the mount!
I'm fixed upon it, Mount of Thy redeeming love.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Sorr'wing I shall be in spirit, Till released from flesh and sin, Yet from what I do inherit, Here Thy praises I'll begin;
Here I raise my Ebenezer;
Here by Thy great help I've come;
And I hope, by Thy good pleasure, Safely to arrive at home.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Jesus sought me when a stranger, Wand'ring from the fold of God;
He, to rescue me from danger, Interposed His precious blood;
How His kindness yet pursues me Mortal tongue can never tell, Clothed in flesh, till death shall loose me I cannot proclaim it well.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O to grace how great a debtor Daily I'm constrained to be!
Let Thy goodness, like a fetter, Bind my wand'ring heart to Thee.
Prone to wander, Lord, I feel it, Prone to leave the God I love;
Here's my heart, O take and seal it, Seal it for Thy courts above.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: O that day when freed from sinning, I shall see Thy lovely face;
Clothèd then in blood washed linen How I'll sing Thy sovereign grace;
Come, my Lord, no longer tarry, Take my ransomed soul away;
Send thine angels now to carry Me to realms of endless day.

hymns.title: Come, Thou Long-Expected Jesus
hymns.author: Charles Wesley
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, Thou long expected Jesus Born to set Thy people free;
From our fears and sins release us, Let us find our rest in Thee.
Israel's Strength and Consolation, Hope of all the earth Thou art;
Dear Desire of every nation, Joy of every longing heart.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Born Thy people to de liver, Born a child and yet a King, Born to reign in us forever, Now Thy gracious kingdom bring.
By Thine own eternal Spirit Rule in all our hearts alone;
By Thine all sufficient merit, Raise us to Thy glorious throne.

hymns.title: Come, Ye Disconsolate
hymns.author: verses 1-2 Thomas Moore
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, ye disconsolate, wher'er ye languish;
Come to the mercy seat, fervently kneel.
Here bring your wounded hearts, here tell your anguish;
Earth has no sorrow that Heav'n cannot heal.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Joy of the desolate, light of the straying, Hope of the penitent, fadeless and pure, Here speaks the Comforter, tenderly saying, "Earth has no sorrow that Heav'n cannot cure."

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Here see the Bread of Life; see waters flowing Forth from the throne of God, pure from above;
Come to the feast of love; come, ever knowing Earth has no sorrow but Heav'n can remove.

hymns.title: Come, Your Heart and Voices Raising
hymns.author: Paul Gerhardt
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, your hearts and voices raising, Christ the Lord with gladness praising;
Loudly sing His love amazing, Worthy folk of Christendom.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Sin and death may well be groaning, Satan now may well be moaning;
We, our full salvation owning, Cast our every care away.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: See how God, for us providing, Gave His Son and life abiding;
He our weary steps is guiding From earth's woe to heav'nly joy.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Christ, from heav'n to us descending And in love our race befriending, In our need His help extending, Saved us from the wily Foe.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Jacob's Star in all its splendor Beams with comfort sweet and tender, Forcing Satan to surrender, Breaking all the pow'rs of hell.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: From the bondage that oppressed us, From sin's fetters that possessed us, From the grief that sore distressed us, We, the captives, now are free.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Oh, the joy beyond expressing When by faith we grasp this blessing And to Thee we come confessing, That our freedom thou hast wrought!

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: Gracious Child, we pray Thee, hear us, From Thy lowly manger cheer us, Gently lead us and be near us Till we join the angelic choir.

hymns.title: Comfort, Comfort Ye My People
hymns.author: Johann Olearius (Oelschlaeger)
hymns.composer: from Trente Quatre Pseaumes de David, Geneva
hymns.tune: Freu dich sehr, o meine Seele
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Comfort, comfort ye My people, Speak ye peace, thus saith our God;
Comfort those who sit in darkness, Mourning 'neath their sorrow's load;
Speak ye to Jerusalem Of the peace that waits for them;
Tell her that her sins I cover, And her warfare now is over.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: For the herald's voice is crying In the desert far and near, Bidding all men to repentance, Since the kingdom now is here.
O that warning cry obey!
Now prepare for God a way!
Let the valleys rise to meet Him, And the hills bow down to greet Him.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Yea, her sins our God will pardon, Blotting out each dark misdeed;
All that well deserved His anger He will no more see nor heed.
She has suffered many a day, Now her griefs have passed away, God will change her pining sadness Into ever springing gladness.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Make ye straight what long was crooked, Make the rougher places plain: Let your hearts be true and humble, As befits His holy reign, For the glory of the Lord O'er the earth is shed abroad, And all flesh shall see the token That His Word is never broken.

hymns.title: Creator of the Stars of Night
hymns.author: anonymous, 7th Century. Translated by John Mason Neale
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Creator of the stars of night, Thy people's everlasting light, Jesu, Redeemer, save us all, And hear Thy servants when they call.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thou, grieving that the ancient curse Should doom to death a universe, Hast found the med'cine, full of grace, To save and heal a ruined race.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thou cam'st the Bridegroom of the bride, As drew the world to ev'ning tide;
Proceeding from a virgin shrine, The spotless Victim all divine.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: At whose dread name, majestic now, All knees must bend, all hearts must bow;
All things celestial Thee shall own, And things terrestrial, Lord alone.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: O Thou whose coming is with dread To judge and doom the quick and dead, Preserve us while we dwell below, From every insult of the foe.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Δ To God the Father, God the Son, And God the Spirit, Three in One, Laud, honor, might, and glory be, From age to age eternally.

hymns.title: Crown Him With Many Crowns
hymns.author: Verses 1, 4, 5, 6 & 9: Matthew Bridges, The Passion of Jesus
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Crown Him with many crowns, the Lamb upon His throne.
Hark!
How the heav'nly anthem drowns all music but its own.
Awake, my soul, and sing of Him who died for thee, And hail Him as thy matchless King through all eternity.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Crown Him the virgin's Son, the God incarnate born, Whose arm those crimson trophies won which now His brow adorn;
Fruit of the mystic rose, as of that rose the stem;
The root whence mercy ever flows, the Babe of Bethlehem.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Crown Him the Son of God, before the worlds began, And ye who tread where He hath trod, crown Him the Son of Man;
Who every grief hath known that wrings the human breast, And takes and bears them for His own, that all in Him may rest.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Crown Him the Lord of life, who triumphed o'er the grave, And rose victorious in the strife for those He came to save.
His glories now we sing, Who died, and rose on high, Who died eternal life to bring, and lives that death may die.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Crown Him the Lord of peace, Whose pow'r a scepter sways From pole to pole, that wars may cease, and all be prayer and praise.
His reign shall know no end, and round His pierced feet Fair flow'rs of paradise extend their fragrance ever sweet.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Crown Him the Lord of love, behold His hands and side, Those wounds, yet visible above, in beauty glorified.
No angel in the sky can fully bear that sight, But downward bends his burning eye at mysteries so bright.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Crown Him the Lord of Heaven, enthroned in worlds above, Crown Him the King to Whom is given the wondrous name of Love.
Crown Him with many crowns, as thrones before Him fall;
