I live in Thee and Thou in me.
No sorrow fills my soul, delighted It finds its only joy in Thee.

verses.label: Verse 13
lines: Who can condemn me now?
For surely The Lord is nigh, who justifies.
No hell I fear, and thus securely With Jesus I to Heaven rise.

verses.label: Verse 14
lines: Though death may threaten with disaster, It cannot rob me of my cheer;
For He Who is of death the Master, With aid and comfort e'er is near.

verses.label: Verse 15
lines: My heart has now become Thy dwelling, O blessèd, holy Trinity.
With angels I, Thy praises telling, Shall live in joy eternally.

hymns.title: If God Had Not Been on Our Side
hymns.author: Martin Luther
hymns.tune: Wär Gott nicht mit uns diese Zeit (1524)
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: If God had not been on our side And had not come to aid us, The foes with all their pow'r and pride Would surely have dismayed us;
For we, His flock, would have to fear The threat of men both far and near Who rise in might against us.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Their furious wrath, did God permit, Would surely have consumed us And as a deep and yawning pit With life and limb entombed us.
Like men o'er whom dark waters roll Their wrath would have engulfed our soul And, like a flood, o'erwhelmed us.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Blest be the Lord, who foiled their threat That they could not devour us;
Our souls, like birds, escaped their net, They could not overpow'r us.
The snare is broken we are free!
Our help is ever, Lord, in Thee, Who madest earth and heaven.

hymns.title: If God Himself Be For Me
hymns.author: Paul Gerhardt
hymns.composer: or  traditional German found in , 17th Century
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: If God Himself be for me, I may a host defy;
For when I pray, before me My foes, confounded, fly.
If Christ, my Head and Master, Befriend me from above, What foe or what disaster Can drive me from His love?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: This I believe, yea, rather, Of this I make my boast, That God is my dear Father, The Friend who loves me most, And that, whate'er betide me, My Savior is at hand Through stormy seas to guide me And bring me safe to land.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: I build on this foundation, That Jesus and His blood Alone are my salvation, The true, eternal good.
Without Him all that pleases Is valueless on earth;
The gifts I owe to Jesus Alone my love are worth.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: My Jesus is my Splendor, My Sun, my Light, alone;
Were He not my Defender Before God's awe— full throne, I never should find favor And mercy in His sight, But be destroyed forever As darkness by the light.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: He canceled my offenses, Delivered me from death;
He is the Lord who cleanses My soul from sin through faith.
In Him I can be cheerful, Bold, and undaunted aye;
In Him I am not fearful Of God's great Judgment Day.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Naught, naught, can now condemn me Nor set my hope aside;
Now hell no more can claim me, Its fury I deride.
No sentence e'er reproves me, No ill destroys my peace;
For Christ, my Savior, loves me And shields me with His grace.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: His Spirit in me dwelleth, And o'er my mind He reigns.
All sorrow He dispelleth And soothes away all pains.
He crowns His work with blessing And helpeth me to cry, "My Father!" without ceasing, To Him who dwells on high.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: And when my soul is lying Weak, trembling, and opprest, He pleads with groans and sighing That cannot be exprest;
But God's quick eye discerns them, Although they give no sound, And into language turns them E'en in the heart's deep ground.

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: To mine His Spirit speaketh Sweet word of holy cheer, How God to him that seeketh For rest is always near And how He hath erected A city fair and new, Where what our faith expected We evermore shall view.

verses.label: Verse 10
lines: In yonder home doth flourish My heritage, my lot;
Though here I die and perish, My heaven shall fail me not.
Though care my life oft saddens And causeth tears to flow, The light of Jesus gladdens And sweetens every woe.

verses.label: Verse 11
lines: Who clings with resolution To Him whom Satan hates Must look for persecution;
For him the burden waits Of mockery, shame, and losses, Heaped on his blameless head;
A thousand plagues and crosses Will be his daily bread.

verses.label: Verse 12
lines: From me this is not hidden, Yet I am not afraid;
I leave my cares, as bidden, To whom my vows were paid.
Though life and limb it cost me And everything I won, Unshaken shall I trust Thee And cleave to Thee alone.

verses.label: Verse 13
lines: Though earth be rent asunder, Thou'rt mine eternally;
Not fire nor sword nor thunder Shall sever me from Thee;
Not hunger, thirst, nor danger, Not pain nor poverty Nor mighty princes' anger Shall ever hinder me.

verses.label: Verse 14
lines: No angel and no gladness, No throne, no pomp, no show, No love, no hate, no sadness, No pain, no depth of woe, No scheme of man's contrivance, However small or great, Shall draw me from Thy guidance Nor from Thee separate.

verses.label: Verse 15
lines: My heart for joy is springing And can no more be sad, 'Tis full of mirth and singing, Sees naught but sunshine glad.
The Sun that cheers my spirit Is Jesus Christ, my King;
That which I shall inherit Makes me rejoice and sing.

hymns.title: I Heard The Bells On Christmas Day
hymns.author: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I heard the bells on Christmas day Their old familiar carols play, And wild and sweet the words repeat Of peace on earth, good will to men.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: And thought how, as the day had come, The belfries of all Christendom Had rolled along th'unbroken song Of peace on earth, good will to men.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Till ringing, singing on its way The world revolved from night to day, A voice, a chime, a chant sublime Of peace on earth, good will to men.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: And in despair I bowed my head "There is no peace on earth," I said, "For hate is strong and mocks the song Of peace on earth, good will to men."

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Then pealed the bells more loud and deep: "God is not dead, nor doth He sleep;
The wrong shall fail, the right prevail With peace on earth, good will to men."

hymns.title: I Know That My Redeemer Lives
hymns.author: Samuel Medley
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I know that my Redeemer lives;
What comfort this sweet sentence gives!
He lives, He lives, who once was dead;
He lives, my ever living Head.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: He lives to bless me with His love, He lives to plead for me above.
He lives my hungry soul to feed, He lives to help in time of need.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: He lives triumphant from the grave, He lives eternally to save, He lives all glorious in the sky, He lives exalted there on high.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: He lives to grant me rich supply, He lives to guide me with His eye, He lives to comfort me when faint, He lives to hear my soul's complaint.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: He lives to silence all my fears, He lives to wipe away my tears He lives to calm my troubled heart, He lives all blessings to impart.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: He lives, my kind, wise, heavenly Friend, He lives and loves me to the end;
He lives, and while He lives, Ill sing;
He lives, my Prophet, Priest, and King.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: He lives and grants me daily breath;
He lives, and I shall conquer death: He lives my mansion to prepare;
He lives to bring me safely there.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: He lives, all glory to His Name!
He lives, my Jesus, still the same.
Oh, the sweet joy this sentence gives, I know that my Redeemer lives!

hymns.title: I Know Whom I Have Believed
hymns.author: Daniel Webster Whittle (a.k.a. Elias Nathan)
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I know not why God's wondrous grace To me He hath made known, Nor why, unworthy, Christ in love Redeemed me for His own.
But I know Whom I have believèd, And am persuaded that He is able To keep that which I've committed Unto Him against that day.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: I know not how this saving faith To me He did impart, Nor how believing in His Word Wrought peace within my heart.
But I know Whom I have believèd, And am persuaded that He is able To keep that which I've committed Unto Him against that day.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: I know not how the Spirit moves, Convincing us of sin, Revealing Jesus through the Word, Creating faith in Him.
But I know Whom I have believèd, And am persuaded that He is able To keep that which I've committed Unto Him against that day.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: I know not what of good or ill May be reserved for me, Of weary ways or golden days, Before His face I see.
But I know Whom I have believèd, And am persuaded that He is able To keep that which I've committed Unto Him against that day.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: I know not when my Lord may come, At night or noonday fair, Nor if I walk the vale with Him, Or meet Him in the air.
But I know Whom I have believèd, And am persuaded that He is able To keep that which I've committed Unto Him against that day.

hymns.title: I'm But A Stranger Here
hymns.author: Thomas Rawson Taylor
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I'm but a stranger here, Heav'n is my home;
Earth is a desert drear, Heav'n is my home.
Danger and sorrow stand round me on every hand;
Heav'n is my fatherland, Heav'n is my home.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: What though the tempest rage, Heav'n is my home;
Short is my pilgrimage, Heav'n is my home;
Time's wild and wintry blast soon shall be over past;
I shall reach home at last, Heav'n is my home.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: There at my Savior's side Heav'n is my home;
I shall be glorified, Heav'n is my home.
There are the good and blest, those I loved most and best;
There, too, I soon shall rest, Heav'n is my home.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Therefore I murmur not, Heav'n is my home;
Whate'er my earthly lot, Heav'n is my home;
And I shall surely stand there at my Lord's right hand.
Heav'n is my fatherland, Heav'n is my home.

hymns.title: Immortal, Invisible, God Only Wise
hymns.author: Walter Chalmers Smith
hymns.composer: or  or  traditional Welsh found in  by John Roberts
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Immortal, invisible, God only wise, In light inaccessible hid from our eyes, Most blessèd, most glorious, the Ancient of Days, Almighty, victorious, Thy great Name we praise.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Unresting, unhasting, and silent as light, Nor wanting, nor wasting, Thou rulest in might;
Thy justice, like mountains, high soaring above Thy clouds, which are fountains of goodness and love.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: To all, life Thou givest, to both great and small;
In all life Thou livest, the true life of all;
We blossom and flourish as leaves on the tree, And wither and perish but naught changeth Thee.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Great Father of glory, pure Father of light, Thine angels adore Thee, all veiling their sight;
But of Thy rich graces this grace, Lord, impart Take the veil from our face, the vile from our heart.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: All laud we would render;
O help us to see 'Tis only the splendor of light hideth Thee, And so let Thy glory, almighty, impart, Through Christ in His story, Thy Christ to the heart.

hymns.title: I Need Thee Every Hour
hymns.author: Annie Sherwood Hawks
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I need Thee ev'ry hour, Most gracious Lord;
No tender voice like Thine Can peace afford.
I need Thee, O I need Thee;
Ev'ry hour I need Thee;
O bless me now, my Savior, I come to Thee!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: I need Thee ev'ry hour, Stay Thou near by;
Temptations lose their pow'r When Thou art nigh.
I need Thee, O I need Thee;
Ev'ry hour I need Thee;
O bless me now, my Savior, I come to Thee!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: I need Thee ev'ry hour, In joy or pain;
Come quickly and abide, Or life is vain.
I need Thee, O I need Thee;
Ev'ry hour I need Thee;
O bless me now, my Savior, I come to Thee!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: I need Thee ev'ry hour, Teach me Thy will;
And Thy rich promises In me fulfill.
I need Thee, O I need Thee;
Ev'ry hour I need Thee;
O bless me now, my Savior, I come to Thee!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: I need Thee ev'ry hour, Most Holy One;
O make me Thine indeed, Thou blessed Son!
I need Thee, O I need Thee;
Ev'ry hour I need Thee;
O bless me now, my Savior, I come to Thee!

hymns.title: In His Temple Now Behold Him
hymns.author: verses 1-3, Henry J. Pye
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: In His temple now behold Him, See the long expected Lord;
Ancient prophets had foretold Him;
God has now fulfilled His word.
Now to praise Him, His redeemèd Shall break forth with one accord.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: In the arms of her who bore Him, Virgin pure, behold Him lie, While his agèd saints adore Him Ere in faith and hope they die.
Hallelujah!
Hallelujah!
Lo, th'incarnate God most high.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Jesus, by Thy presentation, Thou, who didst for us endure, Make us see our great salvation, Seal us with Thy promise sure.
And present us in Thy glory To Thy Father, cleansed and pure.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Prince and Author of salvation, Be Thy boundless love our theme!
Jesus, praise to Thee be given By the world Thou didst redeem.
With the Father and the Spirit, Lord of majesty supreme!

hymns.title: In Peace and Joy I Now Depart
hymns.author: Martin Luther
hymns.tune: Mit Fried und Freud ich Far Dahin
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: In peace and joy I now depart At God's disposing;
For full of comfort is my heart, Soft reposing.
So the Lord hath promised me, And death is but a slumber.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: 'Tis Christ that wrought this work for me, My faithful Savior, Whom Thou hast made mine eyes to see By Thy favor.
Now I know He is my Life, My Help in need and dying.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Him Thou hast unto all set forth Their great Salvation And to His kingdom called the earth, Every nation, By Thy dear and wholesome Word, In every place resounding.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: He is the Hope and saving Light Of lands benighted;
By Him are they who dwelt in night Fed and lighted.
He is Is rael's Praise and Bliss, Their Joy, Reward, and Glory.

hymns.title: In The Bleak MidWinter
hymns.author: Christina Georgina Rossetti
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: In the bleak midwinter, frosty wind made moan, Earth stood hard as iron, water like a stone;
Snow had fallen, snow on snow, snow on snow on snow, In the bleak midwinter, long ago.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Our God, Heav'n cannot hold Him, nor earth sustain;
Heav'n and earth shall flee away when He comes to reign.
In the bleak midwinter a stable place sufficed The Lord God Almighty, Jesus Christ.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Enough for Him, whom cherubim, worship night and day, Breastful of milk, and a mangerful of hay;
Enough for Him, whom angels fall down before, Ox and ass and camel which adore.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Angels and archangels may have gathered there, Cherubim and seraphim thronged the air;
But His mother only, in her maiden bliss, Worshiped the beloved with a kiss.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: What can I give Him, poor as I am?
If I were a shepherd, I would bring a lamb;
If I were a Wise Man, I would do my part;
Yet what can I give Him: give my heart.

hymns.title: In These Our Days So Perilous
hymns.author: Latin c. 6th century, adapted by Martin Luther
hymns.composer: Martin Luther. Found in Klug's Geistliche Lieder
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: In these our days so perilous, Lord, peace in mercy send us;
No God but Thee can fight for us, No God but Thee defend us;
Thou our only God and Savior.

hymns.title: I Place My Trust In God
hymns.author: verses by Dorothy Amstutz, 2007. Refrain by Loren J. Kropf, 2010. Music and
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I placed my trust in God today, For it is He who knows the way, And when uncertainties arise, I trust His judgment, true and wise.
I'll place my trust in God today, Even when I cannot see the way, He'll lead me on with His strong hand, Until I reach the promised land.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: I placed my trust in God today, Alone I could not find my way.
Led by His guidance, safe and sure, I then can rest in peace, secure.
I'll place my trust in God today, Even when I cannot see the way, He'll lead me on with His strong hand, Until I reach the promised land.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: I place my trust in God each day, Committed, yielded to His way.
Naught can o'erthrow me when I'm there Beneath His everlasting care.
I'll place my trust in God today, Even when I cannot see the way, He'll lead me on with His strong hand, Until I reach the promised land.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: I'll place my trust in God each day, And while I walk the narrow way, Each burden God will help me bear;
He hears my cry, my ev'ry prayer.
I'll place my trust in God today, Even when I cannot see the way, He'll lead me on with His strong hand, Until I reach the promised land.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: I'll place my trust in God each day, Yes, even though the skies are gray.
Led by His hand, I will not fear;
I know that God is always near.
I'll place my trust in God today, Even when I cannot see the way, He'll lead me on with His strong hand, Until I reach the promised land.

hymns.title: Isaiah, Mighty Seer, In Days of Old
hymns.author: Martin Luther
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Isaiah, mighty seer, in days of old The Lord of all in Spirit did behold High on a lofty throne, in splendor bright, With flowing train that filled the Temple quite.
Above the throne were stately seraphim, Six wings had they, these messengers of Him.

With twain they veiled their faces, as was meet, With twain in reverent awe they hid their feet, And with the other twain aloft they soared, One to the other called and praised the Lord: "Holy is God, the Lord of Sabaoth!
Holy is God, the Lord of Sabaoth!
Holy is God, the Lord of Sabaoth!
Behold, His glory filleth all the earth!" The beams and lintels trembled at the cry, And clouds of smoke enwrapped the throne on high.

hymns.title: I Sing the Mighty Power of God
hymns.author: Isaac Watts
hymns.composer: from Gesangbuch der Herzogl. Hofkapelle, Wurttemberg
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I sing the mighty pow'r of God, That made the mountains rise;
That spread the flowing seas abroad, And built the lofty skies.
I sing the wisdom that ordained The sun to rule the day: The moon shines full at His command, And all the stars obey.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: I sing the goodness of the Lord, That filled the earth with food: He formed the creatures with His word, And then pronounced them good.
Lord, how Thy wonders are displayed, Where'er I turn my eye;
If I survey the ground I tread, Or gaze upon the sky.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: There's not a plant or flow'r below, But makes Thy glories known;
And clouds arise and tempests blow, By order from Thy throne.
Creatures that borrow life from Thee Are subject to Thy care: There's not a place where we can flee, But God is present there.

hymns.title: It Came Upon A Midnight Clear
hymns.author: Edmund H. Sears
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: It came upon the midnight clear, That glorious song of old, From angels bending near the earth, To touch their harps of gold;
"Peace on the earth, good will to men, From Heaven's all gracious King." The world in solemn stillness lay, To hear the angels sing.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Still through the cloven skies they come With peaceful wings unfurled, And still their heavenly music floats O'er all the weary world;
Above its sad and lowly plains, They bend on hov'ring wing, And ever over its Babel sounds The blessèd angels sing.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Yet with the woes of sin and strife The world has suffered long;
Beneath the angel strain have rolled Two thousand years of wrong;
And man, at war with man, hears not The love— song which they bring;
O hush the noise, ye men of strife And hear the angels sing.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: And ye, beneath life's crushing load, Whose forms are bending low, Who toil along the climbing way With painful steps and slow, Look now! for glad and golden hours Come swiftly on the wing.
O rest beside the weary road, And hear the angels sing!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: For lo! the days are hast'ning on, By prophet— bards foretold, When with the ever circling years Comes round the age of gold;
When peace shall over all the earth Its ancient splendors fling, And the whole world send back the song Which now the angels sing.

hymns.title: It Is Well With My Soul(When Peace Like a River)
hymns.author: Horatio G. Spafford
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When peace, like a river, attendeth my way, When sorrows like sea billows roll;
Whatever my lot, Thou has taught me to say, It is well, it is well, with my soul.
It is well, with my soul, It is well, it is well with my soul.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Though Satan should buffet, though trials should come, Let this blest assurance control, That Christ has regarded my helpless estate, And hath shed His own blood for my soul.
It is well, with my soul, It is well, it is well with my soul.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: My sin, oh, the bliss of this glorious thought!
My sin, not in part but the whole, Is nailed to the cross, and I bear it no more, Praise the Lord, praise the Lord, O my soul!
It is well, with my soul, It is well, it is well with my soul.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: For me, be it Christ, be it Christ hence to live: If Jordan above me shall roll, No pang shall be mine, for in death as in life Thou wilt whisper Thy peace to my soul.
It is well, with my soul, It is well, it is well with my soul.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: But, Lord, 'tis for Thee, for Thy coming we wait, The sky, not the grave, is our goal;
Oh trump of the angel!
Oh voice of the Lord!
Blessèd hope, blessèd rest of my soul!
It is well, with my soul, It is well, it is well with my soul.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: And Lord, haste the day when my faith shall be sight, The clouds be rolled back as a scroll;
The trump shall resound, and the Lord shall descend, Even so, it is well with my soul.
It is well, with my soul, It is well, it is well with my soul.

hymns.title: I Walk In Danger All The Way
hymns.author: Hans Adolf Brorson
hymns.composer: or  from Freylinghausen's Geistreiches Gesangbuch, Halle
hymns.tune: Der Lieben Sonne Lich Und Pracht
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I walk in danger all the way;
The thought shall never leave me That Satan, who has marked his prey, Is plotting to deceive me.
This foe with hidden snares May seize me unawares If e'er I fail to watch and pray: I walk in danger all the way.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: I pass through trials all the way, With sin and ills contending;
In patience I must bear each day The cross of God's own sending;
Oft in adversity I know not where to flee;
When storms of woe my soul dismay, I pass through trials all the way.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Death doth pursue me all the way, Nowhere I rest securely, He comes by night, he comes by day, He takes his prey most surely;
A failing breath and I In death's strong grasp may lie To face eternity for aye: Death doth pursue me all the way.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: I walk 'mongst angels all the way They shield me and befriend me;
All Satan's pow'r is held at bay When heav'nly hosts attend me;
They are my sure defense, All fear and sorrow, hence!
Unharmed by foes, do what they may, I walk 'mongst angels all the way.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: I walk with Jesus all the way, His guidance never fails me, Within His wounds I find a stay, When Satan's pow'r assails me;
And by His footsteps led, My path I safely tread, In spite of ills that threaten may, I walk with Jesus all the way.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: My walk is heav'nward all the way, Await, my soul, the morrow, When thou shalt find release for aye From all thy sin and sorrow;
All worldly pomp, begone, To heav'n I now press on;
For all the world I would not stay, My walk is heav'nward all the way.

hymns.title: Jerusalem the Golden
hymns.author: Bernard of Cluny
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jerusalem the golden, with milk and honey blest, Beneath thy contemplation sink heart and voice oppressed.
I know not, O I know not, what joys await us there, What radiancy of glory, what bliss beyond compare.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: They stand, those halls of Zion, all jubilant with song, And bright with many'an angel, and all the martyr throng;
The Prince is ever in them, the daylight is serene.
The pastures of the blessèd are decked in glorious sheen.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: There is the throne of David, and there, from care released, The shout of them that triumph, the song of them that feast;
And they are with their Leader, who conquered in the fight, And won for them forever their gleaming robes of white.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O sweet and blessèd country, the home of God's elect!
O sweet and blessèd country, that eager hearts expect!
Jesus, in mercy bring us to that dear land of rest, Who art, with God the Father, and Spirit, ever blessed.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Brief life is here our portion, brief sorrow, short lived care;
The life that knows no ending, the tearless life, is there.
O happy retribution!
Short toil, eternal rest;
For mortals and for sinners, a mansion with the blest.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: That we should look, poor wanderers, to have our home on high!
That worms should seek for dwellings beyond the starry sky!
And now we fight the battle, but then shall wear the crown Of full and everlasting, and passionless renown.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: And how we watch and struggle, and now we live in hope, And Zion in her anguish with Babylon must cope;
But he whom now we trust in shall then be seen and known, And they that know and see Him shall have Him for their own.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: For thee, O dear, dear country, mine eyes their vigils keep;
For very love, beholding, thy happy name, they weep: The mention of thy glory is unction to the breast, And medicine in sickness, and love, and life, and rest.

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: O one, O only mansion!
O paradise of joy!
Where tears are ever banished, and smiles have no alloy;
The cross is all thy splendor, the Crucified thy praise, His laud and benediction thy ransomed people raise.

verses.label: Verse 10
lines: Jerusalem the glorious!
Glory of the elect!
O dear and future vision that eager hearts expect!
E'en now by faith I see thee, e'en here thy walls discern;
To thee my thoughts are kindled, and strive, and pant, and yearn.

verses.label: Verse 11
lines: Jerusalem, the only, that look'st from heaven below, In thee is all my glory, in me is all my woe!
And though my body may not, my spirit seeks thee fain, Till flesh and earth return me to earth and flesh again.

verses.label: Verse 12
lines: Jerusalem, exulting on that securest shore, I hope thee, wish thee, sing thee, and love thee evermore!
I ask not for my merit: I seek not to deny My merit is destruction, a child of wrath am I.

verses.label: Verse 13
lines: But yet with faith I venture and hope upon the way, For those perennial guerdons I labor night and day.
The best and dearest Father Who made me, and Who saved, Bore with me in defilement, and from defilement laved.

verses.label: Verse 14
lines: When in His strength I struggle, for very joy I leap;
When in my sin I totter, I weep, or try to weep: And grace, sweet grace celestial, shall all its love display, And David's royal fountain purge every stain away.

verses.label: Verse 15
lines: O sweet and blessèd country, shall I e'er see thy face?
O sweet and blessèd country, shall I e'er win thy grace?
I have the hope within me to comfort and to bless!
O shall I e'er win the prize?
O tell me, tell me, Yes!

hymns.title: Jesus Came, the Heavens Adoring
hymns.author: Godfrey Thring
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus came, the heav'ns adoring, Came with peace from realms on high;
Jesus came for man's redemption, Lowly came on earth to die.
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Came in deep humility.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Jesus comes again in mercy, When our hearts are bowed with care;
Jesus comes again in answer To an earnest, heartfelt prayer;
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Comes to save us from despair.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Jesus comes to hearts rejoicing, Bringing news of sins forgiv'n;
Jesus comes in sounds of gladness, Leading souls redeemed to Heav'n;
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Now the gate of death is riv'n.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Jesus comes in joy and sorrow, Shares alike our hopes and fears;
Jesus comes, whate'er befalls us, Glads our hearts, and dries our tears;
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Cheering e'en our failing years.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Jesus comes on clouds triumphant, When the heav'ns shall pass away;
Jesus comes again in glory;
Let us then our homage pay, Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Till the dawn of endless day.

hymns.title: Jesus Christ Is Risen Today
hymns.author: 14th Century Bohemian Latin carol. Stanzas 1-3 translated in John Arnold's Compleat Psalmist
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus Christ is ris'n today, Alleluia!
Our triumphant holy day, Alleluia!
Who did once, upon the cross, Alleluia!
Suffer to redeem our loss, Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Hymns of praise then let us sing, Alleluia!
Unto Christ, our heav'nly King, Alleluia!
Who endured the cross and grave, Alleluia!
Sinners to redeem and save, Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: But the pain which He endured, Alleluia!
Our salvation hath procured, Alleluia!
Now above the sky He's king, Alleluia!
Where the angels ever sing, Alleluia!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Δ Sing we to our God above, Alleluia!
Praise eternal as His love, Alleluia!
Praise Him, all you heav'nly host, Alleluia!
Father, Son, and Holy Ghost, Alleluia!

hymns.title: Jesus Christ, Our Blessed Savior
hymns.author: John Hus (
hymns.tune: Jesus Christus Unser Heiland, Der Von Uns (Walter)
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus Christ, our Blessed Savior, Turned away God's wrath forever;
Suff'ring pains no tongue can tell, He saved us from the pains of hell.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: To remind us that, to save us He hath died, His flesh He gave us With this bread, a quick'ning food, And with this wine, His precious blood.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Whoso to this board repaireth, Take good heed how He prepareth;
Death instead of life shall He Receive who comes unworthily.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Praise the Father, God in heaven, Who such dainty food hath given, And for misdeeds thou hast done Gave to die His beloved Son.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Thou shalt hold with faith unshaken, That this food is to be taken By the sick who are distressed, By those whose heart is sin— oppressed.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: To such grace and mercy turneth Every soul that truly mourneth;
Art thou well?
Avoid this board, Else thou reapest an ill reward.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Christ says: "Come ye heavy-laden, I your weary hearts will gladden;
They that are yet strong and well, Despise the best physician's skill.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: Couldst thou earn thine own salvation, Useless were my death and passion;
This feast is not spread for thee, If thine own helper thou wilt be.

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: If thou this believest truly, And confession makest duly, Thou a welcome guest art here, This heavenly food thy soul shall cheer.

verses.label: Verse 10
lines: But the fruits must not be missing, Love thy neighbor without ceasing, That true love let him receive, Which here to thee thy God doth give.

hymns.title: Jesus Christ Who Came to Save
hymns.author: Martin Luther
hymns.tune: Jesus Christus Unser Heiland, Der Den Tod (Klug)
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus Christ, who came to save, And overcame the grave, Is now arisen, And sin hath bound in prison.
Kyri' eleison.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Who withouten sin was found, Bore our transgression's wound.
He is our Savior, And brings us to God's favor.
Kyri' eleison.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Life and mercy, sin and death, All in His hands He hath;
Them He'll deliver, Who trust in Him forever.
Kyri' eleison.

hymns.title: Jesus Grant That Balm and Healing
hymns.author: Johann Heermann
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus, grant that balm and healing In Thy holy wounds I find, Every hour that I am feeling, Pains of body and of mind.
Should some evil thought within Tempt my treacherous heart to sin, Show the peril, and from sinning Keep me ere its first beginning.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Should some lust or sharp temptation Prove too strong for flesh and blood, Let me think upon Thy Passion, And the breach is soon made good.
Or should Satan press me hard, Let me then be on my guard, Saying, "Christ for me was wounded," That the tempter flee confounded.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: If the world my heart entices On the broad and easy road With its mirth and luring vices, Let me think upon the load Thou didst carry and endure That I flee all thoughts impure, Banishing each wild emotion, Calm and blest in my devotion.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Every wound that pains or grieves me, By Thy stripes, Lord, is made whole;
When I'm faint, Thy cross revives me, Granting new life to my soul.
Yea, Thy comfort renders sweet Every bitter cup I meet;
For Thy all atoning Passion Has procured my soul's salvation.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: O my God, my Rock and Tower, Grant that in Thy death I trust, Knowing death has lost his power Since Thou trodd'st him in the dust.
Savior, let Thine agony Ever help and comfort me;
When I die, be my Protection, Light and Life and Resurrection.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Jesus, grant that balm and healing In Thy holy wounds I find, Every hour that I am feeling, Pains of body and of mind.
And when I this world must leave, Grant that, Lord, to Thee I cleave, In Thy wounds find consolation And obtain my soul's salvation

hymns.title: Jesus, In Thy Dying Woes
hymns.author: Thomas B. Pollock
hymns.tune: Words on the Cross - The Litany
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus, in Thy dying woes, Even while Thy lifeblood flows, Craving pardon for Thy foes;
Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Savior, for our pardon sue, When our sins Thy pangs renew, For we know not what we do: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O may we, who mercy need, Be like Thee in heart and deed, When with wrong our spirits bleed: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Jesus, pitying the sighs Of the thief, who near Thee dies, Promising him paradise;
Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: May we, in our guilt and shame, Still Thy love and mercy claim, Calling humbly on Thy Name: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: O remember us who pine, Looking from our cross to Thine;
Cheer our souls with hope divine: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Jesus, loving to the end Her whose heart Thy sorrows rend, And Thy dearest human friend: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: May we in Thy sorrows share, And for Thee all peril dare, And enjoy Thy tender care: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: May we all Thy loved ones be, All one holy family, Loving for the love of Thee, Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 10
lines: Jesus, whelmed in fears unknown, With our evil left alone, While no light from Heav'n is shown: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 11
lines: When we vainly seem to pray, and our hope seems far away, In the darkness be our stay.
Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 12
lines: Though no Father seem to hear, Though no light our spirits cheer, Tell our faith that God is near: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 13
lines: Jesus, in Thy thirst and pain, While Thy wounds Thy lifeblood drain, Thirsting more our love to gain: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 14
lines: Thirst for us in mercy still;
All Thy holy work fulfill;
Satisfy Thy loving will: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 15
lines: May we thirst Thy love to know;
Lead us in our sin and woe Where the healing waters flow: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 16
lines: Jesus, all our ransom paid, All Thy Father's will obeyed, All Thy suff'rings perfect made: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 17
lines: Save us in our soul's distress, Be our help to cheer and bless, While we grow in holiness: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 18
lines: Brighten all our heavenward way, With an ever holier ray, Till we pass to perfect day: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 19
lines: Jesus, all Thy labor vast, All Thy woe and conflict past, Yielding up Thy soul at last: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 20
lines: When the death shades round us lower, Guard us from the temper's power, Keep us in that trial hour: Hear us, holy Jesus.

verses.label: Verse 21
lines: May Thy life and death supply Grace to live and grace to die, Grace to reach the home on high: Hear us, holy Jesus.

hymns.title: Jesus Is All the World To Me
hymns.author: Will L. Thompson
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus is all the world to me, My life, my joy, my all;
He is my strength from day to day, Without Him I would fall.
When I am sad, to Him I go, No other one can cheer me so;
When I am sad, He makes me glad, He's my friend.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Jesus is all the world to me, my friend in trials sore;
I go to Him for blessings and He gives them ov'r and o'er.
He sends the sunshine and the rain, He sends the harvest's golden grain;
Sunshine and rain, harvest of grain, He's my friend.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Jesus is all the world to me, and true to Him I'll be;
O how could I this friend deny, when He's so true to me?
Following Him I know I'm right, He watches o'er me day and night;
Following Him, by day and night, He's my friend.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Jesus is all the world to me, I want no better friend;
I trust Him now, I'll trust Him when Life's fleeting days shall end.
Beautiful life with such a friend; beautiful life that has no end;
Eternal life, eternal joy, He's my friend.

hymns.title: Jesus, King of Glory
hymns.author: W. Hope Davison
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus, King of glory, throned above the sky, Jesus, tender Savior, hear Thy children cry.
Pardon our transgressions, cleanse us from our sin, By Thy Spirit help us heav'nly life to win.
Jesus, King of glory, throned above the sky, Jesus, tender Savior, hear Thy children cry.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: On this day of gladness, Bending low the knee In thine earthly temple, Lord, we worship thee;
Celebrate thy goodness, Mercy, grace, and truth, All thy loving guidance Of our heedless youth.
Jesus, King of glory, throned above the sky, Jesus, tender Savior, hear Thy children cry.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: For the little children Who have come to thee;
For the glad, bright spirits Who thy glory see;
For the loved ones resting In thy dear embrace;
For the pure and holy Who behold thy face, Jesus, King of glory, throned above the sky, Jesus, tender Savior, hear Thy children cry.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Help us ever steadfast in the faith to be: In Thy Church's conflicts fighting valiantly.
Loving Savior, strengthen these weak hearts of ours, Through Thy cross to conquer crafty evil powers.
Jesus, King of glory, throned above the sky, Jesus, tender Savior, hear Thy children cry.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: When the shadows lengthen, show us, Lord, Thy way;
Through the darkness lead us to the heav'nly day;
When our course is finished, ended all the strife, Grant us, with the faithful, palms and crowns of life.
Jesus, King of glory, throned above the sky, Jesus, tender Savior, hear Thy children cry.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: For thy faithful servants Who have entered in;
For thy fearless soldiers Who have conquered sin;
For the countless legions Who have followed thee, Heedless of the danger, On to victory,

hymns.title: Jesus Loves Me
hymns.author: Anna B. Warner
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus loves me!
This I know, For the Bible tells me so.
Little ones to Him belong;
They are weak, but He is strong.
Yes, Jesus loves me!
Yes, Jesus loves me!
Yes, Jesus loves me!
The Bible tells me so.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Jesus loves me!
He who died Heaven's gate to open wide;
He will wash away my sin, Let His little child come in.
Yes, Jesus loves me!
Yes, Jesus loves me!
Yes, Jesus loves me!
The Bible tells me so.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Jesus loves me!
He will stay Close beside me all the way;
Thou hast bled and died for me, I will henceforth live for Thee.
Yes, Jesus loves me!
Yes, Jesus loves me!
Yes, Jesus loves me!
The Bible tells me so.

hymns.title: Jesus Shall Reign
hymns.author: Isaac Watts
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus shall reign where'er the sun Does his successive journeys run;
His kingdom stretch from shore to shore Till moons shall wax and wane no more.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: For Him shall endless prayer be made, And praises throng to crown His head;
His Name, like sweet perfume shall rise With every morning sacrifice.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: People and realms of every tongue Dwell on His love with sweetest song;
And infant voices shall proclaim Their early blessings on His Name.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Blessings abound where'er He reigns;
The prisoner leaps to lose his chains, The weary find eternal rest, And all the sons of want are blest.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Where He displays His healing power Death and the curse are known no more;
In Him the tribes of Adam boast More blessings than their father lost.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Let every creature rise and bring Peculiar honors to our King, Angels descend with songs again, And earth repeat the loud Amen.

hymns.title: Jesus Sinners Doth Receive
hymns.author: Erdmann Neumeister
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus sinners doth receive;
Well may we this saying ponder Who in sin's delusions live, And from God and Heaven wander: Here is hope for all who grieve— Jesus sinners doth receive.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: We deserve but grief and shame,— Yet His words, rich grace revealing, Pardon, peace, and life proclaim;
Here their ills have perfect healing Who with humble hearts believe— Jesus sinners doth receive.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: As the shepherd seeks to find His lost sheep that from Him strayeth, So hath Christ each soul in mind, And for its salvation prayeth;
Fain He'd have each wand'rer live: Jesus sinners doth receive.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Come, then, all by guilt oppressed, Jesus calls, and He would make you God's own children, pure and blest, And to glory He would take you;
Think on this, and well believe Jesus sinners doth receive.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Savior, now I come to Thee: Great my sins, a weary burden!
Wilt Thou mercy show to me?
Can I hope to find a pardon?
I will trust: my soul relieve!
Me, a sinner, Lord, receive!

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Rich Thy mercy— strangely good!
O how oft have I offended!
But through Thy redeeming blood All my fear of wrath is ended: Yes I now can witness give: Jesus sinners doth receive!

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Now my conscience is at peace;
From the Law I stand acquitted;
Christ hath purchased my release, And my every sin remitted.
Naught remains my soul to grieve: Jesus sinners doth receive.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: Jesus sinners doth receive!
Happy in His ceaseless favor, Here for Heaven I will live, There shall live with Him forever, "Joy in death these tidings give;
Jesus sinners doth receive!"

hymns.title: Jesus, Tender Shepherd, Hear Me
hymns.author: Mary L. Duncan
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus, tender Shepherd, hear me;
Bless Thy little lamb tonight;
Through the darkness be Thou near me;
Watch my sleep till morning light.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: All this day thy hand has led me, And I thank Thee for Thy care;
Thou hast clothed me, warmed and fed me, Listen to my evening prayer.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Let my sins be all forgiven;
Bless the friends I love so well;
Take me, when I die, to heaven, Happy there with Thee to dwell.

hymns.title: Jesus, Thy Boundless Love To Me
hymns.author: Paul Gerhardt
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus, Thy boundless love to me No thought can reach, no tongue declare;
Unite my thankful heart with Thee And reign without a rival there.
To Thee alone, dear Lord, I live;
Myself to Thee, dear Lord, I give.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O, grant that nothing in my soul May dwell but Thy pure love alone!
Oh, may Thy love possess me whole, My joy, my treasure, and my crown!
All coldness from my heart remove;
My every act, word, thought, be love.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O love, how cheering is thy ray!
All pain before thy presence flies;
Care, anguish, sorrow, melt away Where'er thy healing beams arise.
O Jesus, nothing may I see, Nothing desire or seek, but Thee!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: This love unwearied I pursue And dauntlessly to Thee aspire.
Oh, may Thy love my hope renew Burn in my soul like heav'nly fire!
And day and night be all my care To guard this sacred treasure there.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: My Savior, Thou Thy love to me In shame, in want, in pain, hast showed;
For me, on the accursed tree, Thou pourest forth Thy guiltless blood;
Thy wounds upon my heart impress, Nor aught shall the lov'd stamp efface.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: More hard than marble is my heart, And foul with sins of deepest stain;
But Thou the mighty Savior art, Nor flowed thy cleansing blood in vain;
Ah soften, melt this rock, and may Thy blood wash all these stains away!

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: O that I, as a little child, May follow Thee, and never rest Till sweetly Thou hast breathed Thy mild And lowly mind into my breast!
Nor ever may we parted be, Till I become as one with Thee.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: Still let Thy love point out my way;
How wondrous things Thy love hath wrought!
Still lead me, lest I go astray;
Direct my word, inspire my thought;
And if I fall, soon may I hear Thy voice, and know that love is near.

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: In suffering be Thy love my peace, In weakness be Thy love my power;
And when the storms of life shall cease, Jesus, in that important hour, In death as life be Thou my guide, And save me, Who for me hast died.

hymns.title: Jesus Wants All Of His Children
hymns.author: verses 1-5 Mrs. James W. Wood
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus wants all of His children to be good and true;
He will help if we but ask Him, help us gladly too.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Jesus was so meek and lowly, As a little child.
We will try to be like Jesus, gentle, patient, mild.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Jesus wants us to be loving, helping when we may.
We will try to be kindhearted, merciful each day.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: "Blessed are the pure in heart," thus the Savior taught.
Clean in mind, in soul, in body, pure in every thought.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: When we find our friends are angry, we will to them say, "Jesus wants you to be loving, each and every day."

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: When I know that I have done wrong, to Jesus I flee.
He has paid for all transgressions, He has paid for me!

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Jesus knew that we would fail Him so He came to be Life to all the poor and lost ones, life eternally!

hymns.title: Joyful, Joyful, We Adore Thee
hymns.author: Henry J. van Dyke
hymns.composer: Ludwig van Beethoven; Adapted by Edward Hodges
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Joyful, joyful, we adore Thee, God of glory, Lord of love;
Hearts unfold like flowers before Thee, opening to the sun above.
Melt the clouds of sin and sadness; drive the dark of doubt away;
Giver of immortal gladness, fill us with the light of day!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: All Thy works with joy surround Thee, earth and heaven reflect Thy rays, Stars and angels sing around Thee, center of unbroken praise.
Field and forest, vale and mountain, flowery meadow, flashing sea, Singing bird and flowing fountain call us to rejoice in Thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thou art giving and forgiving, ever blessing, ever blessed, Wellspring of the joy of living, ocean depth of happy rest!
Thou our Father, Christ our Brother, all who live in love are Thine;
Teach us how to love each other, lift us to the joy divine.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Mortals, join the happy chorus, which the morning stars began;
Father love is reigning o'er us, brother love binds man to man.
Ever singing, march we onward, victors in the midst of strife, Joyful music leads us Sunward in the triumph song of life.

hymns.title: Joy to the World
hymns.author: Isaac Watts
hymns.composer: pieced together from  George F. Handel
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Joy to the world, the Lord is come!
Let earth receive her King;
Let ev'ry heart prepare Him room, And Heav'n and nature sing, And Heav'n and nature sing, And Heav'n, and Heav'n, and nature sing.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Joy to the earth, the Savior reigns!
Let men their songs employ;
While fields and floods, rocks, hills and plains Repeat the sounding joy, Repeat the sounding joy, Repeat, repeat, the sounding joy.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: No more let sins and sorrows grow, Nor thorns infest the ground;
He comes to make His blessings flow Far as the curse is found, Far as the curse is found, Far as, far as, the curse is found.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: He rules the world with truth and grace, And makes the nations prove The glories of His righteousness, And wonders of His love, And wonders of His love, And wonders, wonders, of His love.

hymns.title: Just As I Am Without One Plea
hymns.author: Charlotte Elliott
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Just as I am, without one plea, But that Thy blood was shed for me, And that Thou biddest me come to Thee, O Lamb of God, I come, I come.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Just as I am, and waiting not To rid my soul of one dark blot, To Thee, Whose blood can cleanse each spot, O Lamb of God, I come, I come.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Just as I am, though tossed about With many'a conflict, many'a doubt, Fightings and fears within, without, O Lamb of God, I come, I come.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Just as I am, poor, wretched, blind;
Sight riches healing of the mind, Yea, all I need, in Thee to find, O Lamb of God, I come, I come.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Just as I am, Thou wilt receive, Wilt welcome, pardon, cleanse, relieve, Because Thy promise I believe, O Lamb of God, I come, I come.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Just as I am, Thy love unknown Has broken every barrier down;
Now to be Thine, yea Thine alone, O Lamb of God, I come, I come.

hymns.title: Lamp of Our Feet
hymns.author: Bernard Barton
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lamp of our feet! whereby we trace Our path, when wont to stray;
Stream from the fount of heav'nly grace!
Brook by the trav'ler's way!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Bread of our souls! whereon we feed;
True manna from on high!
Our guide, and chart wherein we read Of realms beyond the sky.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Pillar of fire— through watches dark!
Or radiant cloud by day!
When waves would whelm our tossing bark— Our anchor and our stay!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Pole— star on life's tempestuous deep!
Beacon! when doubts surround;
Compass! by which our course we keep;
Our deep sea— lead, to sound!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Riches in poverty! our aid In every needful hour!
Unshaken rock! the pilgrim's shade;
The soldier's fortress tow'r.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Our shield and buckler in the fight!
Vict'ry's triumphant palm!
Comfort in grief! in weakness, might!
In sickness, Gilead's balm.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Childhood's preceptor! manhood's trust!
Old age's firm ally!
Our hope—when we go down to dust, Of immortality.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: Pure oracles of truth divine!
Unlike each fabled dream Given forth from Delphos' mystic shrine Or groves of Academe!

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: Word of the ever-living God!
Will of His glorious Son!
Without Thee, how could earth be trod?
Or Heaven itself be won?

verses.label: Verse 10
lines: Yet to unfold thy hidden worth, Thy mysteries to reveal, That Spirit which first gave thee forth, Thy volume must unseal!

verses.label: Verse 11
lines: And we, if we aright would learn The wisdom it imparts, Must to its heavenly teaching turn With simple, child-like hearts!

hymns.title: Let All Mortal Flesh Keep Silence
hymns.author: from Liturgy of St. James, 4th Century. Translated by Gerard Moultrie
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Let all mortal flesh keep silence, And with fear and trembling stand;
Ponder nothing earthly minded, For with blessing in His hand, Christ our God to earth descendeth, Our full homage to demand.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: King of kings, yet born of Mary, As of old on earth He stood, Lord of lords, in human vesture, In the body and the blood;
He will give to all the faithful His own self for heav'nly food.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Rank on rank the host of heaven Spreads its vanguard on the way, As the Light of light descendeth From the realms of endless day, That the pow'rs of hell may vanish As the darkness clears away.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: At His feet the six wingèd seraph, Cherubim with sleepless eye, Veil their faces to the presence, As with ceaseless voice they cry: Alleluia, Alleluia Alleluia, Lord Most High!

hymns.title: Let Children Hear The Mighty Deeds
hymns.author: Isaac Watts
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Let children hear the mighty deeds Which God performed of old;
Which in our younger years we saw, And which our fathers told.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: He bids us make His glories known, His works of pow'r and grace;
And we'll convey His wonders down Through every rising race.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Our lips shall tell them to our sons, And they again to theirs;
That generations yet unborn May teach them to their heirs.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thus shall they learn in God alone Their hope securely stands, That they may ne'er forget His work But practice His commands.

hymns.title: Let Our Gladness Have No End
hymns.author: From the Kancional, by Tobias Zavorka
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Let our gladness have no end, Hallelujah!
For to earth did Christ descend.
Hallelujah!
On this day God gave us Christ, His Son, to save us;
Christ, His Son, to save us.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: See, the loveliest blooming rose, Hallelujah!
From the branch of Jesse grows.
Hallelujah!
On this day God gave us Christ, His Son, to save us;
Christ, His Son, to save us.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Into flesh is made the Word.
Hallelujah!
He, our refuge and our Lord.
Hallelujah!
On this day God gave us Christ, His Son, to save us;
Christ, His Son, to save us.

hymns.title: Lift High The Cross
hymns.author: George W. Kitchin (
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: From north and south, from east and west they raise in growing unison their songs of praise.
Refrain

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: O Lord, once lifted on the glorious tree, as thou hast promised, draw the world to thee.
Refrain

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: So shall our song of triumph ever be: Praise to the Crucified for victory.
Refrain

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: Let every race and every language tell of him who saves our souls from death and hell.
Refrain

verses.label: Verse 10
lines: From farthest regions let their homage bring, and on his Cross adore their Savior King.
Refrain

verses.label: Verse 11
lines: Set up thy throne, that earth's despair may cease beneath the shadow of its healing peace.
Refrain

verses.label: Verse 12
lines: For thy blest Cross which doth for all atone creation's praises rise before thy throne.
Refrain

hymns.title: Lift Up Your Heads, Ye Mighty Gates
hymns.author: Georg Weissel
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lift up your heads, ye mighty gates!
Behold, the King of glory waits;
The King of kings is drawing near, The Savior of the world is here;
Life and salvation doth He bring, Wherefore rejoice and gladly sing: We praise Thee, Father, now!
Creator, wise art Thou!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The Lord is just, a Helper tried, Mercy is ever at His side, His kingly crown is holiness, His scepter, pity in distress, The end of all our woe He brings;
Wherefore the earth is glad and sings: We praise Thee, Savior, now!
Mighty in deed art Thou!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O blest the land, the city blest, Where Christ the Ruler is confessed!
O happy hearts and happy homes To whom this King in triumph comes!
The cloudless Sun of joy He is, Who bringeth pure delight and bliss.
O Comforter divine, What boundless grace is Thine!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Fling wide the portals of your heart;
Make it a temple, set apart From earthly use for heav'ns employ, Adorned with prayer and love and joy;
So shall your Sovereign enter in, and new and nobler life begin;
To Thee, O God, be praise, For Word, and deed, and grace!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Redeemer, come!
I open wide My heart to Thee; here, Lord, abide!
Let me Thy inner presence feel, Thy grace and love in me reveal.
Thy Holy Spirit guide us on, Until our glorious goal be won!
Eternal praise and fame We offer to Thy Name.

hymns.title: Like The Golden Sun Ascending
hymns.author: Thomas Hansen Kingo
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Like the golden sun ascending, Breaking through the gloom of night, On the earth His glory spending So that darkness takes to flight;
Thus my Jesus from the grave And death's dismal, dreadful cave, Rose triumphant Easter morning, At the early purple dawning.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thanks to Thee, O Christ victorious!
Thanks to Thee, O Lord of life!
Death has now no power o'er us, Thou hast conquered in the strife;
Thanks because Thou didst arise And hast opened paradise!
None can fully sing the glory Of the resurrection story.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: For my heart finds consolation, And my fainting soul grows brave, When I stand in contemplation, At Thy dark and dismal grave;
When I see where Thou didst sleep In death's dungeon dark and deep, Yet didst break all bands asunder, Must I not rejoice and wonder?

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Though I be by sin o'ertaken, Though I lie in helplessness, Though I be by friends forsaken, And must suffer sore distress, Though I be despised, contemned, And by all the world condemned, Though the dark grave yawn before me, Yet the light of hope shines o'er me.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Thou hast died for my transgression, All my sins on Thee were laid;
Thou hast won for me salvation, On the cross my debt was paid;
From the grave I shall arise, And shall meet Thee in the skies;
Death itself is transitory, I shall lift my head in glory.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Satan's arrows all lie broken, Death and hell have met their doom;
Christ, Thy rising is the token: Thou hast triumphed o'er the tomb;
Thou hast buried all my woe, And my cup doth overflow;
By Thy resurrection glorious I shall wave my palms victorious.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: As the Son of God I know Thee, For I see Thy sovereign pow'r;
Sin and death shall not o'erthrow me Even in my dying hour;
For Thy resurrection is Surety for my heav'nly bliss, And my bapt'sm a reflection Of Thy death and resurrection

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: Unto life Thou shalt arouse me By Thy resurrection's pow'r;
Though the hideous grave shall house me, And my flesh the worms devour;
Fire and water may destroy My frail body, yet with joy I shall rise as Thou hast risen From the deep sepulchral prison.

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: Grant me grace, O blessed Savior, And Thy Holy Spirit send, That my walk and my behavior May be pleasing in the end;
That I may not fall again Into death's grim pit and pain When by grace Thou hast received me, And from which Thou hast relieved me.

verses.label: Verse 10
lines: For the joy Thy birth doth give me, For Thy holy, precious Word;
For Thy baptism which doth save me, For Thy gracious festal board;
For Thy death, the bitter scorn, For Thy resurrection morn, Lord I thank Thee and extol Thee, And in heav'n I shall behold Thee.

hymns.title: Lo, God To Heav'n Ascendeth
hymns.author: Gottfried Wilhelm Sacer
hymns.tune: Von Gott will ich Nicht Lazen
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lo, God to Heav'n ascendeth!

Throughout its regions vast With shouts triumphant blendeth the trumpet's thrilling blast: Sing praise to Christ the Lord; sing praise with exultation, King of each heathen nation, the God of Hosts adored!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: With joy is Heav'n resounding Christ's glad return to see;
Behold the saints surrounding the Lord who set them free.
Bright myriads, thronging, come; the cherub band rejoices, And loud seraphic voices Welcome Messiah home.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: No more the way is hidden, Since Christ our Head arose: No more to man forbidden The road to Heav'n that goes.
Our Lord is gone before; yet here He will not leave us, And soon in Heav'n receive us and open wide the door.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Christ is our place preparing; to Heav'n we, too, shall rise, And, joys angelic sharing, Be where our treasure lies: There may each heart be found!
Where Jesus Christ has entered, There let our Hope be centered;
Our course still heav'nward bound.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: May we, His servants, thither In heart and mind ascend, And let us sing together: "We seek Thee, Christ, our friend, Thee, God's exalted Son, our Life, and Way to Heaven, To whom all pow'r is given, our Joy and Hope and Crown."

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Farewell with all thy treasures, O world, to falsehood giv'n!
Thy dross gives no true pleasures;
We seek the joys of Heav'n: The Savior is our Prize;
He comforts us in sadness, And fills our hearts with gladness; to Him we lift our eyes.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: When, on our vision dawning, Will break the wished-for hour Of that all glorious morning, When Christ shall come with power?
O come, thou welcome day!
When we, our Savior meeting, His second advent greeting, Shall hail the heav'n-sent ray.

hymns.title: Lo! He Comes With Clouds Descending
hymns.author: John Cennick
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lo!
He comes with clouds descending, Once for ev'ry sinner slain;
Thousand thousand saints attending, Swell the triumph of His train: Hallelujah!
Hallelujah!
Hallelujah!
God appears on earth to reign.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Ev'ry eye shall now behold Him Robed in dreadful majesty;
Those who set at naught and sold Him, Pierced and nailed Him to the tree, Deeply wailing, deeply wailing, deeply wailing, Shall the true Messiah see.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Ev'ry island, sea, and mountain, Heav'n and earth, shall flee away;
All who hate Him must, confounded, Hear the trump proclaim the day: Come to judgment!
Come to judgment!
Come to judgment!
Come to judgment!
Come away!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Now redemption, long expected, See in solemn pomp appear;
All His saints, by man rejected, Now shall meet Him in the air: Hallelujah!
Hallelujah!
Hallelujah!
See the day of God appear!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Answer Thine own bride and Spirit, Hasten, Lord, the gen'ral doom!
The new Heav'n and earth inherit, Take Thy pining exiles home: All creation, all creation, all creation, Travails! groans! and bids Thee come!

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: The dear tokens of His passion Still His dazzling body bears;
Cause of endless exultation To His ransomed worshipers;
With what rapture, with what rapture, with what rapture Gaze we on those glorious scars!

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Yea, Amen! let all adore Thee, High on Thine eternal throne;
Savior, take the power and glory, Claim the kingdom for Thine own;
O come quickly!
O come quickly!
O come quickly!
Everlasting God, come down!

hymns.title: Lo He Comes! Whom Every Nation
hymns.author: Christopher Wordsworth
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lo He comes! whom every nation, Taught of God, desired to see, Filled with hope and expectation That He would their Savior be.
Sing, o sing with exultation, Lo!
He calls us to our home;
Peace, redemption, joy, salvation, Now from Heav'n to earth are come.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: See He comes!
Whom kings and sages, Prophets, patriarchs of old, Disant climes and countless ages, Waited eager to behold.
Sing, o sing with exultation, Haste we to our Father's home;
Peace, redemption, joy, salvation, Now from Heav'n to earth are come.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Δ See the Lamb of God appearing, God of God, from Heav'n above;
See the Heav'nly Bridegroom cheering His dear Bride with words of love!
Glory to th'Eternal Father, Glory to th'Incarnate Son Glory to the Holy Spirit, Glory to the Three in One!

hymns.title: Lo, How A Rose E'er Blooming
hymns.author: verses 1-2, 15th Century German. Translated by Theodore Baker
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lo, how a Rose e'er blooming from tender stem hath sprung!
Of Jesse's lineage coming, as men of old have sung.
It came, a flow'ret bright, amid the cold of winter, When half spent was the night.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Isaiah 'twas foretold it, the Rose I have in mind;
With Mary we behold it, the virgin mother kind.
To show God's love aright, she bore to men a Savior, When half spent was the night.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The shepherds heard the story proclaimed by angels bright, How Christ, the Lord of glory was born on earth this night.
To Bethlehem they sped and in the manger found Him, As angel heralds said.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: This Flow'r, whose fragrance tender with sweetness fills the air, Dispels with glorious splendor the darkness everywhere;
True Man, yet very God, from sin and death He saves us, And lightens ev'ry load.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: O Savior, Child of Mary, who felt our human woe, O Savior, King of glory, who dost our weakness know;
Bring us at length we pray, to the bright courts of Heaven, And to the endless day!

hymns.title: Look Down, O Lord, From Heaven Behold
hymns.author: Martin Luther
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Look down, O Lord, from heav'n behold, And let thy pity waken!
How few the flock within thy fold, Neglected and forsaken!
Almost thou'lt seek for faith in vain, And those who should thy truth maintain Thy Word from us have taken.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: With frauds which they themselves invent Thy truth they have confounded;
Their hearts are not with one consent On thy pure doctrine grounded;
And, whilst they gleam with outward show, They lead thy people to and fro, In error's maze astounded.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: God surely will uproot all those With vain deceits who store us, With haughty tongue who God oppose, And say, "Who'll stand before us?
By right or might we will prevail;
What we determine cannot fail, For who can lord it o'er us?"

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: For this, saith God, I will arise, These wolves my flock are rending;
I've heard my people's bitter sighs To heav'n my throne ascending: Now will I up, and set at rest Each weary soul by fraud opprest, The poor with might defending.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: The silver sev'n times tried is pure From all adulteration;
So, through God's word, shall men endure Each trial and temptation: Its worth gleams brighter through the cross, And, purified from human dross, It shines through every nation.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Thy truth thou wilt preserve, O Lord, From this vile generation;
Make us to lean upon thy word, With calm anticipation.
The wicked walk on every side When, 'mid thy flock, the vile abide In pow'r and exaltation.

hymns.title: Lord As To Thy Dear Cross
hymns.author: John Hampden Gurney
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, as to Thy dear cross we flee, And plead to be forgiv'n, So let Thy life our pattern be, And form our souls for heav'n.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Help us, through good report and ill, Our daily cross to bear: Like Thee, to do our Father's will, Our brethren's grief to share.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Let grace our selfishness expel, Our earthliness refine;
And kindness in our bosoms dwell, As free and true as Thine.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: If joy shall at Thy bidding fly, And grief's dark day come on, We in our turn would meekly cry, "Father, Thy will be done."

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Kept peaceful in the midst of strife, Forgiving and forgiv'n, O may we lead the pilgrim's life And follow Thee to heav'n.

hymns.title: Lord, Be Thy Word My Rule
hymns.author: Christopher Wordsworth
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, be Thy Word my rule, In it may I rejoice;
Thy glory be my aim, Thy holy will my choice;

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thy promises my hope;
Thy providence my guard;
Thine arm my strong support;
Thyself my great reward.

hymns.title: Lord, Dismiss Us With Thy Blessing
hymns.author: John Fawcett
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, dismiss us with Thy blessing;
Fill our hearts with joy and peace;
Let us each Thy love possessing, Triumph in redeeming grace.
O refresh us, O refresh us, Trav'ling through this wilderness.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thanks we give and adoration For Thy Gospel's joyful sound;
May the fruits of Thy salvation In our hearts and lives abound.
Ever faithful, ever faithful, To the truth may we be found.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: So that when Thy love shall call us, Savior, from the world away, Fear of death shall not appall us, Glad Thy summons to obey.
May we ever, may we ever, Reign with Thee in endless day.

hymns.title: Lord, Enthroned in Heavenly Splendor
hymns.author: George H. Bourne
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, enthroned in heav'nly splendor, First begotten from the dead, You alone, our strong Defender, Now lift up Your people's head.
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Jesus, true and living Bread!
Jesus, true and living Bread!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Here our humblest homage pay we, Here in loving reference bow;
Here for faith's discernment pray we, Lest we fail to know You now.
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Thou art here, we ask not how.
Thou art here, we ask not how.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Though the lowliest form doth veil Thee As of old in Bethlehem, Here as there Thine angels hail Thee, Branch and Flow'r of Jesse's stem.
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
We in worship join with them.
We in worship join with them.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Paschal Lamb, Your offering finished Once for all when You were slain, In its fullness undiminished Shall forevermore remain.
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Cleansing souls from every stain.
Cleansing souls from every stain.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Great High Priest of our profession, Through the veil Thou wentest in, By Thy mighty intercession, Grace and peace for us to win.
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Jesus, true and living Bread!
Jesus, true and living Bread!

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Life imparting heavenly Manna, Smitten Rock with streaming side, Heaven and earth with loud hosanna Worship You, the Lamb Who died.
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Alleluia!
Risen, Ascended, Glorified!

hymns.title: Lord Jesus Think On Me
hymns.author: Synesius of Cyrene, circa 430. Translated by Allen W. Chatfield
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord Jesus, think on me And purge away my sin;
From earthborn passions set me free And make me pure within.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lord Jesus, think on me, With many'a care oppressed;
Let me Thy loving servant be And taste Thy promised rest.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Lord Jesus, think on me Amid the battle's strife;
In all my pain and misery Be Thou my Health and Life.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Lord Jesus, think on me Nor let me go astray;
Through darkness and perplexity Point Thou the heav'nly way.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Lord Jesus, think on me When floods the tempest high;
When on doth rush the enemy, O Savior, be Thou nigh!

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Lord Jesus, think on me That, when the flood is past, I may th'eternal brightness see And share Thy joy at last.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Δ Lord Jesus, think on me That I may sing above To Father, Spirit, and to Thee The strains of praise and love.

hymns.title: Lord, Keep Us Steadfast in Thy Word
hymns.author: Martin Luther
hymns.composer: from KlugGeistliche Lieder auffs new gebessert"
hymns.tune: Erhalt Uns, Herr, bei deinem Wort
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, keep us steadfast in Thy Word;
Curb those who fain by craft and sword Would wrest the kingdom from Thy Son And set at naught all He hath done.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lord Jesus Christ, Thy pow'r make known, For Thou art Lord of lords alone;
Defend Thy Christendom that we May evermore sing praise to Thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O Comforter of priceless worth, Send peace and unity on earth.
Support us in our final strife And lead us out of death to life.

hymns.title: Lord of Glory, Who Hast Bought Us
hymns.author: Eliza S. Alderson
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord of Glory, who hast bought us with Thy lifeblood as the price, Never grudging for the lost ones that tremendous sacrifice;
And with that hast freely given blessings countless as the sand, To th'unthankful and the evil with Thine own unsparing hand.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Grant us hearts, dear Lord, to yield Thee gladly, freely, of Thine own.
With the sunshine of Thy goodness melt our thankless hearts of stone.
Till our cold and selfish natures, warmed by Thee, at length believe That more happy and more blessed 'tis to give than to receive.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Wondrous honor hast Thou given to our humblest charity.
In Thine own mysterious sentence, "Ye have done it unto Me." Can it be, O gracious Master, Thou dost deign for alms to sue, Saying by Thy poor and needy, "Give as I have given you"

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Lord of Glory, who hast bought us with Thy lifeblood as the price, Never grudging for the lost ones that tremendous sacrifice;
Give us faith, to trust Thee boldly; hope, to stay our souls on Thee;
But O, best of all Thy graces, give us Thine own charity.

hymns.title: Lord of Life, All Praise Excelling
hymns.author: Clement C. Moore
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord of life, all praise excelling, Thou, in glory unconfined, Deignst to make Thy humble dwelling With the poor of humble mind.
As Thy love, through all creation, Beams like Thy diffusive light;
So the high and humble station Both are equal in Thy sight.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thus Thy care, for all providing, Warmed Thy faithful prophet's tongue;
Who, the lot of all deciding, To Thy chosen Israel sung.
When Thy harvest yields Thee pleasure, Thou the golden sheaf shalt bind;
To the poor belongs the treasure Of the scattered ears behind.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Still we read Thy Word declaring Mercy, Lord, Thine own decree;
Mercy ev'ry sorrow sharing, Warms the heart resembling Thee.
Still the orphan and the stranger, Still the widow owns Thy care;
Screened by Thee in ev'ry danger, Heard by Thee in ev'ry prayer.

hymns.title: Lord Of Our Life
hymns.author: Matthäus Apelles von Löwenstern
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord of our life, and God of our salvation, Star of our night, and Hope of every nation, Hear and receive Thy church's supplication, Lord God Almighty.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: See round Thine Ark the hungry billows curling!
See how Thy foes their banners are unfurling!
Lord, while their darts envenomed they are hurling, Thou canst preserve us.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Lord, Thou canst help when earthly armor faileth;
Lord, Thou canst save when sin itself assaileth;
Lord, o'er Thy Rock nor death nor hell prevaileth;
Grant us Thy peace, Lord.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Peace, in our hearts, our evil thoughts assuaging, Peace, in Thy Church, where brothers are engaging, Peace, when the world its busy war is waging;
Calm Thy foes raging!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Grant us Thy help till backward they are driven;
Grant them Thy Truth, that they may be forgiven;
Grant peace on earth, and after we have striven, Peace in Thy Heaven.

hymns.title: Lord, Thee I Love with All My Heart
hymns.author: Martin Schalling
hymns.tune: Herzlich Lieb hab ich Dich O Herr
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, Thee I love with all my heart;
I pray Thee, ne'er from me depart;
With tender mercy cheer me.
Earth has no pleasure I would share, Yea, Heav'n itself were void and bare If Thou, Lord, wert not near me.
And should my heart for sorrow break, My trust in Thee can nothing shake.
Thou art the portion I have sought;
Thy precious blood my soul has bought.
Lord Jesus Christ, My God and Lord, my God and Lord, Forsake me not!
I trust Thy Word.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Yea, Lord, 'twas Thy rich bounty gave My body, soul, and all I have In this poor life of labor.
Lord, grant that I in every place May glorify Thy lavish grace And serve and help my neighbor.
Let no false doctrine me beguile, Let Satan not my soul defile.
Give strength and patience unto me To bear my cross and follow Thee.
Lord Jesus Christ, My God and Lord, my God and Lord, In death Thy comfort still afford.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Lord, let at last Thine angels come, To Abr'am's bosom bear me home, That I may die unfearing;
And in its narrow chamber keep My body safe in peaceful sleep Until Thy reappearing.
And then from death awaken me, That these mine eyes with joy may see, O Son of God, Thy glorious face, My Savior and my fount of grace.
Lord Jesus Christ, My prayer attend, my prayer attend, And I will praise Thee without end!

hymns.title: Lord, Who at Cana's Wedding Feast
hymns.author: Adelaide Thrupp
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
