Unheard by mortals are the strains That sweetly soothe the Savior's woe.

hymns.title: To Avert From Men God's Wrath
hymns.author: Latin
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: To avert from men God's wrath Jesus suffered in our stead;
By an ignominious death He a full atonement made;
And by His most precious blood, Brought us, sinners, nigh to God.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: That we never should forget This great Love on us bestowed, He gave us His flesh to eat, and to drink His precious blood;
All who sick and needy are May receive in Him a share.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Hither each afflicted soul May repair, though filled with grief;
To the sick, not to the whole, the Physician brings relief;
Fear not, therefore, but draw nigh, Christ will all your wants supply.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: He who in self— righteousness Fixes any hope or stay, Has not on a wedding dress, And with shame is sent away;
To the hungry, weary heart, He will food and rest impart.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: But examine first your case, Whether you be in the faith;
Do you long for pard'ning grace?
Is your only hope His death?
Then, howe'er your soul's oppressed, Come, you are a worthy guest.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: He who Jesus' mercy knows, Is from wrath and envy freed;
Love unto our neighbor shows That we are His flock indeed;
Thus we may in all our ways Show forth our Redeemer's praise.

hymns.title: Today Thy Mercy Calls Me
hymns.author: Oswald Allen
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Today Thy mercy calls me to wash away my sin;
However great my trespass, whate'er I may have been;
However long from mercy I may have turned away, Thy blood, O Christ, can wash me, and make me clean today.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Today Thy gate is open, and all who enter in Shall find a Father's welcome, and pardon for their sin;
The past shall be forgotten, a present joy be given;
A future grace be promised, a glorious crown in Heav'n.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Today the Father calls me, the Holy Spirit waits, The blessèd angels gather around the heav'nly gates;
No question will be asked me, how often I have come;
Although I oft have wandered, it is my Father's home.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O all embracing mercy, thou ever open door, What shall I do without thee when heart and eyes run o'er?
When all things seem against me, to drive me to despair, I know one gate is open, one ear will hear my prayer.

hymns.title: To God Be The Glory
hymns.author: Fanny Crosby
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: To God be the glory, great things He has done;
So loved He the world that He gave us His Son Who yielded His life an atonement for sin, And opened the life gate that all may go in.
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord, Let the earth hear His voice!
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord, Let the people rejoice!
O come to the Father, through Jesus the Son, And give Him the glory, great things He has done.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O perfect redemption, the purchase of blood, To every believer the promise of God;
The vilest offender who truly believes, That moment from Jesus a pardon receives.
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord, Let the earth hear His voice!
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord, Let the people rejoice!
O come to the Father, through Jesus the Son, And give Him the glory, great things He has done.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Great things He has taught us, great things He has done, And great our rejoicing through Jesus the Son;
But purer, and higher, and greater will be Our wonder, our transport, when Jesus we see.
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord, Let the earth hear His voice!
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord, Let the people rejoice!
O come to the Father, through Jesus the Son, And give Him the glory, great things He has done.

hymns.title: To Jordan Came Our Lord, The Christ
hymns.author: Martin Luther
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: To Jordan came our Lord the Christ, To do God's pleasure willing, And there was by Saint John baptized, All righteousness fulfilling;
There did He consecrate a bath To wash away transgression, And quench the bitterness of death By His own blood and passion;
He would a new life give us.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: So hear ye all, and well perceive What God doth call baptism, And what a Christian should believe Who error shuns and schism: That we should water use, the Lord Declareth it His pleasure;
Not simple water, but the Word And Spirit without measure;
He is the true Baptizer.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: To show us this, He hath His Word With signs and symbols given;
On Jordan's banks was plainly heard The Father's voice from Heaven: "This is My well— beloved Son, In whom My soul delighteth;
Hear Him." Yea, hear Him every one Whom He Himself inviteth, Hear and obey His teaching.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: In tender manhood Jesus straight To holy Jordan wendeth;
The Holy Ghost from Heaven's gate In dovelike shape descendeth;
That thus the truth be not denied, Nor should our faith e'er waver, That the Three Persons all preside, At baptism's holy laver, And dwell with the believer.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Thus Jesus His disciples sent: Go teach ye every nation, That lost in sin they must repent;
And flee from condemnation: He that believes and is baptized, Obtains a mighty blessing;
A newborn man, no more he dies, Eternal life possessing, A joyful heir of Heaven.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Who in this mercy hath not faith, Nor aught therein discerneth, Is yet in sin, condemned to death, And fire that ever burneth;
His holiness avails him not, Nor aught which he is doing;
His inborn sin brings all to naught, And maketh sure his ruin;
Himself he cannot succor.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: The eye of sense alone is dim, And nothing sees but water;
Faith sees Christ Jesus, and in Him The Lamb ordained for slaughter;
She sees the cleansing fountain red With the dear blood of Jesus, Which from the sins inherited From fallen Adam frees us, And from our own misdoings.

hymns.title: To Shepherds As They Watched By Night
hymns.author: Martin Luther
hymns.composer: traditional German from Schumann's Geistliche Lieder, Leipzig
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: To shepherds as they watched by night Appeared a host of angels bright;
Behold the tender Babe, they said, In yonder lowly manger laid.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: At Bethlehem, in David's town, As Micah did of old make known;
'Tis Jesus Christ, your Lord and King, Who doth to all salvation bring.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Oh, then rejoice that through His Son God is with sinners now at one;
Made like yourselves of flesh and blood, Your Brother is th'eternal God.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: What harm can sin and death then do?
The true God now abides with you.
Let hell and Satan rage and chafe, Christ is your Brother ye are safe.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Not one He will or can forsake Who Him his confidence doth make.
Let all his wiles the Tempter try, You may his utmost pow'rs defy.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Ye shall and must at last prevail;
God's own ye are, ye cannot fail.
To God forever sing your praise With joy and patience all your days.

hymns.title: Twas In The Moon of Wintertime
hymns.author: Jean de Brébeuf
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: 'Twas in the moon of wintertime, When all the birds had fled, That mighty Gitchi Manitou Sent angel choirs instead;
Before their light the stars grew dim, And wand'ring hunters heard the hymn: Jesus your King is born, Jesus is born, In excelsis gloria.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Within a lodge of broken bark The tender babe was found, A ragged robe of rabbit skin Enwrapped His beauty 'round;
But as the hunter braves drew nigh, The angel song rang loud and high: Jesus your King is born, Jesus is born, In excelsis gloria.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The earliest moon of wintertime Is not so round and fair As was the ring of glory on The helpless Infant there.
The chiefs from far before Him knelt With gifts of fox and beaver pelt.
Jesus your King is born, Jesus is born, In excelsis gloria.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Oh children of the forest free, Oh sons of Manitou, The holy Child of earth and Heav'n Is born today for you.
Come kneel before the radiant Boy, Who brings you beauty, peace and joy.
Jesus your King is born, Jesus is born, In excelsis gloria.

hymns.title: 'Twas On That Dark, That Doleful Night
hymns.author: Isaac Watts
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: 'Twas on that dark, that doleful night When pow'rs of earth and hell arose Against the Son of God's delight, And friends betrayed Him to His foes:

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Before the mournful scene began, He took the bread, and blessed, and brake: What love through all His actions ran!
What wondrous words of grace He spake!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: "This is My body, broke for sin;
Receive and eat the living food:" Then took the cup, and blessed the wine;
"'Tis the new cov'nant in My blood."

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: For us His flesh with nails was torn, He bore the scourge, He felt the thorn;
And justice poured upon His head Its heavy vengeance in our stead.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: For us His vital blood was spilt, To buy the pardon of our guilt, When, for black crimes of biggest size, He gave His soul a sacrifice.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: "Do this," He cried, "till time shall end, In memory of your dying friend;
Meet at My table, and record The love of your departed Lord."

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Jesus, Thy feast we celebrate, We show Thy death, we sing Thy name, Till Thou return, and we shall eat The marriage supper of the Lamb.

hymns.title: Under His Wings
hymns.author: William Orcutt Cushing
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Under His wings I am safely abiding, Though the night deepens and tempests are wild, Still I can trust Him;
I know He will keep me, He has redeemed me, and I am His child.
Under His wings, under His wings, Who from His love can sever?
Under His wings my soul shall abide, Safely abide forever.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Under His wings, what a refuge in sorrow!
How the heart yearningly turns to His rest!
Often when earth has no balm for my healing, There I find comfort, and there I am blessed.
Under His wings, under His wings, Who from His love can sever?
Under His wings my soul shall abide, Safely abide forever.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Under His wings, oh, what precious enjoyment!
There will I hide till life's trials are o'er;
Sheltered, protected, no evil can harm me, Resting in Jesus, I'm safe evermore.
Under His wings, under His wings, Who from His love can sever?
Under His wings my soul shall abide, Safely abide forever.

hymns.title: Upon The Cross Extended
hymns.author: Paul Gerhardt
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Upon the cross extended, See, world, thy Lord suspended, Thy Savior yields His breath.
The Prince of Life from heaven Himself hath freely given To shame and blows and bitter death.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Come hither now and ponder, 'Twill fill thy soul with wonder, Blood streams from every pore.
Through grief whose depth none knoweth, From His great heart there floweth Sigh after sigh of anguish o'er.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Who is it that hath bruised Thee?
Who hath so sore abused Thee And caused Thee all Thy woe?
While we must make confession Of sin and dire transgression, Thou deeds of evil dost not know.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: I caused Thy grief and sighing By evils multiplying As countless as the sands.
I caused the woes unnumbered With which Thy soul is cumbered, Thy sorrows raised by wicked hands.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: 'Tis I who should be smitten My doom should here be written: Bound hand and foot in hell.
The fetters and the scourging, The floods around Thee surging, 'Tis I who have deserved them well.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: The load Thou takest on Thee, That pressed so sorely on me, it crushed me to the ground.
The cross for me enduring, The crown for me securing, My healing in Thy wounds is found.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: A crown of thorns Thou wearest, My shame and scorn Thou bearest, That I might ransomed be.
My Bondsman, ever willing, My place with patience filling, From sin and guilt hast made me free.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: Into death's jaws Thou springest, Deliv'rance to me bringest From such a monster dire.
My death away Thou takest, Thy grave its grave Thou makest;
Of love, O unexampled fire!

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: Thy cords of love, my Savior, Bind me to Thee forever, I am no longer mine.
To Thee I gladly tender All that my life can render And all I have to Thee resign.

verses.label: Verse 10
lines: Not much can I be giving In this poor life I'm living, But one thing do I say: Thy death and sorrows ever, Till soul from body sever, My heart remember shall for aye.

verses.label: Verse 11
lines: Thy cross I'll place before me, Its saving power be o'er me, Wherever I may be;
Thine innocence revealing, Thy love and mercy sealing, The pledge of truth and constancy.

verses.label: Verse 12
lines: How God at our transgression To anger gives expression, How loud His thunder rolls, How fearfully He smiteth, How sorely He requiteth,- All this Thy sufferings teach my soul.

verses.label: Verse 13
lines: From them shall I be learning, How I may be adorning, My heart with quietness, And how I still should love them Whose malice aye doth move them To grieve me by their wickedness.

verses.label: Verse 14
lines: When evil men revile me, With wicked tongues defile me, I'll curb my vengeful heart.
The unjust wrong I'll suffer, Unto my neighbor offer Forgiveness for each bitter smart.

verses.label: Verse 15
lines: I'll on the cross unite me To Thee, what doth delight me I'll there renounce for aye.
Whate'er Thy Spirit's grieving, There I'll for aye be leaving, As much as in my strength doth lay.

verses.label: Verse 16
lines: Thy groaning and thy sighing, Thy bitter tears and dying, With which Thou wast opprest,- They shall, when life is ending, Be guiding and attending My way to Thine eternal rest.

hymns.title: Wake, Awake, for Night Is Flying
hymns.author: Phillipp Nicolai
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Wake, awake, for night is flying;
The watchmen on the heights are crying: Awake, Jerusalem, at last!
Midnight hears the welcome voices And at the thrilling cry rejoices;
Come forth, ye virgins, night is past;
The Bridegroom comes, awake;
Your lamps with gladness take;
Alleluia!
And for His marriage feast prepare For ye must go and meet Him there.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Zion hears the watchmen singing, And all her heart with joy is springing;
She wakes, she rises from her gloom;
For her Lord comes down all glorious, The strong in grace, in truth victorious.
Her Star is ris'n, her Light is come.
Ah come, Thou blessed One, God's own beloved Son: Alleluia!
We follow till the halls we see Where Thou hast bid us sup with Thee

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Now let all the heav'ns adore Thee, And saints and angels sing before Thee, With harp and cymbal's clearest tone;
Of one pearl each shining portal, Where we are with the choir immortal Of angels round Thy dazzling throne;
Nor eye hath seen, nor ear hath yet attained to hear What there is ours, but we rejoice and sing to Thee Our hymn of joy eternally.

hymns.title: We All Believe in One True God
hymns.author: Medieval text expanded by Martin Luther
hymns.composer: or  Medieval tune altered by Martin Luther. Found in Walter's Geistliche Gesangbüchlein
hymns.tune: Wir Glauben all an Einen Gott, Schoepfer
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: We all believe in one true God, Who created earth and heaven, The Father, who to us in love Hath the right of children given.
He both soul and body feedeth, All we need He doth provide us;
He through snares and perils leadeth, Watching that no harm betide us.
He careth for us day and night, All things are governed by His might.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: We all believe in Jesus Christ, His own Son, our Lord, possessing An equal Godhead, throne, and might, Source of every grace and blessing.
Born of Mary, virgin mother, By the power of the Spirit, Made true man, our elder Brother, That the lost might life inherit;
Was crucified for sinful men And raised by God to life again.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: We all confess the Holy Ghost, Who sweet grace and comfort giveth And with the Father and the Son In eternal glory liveth;
Who the Church, His own creation, Keeps in unity of spirit.
Here forgiveness and salvation Daily come through Jesus' merit.
All flesh shall rise, and we shall be In bliss with God eternally.

hymns.title: We Gather Together
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: We gather together to ask the Lord's blessing;
He chastens and hastens His will to make known.
The wicked oppressing now cease from distressing.
Sing praises to His Name;
He forgets not His own.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Beside us to guide us, our God with us joining, Ordaining, maintaining His kingdom divine;
So from the beginning the fight we were winning;
Thou, Lord, were at our side, all glory be Thine!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: We all do extol Thee, Thou Leader triumphant, And pray that Thou still our Defender will be.
Let Thy congregation escape tribulation;
Thy Name be ever praised!
O Lord, make us free!

hymns.title: We Give Thee But Thine Own
hymns.author: William W. How
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: We give Thee but Thine own, Whate'er the gift may be;
All that we have is Thine alone, A trust, O Lord, from Thee.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: May we Thy bounties thus As stewards true receive, And gladly, as Thou blessest us, To Thee our firstfruits give.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O hearts are bruised and dead, And homes are bare and cold, And lambs for whom the Shepherd bled Are straying from the fold.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: To comfort and to bless, To find a balm for woe, To tend the lone and fatherless Is angels' work below.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: The captive to release, To God the lost to bring, To teach the way of life and peace?
It is a Christlike thing.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: And we believe Thy Word, Though dim our faith may be;
Whate'er for Thine we do, O Lord, We do it unto Thee.

hymns.title: We Know Thee Who Thou Art
hymns.author: William Bright
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: We know Thee who Thou art, Lord Jesus, Mary's Son;
We know the yearnings of Thy heart To end Thy work begun.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: That sacred fount of grace, 'Mid all the bliss of Heav'n, Has joy whene'er we seek Thy face, And kneel to be forgiv'n.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Brought home from ways perverse, At peace Thine arms within, We pray Thee, shield us from the curse Of falling back to sin.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: We dare not ask to live Henceforth from trials free;
But oh! when next they tempt us, give More strength to cling to Thee.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: We know Thee who Thou art, Our own redeeming Lord;
Be Thou by will, and mind, and heart, Accepted, loved, adored.

hymns.title: We Now Implore God the Holy Ghost
hymns.author: v.1 ancient German. verses 2-4 Martin Luther
hymns.composer: ancient German found in Walter's Geistliche Gesangbüchlein
hymns.tune: Nun Bitten wir den Heiligen Geist
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: We now implore God the Holy Ghost For the true faith, which we need the most, That in our last moments He may befriend us And, as homeward we journey, attend us.
Lord, have mercy.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Shine in our hearts, O most precious Light, That we Jesus Christ may know aright, Clinging to our Savior, whose blood hath bought us.
Who again to our homeland hath brought us.
Lord, have mercy.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thou sacred Love, grace on us bestow, Set our hearts with heav'nly fire aglow That with hearts united we love each other, Of one mind, in peace with ev'ry brother.
Lord, have mercy!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thou highest Comfort in ev'ry need, Grant that neither shame nor death we heed, That e'en then our courage may never fail us When the Foe shall accuse and assail us.
Lord, have mercy!

hymns.title: Were You There?
hymns.author: African-American Spiritual, before
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Were you there when they crucified my Lord?
Were you there when they crucified my Lord?
Sometimes it causes me to tremble, tremble, tremble.
Were you there when they crucified my Lord?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Were you there when they nailed Him to the tree?
Were you there when they nailed Him to the tree?
Sometimes it causes me to tremble, tremble, tremble.
Were you there when they nailed Him to the tree?

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Were you there when they pierced Him in the side?
Were you there when they pierced Him in the side?
Oh!
Sometimes it causes me to tremble, tremble, tremble.
Were you there when they pierced Him in the side?

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Were you there when the sun refused to shine?
Were you there when the sun refused to shine?
Sometimes it causes me to tremble, tremble, tremble.
Were you there when the sun refused to shine?

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Were you there when they laid Him in the tomb?
Were you there when they laid Him in the tomb?
Sometimes it causes me to tremble, tremble, tremble.
Were you there when they laid Him in the tomb?

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Were you there when God raised Him from the tomb?
Were you there when God raised Him from the tomb?
Oh!
Sometimes it causes me to tremble, tremble, tremble.
Were you there when God raised Him from the tomb?

hymns.title: What A Friend We Have In Jesus
hymns.author: Joseph M. Scriven
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: What a Friend we have in Jesus, all our sins and griefs to bear!
What a privilege to carry everything to God in prayer!
O what peace we often forfeit, O what needless pain we bear, All because we do not carry everything to God in prayer.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Have we trials and temptations?
Is there trouble anywhere?
We should never be discouraged; take it to the Lord in prayer.
Can we find a friend so faithful who will all our sorrows share?
Jesus knows our every weakness; take it to the Lord in prayer.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Are we weak and heavy laden, cumbered with a load of care?
Precious Savior, still our refuge, take it to the Lord in prayer.
Do your friends despise, forsake you?
Take it to the Lord in prayer!
In His arms He'll take and shield you; you will find a solace there.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Blessed Savior, Thou hast promised Thou wilt all our burdens bear May we ever, Lord, be bringing all to Thee in earnest prayer.
Soon in glory bright unclouded there will be no need for prayer Rapture, praise and endless worship will be our sweet portion there.

hymns.title: What Child Is This?
hymns.author: William Chatterton Dix
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: What Child is this who, laid to rest On Mary's lap is sleeping?
Whom angels greet with anthems sweet, While shepherds watch are keeping?
This, this is Christ the King, Whom shepherds guard and angels sing;
Haste, haste, to bring Him laud, The Babe, the Son of Mary.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Why lies He in such mean estate, Where ox and ass are feeding?
Good Christians, fear, for sinners here The silent Word is pleading.
Nails, spear shall pierce Him through, The cross be borne for me, for you.
Hail, hail the Word made flesh, The Babe, the Son of Mary.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: So bring Him incense, gold and myrrh, Come peasant, king to own Him;
The King of kings salvation brings, Let loving hearts enthrone Him.
Raise, raise a song on high, The virgin sings her lullaby.
Joy, joy for Christ is born, The Babe, the Son of Mary.

hymns.title: What Wondrous Love Is This?
hymns.author: Anonymous before
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: What wondrous love is this;
O my soul!
O my soul!
What wondrous love is this;
O my soul!
What wondrous love is this;
That caused the Lord of bliss To bear the dreadful curse, For my soul, For my soul, To bear the dreadful curse, For my soul?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: When I was sinking down, sinking down, sinking down When I was sinking down, sinking down When I was sinking down, Beneath God's righteous frown, Christ laid aside His crown, For my soul!
For my soul!
Christ laid aside His crown, For my soul!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Ye winged seraphs fly!
Bear the news, Bear the news, Ye winged seraphs fly!
Bear the news, Ye winged seraphs fly!
Like comets through the sky, Fill vast eternity with the news, with the news.
Fill vast eternity with the news.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: To God and to the Lamb, I will sing, I will sing, To God and to the Lamb, I will sing, To God and to the Lamb, And to the Great I AM, While millions join the theme, I will sing.
I will sing.
While millions join the theme, I will sing.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Come friends of Zion's King, Join the praise, Join the praise, Come friends of Zion's King, Join the praise, Come friends of Zion's King, With hearts and voices sing, And strike each tuneful string In His praise!
In His praise!
And strike each tuneful string In His praise!

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Thus, while from death we're free, we'll sing on, we'll sing on, Thus, while from death we're free, we'll sing on, Thus, while from death we're free, We'll sing and joyful be, And through eternity We'll sing on.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: And when to that bright world we arise, we arise And when to that bright world we arise, When to that world we go, Free from all pain and woe, We'll join the happy throng, And sing on!

hymns.title: When I Survey the Wondrous Cross
hymns.author: Isaac Watts
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When I survey the wondrous cross On which the Prince of Glory died, My rich-est gain I count but loss, And pour contempt on all my pride.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Forbid it, Lord, that I should boast, Save in the death of Christ my God!
All the vain things that charm me most, I sacrifice them to His blood.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: See from His head, His hands, His feet, Sorrow and love flow mingled down!
Did e'er such love and sorrow meet, Or thorns compose so rich a crown?

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: His dying crimson, like a robe, Spreads o'er His body on the tree;
Then I am dead to all the globe, And all the globe is dead to me.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Were the whole realm of nature mine, That were a present far too small;
Love so amazing, so divine, Demands my soul, my life, my all.

hymns.title: When The Roll Is Called Up Yonder
hymns.author: James Milton Black
hymns.tune: When The Roll Is Called Up Yonder
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When the trumpet of the Lord shall sound, and time shall be no more, And the morning breaks, eternal, bright and fair;
When the saved of earth shall gather over on the other shore, And the roll is called up yonder, I'll be there.
When the roll is called up yonder, When the roll is called up yonder, When the roll is called up yonder, When the roll is called up yonder, I'll be there.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: On that bright and cloudless morning when the dead in Christ shall rise, And the glory of His resurrection share;
When His chosen ones shall gather to their home beyond the skies, And the roll is called up yonder, I'll be there.
When the roll is called up yonder, When the roll is called up yonder, When the roll is called up yonder, When the roll is called up yonder, I'll be there.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Let us labor for the Master from the dawn till setting sun, Let us talk of all His wondrous love and care;
Then when all of life is over, and our work on earth is done, And the roll is called up yonder, I'll be there.
When the roll is called up yonder, When the roll is called up yonder, When the roll is called up yonder, When the roll is called up yonder, I'll be there.

hymns.title: Where He Leads Me
hymns.author: Edward (or Ernest) William Blandy
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I can hear my Savior calling, I can hear my Savior calling, I can hear my Savior calling, "Take thy cross and follow, follow Me." Where He leads me I will follow, Where He leads me I will follow, Where He leads me I will follow;
I'll go with Him, with Him, all the way.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: I'll go with Him through the garden, I'll go with Him through the garden, I'll go with Him through the garden, I'll go with Him, with Him all the way.
Where He leads me I will follow, Where He leads me I will follow, Where He leads me I will follow;
I'll go with Him, with Him, all the way.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: I'll go with Him through the judgment, I'll go with Him through the judgment, I'll go with Him through the judgment, I'll go with Him, with Him all the way.
Where He leads me I will follow, Where He leads me I will follow, Where He leads me I will follow;
I'll go with Him, with Him, all the way.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: He will give me grace and glory, He will give me grace and glory, He will give me grace and glory, And go with me, with me all the way.
Where He leads me I will follow, Where He leads me I will follow, Where He leads me I will follow;
I'll go with Him, with Him, all the way.

hymns.title: While O'er Our Guilty Land, O Lord
hymns.author: verses 1-3, Samuel Davies
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: While o'er our guilty land, O Lord, We view the terrors of Your sword, O where can all the helpless fly?
To whom but You direct their cry?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: On You, our guardian God, we call;
Before Your throne of grace we fall;
But is there no deliv'rance there?
And must we perish in despair?

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: See, we repent, we weep, we mourn;
To our forsaken God we turn;
O spare our guilty country, spare The Church which You have planted there.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: We are the old, the sick, the lame;
We blind and deaf cry Jesus' Name.
We plead Your mercy for His sake, And trust that You will not forsake.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Christ is our only righteousness, His death for us is our redress.
Your promise stronger than our sin;
Blot out O Lord what we have been.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: We are your children after all, Redeemed by Christ after the fall.
Marked with Your Name in Baptism, Our home remains in Your Kingdom.

hymns.title: Whiter Than Snow
hymns.author: James Nicholson
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord Jesus, I long to be perfectly whole;
I want Thee forever to live in my soul, Break down ev'ry idol, cast out ev'ry foe;
Now wash me and I shall be whiter than snow.
Whiter than snow, yes, whiter than snow;
Now wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lord Jesus, look down from Thy throne in the skies, And help me to make a complete sacrifice;
I give up myself, and whatever I know, Now wash me and I shall be whiter than snow.
Whiter than snow, yes, whiter than snow;
Now wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Lord Jesus, for this I most humbly entreat, I wait, blessed Lord, at Thy crucified feet, By faith, for my cleansing, I see Thy blood flow, Now wash me and I shall be whiter than snow.
Whiter than snow, yes, whiter than snow;
Now wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Lord Jesus, Thou seest I patiently wait, Come now, and within me a new heart create;
To those who have sought Thee, Thou never said'st ``No,'' Now wash me and I shall be whiter than snow.
Whiter than snow, yes, whiter than snow;
Now wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.

hymns.title: Why Should Cross and Trial Grieve Me?
hymns.author: Paul Gerhardt
hymns.composer: or  or  or  Johann Georg Ebeling
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Why should cross and trial grieve me?
Christ is near With His cheer;
Never will He leave me.
Who can rob me of the heaven That God's Son, For my own, To my faith hath given?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Nought not e'en the life I'm living, Is mine own, God alone All to me is giving.
Must I then His own restore Him?
Though bereft Of my flesh Still shall I adore Him.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Though a heavy cross I'm bearing, And my heart Feels the smart, Shall I be despairing?
God can help me, who doth send it, He doth know All my woe And how best to end it.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: God oft gives me days of gladness, Shall I grieve If He give Seasons too of sadness?
God is good, and tempers ever All my ill, And He will Wholly leave me never.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Though united world and devil, All their pow'r Can no more Do but mock and quibble.
Let them now employ their jeering, Christ will here Then appear Strike their hearts with fearing.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: True believers shrinking never, Where they dwell Should reveal Their true colors ever.
When approaching death may scare them, Still should they Patient stay And with courage bear them.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Death can never kill us even, But relief From all grief To us then is given.
It doth close life's mournful story, Make a way That we may Pass to heav'nly glory.

verses.label: Verse 8
lines: There I'll reap enduring pleasure, After woe Here below Suffered in large measure.
Lasting good we find here never, All that earth Deems as worth Vanishes for ever.

verses.label: Verse 9
lines: What is all this life possesses?
But a hand Full of sand That the heart distresses.
Noble gifts that pall me never, Christ so free There gives me To enjoy for ever.

verses.label: Verse 10
lines: Shepherd!
Lord! joy's fountain ever, Thou art mine, I am Thine, No one can us sever.
I am Thine, because Thou bought me;
Lost I stood, But Thy blood Free salvation brought me.

verses.label: Verse 11
lines: Thou art mine;
I love and own Thee, Light of joy, Ne'er shall I, From my heart dethrone Thee.
Savior, let me soon behold Thee Face to face, Thy embrace May it soon enfold me!

hymns.title: Wilt Thou, O Man, Live Happily
hymns.author: Martin Luther
hymns.composer: attr. Martin Luther. Found in Walter's Geistliche Gesangbüchlein
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Wilt thou, O man, live happily, And dwell with God eternally, The ten commandments keep, for thus Our God Himself biddeth us.
Kyr' eleison!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: I am the Lord and God! take heed No other god doth thee mislead;
Thy heart shall trust alone in Me, My kingdom then thou shalt be.
Kyr' eleison!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Honor My Name in word and deed, And call on Me in time of need: Hallow the Sabbath, that I may Work in thy heart on that day.
Kyr' eleison!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Obedient always, next to Me, To father and to mother be;
Kill no man: even anger dread;
Keep sacred thy marriage bed.
Kyr' eleison!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Steal not, nor do thy neighbor wrong By bearing witness with false tongue;
Thy neighbor's wife desire thou not, Nor grudge him aught he hath got.
Kyr' eleison!

hymns.title: Within the Father's House
hymns.author: James Russell Woodford
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Within the Father's house The Son hath found His home;
And to His temple suddenly The Lord of Life hath come.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The doctors of the law Gaze on the wondrous child, And marvel at His gracious words Of wisdom undefiled.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Yet not to them is giv'n The mighty truth to know, To lift the fleshly veil which hides Incarnate God below.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The secret of the Lord Escapes each human eye, And faithful pond'ring hearts await The full epiphany.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Lord, vis-it Thou our souls, And teach us by Thy grace Each dim revealing of Thyself With loving awe to trace;

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Till from our darkened sight The cloud shall pass away, And on the cleansèd soul shall burst The everlasting day.

verses.label: Verse 7
lines: Δ Till we behold Thy face, And know, as we are known, Thee, Father, Son, and Holy Ghost, Co-equal Three in One.

hymns.title: You Parents Hear What Jesus Taught
hymns.author: Ludwig Helmbold (
hymns.tune: Herr Jesu Christ, Dict Zu Uns Wend
hymns.source: openhymnal.org

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: You parents, hear what Jesus taught When little ones to him were brought: Forbid them not, but heed my plea Permitting them to come to me.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Obey your Lord and let his truth Be taught your children in their youth That they in church and school may dwell And learn their Savior's praise to tell.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: For if you love them as you ought, To Christ your children will be brought.
If thus you place them in his care, All in your family well shall fare.

hymns.title: When before thy throne we kneel
hymns.author: Bowring
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When before thy throne we kneel,
Filled with awe and holy fear,
Teach us, O our God! to feel
All thy sacred presence near.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Check each proud and wandering thought
When on thy great name we call;
Man is nought--is less than nought:
Thou, our God, art all in all.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Weak, imperfect creatures, we
In this vale of darkness dwell;
Yet presume to look to thee,
'Midst thy light ineffable.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O, receive the praise that dares
Seek thy heaven-exalted throne;
Bless our offerings, hear our pray'rs,
Infinite and Holy One!

hymns.title: Come, thou Almighty King
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, thou Almighty King!
Help us thy name to sing;
Help us to praise!
Father all glorious,
O'er all victorious,
Come and reign over us,
Ancient of days!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Come, thou all gracious Lord!
By heaven and earth adored,
Our prayer attend!
Come, and thy children bless;
Give thy good word success;
Make thine own holiness
On us descend!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Never from us depart;
Rule thou in every heart,
Hence, evermore!
Thy sovereign majesty
May we in glory see,
And to eternity
Love and adore.

hymns.title: Stand up and bless the Lord
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Stand up and bless the Lord,
Ye people of his choice;
Stand up, and bless the Lord your God,
With heart, and soul and voice.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Though high above all praise,
Above all blessing high,
Who would not fear his holy name,
And laud and magnify?

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O for the living flame
From his own altar brought,
To touch our lips, our minds inspire,
And wing to heaven our thought!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: There, with benign regard,
Our hymns he deigns to hear;
Though unrevealed to mortal sense,
The spirit feels him near.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Stand up and bless the Lord,
The Lord your God adore;
Stand up and bless his glorious name,
Henceforth for evermore.

hymns.title: Before Jehovah's awful throne
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Before Jehovah's awful throne,
Ye nations, bow with sacred joy;
Know that the Lord is God alone;
He can create, and he destroy.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: His sovereign power, without our aid,
Made us of clay, and formed us men;
And when, like wandering sheep we strayed,
He brought us to his fold again.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: We are his people; we his care;
Our souls, and all our mortal frame:
What lasting honors shall we rear,
Almighty Maker, to thy name?

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: We'll crowd thy gates, with thankful songs
High as the heaven our voices raise;
And earth, with her ten thousand tongues,
Shall fill thy courts with sounding praise.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Wide as the world is thy command;
Vast as eternity thy love;
Firm as a rock thy truth shall stand,
When rolling years shall cease to move.

hymns.title: O, Come, loud anthems let us sing
hymns.author: Tate & Brady
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O, Come, loud anthems let us sing,
Loud thanks to our Almighty King;
For we our voices high should raise,
When our salvation's Rock we praise.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Into his presence let us haste,
To thank him, for his favors past;
To him address, in joyful songs,
The praise that to his name belongs.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O, let us to his courts repair,
And bow with adoration there;
With joy and fear devoutly all
Before the Lord, our Maker, fall!

hymns.title: Great God! attend, while Zion sings
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Great God! attend, while Zion sings
The joy that from thy presence springs;
To spend one day with thee, on earth,
Exceeds a thousand days of mirth.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Might I enjoy the meanest place
Within thy house, O God of grace,
Not tents of ease, nor thrones of power,
Should tempt my feet to leave thy door.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: God is our Sun--he makes our day;
God is our shield--he guards our way;
All needful grace he will bestow,
And crown that grace with glory too.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O God! our king, whose sovereign sway
The glorious hosts of heaven obey,
Thy willing servants may we be,
For blest are they who trust in thee.

hymns.title: When the worn spirit wants repose
hymns.author: Edmeston
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When the worn spirit wants repose,
And sighs her God to seek,
How sweet to hail the evening's close
That ends the weary week!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: How sweet to hail the early dawn
That opens on the sight,
When first that soul-reviving morn
Beams its new rays of light!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Blest day! thine hours too soon will cease
Yet, while they gently roll,
Breathe, Heavenly Spirit, source of peace,
A sabbath o'er my soul!

hymns.title: Blest day of God! most calm, most bright
hymns.author: Codman's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Blest day of God! most calm, most bright,
The first and best of days;
The laborer's rest, the saint's delight,
The day of prayer and praise.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: My Saviour's face made thee to shine;
His rising thee did raise;
And made thee heavenly and divine
Beyond all other days.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The first fruits oft a blessing prove
To all the sheaves behind;
And they who do the Sabbath love,
A happy week will find.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: This day I must to God appear;
For, Lord, the day is thine;
Help me to spend it in thy fear,
And thus to make it mine.

hymns.title: How sweet, upon this sacred day
hymns.author: Mrs. Follen
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How sweet, upon this sacred day,
The best of all the seven,
To cast our earthly thoughts away,
And think of God and heaven!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: How sweet to be allowed to pray
Our sins may be forgiven!
With filial confidence to say,
"Father, who art in heaven!"

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: How sweet the words of peace to hear
From him to whom 'tis given
To wake the penitential tear,
And lead the way to heaven!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: And if, to make our sins depart,
In vain the will has striven,
He who regards the inmost heart
Will send his grace from heaven.

hymns.title: Great God, this sacred day of thine
hymns.author: Mrs. Steele
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Great God, this sacred day of thine
Demands our souls' collected powers.
May we employ in work divine
These solemn, these devoted hours;
O may our souls adoring own
The grace which calls us to thy throne.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Hence, ye vain cares and trifles, fly;
Where God resides appear no more;
Omniscient God, thy piercing eye
Can every secret thought explore:
O may thy grace our hearts refine,
And fix our thoughts on things divine.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The word of life dispensed to-day
Invites us to a heavenly feast.
May every ear the call obey;
Be every heart a humble guest;
O bid the wretched sons of need
On soul-reviving dainties feed.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thy spirit's powerful aid impart;
O may thy word, with life divine,
Engage the ear, and warm the heart;
Then shall the day indeed be thine;
Then shall our souls adoring own
The grace which calls us to thy throne.

hymns.title: Father in heaven, to thee my heart
hymns.author: H. Ware, Jr
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father in heaven, to thee my heart
Would lift itself in prayer;
Drive from my soul each earthly thought
And show thy presence there.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Each moment of my life renews
The mercies of my Lord,
Each moment is itself a gift
To bear me on to God.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O, help me break the galling chains,
This world has round me thrown,
Each passion of my heart subdue,
Each darling sin disown.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O Father, kindle in my breast
A never dying flame
Of holy love, of grateful trust
In thine almighty name.

hymns.title: Welcome, delightful morn
hymns.author: Hayward
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Welcome, delightful morn,
Thou day of sacred rest!
We hail thy glad return:
Lord, make these moments blest.
From low delights and mortal toys
We soar to reach immortal joys.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Now may the King descend,
And fill his throne of grace;
Thy sceptre, Lord, extend,
While we address thy face.
O let us feel thy quickening word,
And learn to know and fear the Lord.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Descend, celestial Dove,
With all thy quickening powers;
Disclose a Saviour's love,
And bless these sacred hours:
Then shall our souls new life obtain,
Nor sabbaths be enjoyed in vain.

hymns.title: I'll bless Jehovah's glorious name
hymns.author: Day
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I'll bless Jehovah's glorious name,
Whose goodness heaven and earth proclaim,
With every morning light;
And at the close of every day,
To him my cheerful homage pay,
Who guards me through the night.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Then in his churches to appear,
And pay my humble worship there,
Shall be my sweet employ:
The day that saw my Saviour rise
Shall dawn on my delighted eyes
With pure and holy joy.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: With grateful sorrow in my breast,
I'll celebrate the dying feast
Of my exalted Lord;
And, while his perfect love I view,
His bright example I'll pursue,
And meditate his word.

hymns.title: How pleased and blest was I
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How pleased and blest was I
To hear the people cry,
"Come, let us seek our God to-day!"
Yes, with a cheerful zeal,
We haste to Zion's hill,
And there our vows and honors pay.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Zion, thrice happy place,
Adorned with wondrous grace,
And walls of strength embrace thee round;
In thee our tribes appear,
To pray, and praise, and hear
The sacred gospel's joyful sound.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: May peace attend thy gate,
And joy within thee wait,
To bless the soul of every guest;
The man who seeks thy peace,
And wishes thine increase,
A thousand blessings on him rest.

hymns.title: Love divine, all love excelling
hymns.author: Wesley's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Love divine, all love excelling,
Joy of heaven, to earth come down!
Fix in us thy humble dwelling,
All thy faithful mercies crown.
Father! thou art all compassion,
Pure, unbounded love thou art;
Visit us with thy salvation,
Enter every longing heart.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Breathe, O breathe thy loving spirit
Into every troubled breast;
Let us all in thee inherit,
Let us find thy promised rest.
Come, almighty to deliver,
Let us all thy life receive,
Graciously come down, and never,
Never more thy temples leave.

hymns.title: O Thou, to whom, in ancient time
hymns.author: Pierpont
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O Thou, to whom, in ancient time,
The lyre of Hebrew bards was strung,
Whom kings adored in song sublime,
And prophets praised with glowing tongue!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Not now on Zion's height alone
Thy favored worshipper may dwell;
Nor where, at sultry noon, thy Son,
Sat weary, by the Patriarch's well.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: From every place below the skies,
The grateful song, the fervent prayer--
The incense of the heart--may rise
To Heaven, and find acceptance there.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: To thee shall age, with snowy hair,
And strength and beauty bend the knee,
And childhood lisp, with reverent air,
Its praises and its prayers to thee.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: O Thou, to whom, in ancient time,
The lyre of prophet-bards was strung,
To thee, at last, in every clime
Shall temples rise, and praise be sung.

hymns.title: Within thy house, O Lord, our God
hymns.author: Presbyterian Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Within thy house, O Lord, our God,
In glory now appear;
Make this a place of thine abode,
And shed thy blessings here.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: When we thy mercy-seat surround,
Thy Spirit, Lord, impart;
And let thy gospel's joyful sound
With power reach every heart.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Here let the blind their sight obtain;
Here give the mourners rest;
Let Jesus here triumphant reign,
Enthroned in every breast.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Here let the voice of sacred joy
And humble prayer arise,
Till higher strains our tongues employ
In realms beyond the skies.

hymns.title: Ye that obey the immortal King
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Ye that obey the immortal King,
Attend his holy place;
Bow to the glories of his name,
And sing his wondrous grace.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lift up your hands by morning light,
And raise your thanks on high;
Send your admiring thoughts, by night,
Above the starry sky.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The God of Zion cheer your hearts
With rays of quickening grace:
'Tis he that spreads the heavens abroad,
Whose presence fills the place.

hymns.title: Glad was my heart to hear
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Glad was my heart to hear
My old companions say
Come, in the house of God appear,
For 'tis a holy day.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Our willing feet shall stand
Within thy temple-door;
While young and old in many a band
Shall throng the sacred floor.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Within these walls be peace
And harmony be found:
Zion, in all thy palaces,
Prosperity abound!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: For friends and brethren dear,
Our prayer shall never cease
Oft as they meet for worship here,
God send his people peace!

hymns.title: Come to the house of prayer
hymns.author: E. Taylor
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come to the house of prayer,
O ye afflicted, come:
The God of peace shall meet you there--
He makes that house his home.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Come to the house of praise,
Ye who are happy now;
In sweet accord your voices raise,
In kindred homage bow.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Ye aged, hither come,
For ye have felt his love:
Soon shall your trembling tongues be dumb,
Your lips forget to move.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Ye young, before his throne,
Come, bow; your voices raise;
Let not your hearts his praise disown
Who gives the power to praise.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Thou, whose benignant eye
In mercy looks on all--
Who see'st the tear of misery,
And hear'st the mourner's call--

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Up to thy dwelling-place
Bear our frail spirits on,
Till they outstrip time's tardy pace,
And heaven on earth be won.

hymns.title: Lord of the worlds above
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord of the worlds above,
How pleasant and how fair
The dwellings of thy love,
Thine earthly temples, are!
To thine abode my heart aspires,
With warm desires to see my God.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O happy souls that pray
Where God appoints to hear!
O happy men that pay
Their constant service there!
They praise thee still; and happy they
Who love the way to Zion's hill.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: They go from strength to strength,
Throughout these mortal years,
Till each arrives at length,
Till each in heaven appears:
O glorious seat, when God, our King,
Shall thither bring our willing feet!

hymns.title: Lord, we come before thee now
hymns.author: Hammond
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, we come before thee now;
At thy feet we humbly bow;
O, do not our suit disdain;
Shall we seek thee, Lord, in vain?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: In thine own appointed way,
Now we seek thee; here we stay;
Lord, from hence we would not go,
Till a blessing thou bestow.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Comfort those who weep and mourn;
Let the time of joy return;
Those that are cast down, lift up;
Make them strong in faith and hope.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Grant that all may seek and find
Thee a God supremely kind;
Heal the sick; the captive free;
Let us all rejoice in thee.

hymns.title: Thy presence, gracious God, afford
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Thy presence, gracious God, afford;
Prepare us to receive thy word;
Now let thy voice engage our ear,
And faith be mixed with what we hear.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Distracting thoughts and cares remove,
And fix our hearts and hopes above;
With food divine may we be fed,
And satisfied with living bread.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: To us the sacred word apply
With sovereign power and energy;
And may we, in thy faith and fear,
Reduce to practice what we hear.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Father, in us thy Son reveal;
Teach us to know and do thy will;
Thy saving power and love display,
And guide us to the realms of day.

hymns.title: Forth from the dark and stormy sky
hymns.author: Heber
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Forth from the dark and stormy sky,
Lord, to thine altar's shade we fly;
Forth from the world, its hope and fear,
Father, we seek thy shelter here:
Weary and weak, thy grace we pray;
Turn not, O Lord, thy guests away.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Long have we roamed in want and pain;
Long have we sought thy rest in vain;
Wildered in doubt, in darkness lost,
Long have our souls been tempest-tost:
Low at thy feet our sins we lay;
Turn not, O Lord, thy guests away.

hymns.title: There's a refuge of peace from the tempests that beat
hymns.author: Edmeston
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: There's a refuge of peace from the tempests that beat,
From the dark clouds that threaten, the wild wind that blows;
A holy, a sweet and a lovely retreat,
A spring of refreshment, a place of repose.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: 'Tis the house of my God, 'tis the dwelling of prayer,
The temple all hallowed by blessing and praise;
If sorrow and faithlessness conquer me, there
My heart to the throne of his grace I can raise.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: For a refuge like this, ah, what praises are due!
For a rest so serene, for a covert so fair:
Ah, why are the seasons of worship so few?
And why are so seldom the meetings of prayer?

hymns.title: Far from mortal cares retreating
hymns.author: J. Taylor
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Far from mortal cares retreating,
Sordid hopes, and vain desires,
Here our willing footsteps meeting,
Every heart to heaven aspires.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: From the fount of glory beaming,
Light celestial cheers our eyes,
Mercy from above proclaiming
Peace and pardon from the skies.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Who may share this great salvation?
Every pure and humble mind,
Every kindred, tongue, and nation,
From the stains of guilt refined.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Blessings all around bestowing,
God withholds his care from none,
Grace and mercy ever flowing
From the fountain of his throne.

hymns.title: Great Shepherd of thy people, hear
hymns.author: Newton
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Great Shepherd of thy people, hear;
Thy presence now display;
We kneel within thy house of prayer;
O, give us hearts to pray.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The clouds which veil thee from our sight,
In pity, Lord, remove;
Dispose our minds to hear aright
The message of thy love.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Help us, with holy fear and joy,
To kneel before thy face;
O, make us, creatures of thy power,
The children of thy grace.

hymns.title: Lord, before thy presence come
hymns.author: J. Taylor
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, before thy presence come,
Bow we down with holy fear;
Call our erring footsteps home,
Let us feel that thou art near.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Wandering thoughts and languid powers
Come not where devotion kneels;
Let the soul expand her stores,
Glowing with the joy she feels.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: At the portals of thine house,
We resign our earth-born cares;
Nobler thoughts our souls engross,
Songs of praise and fervent prayers.

hymns.title: Holy and reverend is the name
hymns.author: Rippon's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Holy and reverend is the name
Of our eternal King;
Thrice holy Lord, the angels cry;
Thrice holy, let us sing.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The deepest reverence of the mind
Pay, O my soul, to God;
Lift with thy hands a holy heart
To his sublime abode.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: With sacred awe pronounce his name,
Whom words nor thoughts can reach;
A broken heart shall please him more
Than the best forms of speech.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thou holy God! preserve my soul
From all pollution free;
The pure in heart are thy delight,
And they thy face shall see.

hymns.title: How sweet to bless the Lord
hymns.author: Urwick's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How sweet to bless the Lord,
And in his praises join,
With saints his goodness to record,
And sing his power divine!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: These seasons of delight
The dawn of glory seem,
Like rays of pure, celestial light,
Which on our spirits beam.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O, blest assurance this;
Bright morn of heavenly day;
Sweet foretaste of eternal bliss,
That cheers the pilgrim's way.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thus may our joys increase,
Our love more ardent grow,
While rich supplies of Jesus' grace
Refresh our souls below.

hymns.title: My Soul, how lovely is the place
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: My Soul, how lovely is the place
To which thy God resorts!
'Tis heaven to see his smiling face,
Though in his earthly courts.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: There the great Monarch of the skies
His saving power displays;
And light breaks in upon our eyes
With kind and quickening rays.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: With his rich gifts the heavenly Dove
Descends and fills the place,
While Christ reveals his wondrous love,
And sheds abroad his grace.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: There, mighty God, thy words declare
The secrets of thy will;
And still we seek thy mercy there,
And sing thy praises still.

hymns.title: Again our earthly cares we leave
hymns.author: Pratt's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Again our earthly cares we leave,
And to thy courts repair;
Again, with joyful feet we come
To meet our Saviour here.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The feeling heart, the melting eye,
The humble mind, bestow;
And shine upon us from on high,
To make our graces grow.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: May we in faith receive thy word,
In faith present our prayers,
And in the presence of our Lord
Unbosom all our cares.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Show us some token of thy love,
Our fainting hope to raise,
And pour thy blessing from above,
That we may render praise.

hymns.title: For thee, O God, our constant praise
hymns.author: Tate & Brady
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: For thee, O God, our constant praise
In Zion waits, thy chosen seat;
Our promised altars there we'll raise,
And all our zealous vows complete.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O thou, who to my humble prayer
Didst always bend thy listening ear,
To thee shall all mankind repair,
And at thy gracious throne appear.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Our sins, though numberless, in vain
To stop thy flowing mercy try;
For thou wilt cleanse the guilty stain,
And wash away the crimson dye.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Blest is the man, who, near thee placed,
Within thy sacred dwelling lives;
Whilst we at humbler distance taste
The vast delight thy worship gives.

hymns.title: O God, whose presence glows in all
hymns.author: Frothingham
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O God, whose presence glows in all,
Within, around us, and above!
Thy word we bless, thy name we call,
Whose word is Truth, whose name is Love.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: That truth be with the heart believed
Of all who seek this sacred place;
With power proclaimed, in peace received--
Our spirits' light, thy Spirit's grace.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: That love its holy influence pour,
To keep us meek, and make us free,
And throw its binding blessing more
Round each with all, and all with thee.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Send down its angel to our side--
Send in its calm upon the breast;
For we would know no other guide,
And we can need no other rest.

hymns.title: Lord! when thy people seek thy face
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord! when thy people seek thy face,
And dying sinners pray to live,
Hear thou in heaven, thy dwelling-place,
And, when thou hearest, O forgive!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Here, when thy messengers proclaim
The blessed Gospel of thy Son,
Still, by the power of his great name,
Be mighty signs and wonders done.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: But will indeed Jehovah deign
Here to abide, no transient guest?
Here will the world's Redeemer reign,
And here the Holy Spirit rest?

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: That glory never hence depart!
Yet choose not, Lord, this house alone;
Thy kingdom come to every heart,
In every bosom fix thy throne.

hymns.title: Safely through another week
hymns.author: J. Newton
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Safely through another week
Thou hast brought us on our way;
Let us now thy blessing seek,
Waiting in thy courts to-day:
Day, of all the week the best--
Emblem of eternal rest.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Mercies multiplied each hour
Through the week our praise demand:
Guarded by almighty power,
Fed and guided by thy hand,
May we not forgetful be,
Nor ungrateful, Lord, to thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: While we seek supplies of grace
Through the dear Redeemer's name,
Show thy reconciling face,
Take away our sin and shame.
From our worldly cares set free,
May we rest this day in thee.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: May the gospel's joyful sound
Conquer sinners, comfort saints;
Make the fruits of grace abound;
Bring relief from all complaints.
Thus let all our sabbaths prove,
Till we join the church above.

hymns.title: How shall we praise thee, Lord of light
hymns.author: Bowring
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How shall we praise thee, Lord of light!
How shall we all thy love declare!
The earth is veiled in shades of night,
But heaven is open to our prayer,--
That heaven so bright with stars and suns--
That glorious heaven which has no bound,
Where the full tide of being runs,
And life and beauty glow around.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: We would adore thee, God sublime!
Whose power and wisdom, love and grace,
Are greater than the round of time,
And wider than the bounds of space,
O how shall thought expression find,
All lost in thine immensity!
How shall we seek thee, glorious Mind,
Amid thy dread infinity!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: But thou art present with us here,
As in thy glittering, high domain;
And grateful hearts and humble fear
Can never seek thy face in vain.
Help us to praise thee, Lord of light!
Help us thy boundless love declare;
And, here within thy courts to-night,
Aid us, and hearken to our prayer.

hymns.title: O Father! though the anxious fear
hymns.author: Mrs. Barbauld
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O Father! though the anxious fear
May cloud to-morrow's way,
No fear nor doubt shall enter here,--
All shall be thine to-day.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: We will not bring divided hearts
To worship at thy shrine;
But each unworthy thought departs,
And leaves this temple thine.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Then sleep to-day, tormenting cares,
Of earth and folly born;
Ye shall not dim the light that streams
From this celestial morn.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: To-morrow will be time enough
To feel your harsh control;
Ye shall not violate this day,
The Sabbath of the soul.

hymns.title: Lord, in heaven, thy dwelling-place
hymns.author: Bowring
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, in heaven, thy dwelling-place,
Hear the praises of our race,
And, while hearing, let thy grace
Dews of sweet forgiveness pour;
While we know, benignant King,
That the praises which we bring
Are a worthless offering
Till thy blessing makes it more.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: More of truth, and more of might,
More of love, and more of light,
More of reason, and of right,
From thy pardoning grace be given!
It can make the humblest song
Sweet, acceptable, and strong,
As the strains the angels' throng
Pour around the throne of heaven.

hymns.title: This day let grateful praise ascend
hymns.author: M. W. Hale
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: This day let grateful praise ascend
To thee, our Father, and our Friend,
Thee, Author of this holy light,
Thee, throned in boundless power and might.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O, let the sacred hours be given
To truth, to duty, and to heaven;
While trusting faith and holy love
Rise fervent to thy throne above.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Grant that our earthly Sabbaths be
But dawnings of eternity,
To shadow forth the glorious rest,
The heavenly quiet of the blest.

hymns.title: This day the Lord hath called his own
hymns.author: Bathurst
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: This day the Lord hath called his own;
O, let us, then, his praise declare,
Fix our desires on him alone,
And seek his face with fervent prayer.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lord, in thy love we would rejoice,
Which bids the burdened soul be free,
And, with united heart and voice,
Devote these sacred hours to thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Now let the world's delusive things
No more our grovelling thoughts employ
But Faith be taught to stretch her wings,
In search of heaven's unfailing joy.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O, let these earthly Sabbaths, Lord,
Be to our lasting welfare blest;
The purest comfort here afford,
And fit us for eternal rest.

hymns.title: Hail to the Sabbath day
hymns.author: Bulfinch
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Hail to the Sabbath day!
The day divinely given,
When men to God their homage pay,
And earth draws near to heaven.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lord, in this sacred hour,
Within thy courts we bend,
And bless thy love, and own thy power,
Our Father and our Friend.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: But thou art not alone
In courts by mortals trod;
Nor only is the day thine own
When man draws near to God.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thy temple is the arch
Of yon unmeasured sky;
Thy Sabbath, the stupendous march
Of grand eternity.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Lord, may that holier day
Dawn on thy servants' sight;
And purer worship may we pay
In heaven's unclouded light.

hymns.title: Sweet is the task, O Lord
hymns.author: Spirit of the Psalms
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Sweet is the task, O Lord,
Thy glorious acts to sing,
To praise thy name, and hear thy word,
And grateful offerings bring.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Sweet, at the dawning hour,
Thy boundless love to tell;
And when the night-wind shuts the flower,
Still on the theme to dwell.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Sweet, on this day of rest,
To join in heart and voice,
With those who love and serve thee best,
And in thy name rejoice.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: To songs of praise and joy,
Be every Sabbath given,
That such may be our blest employ
Eternally in heaven.

hymns.title: Blest hour, when mortal man retires
hymns.author: Raffles
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Blest hour, when mortal man retires
To hold communion with his God,
To send to heaven his warm desires,
And listen to the sacred word.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Blest hour, when earthly cares resign
Their empire o'er his anxious breast,
While, all around, the calm divine
Proclaims the holy day of rest.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Blest hour, when God himself draws nigh,
Well pleased his people's voice to hear,
To hush the penitential sigh,
And wipe away the mourner's tear.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Blest hour! for, where the Lord resorts,
Foretastes of future bliss are given,
And mortals find his earthly courts
The house of God, the gate of heaven.

hymns.title: Praise waits in Zion, Lord, for thee
hymns.author: Sir J. E. Smith
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Praise waits in Zion, Lord, for thee;
Thy saints adore thy holy name;
Thy creatures bend th' obedient knee,
And, humbly, thy protection claim.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thy hand has raised us from the dust;
The breath of life thy Spirit gave;
Where, but in thee, can mortals trust?
Who, but our God, has power to save?

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Still may thy children in thy word
Their common trust and refuge see;
O bind us to each other, Lord,
By one great tie,--the love of thee.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: So shall our sun of hope arise,
With brighter still and brighter ray,
Till thou shalt bless our longing eyes
With beams of everlasting day.

hymns.title: Welcome, sweet day of rest
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Welcome, sweet day of rest,
That saw the Lord arise;
Welcome to this reviving breast
And these rejoicing eyes.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The King himself comes near,
And feasts his saints to-day;
Here we may sit, and see him here,
And love, and praise, and pray.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: One day, amid the place
Where my dear Lord hath been,
Is sweeter than ten thousand days
Of folly and of sin.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: My willing soul would stay
In such a frame as this,
Till called to rise and soar away
To everlasting bliss.

hymns.title: Come, sound his praise abroad
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, sound his praise abroad,
And hymns of glory sing:
Jehovah is the sovereign God,
The universal King.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: He formed the deeps unknown;
He gave the seas their bound;
The watery worlds are all his own,
And all the solid ground.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Come, worship at his throne;
Come, bow before the Lord;
We are his works, and not our own:
He formed us by his word.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: To-day attend his voice,
Nor dare provoke his rod;
Come, like the people of his choice,
And own your gracious God.

hymns.title: With sacred joy we lift our eyes
hymns.author: Jervis
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: With sacred joy we lift our eyes
To those bright realms above--
That glorious temple in the skies
Where dwells eternal love.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thee we adore, and, Lord, to thee
Our filial duty pay;
Thy service, unconstrained and free,
Conducts to endless day.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: While in thy house of prayer we kneel
With trust and holy fear,
Thy mercy and thy truth reveal,
And lend a gracious ear.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: With fervor teach our hearts to pray,
And tune our lips to sing;
Nor from thy presence cast away
The sacrifice we bring.

hymns.title: How welcome to the soul, when pressed
hymns.author: Newton
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How welcome to the soul, when pressed
With six days' noise, and care, and toil,
Is the returning day of rest,
Which hides us from the world awhile!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: How happy they, whose lot is cast
Where Christ invites the "weary" yet;
They find their sorrows quickly past,
And all their burdens soon forget.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Though pinched with poverty at home,
With sharp afflictions daily fed,
It makes amends, if they can come
To God's own house for heavenly bread.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: We thank thee for thy day, O Lord!
And here thy promised presence seek;
Open thy hand with blessings stored,
And give us manna for the week.

hymns.title: Father of all! where shall we find
hymns.author: Butcher
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father of all! where shall we find
A temple suited to thy praise?
To thee, the uncreated Mind,
What earthly altar shall we raise?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: We'll call a multitude around,
And gladly seek the house of prayer;
There thy salvation we have found,
And still, O God, we'll seek it there.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: From breast to breast the holy flame
Shall kindle round the sacred place:
At once we'll hymn our Father's name,
At once we'll seek our Father's face.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: There, heavenly Father, condescend
To meet us with peculiar love;
And when the hymns of earth shall end,
We'll give thee nobler hymns above.

hymns.title: Father! adored in worlds above
hymns.author: Pope's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father! adored in worlds above,
Thy glorious name be hallowed still;
Thy kingdom come with power and love,
And earth, like heaven, obey thy will.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lord! make our daily wants thy care;
Forgive the sins which we forsake:
And, as we in thy kindness share,
Let fellow-men of ours partake.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Evils beset us every hour;
Thy kind protection we implore:
Thine is the kingdom, thine the power;
Be thine the glory evermore!

hymns.title: Come, let us join our souls to God
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, let us join our souls to God
In everlasting bands,
And seize the blessings he bestows
With eager hearts and hands.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Come, let us to his temple haste,
And seek his favor there,
Before his footstool humbly bow,
And offer fervent prayer.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Come, let us share, without delay,
The blessings of his grace;
Nor shall the years of distant life
Their mem'ry e'er efface.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O, may our children ever haste
To seek their fathers' God,
Nor e'er forsake the happy path
Their fathers' feet have trod.

hymns.title: How lovely are thy dwellings fair
hymns.author: Milton
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How lovely are thy dwellings fair,
O Lord of hosts, how dear
The pleasant tabernacles are
Where thou dost dwell so near!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Happy, who in thy house reside,
Where thee they ever praise,
Happy, whose strength in thee doth bide,
And in their hearts thy ways.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: They pass through sorrow's thirsty vale,
That dry and barren ground,
As through a fruitful, wat'ry dale,
Where springs and showers abound.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: They journey on from strength to strength,
With joy and gladsome cheer,
Till all before our God at length
In Zion do appear.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: For God the Lord, both sun and shield,
Gives grace and glory bright;
No good from them shall be withheld
Whose ways are just and right.

hymns.title: Lo, God is here! Let us adore
hymns.author: Salisbury Co
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lo, God is here! Let us adore,
And humbly bow before his face;
Let all within us feel his power;
Let all within us seek his grace.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lo, God is here! Him, day and night
United choirs of angels sing:
To him, enthroned above all height,
Heaven's host their noblest homage bring.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Being of beings! may thy praise
Thy courts with grateful fragrance fill:
Still may we stand before thy face--
Still hear and do thy sovereign will.

hymns.title: We bless thee for this sacred day
hymns.author: New York Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
