lines: We bless thee for this sacred day,
Thou who hast every blessing given,
Which sends the dreams of earth away,
And yields a glimpse of opening heaven.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lord, in this day of holy rest,
We would improve the calm repose;
And, in thy service truly blest,
Forget the world, its joys and woes.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Lord! may thy truth, upon the heart,
Now fall and dwell as heavenly dew,
And flowers of grace in freshness start
Where once the weeds of error grew.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: May prayer now lift her sacred wings,
Contented with that aim alone
Which bears her to the King of kings,
And rests her at his sheltering throne.

hymns.title: On the first Christian Sabbath eve
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: On the first Christian Sabbath eve,
When his disciples met
O'er his lost fellowship to grieve,
Nor knew the Scripture yet,--

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lo! in their midst his form was seen,--
The form in which he died;
Their Master's marred and wounded mien,--
His hands, his feet, his side.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Then were they glad their Lord to know,
And hailed him, yet with fear;--
Jesus, again thy presence show;
Meet thy disciples here.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Be in our midst; let faith rejoice
Our risen Lord to view,
And make our spirits hear thy voice
Say, "Peace be unto you!"

hymns.title: How did my heart rejoice to hear
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How did my heart rejoice to hear
My friends devoutly say,
"In Zion let us all appear,
And keep the solemn day!"

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Up to her courts, with joys unknown,
The holy tribes repair:
The Son of David holds his throne,
And sits in judgment there.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Peace be within this sacred place,
And joy a constant guest;
With holy gifts and heavenly grace
Be her attendants blest.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: My soul shall pray for Zion still
While life or breath remains;
There my best friends, my kindred, dwell;
There God, my Saviour, reigns.

hymns.title: Another six days' work is done
hymns.author: Stennett
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Another six days' work is done,
Another Sabbath is begun:
Return, my soul, enjoy thy rest,
Improve the day which God hath blest.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O that our thoughts and thanks may rise,
As grateful incense, to the skies,
And draw from heaven that sweet repose,
Which none but he that feels it knows!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: This heavenly calm within the breast
Is the dear pledge of glorious rest,
Which for the church of God remains,
The end of cares, the end of pains.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: In holy duties let the day--
In holy pleasures--pass away:
How sweet, a Sabbath thus to spend,
In hope of one that ne'er shall end!

hymns.title: Choice of God, thou blessed day
hymns.author: Miss H. F. Gould
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Choice of God, thou blessed day,
At thy dawn the grave gave way
To the power of him within,
Who had, sinless, bled for sin.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thine the radiance to illume
First, for man, the dismal tomb,
When its bars their weakness owned,
There revealing death dethroned.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Then the Sun of righteousness
Rose, a darkened world to bless,
Bringing up from mortal night
Immortality and light.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Day of glory, day of power,
Sacred be thine every hour,
Emblem, earnest of the rest
That remaineth for the blest!

hymns.title: Again the Lord of life and light
hymns.author: Mrs. Bareauld
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Again the Lord of life and light
Awakes the kindling ray,
Unseals the eyelids of the morn,
And pours increasing day.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O what a night was that which wrapped
The heathen world in gloom!
O what a sun which broke, this day,
Triumphant from the tomb!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: This day be grateful homage paid,
And loud hosannas sung;
Let gladness dwell in every heart,
And praise on every tongue.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Ten thousand differing lips shall join
To hail this welcome morn,
Which scatters blessings from its wings
To nations yet unborn.

hymns.title: Sweet is the work, my God, my King
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Sweet is the work, my God, my King,
To praise thy name, give thanks, and sing;
To show thy love by morning light,
And talk of all thy truth at night.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Sweet is the day of sacred rest!
No mortal cares shall seize my breast;
O may my heart in tune be found,
Like David's harp of solemn sound.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: My heart shall triumph in the Lord,
And bless his works, and bless his word:
Thy works of grace, how bright they shine,
How deep thy counsels, how divine!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: But I shall share a glorious part
When grace hath well refined my heart,
And, raised to holier courts above,
I praise thee with a purer love.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Then shall I see, and hear, and know,
All I desired or wished below;
And every power find sweet employ
In that eternal world of joy.

hymns.title: The joyful morn, my God, is come
hymns.author: Merrick
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The joyful morn, my God, is come,
That calls me to thy sacred dome,
Thy presence to adore:
My feet the summons shall attend,
With willing steps thy courts ascend
And tread the hallowed floor.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: With holy joy I hail the day,
That warns my thirsting soul away;
What transports fill my breast!
For, lo! my great Redeemer's power
Unfolds the everlasting door,
And leads me to his rest!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Hither, from earth's remotest end,
Lo! the redeemed of God ascend,
Their tribute hither bring;
Here, crowned with everlasting joy,
In hymns of praise their tongues employ,
And hail the immortal King.

hymns.title: Early, my God, without delay
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Early, my God, without delay,
I haste to seek thy face;
My thirsty spirit faints away
Without thy cheering grace.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: So pilgrims on the scorching sand,
Beneath a burning sky,
Long for a cooling stream at hand;
And they must drink, or die.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Not life itself, with all its joys,
Can my best passions move,
Or raise so high my cheerful voice,
As thy forgiving love.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thus, till my last expiring day,
I'll bless my God and King;
Thus will I lift my hands to pray,
And tune my lips to sing.

hymns.title: Hosanna! Lord, thine angels cry
hymns.author: Heber
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Hosanna! Lord, thine angels cry:
Hosanna! Lord, we hear reply:
Above, beneath us, and around,
The dead and living swell the sound.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O Father! with protecting care
Meet us in this, thy house of prayer;
Assembled in Messiah's name,
Thy promised blessing here we claim.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: But, chiefest, in our cleansed breast,
Eternal! let thy Spirit rest;
And make our secret soul to be
A temple pure, and worthy thee.

hymns.title: My God, accept my early vows
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: My God, accept my early vows,
Like morning incense, in thy house;
And let my nightly worship rise
Sweet as the evening sacrifice.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Watch o'er my lips, and guard them, Lord,
From every rash and heedless word;
Nor let my feet incline to tread
The guilty path where sinners lead.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O may the righteous, when I stray,
Smite and reprove my wandering way;
Their gentle words, like ointment shed,
Shall never bruise, but cheer, my head.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: When I behold them prest with grief
I'll cry to heaven for their relief;
And by my warm petitions prove
How much I prize their faithful love.

hymns.title: How pleasant, how divinely fair
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How pleasant, how divinely fair,
O Lord of Hosts, thy dwellings are!
With long desire my spirit faints
To meet the assemblies of thy saints.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Blest are the souls who find a place
Within the temple of thy grace;
There they behold thy gentler rays,
And seek thy face and learn thy praise.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Blest are the men whose hearts are set
To find the way to Zion's gate;
God is their Strength; and through the road
They lean upon their Helper, God.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Cheerful they walk with growing strength,
Till all shall meet in heaven at length;
Till all before thy face appear,
And join the nobler worship there.

hymns.title: While thus thy throne of grace we seek
hymns.author: C. Robbins
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: While thus thy throne of grace we seek,
O God, within our spirits speak!
For we will hear thy voice to-day,
Nor turn our hardened hearts away.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Speak in thy gentlest tones of love,
Till all our best affections move;
We long to hear no meaner call,
But feel that Thou art all in all.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: To conscience speak thy quickening word,
Till all its sense of sin is stirred:
For we would leave no stain of guile,
To cloud the radiance of thy smile.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Speak, Father, to the anxious heart,
Till every fear and doubt depart:
For we can find no home or rest,
Till with thy Spirit's whispers blest.

hymns.title: Here, gracious God! do thou
hymns.author: Roman Breviary
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Here, gracious God! do thou
For evermore draw nigh;
Accept each faithful prayer,
And mark each suppliant sigh:
In copious shower, on all who pray,
This holy day, thy blessings pour.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Here may we find from heaven
The grace which we implore;
And may that grace once given,
Be with us evermore:
Until that day, when all the blest
To endless rest are called away.

hymns.title: Called by the Sabbath bells away
hymns.author: Sun. School H. B
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Called by the Sabbath bells away,
Unto thy holy temple, Lord,
I'll go, with willing mind to pray,
To praise thy name and hear thy word.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O sacred day of peace and joy,
Thy hours are ever dear to me;
Ne'er may a sinful thought destroy
The holy calm I find in thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Dear are thy peaceful hours to me,
For God has given them in his love,
To tell how calm, how blest shall be
The endless day of heaven above.

hymns.title: When, as returns this solemn day
hymns.author: Mrs. Barbauld
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When, as returns this solemn day,
Man comes to meet his Maker, God,
What rites, what honors shall he pay?
How spread his Sovereign's praise abroad?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: From marble domes and gilded spires,
Shall curling clouds of incense rise?
And gems, and gold, and garlands deck
The costly pomp of sacrifice?

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Vain, sinful man! creation's Lord
Thy golden offerings well may spare:
But give thy heart, and thou shalt find
Here dwells a God who heareth prayer.

hymns.title: Sacred day, forever blest
hymns.author: Sun. School H. B
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Sacred day, forever blest!
Day of all our days the best!
Welcome hours of praise and prayer,
Free from toil, fatigue, and care!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Happy, truly happy, Lord,
Those who hear and read thy word!
Happy those who dwell with thee!
Who thy grace and glory see.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: We once more have heard thy voice,
Lord, in thee our souls rejoice;
Borne by faith to worlds on high,
Called to reign above the sky.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Though this day of rest we close,
Still in thee our hearts repose;
Guide and guard us all our days:
O may all our lives be praise!

hymns.title: At the portals of thy house
hymns.author: J. Taylor
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: At the portals of thy house,
Lord, we leave our mortal cares:
Nobler thoughts our souls engage,
Songs of praise, and fervent prayers.
Pure and contrite hearts alone
Find acceptance at thy throne.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Hapless men, whose footsteps stray
From the temple of the Lord!
Teach them Zion's heavenly way;
To their feet thy light afford.
Let the world unite to raise
Solemn harmonies of praise.

hymns.title: Father of omnipresent grace
hymns.author: C. Wesley
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father of omnipresent grace!
We seem agreed to seek thy face:
But every soul assembled here
Doth naked in thy sight appear;
Thou know'st who only bows the knee,
And who in heart approaches thee.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: To-day, while it is called to-day,
Awake and stir us up to pray;
The spirit of thy word impart,
And breathe the life into our heart;
Our weakness help, our darkness chase,
And guide us by the light of grace.

hymns.title: Eternal Source of light and thought
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Eternal Source of light and thought!
Be all beneath thyself forgot,
Whilst thee, great parent-mind, we own,
In prostrate homage round thy throne.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Whilst in themselves our souls survey
Of thee some faint reflected ray,
They wondering to their Father rise:
His power how vast! his thoughts how wise!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O may we live before thy face,
The willing subjects of thy grace;
And through each path of duty move,
With filial awe, and filial love.

hymns.title: God in his temple let us meet
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: God in his temple let us meet,
In spirit, low before him bend:
Here he hath fixed his mercy-seat,
Here on his Sabbath we attend.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Arise into thy resting-place,
Thou, and thine ark of strength, O Lord!
Shine through the veil, we seek thy face:
Speak, for we hearken to thy word.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: With righteousness thy priests array:
Joyful thy favored people be:
Let those who teach, and those who pray,
Let all--be holiness to thee!

hymns.title: Creator Spirit, by whose light
hymns.author: Dryden
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Creator Spirit, by whose light
The sleeping worlds were called from night!
Come, visit every pious mind,
Come, pour thy joys on human kind;
From sin and sorrow set us free,
And make us temples worthy thee.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Plenteous in grace descend from high,
Rich in thy sevenfold energy;
Our frailty help, our vice control,
Thou ruler of our secret soul!
And, lest our feet should haply stray
Protect and guide us in the way.

hymns.title: Spirit of grace, and health, and power
hymns.author: J. Wesley
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Spirit of grace, and health, and power!
Fountain of light and love below!
Abroad thy healing influence shower;
On all thy servants let it flow.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Inflame our hearts with perfect love;
In us the work of faith fulfil:
So not heaven's host shall swifter move,
Than we on earth to do thy will.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Father! 'tis thine each day to yield
Thy children's wants a fresh supply;
Thou cloth'st the lilies of the field,
And hearest the young ravens cry.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: On thee we cast our care; we live
Through thee, who know'st our every need:
O feed us with thy grace, and give
Our souls this day the living bread!

hymns.title: Shine on our souls, eternal God
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Shine on our souls, eternal God!
With rays of beauty shine;
O let thy favor crown our days,
And all their round be thine.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Did we not raise our hands to thee,
Our hands might toil in vain;
Small joy success itself could give,
If thou thy love restrain.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: With thee let every week begin;
With thee each day be spent;
For thee each fleeting hour improved,
Since each by thee is lent.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thus cheer us through the checkered road,
Till all our labors cease,
And heaven refresh our weary souls
With everlasting peace.

hymns.title: Softly fades the twilight ray
hymns.author: S. F. Smith
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Softly fades the twilight ray
Of the holy Sabbath day;
Gently as life's setting sun;
When the Christian's course is run.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Night her solemn mantle spreads
O'er the earth, as daylight fades;
All things tell of calm repose
At the holy Sabbath's close.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Peace is on the world abroad;
'Tis the holy peace of God,--
Symbol of the peace within,
When the spirit rests from sin.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Still the Spirit lingers near,
Where the evening worshipper
Seeks communion with the skies,
Pressing onward to the prize.

hymns.title: Ere to the world again we go
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Ere to the world again we go,
Its pleasures, cares, and idle show,
Thy grace once more, O God, we crave,
From folly and from sin to save.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: May the great truths we here have heard--
The lessons of thy holy word--
Dwell in our inmost bosoms deep,
And all our souls from error keep.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Oh! may the influence of this day,
Long as our memory with us stay,
And as an angel guardian prove,
To guide us to our home above.

hymns.title: Eternal Source of life and light
hymns.author: Cappe's Sel
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Eternal Source of life and light,
Supremely good and wise,
To thee we bring our grateful vows,
To thee lift up our eyes.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Our dark and erring minds illume
With truth's celestial rays;
Inspire our hearts with sacred love,
And tune our lips to praise.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Safely conduct us, by thy grace,
Through life's perplexing road;
And place us, when that journey's o'er
At thy right hand, O God.

hymns.title: Come, thou soul-transforming Spirit
hymns.author: Jay
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, thou soul-transforming Spirit,
Bless the sower and the seed;
Let each heart thy grace inherit;
Raise the weak, the hungry feed;
From the gospel
Now supply thy people's need.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O, may all enjoy the blessing
Which thy word's designed to give,
Let us all, thy love possessing,
Joyfully the truth receive,
And forever
To thy praise and glory live.

hymns.title: Again our ears have heard the voice
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Again our ears have heard the voice
At which the dead shall live;
O, may the sound our hearts rejoice,
And strength immortal give!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: And have we heard the word with joy?
And have we felt its power?
To keep it be our blest employ,
Till life's extremest hour.

hymns.title: Israel's Shepherd, guide me, feed me
hymns.author: Bickersteth
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Israel's Shepherd, guide me, feed me.
Through my pilgrimage below,
And beside the waters lead me,
Where thy flock rejoicing go.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lord, thy guardian presence ever,
Meekly kneeling, I implore;
I have found thee, and would never,
Never wander from thee more.

hymns.title: Father! bless thy word to all
hymns.author: Peabody's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father! bless thy word to all;
Quick and powerful may it prove;
O, may sinners hear thy call,
May thy people grow in love.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Father, bid the world rejoice;
Send thy heavenly truth abroad;
May the nations hear thy voice,
Hear it, and return to God.

hymns.title: O God, by whom the seed is given
hymns.author: Bp. Heber
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O God, by whom the seed is given,
By whom the harvest blest;
Whose word, like manna showered from heaven
Is planted in our breast.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Preserve it from the passing feet,
And plunderers of the air;
The sultry sun's intenser heat,
And weeds of worldly care!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Though buried deep, or thinly strewn,
Do thou thy grace supply:
The hope in earthly furrows sown
Shall ripen in the sky.

hymns.title: Now, Lord, the heavenly seed is sown
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Now, Lord, the heavenly seed is sown,
Be it thy servants' care
Thy heavenly blessing to bring down
By humble, fervent prayer.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: In vain we plant without thine aid,
And water, too, in vain:
Lord of the harvest, God of grace,
Send down thy heavenly rain.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Then shall our cheerful hearts and tongues
Begin this song divine--
"Thou, Lord, hast given the rich increase,
And be the glory thine."

hymns.title: From worship, now, thy church dismiss
hymns.author: H. Ballou
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: From worship, now, thy church dismiss--
But not without thy blessing, Lord;
O grant a taste of heavenly bliss,
And seal instruction from thy word.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Oft may these pleasant scenes return
When we shall meet to worship thee;
Oft may our hearts within us burn
To hear thy word, thy goodness see.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And when these pleasant scenes are past,
To thee, our God, O may we come,
And meet th' assembled world at last,
In Zion, our eternal home.

hymns.title: Kind Lord, before thy face
hymns.author: E. Turner
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Kind Lord, before thy face
Again with joy we bow,
For all the gifts and grace
Thou dost on us bestow.
Our tongues would all thy love proclaim,
And chant the honors of thy name.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Here, in thine earthly house,
Our joyful souls have met;
Here paid our solemn vows,
And felt our union sweet.
For this our tongues thy love proclaim,
And chant the honors of thy name.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Now may we dwell in peace
Till here again we come;
And may our love increase
Till thou shalt bring us home.
Then shall our tongues thy love proclaim,
And chant the honors of thy name.

hymns.title: Lo! the day of rest declineth
hymns.author: C. Robbins
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lo! the day of rest declineth;
Gather fast the shades of night--
May the Sun that ever shineth,
Fill our souls with heavenly light.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Softly now the dew is falling;
Peace o'er all the scene is spread;--
On his children meekly calling,
Purer influence God will shed.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: While thine ear of love addressing,
Thus our parting hymn we sing,
Father, give thine evening blessing;
Fold us safe beneath thy wing.

hymns.title: Soon will our fleeting hours be past
hymns.author: Kippis' Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Soon will our fleeting hours be past;
And, as the setting sun
Sinks downward in the radiant west,
Our parting beams be gone.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: May He, from whom all blessings flow,
Our sacred rites attend,
Uniting all in wisdom's ways,
Till life's short journey end;

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And as the rapid sands run down,
Our virtue still improve,
Till each receive the glorious crown
Of never-fading love.

hymns.title: Lord, now we part in thy blest name
hymns.author: Heber
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, now we part in thy blest name,
In which we here together came:
Grant us our few remaining days
To work thy will and spread thy praise.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Teach us in life and death to bless
The Lord our strength and righteousness;
And grant us all to meet above;
Then shall we better sing thy love.

hymns.title: All ye nations, praise the Lord
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: All ye nations, praise the Lord;
All ye lands, your voices raise;
Heaven and earth, with loud accord,
Praise the Lord, forever praise.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: For his truth and mercy stand,
Past and present, and to be,
Like the years of his right hand,
Like his own eternity.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Praise him, ye who know his love;
Praise him from the depths beneath;
Praise him in the heights above;
Praise your Maker, all that breathe.

hymns.title: Lord, how delightful 'tis to see
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, how delightful 'tis to see
A whole assembly worship thee;
At once they sing, at once they pray,
They hear of heaven and learn the way.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O, write upon our memory, Lord,
The text and doctrines of thy word:
That we may break thy laws no more,
But love thee better than before.

hymns.title: Part in peace! is day before us
hymns.author: S. F. Adams
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Part in peace! is day before us?
Praise his name for life and light;
Are the shadows lengthening o'er us?
Bless His care who guards the night.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Part in peace! with deep thanksgiving,
Rendering, as we homeward tread,
Gracious service to the living,
Tranquil memory to the dead.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Part in peace! such are the praises
God, our Maker, loveth best;
Such the worship that upraises
Human hearts to heavenly rest.

hymns.title: While now upon this Sabbath eve
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: While now upon this Sabbath eve,
Thy house, Almighty God, we leave
'Tis sweet, as sinks the setting sun,
To think on all our duties done.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Oh! evermore may all our bliss
Be peaceful, pure, divine, like this;
And may each Sabbath, as it flies,
Fit us for joy beyond the skies.

hymns.title: Millions within thy courts have been
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Millions within thy courts have been;
Millions this day have bent the knee;
But thou, soul-searching God! hast seen
The hearts of all that worshipped thee.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: From east to west the sun surveyed,
From north to south, adoring throngs;
And still, where evening stretched her shade,
The stars came forth to hear their songs.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And not a prayer, a tear, a sigh,
Hath failed this day some suit to gain;
To those in trouble thou wert nigh;
Not one hath sought thy face in vain.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Yet one prayer more;--and be it one,
In which both heaven and earth accord:
Fulfil thy promise to thy Son;
Let all that breathe call Jesus Lord!

hymns.title: Thy name be hallowed evermore
hymns.author: Moravian
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Thy name be hallowed evermore;
O God! thy kingdom come with power!
Thy will be done, and day by day,
Give us our daily bread, we pray:

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lord! evermore to us be given
The living bread that came from heaven:
Water of life on us bestow,
Thou art the Source, the Fountain thou.

hymns.title: There is a God--all nature speaks
hymns.author: Mrs. Steele
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: There is a God--all nature speaks,
Through earth, and air, and sea, and skies:
See, from the clouds his glory breaks,
When first the beams of morning rise.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The rising sun, serenely bright,
O'er the wide world's extended frame
Inscribes, in characters of light,
His mighty Maker's glorious name.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The flowery tribes, all blooming, rise
Above the weak attempts of art;
Their bright, inimitable dyes
Speak sweet conviction to the heart.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Ye curious minds, who roam abroad,
And trace creation's wonders o'er,
Confess the footsteps of a God;
Come, bow before him, and adore.

hymns.title: My Father! cheering name
hymns.author: Mrs. Steele
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: My Father! cheering name!
O, may I call thee mine?
Give me the humble hope to claim
A portion so divine.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Whate'er thy will denies,
I calmly would resign;
For thou art just, and good, and wise:
O, bend my will to thine!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Whate'er thy will ordains,
O give me strength to bear
Still let me know a father reigns,
And trust a father's care.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thy ways are little known
To my weak, erring sight;
Yet shall my soul, believing, own
That all thy ways are right.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: My Father!--blissful name!
Above expression dear!
If thou accept my humble claim,
I bid adieu to fear.

hymns.title: Father! to thy kind love we owe
hymns.author: Bryant
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father! to thy kind love we owe
All that is fair and good below;
Bestower of the health that lies
On tearless cheeks and cheerful eyes!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Giver of sunshine and of rain!
Ripener of fruits on hill and plain!
Fountain of light, that, rayed afar,
Fills the vast urns of sun and star!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Yet deem we not that thus alone,
Thy mercy and thy love are shown;
For we have learned, with higher praise,
And holier names, to speak thy ways.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: In woe's dark hour, our kindest stay!
Sole trust when life shall pass away!
Teacher of hopes that light the gloom
Of death, and consecrate the tomb!

hymns.title: 'Twas God who fixed the rolling spheres
hymns.author: Martineau's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: 'Twas God who fixed the rolling spheres,
And stretched the boundless skies,
Who formed the plan of endless years,
And bade the ages rise.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: From everlasting is his might,
Immense and unconfined;
He pierces through the realms of light,
And rides upon the wind.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: He darts along the burning sky;
Loud thunders round him roar;
Through worlds above his terrors fly,
While worlds below adore.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: He speaks,--great nature's wheels stand still
And leave their wonted round;
The mountains melt; each trembling hill
Forsakes its ancient bound.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Ye worlds, and every living thing,
Fulfil his high command;
Pay grateful homage to your King,
And own his ruling hand.

hymns.title: The Lord our God is clothed with might
hymns.author: H. K. White
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The Lord our God is clothed with might;
The winds obey his will;
He speaks, and in the heavenly height
The rolling sun stands still.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Rebel, ye waves, and o'er the land
With threatening aspect roar;
The Lord uplifts his awful hand,
And chains you to the shore.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Ye winds of night, your force combine
Without his high behest,
Ye shall not, in the mountain pine,
Disturb the sparrow's nest.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: His voice sublime is heard afar;
In distant peals it dies;
He binds the whirlwinds to his car,
And sweeps the howling skies.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Ye nations, bend; in reverence bend;
Ye monarchs, wait his nod,
And bid the choral song ascend
To celebrate our God.

hymns.title: In all my vast concerns with thee
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: In all my vast concerns with thee,
In vain my soul would try
To shun thy presence, Lord, or flee
The notice of thine eye.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thine all-surrounding sight surveys
My rising and my rest;
My public walks, my private ways,
And secrets of my breast.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: My thoughts lie open to the Lord,
Before they're formed within;
And ere my lips pronounce the word,
He knows the sense I mean.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O, wondrous knowledge, deep and high;
Where can a creature hide?
Within thy circling arms I lie,
Beset on every side.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: So let thy grace surround me still,
And like a bulwark prove,
To guard my soul from every ill,
Secured by sovereign love.

hymns.title: Ere mountains reared their forms sublime
hymns.author: Spirit of the Psalms
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Ere mountains reared their forms sublime,
Or heaven and earth in order stood,
Before the birth of ancient time,
From everlasting thou art God.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: A thousand ages, in their flight,
With thee are as a fleeting day;
Past, present, future, to thy sight
At once their various scenes display.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: But our brief life's a shadowy dream,
A passing thought, that soon is o'er,
That fades with morning's earliest beam,
And fills the musing mind no more.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: To us, O Lord, the wisdom give,
Each passing moment so to spend,
That we at length with thee may live
Where life and bliss shall never end.

hymns.title: Beyond, beyond that boundless sea
hymns.author: Conder
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Beyond, beyond that boundless sea,
Above that dome of sky,
Farther than thought itself can flee,
Thy dwelling is on high;
Yet dear the awful thought to me,
That thou, my God, art nigh.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: We hear thy voice when thunders roll
Through the wide fields of air;
The waves obey thy dread control:
Yet still thou art not there.
Where shall I find Him, O my soul,
Who yet is everywhere?

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O, not in circling depth, or height,
But in the conscious breast,
Present to faith, though veiled from sight,
There does his spirit rest.
O come, thou Presence Infinite,
And make thy creatures blest.

hymns.title: Lord, thou hast searched and seen me through
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, thou hast searched and seen me through;
Thine eye commands, with piercing view,
My rising and my resting hours,
My heart and flesh, with all their powers.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Within thy circling power I stand;
On every side I find thy hand:
Awake, asleep, at home, abroad,
I am surrounded still with God.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Amazing knowledge, vast and great!
What large extent! what lofty height!
My soul, with all the powers I boast,
Is in the boundless prospect lost.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O may these thoughts possess my breast
Where'er I rove, where'er I rest!
Nor let my weaker passions dare
Consent to sin; for God is there.

hymns.title: How precious are thy thoughts of peace
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How precious are thy thoughts of peace,
O God! to me,--how great the sum!
New every morn, they never cease;
They were, they are, and yet shall come,
In number and in compass more
Than ocean's sand, or ocean's shore.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Search me, O God! and know my heart,
Try me, my secret soul survey;
And warn thy servant to depart
From every false and evil way:
So shall thy truth my guidance be,
In life and immortality.

hymns.title: Father of all, omniscient Mind
hymns.author: Blacklock
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father of all, omniscient Mind,
Thy wisdom who can comprehend?
Its highest point what eye can find,
Or to its lowest depths descend?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: If up to heaven's ethereal height,
Thy prospect to elude, I rise,
In splendor there supremely bright,
Thy presence shall my sight surprise.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thee, mighty God, my wondering soul,
Thee, all her conscious powers adore,
Whose being circumscribes the whole,
Whose eyes the universe explore.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thine essence fills this breathing frame;
It glows in every vital part,
Lights up our souls with livelier flame,
And feeds with life each beating heart.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: To thee, from whom our being came,
Whose smile is all the heaven we know,
Inspired with this exalted theme,
To thee our grateful strains shall flow.

hymns.title: Great God, how infinite art thou
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Great God, how infinite art thou!
How weak and frail are we!
Let the whole race of creatures bow,
And homage pay to thee.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thy throne eternal ages stood,
Ere earth or heaven was made;
Thou art the ever-living God,
Were all the nations dead.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Eternity, with all its years,
Stands present in thy view;
To thee there's nothing old appears,
Great God, there's nothing new.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Our lives through varying scenes are drawn,
And vexed with trifling cares,
While thine eternal thought moves on
Thine undisturbed affairs.

hymns.title: The Lord Jehovah reigns
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The Lord Jehovah reigns,
And royal state maintains,
His head with awful glories crowned,
Arrayed in robes of light,
Begirt with sovereign might,
And rays of majesty around.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Upheld by thy commands,
The world securely stands,
And skies and stars obey thy word;
Thy throne was fixed on high
Ere stars adorned the sky;
Eternal is thy kingdom, Lord.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thy promises are true;
Thy grace is ever new;
There fixed, thy church shall ne'er remove;
Thy saints, with holy fear,
Shall in thy courts appear,
And sing thine everlasting love.

hymns.title: God is love; his mercy brightens
hymns.author: Bowring
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: God is love; his mercy brightens
All the path in which we rove;
Bliss he wakes, and woe he lightens;
God is wisdom, God is love.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Chance and change are busy ever;
Man decays, and ages move;
But his mercy waneth never;
God is wisdom, God is love.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: E'en the hour that darkest seemeth
Will his changeless goodness prove;
From the gloom his brightness streameth,
God is wisdom, God is love.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: He with earthly cares entwineth
Hope and comfort from above:
Everywhere his glory shineth;
God is wisdom, God is love.

hymns.title: The Spirit moved upon the waves
hymns.author: Fergus
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The Spirit moved upon the waves
That darkly rolled, a shoreless sea;
He spake the word, and light burst forth,
A glorious, bright immensity.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: At his command, the mountains heaved
Their rocky pinnacles on high,
Island and continent displayed
Their desert grandeur to the sky.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The voice of God was heard again,
And lovely flowers and graceful trees
Appeared on every vale and plain,
And perfumes floated on the breeze.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The word went forth, and vast and high
The heavenly orbs gave out their light,
O'er all the earth and sea and sky;
The rulers of the day and night.

hymns.title: Above, below, where'er I gaze
hymns.author: Montgomery's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Above, below, where'er I gaze,
Thy guiding finger, Lord, I view,
Traced in the midnight planets' blaze,
Or glist'ning in the morning dew:
Whate'er is beautiful or fair,
Is but thine own reflection there.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: And when the radiant orb of light
Hath tipped the mountain tops with gold
Smote with the blaze, my weary sight
Shrinks from the wonders I behold;
That ray of glory, bright and fair,
Is but thy living shadow there.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thine is the silent noon of night,
The twilight eve, the dewy morn;
Whate'er is beautiful and bright,
Thy hands have fashioned to adorn.
Thy glory walks in every sphere,
And all things whisper, "God is here."

hymns.title: How shall I praise th' eternal God
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How shall I praise th' eternal God,
That infinite Unknown?
Who can ascend his high abode,
Or venture near his throne?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Those watchful eyes that never sleep,
Survey the world around:
His wisdom is a boundless deep,
Where all our thoughts are drowned.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Speak we of strength, his arm is strong,
To save or to destroy:
To him eternal years belong,
And never-ending joy.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: He knows no shadow of a change,
Nor alters his decrees;
Firm as a rock his truth remains,
To guard his promises.

hymns.title: The heaven of heavens cannot contain
hymns.author: Drennan
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The heaven of heavens cannot contain
The universal Lord;
Yet he in humble hearts will deign
To dwell and be adored.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Where'er ascends the sacrifice
Of fervent praise and prayer,
Or on the earth, or in the skies,
The God of heaven is there.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: His presence is diffused abroad
Through realms, through worlds unknown;
Who seek the mercies of our God
Are ever near his throne.

hymns.title: Is there a lone and dreary hour
hymns.author: Mrs. Gilman
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Is there a lone and dreary hour,
When worldly pleasures lose their power?
My Father! let me turn to thee,
And set each thought of darkness free.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Is there a time of rushing grief,
Which scorns the prospect of relief?
My Father! break the cheerless gloom,
And bid my heart its calm resume.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Is there an hour of peace and joy,
When hope is all my soul's employ?
My Father! still my hopes will roam,
Until they rest with thee, their home.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The noontide blaze, the midnight scene,
The dawn, or twilight's sweet serene,
The glow of life, the dying hour,
Shall own my Father's grace and power.

hymns.title: Almighty Former of creation's plan
hymns.author: Mme. Guion
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Almighty Former of creation's plan,
Faintly reflected in thine image, man;
Holy and just,--the greatness of whose name
Rules and supports this universal frame:--

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Whose spirit fills the infinitude of space,--
Who art thyself thine own vast dwelling place;--
Soul of our soul, whom yet no sense of ours
Discerns, eluding our most active powers:--

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Encircling shades attend thine awful throne,
That veil thy face, and keep thee still unknown;
Unknown, though dwelling in our inmost part,
Lord of the thoughts, and Sovereign of the heart!

hymns.title: There's not a star whose twinkling light
hymns.author: Wallace
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: There's not a star whose twinkling light
Illumes the distant earth,
And cheers the solemn gloom of night,
But goodness gave it birth.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: There's not a cloud whose dews distil
Upon the parching clod,
And clothe with verdure vale and hill,
That is not sent by God.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: There's not a place in earth's vast round,
In ocean deep, or air,
Where skill and wisdom are not found;
For God is everywhere.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Around, within, below, above,
Wherever space extends,
There Heaven displays its boundless love,
And power with goodness blends.

hymns.title: Eternal Wisdom, thee we praise
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Eternal Wisdom, thee we praise;
Thee all thy creatures sing:
While with thy name, rocks, hills, and seas,
And heaven's high palace, ring.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thy hand, how wide it spread the sky!
How glorious to behold!
Tinged with a blue of heavenly dye,
And decked with sparkling gold.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thy glories blaze all nature round,
And strike the gazing sight,
Through skies, and seas, and solid ground,
With terror and delight.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Almighty power, and equal skill,
Shine through the worlds abroad,
Our souls with vast amazement fill,
And speak the builder, God.

hymns.title: My Maker and my King
hymns.author: Mrs. Steele
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: My Maker and my King!
To thee my all I owe:
Thy sovereign bounty is the spring,
From whence my blessings flow.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thou ever good and kind!
A thousand reasons move,
A thousand obligations bind
My heart to grateful love.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The creature of thy hand,
On thee alone I live:
My God! thy benefits demand
More praise than tongue can give.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O let thy grace inspire
My soul with strength divine;
Let all my powers to thee aspire,
And all my days be thine.

hymns.title: High in the heavens, eternal God
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: High in the heavens, eternal God!
Thy goodness in full glory shines;
Thy truth shall break through every cloud
That veils and darkens thy designs.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Forever firm thy justice stands,
As mountains their foundations keep;
Wise are the wonders of thy hands;
Thy judgments are a mighty deep.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thy providence is kind and large;
Both man and beast thy bounty share;
The whole creation is thy charge,
But saints are thy peculiar care.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Life, like a fountain, rich and free,
Springs from the presence of my Lord;
And in thy light our souls shall see
The glories promised in thy word.

hymns.title: Great God! in vain man's narrow view
hymns.author: Kippis
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Great God! in vain man's narrow view
Attempts to look thy nature through;
Our laboring powers with reverence own
Thy glories never can be known.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Not the high seraph's mighty thought,
Who countless years his God has sought,
Such wondrous height or depth can find,
Or fully trace thy boundless mind.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And yet thy kindness deigns to show
Enough for mortal minds to know;
While wisdom, goodness, power divine,
Through all thy works and conduct shine.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O, may our souls with rapture trace
Thy works of nature and of grace:
Explore thy sacred truth, and still
Press on to know and do thy will.

hymns.title: Through endless years thou art the same
hymns.author: Tate & Brady
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Through endless years thou art the same,
O thou eternal God;
Each future age shall know thy name,
And tell thy works abroad.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The strong foundations of the earth
Of old by thee were laid;
By thee the beauteous arch of heaven
With matchless skill was made.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Soon may this goodly frame of things
Created by thy hand,
Be, like a vesture, laid aside,
And changed at thy command.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: But thy perfections, all divine,
Eternal as thy days,
Through everlasting ages shine,
With undiminished rays.

hymns.title: God moves in a mysterious way
hymns.author: Cowper
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: God moves in a mysterious way,
His wonders to perform;
He plants his footsteps in the sea,
And rides upon the storm.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Ye fearful saints, fresh courage take;
The clouds ye so much dread
Are big with mercy, and shall break
In blessings on your head.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Judge not the Lord by feeble sense,
But trust him for his grace;
Behind a frowning providence
He hides a smiling face.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: His purposes will ripen fast
Unfolding every hour;
The bud may have a bitter taste,
But sweet will be the flower.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Blind unbelief is sure to err,
And scan his work in vain;
God is his own interpreter,
And he will make it plain.

hymns.title: In darkness as in light
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: In darkness as in light,
Hidden alike from view,
I sleep, I wake within His sight,
Who looks existence through.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: From the dim hour of birth,
Through every changing state
Of mortal pilgrimage on earth,
Till its appointed date;

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: All that I am,--have been,--
All that I yet may be,
He sees at once, as he hath seen,
And shall forever see.

hymns.title: Lord! thou art good: all nature shows
hymns.author: Browne
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord! thou art good: all nature shows
Its mighty Author kind:
Thy bounty through creation flows,
Full, free, and unconfined.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The whole, and every part, proclaims
Thine infinite good-will;
It shines in stars, and flows in streams,
And blooms on every hill.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: We view it o'er the spreading main,
And heavens which spread more wide;
It drops in gentle showers of rain,
And rolls in every tide.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Through the vast whole it pours supplies,
Spreads joy through every part:
O, may such love attract my eyes,
And captivate my heart!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: My highest admiration raise,
My best affections move!
Employ my tongue in songs of praise,
And fill my heart with love!

hymns.title: O Thou, by long experience tried
hymns.author: Mme. Guion
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O Thou, by long experience tried,
Near whom no grief can long abide;--
My Lord, how full of sweet content
My years of pilgrimage are spent!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: All scenes alike engaging prove,
To souls impressed with sacred love;
Where'er they dwell, they dwell in thee,
In heaven, in earth, or on the sea.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: To them remains nor place nor time;
Their country is in every clime;
They can be calm and free from care
On any shore, since God is there.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: While place we seek, or place we shun,
The soul finds happiness in none;
But with a God to guide our way,
'Tis equal joy to go or stay.

hymns.title: Thy kingdom, Lord, forever stands
hymns.author: Eng. Bap. Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Thy kingdom, Lord, forever stands,
While earthly thrones decay;
And time submits to thy commands,
While ages roll away.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thy sovereign bounty freely gives
Its unexhausted store;
And universal nature lives
On thy sustaining power.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Holy and just in all its ways
Is Providence divine;
In all its works, immortal rays
Of power and mercy shine.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The praise of God--delightful theme!--
Shall fill my heart and tongue;
Let all creation bless his name,
In one eternal song.

hymns.title: Exalt the Lord our God
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Exalt the Lord our God,
And worship at his feet;
His nature is all holiness,
And mercy is his seat.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: When Israel was his church,
When Aaron was his priest,
When Moses cried, when Samuel prayed,
He gave his people rest.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Oft he forgave their sins,
Nor would destroy their race;
And oft he made his vengeance known,
When they abused his grace.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Exalt the Lord our God,
Whose grace is still the same;
Still he's a God of holiness,
And jealous for his name.

hymns.title: O Thou, to whom all creatures bow
hymns.author: Tate & Brady
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O Thou, to whom all creatures bow
Within this earthly frame,
Through all the world how great art thou!
How glorious is thy name!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: When heaven, thy glorious work on high,
Employs my wondering sight,--
The moon, that nightly rules the sky,
With stars of feebler light,--

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Lord, what is man, that he is blessed
With thy peculiar care!
Why on his offspring is conferred
Of love so large a share?

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O Thou, to whom all creatures bow
Within this earthly frame,
Through all the world how great art thou!
How glorious is thy name!

hymns.title: God of the universe! whose hand
hymns.author: Wm. Taylor
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: God of the universe! whose hand
Hath sown with suns the fields of space,
Round which, obeying thy command,
Unnumbered worlds fulfil their race:

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: How vast the region, where thy will
Existence, form, and order gives!
Pleased the wide cup with joy to fill,
For all that grows, and feels, and lives.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Lord! while we thank thee, let us learn
Beneficence to all below;
Those praise thee best, whose bosoms burn
Thy gifts on others to bestow.

hymns.title: Holy as thou, O Lord, is none
hymns.author: C. Wesley
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Holy as thou, O Lord, is none!
Thy holiness is all thine own;
A drop of that unbounded sea
Is ours, a drop derived from thee.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: And when thy purity we share,
Only thy glory we declare;
And humbled into nothing own,
Holy and pure is God alone.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Sole self-existent God and Lord,
By all the heavenly hosts adored!
Let all on earth bow down to thee,
And own thy peerless majesty.

hymns.title: The God who reigns alone
hymns.author: Drummond
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The God who reigns alone
O'er earth, and sea, and sky,
Let man with praises own,
And sound his honors high.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Him all in heaven above,
Him all on earth below,
The exhaustless Source of love,
The great Creator know.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: He formed the living flame,
He gave the reasoning mind;
Then only He may claim
The worship of mankind.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: So taught his only Son,
Blessed messenger of grace!
The Eternal is but one,
No second holds his place.

hymns.title: Jehovah God! thy gracious power
hymns.author: Thomson
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jehovah God! thy gracious power
On every hand we see;
O may the blessings of each hour
Lead all our thoughts to thee.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: If, on the wings of morn, we speed
To earth's remotest bound,
Thy hand will there our footsteps lead,
Thy love, our path surround.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thy power is in the ocean deeps,
And reaches to the skies;
Thine eye of mercy never sleeps,
Thy goodness never dies.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: In all the varying scenes of time,
On thee our hopes depend;
Through every age, in every clime,
Our Father, and our Friend!

hymns.title: Almighty God! thy wondrous works
hymns.author: Beddome
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Almighty God! thy wondrous works
Of providence and grace,
An angel's perfect mind exceed,
And all our pride abase.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Stupendous heights! amazing depths!
Creatures in vain explore:
Or, if a transient glimpse we gain,
'Tis faint and quickly o'er.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Though all the mysteries lie concealed
Beyond what we can see,
Grant us the knowledge of ourselves,
The knowledge, Lord, of thee.

hymns.title: Thou, Lord, by strictest search hast known
hymns.author: Tate & Brady
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Thou, Lord, by strictest search hast known
My rising up and lying down;
My secret thoughts are known to thee,
Known long before conceived by me.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O could I so perfidious be,
To think of once deserting thee!
Where, Lord, could I thy influence shun?
Or whither from thy presence run?

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: If I the morning's wings could gain,
And fly beyond the western main,
Thy swifter hand would first arrive,
And there arrest thy fugitive.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Or should I try to shun thy sight
Beneath the sable wings of night,
One glance from thee, one piercing ray,
Would kindle darkness into day.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Search, try, O God, my thoughts and heart,
If mischief lurks in any part;
Correct me where I go astray,
And guide me in thy perfect way.

hymns.title: Thou art, almighty Lord of all
hymns.author: W. Ray
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Thou art, almighty Lord of all,
From everlasting still the same;
Before thee dazzling seraphs fall,
And veil their faces in a flame,
To see such bright perfections glow--
Such floods of glory from thee flow.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: What mortal hand shall dare to paint
A semblance of thy glory, Lord?
The brightest rainbow-tints are faint;
The brightest stars of heaven afford
But dim effusions of those rays
Of light that round Jehovah blaze.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The sun himself is but a gleam,
A transient meteor, from thy throne;
And every frail and fickle beam,
That ever in creation shone,
Is nothing, Lord, compared to thee
In thy own vast immensity.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: But though thy brightness may create
All worship from the hosts above,
What most thy name must elevate
Is, that thou art a God of love;
And mercy is the central sun
Of all thy glories joined in one.

hymns.title: Can creatures to perfection find
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Can creatures to perfection find
Th' eternal, uncreated Mind?
Or can the largest stretch of thought
Measure and search his nature out?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: God is a King of power unknown;
Firm are the orders of his throne;
If he resolve, who dare oppose,
Or ask him why or what he does?

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: He frowns, and darkness veils the moon
The fainting sun grows dim at noon:
The pillars of heaven's starry roof
Tremble and start at his reproof.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: These are a portion of his ways:
But who shall dare describe his face?
Who can endure his light, or stand
To hear the thunders of his hand?

hymns.title: Since o'er thy footstool here below
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Since o'er thy footstool here below
Such radiant gems are strown,
O what magnificence must glow,
Great God, about thy throne!
So brilliant here these drops of light--
There the full ocean rolls--how bright!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: If night's blue curtain of the sky--
With thousand stars inwrought,
Hung like a royal canopy
With glittering diamonds fraught--
Be, Lord, thy temple's outer veil,
What splendor at the shrine must dwell!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The dazzling sun, at noon-day hour--
Forth from his flaming vase
Flinging o'er earth the golden shower
Till vale and mountain blaze--
But shows, O Lord, one beam of thine:
What, then, the day where thou dost shine?

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O how shall these dim eyes endure
That noon of living rays!
Or how our spirits so impure,
Upon thy glory gaze!--
Anoint, O Lord, anoint our sight,
And fit us for that world of light.

hymns.title: The Lord descended from above
hymns.author: Sternhold
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The Lord descended from above,
And bowed the heavens most high,
And underneath his feet he cast
The darkness of the sky.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: On cherubim and seraphim
Full royally he rode,
And on the wings of mighty winds
Came flying all abroad.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: He sat serene upon the floods,
Their fury to restrain,
And he, as sovereign Lord and King,
Forevermore shall reign.

hymns.title: Let the whole race of creatures lie
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Let the whole race of creatures lie
Abased before the Lord:
Whate'er his mighty hand has formed
He governs with a word.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Ten thousand ages ere the skies
Were into motion brought,
All the long years and worlds to come
Stood present to his thought.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Trusting thy wisdom, God of love,
We would not wish to know
What, in the book of thy decrees,
Awaits us here below

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Be this alone our fervent prayer,--
Whate'er our lot shall be,
Or joys, or sorrows, may they form
Our souls for heaven and thee.

hymns.title: All-powerful, self-existent God
hymns.author: Walker's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: All-powerful, self-existent God,
Who all creation dost sustain!
Thou wast, and art, and art to come,
And everlasting is thy reign!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Fixed and eternal as thy days,
Each glorious attribute divine,
Through ages infinite, shall still
With undiminished lustre shine.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Fountain of being! Source of good!
Immutable thou dost remain!
Nor can the shadow of a change
Obscure the glories of thy reign.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Earth may with all her powers dissolve,
If such the great Creator's will;
But thou forever art the same,
I AM, is thy memorial still.

hymns.title: There's not a place in earth's vast round
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: There's not a place in earth's vast round,
In ocean deep, or air,
Where skill and wisdom are not found,
For God is everywhere.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Around, within, below, above,
Wherever space extends,
There heaven displays its boundless love,
And power with mercy blends.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Then rise, my soul, and sing his name,
And all his praise rehearse,
Who spread abroad earth's wondrous frame,
And built the universe.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Where'er thine earthly lot is cast,
His power and love declare;
Nor think the mighty theme too vast,
For God is everywhere.

hymns.title: Thy ways, O Lord, with wise design
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Thy ways, O Lord, with wise design,
Are framed upon thy throne above,
And every dark or bending line
Meets in the centre of thy love.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: With feeble light, and half obscure,
Poor mortals thine arrangements view,
Not knowing that the least are sure,
And the mysterious just and true.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: They neither know nor trace the way;
But, trusting to thy piercing eye,
None of their feet to ruin stray,
Nor shall the weakest fail or die.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: My favored soul shall meekly learn
To lay her reason at thy throne;
Too weak thy secrets to discern,
I'll trust thee for my guide alone.

hymns.title: Be thou, O God, exalted high
hymns.author: Tate & Brady
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Be thou, O God, exalted high;
And as thy glory fills the sky,
So let it be on earth displayed,
Till thou art here, as there, obeyed.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O God, our hearts are fixed and bent
Their thankful tribute to present;
And, with the heart, the voice, we'll raise
To thee, our God, in songs of praise.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thy praises, Lord, we will resound
To all the listening nations round;
Thy mercy highest heaven transcends;
Thy truth beyond the clouds extends.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Be thou, O God, exalted high;
And as thy glory fills the sky,
So let it be on earth displayed,
Till thou art here, as there, obeyed.

hymns.title: Holy, holy, holy Lord
hymns.author: Salisbury Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Holy, holy, holy Lord,
Be thy glorious name adored;
Lord, thy mercies never fail;
Hail, celestial goodness, hail!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Though unworthy, Lord, thine ear,
Deign our humble songs to hear;
Purer praise we hope to bring,
When around thy throne we sing.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: There no tongue shall silent be;
All shall join in harmony;
That, through heaven's capacious round,
Praise to thee may ever sound.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Lord, thy mercies never fail;
Hail, celestial goodness, hail!
Holy, holy, holy Lord,
Be thy glorious name adored.

hymns.title: O, worship the King, all glorious above
hymns.author: Grant
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O, worship the King, all glorious above,
And gratefully sing his wonderful love,
Our Shield and Defender, the Ancient of Days,
Pavilioned in splendor, and girded with praise.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thy bountiful care what tongue can recite?
It breathes in the air, it shines in the light,
It streams from the hills, it descends to the plain,
And sweetly distils in the dew and the rain.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Frail children of dust, and feeble as frail,
In thee do we trust, nor find thee to fail;
Thy mercies how tender! how firm to the end!
Our Maker, Defender, Redeemer, and Friend.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Father Almighty, how faithful thy love!
While angels delight to hymn thee above,
The humbler creation, though feeble their lays
With true adoration shall lisp to thy praise.

hymns.title: Praise ye the Lord; on every height
hymns.author: Hemans
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Praise ye the Lord; on every height
Songs to his glory raise;
Ye angel hosts, ye stars of night,
Join in immortal praise.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O fire and vapor, hail and snow,
Ye servants of his will;
O stormy winds, that only blow
His mandates to fulfil;--

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Mountains and rocks, to heaven that rise
Fair cedars of the wood;
Creatures of life that wing the skies,
Or track the plains for food;--

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Judges of nations; kings, whose hand
Waves the proud sceptre high;
O youths and virgins of the land;
O age and infancy;--

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Praise ye his name, to whom alone
All homage should be given,
Whose glory, from th' eternal throne,
Spreads wide o'er earth and heaven.

hymns.title: Let us, with a gladsome mind
hymns.author: Milton
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Let us, with a gladsome mind,
Praise the Lord, for he is kind;
For his mercies shall endure,
Ever faithful, ever sure.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Let us sound his name abroad,
For of gods he is the God;
Who, with all-commanding might,
Filled the new-made world with light;

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Caused the golden-tressed sun
All day long his course to run;
And the moon to shine by night,
'Mongst her spangled sisters bright.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: His own people he did bless,
In the wasteful wilderness;
He hath, with a piteous eye,
Viewed us in our misery.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: All his creatures he doth feed;
His full hand supplies their need;
Let us, therefore, warble forth
His high majesty and worth.

hymns.title: O render thanks to God above
hymns.author: Tate & Brady
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O render thanks to God above,
The fountain of eternal love;
Whose mercy firm through ages past
Has stood and shall forever last.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Who can his mighty deeds express?--
Not only vast, but numberless!
What mortal eloquence can raise
His tribute of immortal praise?

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Happy are they, and only they,
Who from thy judgments never stray;
Who know what's right, nor only so,
But always practise what they know.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Extend to me that favor, Lord,
Thou to thy chosen dost afford:
When thou return'st to set them free,
Let thy salvation visit me.

hymns.title: Glory be to God on high
hymns.author: J. Taylor
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Glory be to God on high,
God, whose glory fills the sky;
Peace on earth to man forgiven,
Man, the well-beloved of Heaven.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Favored mortals! raise the song;
Endless thanks to God belong;
Hearts o'erflowing with his praise,
Join the hymns your voices raise.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Mark the wonders of his hand!
Power no empire can withstand;
Wisdom, angel's glorious theme;
Goodness, one eternal stream.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Awful Being! from thy throne
Send thy promised blessings down;
Let thy light, thy truth, thy peace,
Bid our raging passions cease.

hymns.title: To thee, great Source of light
hymns.author: Sacred Lyrics
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: To thee, great Source of light!
My thankful voice I'll raise;
And all my powers unite
To celebrate thy praise;
And, till my voice is lost in death,
May praise employ my every breath.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: And when this feeble tongue
Lies silent in the dust,
My soul shall dwell among
The spirits of the just;
Then, with the shining hosts above,
In nobler strains I'll sing thy love.

hymns.title: Praise ye the Lord, around whose throne
hymns.author: H. Ballou, 2d
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Praise ye the Lord, around whose throne
All heaven in ceaseless worship waits,
Whose glory fills the worlds unknown--
Praise ye the Lord from Zion's gates.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: With mingling souls and voices join;
To him the swelling anthem raise;
Repeat his name with joy divine,
And fill the temple with his praise.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: All-gracious God, to thee we owe
Each joy and blessing time affords,--
Light, life, and health, and all below,
Spring from thy presence, Lord of lords.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thine be the praise, for thine the love
That freely all our sins forgave,
Pointed our dying eyes above,
And showed us life beyond the grave.

hymns.title: My God, my King, thy various praise
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: My God, my King, thy various praise
Shall fill the remnant of my days;
Thy grace employ my humble tongue
Till death and glory raise the song.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The wings of every hour shall bear
Some thankful tribute to thine ear;
And every setting sun shall see
New works of duty done for thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Let distant times and nations raise
The long succession of thy praise,
And unborn ages make my song
The joy and labor of their tongue.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: But who can speak thy wondrous deeds?
Thy greatness all our thoughts exceeds
Vast and unsearchable thy ways:
Vast and immortal be thy praise.

hymns.title: Praise ye Jehovah's name
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Praise ye Jehovah's name;
Praise through his courts proclaim;
Rise and adore;--
High o'er the heavens above
Sound his great acts of love,
While his rich grace we prove,
Vast as his power.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Now let the trumpet raise
Sounds of triumphant praise
Wide as his fame;
There let the harp be found;
Organs, with solemn sound,
Roll your deep notes around,
Filled with his name.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: While his high praise ye sing,
Shake every sounding string:
Sweet the accord!--
He vital breath bestows:
Let every breath that flows
His noblest fame disclose--
Praise ye the Lord.

hymns.title: Ye boundless realms of joy
hymns.author: Tate & Brady
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Ye boundless realms of joy,
Exalt your Maker's name;
His praise your songs employ
Above the starry frame:
Your voices raise,
Ye cherubim
And seraphim,
To sing his praise.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Let all adore the Lord,
And praise his holy name,
By whose almighty word
They all from nothing came;
And all shall last,
From changes free;
His firm decree
Stands ever fast.

hymns.title: Begin, my soul, th' exalted lay
hymns.author: Ogilvie
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Begin, my soul, th' exalted lay;
Let each enraptured thought obey,
And praise th' Almighty's name.
Lo, heaven and earth and seas and skies
In one melodious concert rise
To swell th' inspiring theme.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thou heaven of heavens, his vast abode--
Ye clouds, proclaim your Maker, God;
Ye thunders, speak his power.
Lo, on the lightning's rapid wings
In triumph rides the King of Kings:
Th' astonished worlds adore.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Ye deeps with roaring billows rise
To join the thunders of the skies--
Praise him who bids you roll.
His praise in softer notes declare,
Each whispering breeze of yielding air,
And breathe it to the soul.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Wake, all ye soaring throngs, and sing;
Ye cheerful warblers of the spring,
Harmonious anthems raise
To him who shaped your finer mould,
Who tipped your glittering wings with gold,
And tuned your voice to praise.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Let man, by nobler passions swayed,
The feeling heart, the reasoning head,
In heavenly praise employ:
Spread the Creator's name around,
Till heaven's wide arch repeat the sound--
The general burst of joy.

hymns.title: O praise ye the Lord--prepare a new song
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O praise ye the Lord--prepare a new song,
And let all his saints in full concert join;
With voices united the anthem prolong,
And show forth his praises with music divine.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Let praise to the Lord, who made us, ascend;
Let each grateful heart be glad in its King;
The God whom we worship our songs will attend,
And view with complacence the offering we bring.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Be joyful, ye saints sustained by his might,
And let your glad songs awake with each morn;
For those who obey him are still his delight--
His hand with salvation the meek will adorn.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Then praise ye the Lord--prepare a glad song,
And let all his saints in full concert join;
With voices united the anthem prolong,
And show forth his praises with music divine.

hymns.title: Wide as his vast dominion lies
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Wide as his vast dominion lies,
Make the Creator's name be known;
Loud as his thunders speak his praise,
And sound it lofty as his throne.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Jehovah!--'tis a glorious word;
O may it dwell on every tongue;
But saints, who best have known the Lord,
Are bound to raise the noblest song.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Speak of the wonders of that love
Which Gabriel plays on every chord;
From all below, and all above,
Loud hallelujahs to the Lord.

hymns.title: O God, we praise thee, and confess
hymns.author: Patrick
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O God, we praise thee, and confess,
That thou the only Lord
And everlasting Father art,
By all the earth adored.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: To thee all angels cry aloud--
To thee the powers on high,
Both cherubim and seraphim,
Continually do cry--

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: "O holy, holy, holy Lord,
Whom heavenly hosts obey,
The world is with the glory filled
Of thy majestic sway."

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Th' apostles' glorious company,
And prophets, crowned with light,
With all the martyrs' noble host,
Thy constant praise recite.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: The holy church throughout the world,
