Return, ye ransomed sinners, home.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The gospel trumpet hear,
The news of pardoning grace:
Ye happy souls, draw near;
Behold your Saviour's face:
The year of jubilee is come;
Return, ye ransomed sinners, home.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Jesus, our great High Priest,
Has full assurance made;
Ye weary spirits, rest;
Ye mourning souls, be glad:
The year of jubilee is come;
Return, ye ransomed sinners, home.

hymns.title: Praise to God, the great Creator
hymns.author: J. Taylor
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Praise to God, the great Creator,
Bounteous Source of every joy,
He whose hand upholds all nature,
He whose word can all destroy!
Saints with pious zeal attending,
Now the grateful tribute raise;
Solemn songs, to heaven ascending,
Join the universal praise.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Here indulge each grateful feeling;
Lowly bend with contrite souls;
Here, his milder grace revealing,
Here no peal of thunder rolls:
Lo, the sacred page before us
Bears the promise of his love,
Full of mercy to restore us,
Mercy beaming from above.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Every secret fault confessing,
Deed unrighteous, thought of sin,
Seize, O seize the proffered blessing,
Grace from God, and peace within!
Heart and voice with rapture swelling,
Still the song of glory raise;
On the theme immortal dwelling,
Join the universal praise.

hymns.title: Brighter shines the gospel day
hymns.author: J. G. Adams
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Brighter shines the gospel day
On our Zion's mountains;
Clearer has become the way
To her living fountains.
Hark! the stirring trumpet tone
Hath o'er every hill-top flown;
Error's hosts retiring see!
Superstition's minions flee!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: From the luring haunts of sin
Where the soul is blighted,
Christ invites--come enter in
To the temple lighted
With the beams of pardoning love--
With the wisdom from above;
Leave the woes of sin behind,
And a rest perpetual find.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Come from error's hoary shrine,
Jew or Gentile seeking
For the way of life divine--
Hear this voice now speaking!
Willing hearts and hands prepare
Christ's redeeming grace to share;
Join our triumph-strain, and sing
Zion's Universal King.

hymns.title: O, what amazing words of grace
hymns.author: Medley
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O, what amazing words of grace
Are in the gospel found!
Suited to every sinner's case,
Who hears the joyful sound.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Come, then, with all your wants and wounds;
Your every burden bring;
Here love, unchanging love, abounds,
A deep, celestial spring.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: This spring with living water flows,
And heavenly joy imparts;
Come, thirsty souls, your wants disclose,
And drink with thankful hearts.

hymns.title: Now is the day of grace
hymns.author: Select Hymns
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Now is the day of grace;
Now to the Father come;
The Lord is calling, "Seek my face,
And I will guide you home."

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The Saviour bids you speed;
O, wherefore then delay?
He calls in love; he sees your need;
He bids you come to-day.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: To-day the prize is won;
The promise is to save;
Then, O, be wise; to-morrow's sun
May shine upon your grave.

hymns.title: Ye wretched, hungry, starving poor
hymns.author: Mrs. Steele
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Ye wretched, hungry, starving poor,
Behold a royal feast,
Where mercy spreads her bounteous store,
For every humble guest!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: See, Jesus stands with open arms!
He calls, he bids you come;--
Guilt holds you back, and fear alarms,--
But see, there yet is room!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Come then, and with his people taste
The blessings of his love;
While hope attends the sweet repast,
Of nobler joys above.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: There, with united heart and voice,
Before the eternal throne,
Ten thousand thousand souls rejoice
In ecstasies unknown.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: And yet ten thousand thousand more
Are welcome still to come:
Ye longing souls, the grace adore;--
Approach, there yet is room.

hymns.title: From the holy mount above
hymns.author: Hawes
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: From the holy mount above,
Glowing in the light of love,
What melodious sounds we hear,
Bursting on the ravished ear!
"At the feast there yet is room--
Come and welcome, sinner, come.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: "Thou shalt be a welcome guest,
By the Lord divinely blest;--
In the word of truth believe,--
All thy sinful pleasures leave,
And no more in darkness roam,--
Come and welcome, sinner, come.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: "God is thy unchanging Friend;
He will love thee to the end,
And at last thy soul convey
To the realms of endless day,
To a blessed spirit-home,--
Come and welcome, sinner, come."

hymns.title: Sinner, rouse thee from thy sleep
hymns.author: Episcopal Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Sinner, rouse thee from thy sleep;
Wake, and o'er thy folly weep;
Raise thy spirit, dark and dead;
Jesus waits his light to shed.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Wake from sleep; arise from death;
See the bright and living path;
Watchful, tread that path; be wise;
Leave thy folly; seek the skies.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Leave thy folly; cease from crime;
From this hour redeem thy time;
Life secure without delay;
Brief is this thy mortal day.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O, then, rouse thee from thy sleep;
Wake, and o'er thy folly weep;
Jesus calls from death and night;
Jesus waits to shed his light.

hymns.title: Peace, troubled soul, whose plaintive moan
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Peace, troubled soul, whose plaintive moan
Hath taught these rocks the notes of woe;
Cease thy complaints, suppress thy groan,
And let thy tears forget to flow:
Behold the precious balm is found,
Which lulls thy pain, which heals thy wound.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Come, freely come; by sin oppressed,
Unburden here the weighty load,
Here find thy refuge and thy rest,
And trust the mercy of thy God:
Thy God's thy Father,--glorious word!
Forever love and praise the Lord.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: As spring the winter, day the night,
Peace sorrow's gloom shall chase away,
And smiling joy, a seraph bright,
Shall tend thy steps and near thee stay;
Whilst glory weaves th' immortal crown,
And waits to claim thee for her own.

hymns.title: Pilgrim, burdened with thy sin
hymns.author: Bowring
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Pilgrim, burdened with thy sin,
Come the way to Zion's gate,
There, till mercy speaks within,
Knock, and weep, and watch and wait--
Knock--he knows the sinner's cry,
Weep--he loves the mourner's tears,
Watch--for saving grace is nigh,
Wait--till heavenly grace appears.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Hark, it is thy Saviour's voice!
"Welcome, pilgrim, to thy rest."
Now within the gate rejoice,
Safe, and owned, and bought and blest--
Safe--from all the lures of vice,
Owned--by joys the contrite know,
Bought--by love and life the price,
Blest--the mighty debt to owe!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Holy pilgrim! what for thee,
In a world like this, remains?
From thy guarded breast shall flee
Fear, and shame, and doubt and pains--
Fear--the hope of heaven shall fly,
Shame--from glory's view retire,
Doubt--in full belief shall die,
Pain--in endless bliss expire.

hymns.title: Bright was the guiding star that led
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Bright was the guiding star that led,
With mild benignant ray,
The Gentiles to the lowly shed
Where the Redeemer lay.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: But lo! a brighter, clearer light
Now points to his abode
It shines through sin and sorrow's night
To guide us to our Lord.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O haste to follow where it leads;
The gracious call obey,
Be rugged wilds, or flowery meads,
The Christian's destined way.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O gladly tread the narrow path
While light and grace are given;
We'll meekly follow Christ on earth,
And reign with him in heaven.

hymns.title: Return, O wanderer, now return
hymns.author: Collyer
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Return, O wanderer, now return,
And seek thy Father's face;
Those new desires, which in thee burn,
Were kindled by his grace.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Return, O wanderer, now return;
He hears thy humble sigh;
He sees thy softened spirit mourn,
When no one else is nigh.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Return, O wanderer, now return;
Thy Father bids thee live;
Go to his feet, and grateful learn
How freely he'll forgive.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Return, O wanderer, now return,
And wipe the falling tear;
Thy Father calls--no longer mourn;
'T is love invites thee near.

hymns.title: Were not the sinful Mary's tears
hymns.author: Moore
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Were not the sinful Mary's tears
An offering worthy heaven,
When, o'er the faults of former years,
She wept, and was forgiven?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: When, bringing every balmy sweet
Her day of luxury stored,
She o'er her Saviour's hallowed feet
The precious ointment poured;

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Were not those sweets so freely shed,
That shame, those weeping eyes,
And the sunk heart which inly bled,
Heaven's noblest sacrifice?

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thou that hast slept in error's sleep,
O wouldst thou wake to heaven,
Like Mary kneel, like Mary weep;
"Love much," and be forgiven!

hymns.title: Brother, hast thou wandered far
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Brother, hast thou wandered far
From thy father's happy home?
With thyself and God at war?
Turn thee, brother, homeward come.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Hast thou wasted all the powers
God for noble uses gave?
Squandered life's most golden hours?
Turn thee, brother, God can save!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Is a mighty famine now
In thy heart and in thy soul?
Discontent upon thy brow?
Turn thee, God will make thee whole!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Fall before him on the ground,
Pour thy sorrow in his ear,
Seek him, while he may be found,
Call upon him, while he's near.

hymns.title: The Spirit, in our hearts
hymns.author: Episcopal Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The Spirit, in our hearts,
Is whispering, "Sinner, come!"
The Bride, the Church of Christ, proclaims
To all his children, "Come!"

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Let him that heareth say
To all about him, "Come!"
Let him that thirsts for righteousness,
To Christ, the Fountain, come!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Yes, whosoever will,
O, let him freely come,
And freely drink the stream of life;
'Tis Jesus bids him come.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Lo, Jesus, who invites,
Declares, "I quickly come:"
Lord, even so! I wait thine hour:
Jesus, my Saviour, come!

hymns.title: Blest are the souls that hear and know
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Blest are the souls that hear and know
The gospel's joyful sound;
Peace shall attend the paths they go,
And light their steps surround.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Their joy shall bear their spirits up,
Through their Redeemer's name;
His righteousness exalts their hope,
Nor dares the world condemn.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The Lord, our glory and defence,
Strength and salvation gives;
Israel, thy King forever reigns,
Thy God forever lives.

hymns.title: The King of heaven his table spreads
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The King of heaven his table spreads,
And dainties crown the board:
Not Paradise, with all its joys,
Could such delight afford.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Ye hungry poor, that long have strayed
In sin's dark mazes, come;
Come from your most obscure retreats
And grace shall find you room.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Millions of souls, in glory now,
Were fed and feasted here;
And millions more, still on the way,
Around the board appear.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Yet are his house and heart so large,
That millions more may come;
Nor could the whole assembled world
O'erfill the spacious room.

hymns.title: Ye sons of earth, arise
hymns.author: Pratt's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Ye sons of earth, arise,
Ye creatures of a day;
Redeem the time--be bold--be wise,
And cast your bonds away.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The year of gospel grace
With us rejoice to see,
And thankfully in Christ embrace
Your proffered liberty.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Blest Saviour, Lord of all,
God help us to receive;
Obedient to thy gracious call,
O, bid us turn and live.

hymns.title: Hear what a Saviour's voice
hymns.author: E. Turner
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Hear what a Saviour's voice,
To sinners, does proclaim;
O, all ye ransomed souls, rejoice
In your Redeemer's name.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Where sin and death have reigned,
And all their power employed,
There are his love and light maintained,
And heavenly truth enjoyed.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The needy, starving poor
Are filled with living bread;
The opening of the prison door
Proclaims the captive freed.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The thirsty, panting soul,
That longs for springs of grace,
Beholds celestial waters roll,
And floods of righteousness.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: My God, my Saviour too,
I would thy love proclaim,
Partake of what is brought to view,
And sing thy glorious name.

hymns.title: Behold, the morning sun
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Behold, the morning sun
Begins his glorious way;
His beams through all the nations run,
And life and light convey.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: But where the gospel comes,
It spreads diviner light;
It calls dead sinners from their tombs,
And gives the blind their sight.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: How perfect is thy word!
And all thy judgments just!
Forever sure thy promise, Lord,
And we securely trust.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: My gracious God, how plain
Are thy directions given!
O, may we never read in vain,
But find the path to heaven.

hymns.title: Come to the living waters, come
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come to the living waters, come!
Gladly obey your Maker's call:--
Return, ye weary wand'rers, home,
And find his grace is free for all.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: See from the rock a fountain rise;
For you in healing streams it rolls;
Money ye need not bring, nor price,
Ye weary, heavy-laden souls.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: In search of empty joys below,
Why toil with unavailing strife?
Whither, ah! whither would ye go?
Christ hath the words of endless life.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Your willing ears and hearts incline,
His words believingly receive;
Quicken'd, you then, by faith divine,
A heavenly life on earth shall live.

hymns.title: How gracious the promise, how soothing the word
hymns.author: S. F. Streeter
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How gracious the promise, how soothing the word
That came from the lips of our merciful Lord!
"Ye lone, and ye weary, ye sad and oppressed,
Come, learn of your Saviour, and ye shall find rest."

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Ye proud, from the paths of ambition depart,
For meek was your Master, and lowly of heart.
And all who have sinned and have wandered astray,
Come, walk in the light and the truth and the way.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Ye heart-stricken sons, and ye daughters of woe,
For you the fresh fountains of comfort o'erflow;
Your souls to the blessed Redeemer unite,--
His yoke it is easy, his burden is light.

hymns.title: Come! said Jesus' sacred voice
hymns.author: Mrs. Barbauld
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come! said Jesus' sacred voice,
Come, and make my paths your choice;
I will guide you to your home;
Weary pilgrim, hither come!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thou who, houseless, sole, forlorn,
Long hast borne the proud world's scorn,
Long hast roamed the barren waste,
Weary pilgrim, hither haste!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Ye who, tossed on beds of pain,
Seek for ease, but seek in vain;
Ye, whose swollen and sleepless eyes
Watch to see the morning rise;

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Ye, by fiercer anguish torn,
In remorse for guilt who mourn,
Here repose your heavy care:
Who the stings of guilt can bear?

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Sinner, come! for here is found
Balm that flows for every wound;
Peace that ever shall endure,
Rest eternal, sacred, sure.

hymns.title: Jesus shall reign where'er the sun
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus shall reign where'er the sun
Does his successive journeys run;
His kingdom stretch from shore to shore
Till moons shall wax and wane no more.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: For him shall endless prayer be made,
And endless praises crown his head;
His name, like sweet perfume, shall rise
With every morning sacrifice.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: People and realms of every tongue
Dwell on his love with sweetest song;
And infant voices shall proclaim
Their early blessings on his name.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Blessings abound where'er he reigns;
The joyful prisoner bursts his chains;
The weary find eternal rest,
And all the sons of want are blest.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Let every creature rise and bring
Peculiar honors to our King;
Angels descend with songs again
And earth repeat the loud Amen.

hymns.title: The Lord will come, and not be slow
hymns.author: Milton
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The Lord will come, and not be slow;
His footsteps cannot err;
Before him righteousness shall go,
His royal harbinger.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The nations all whom thou hast made
Shall come, and all shall frame
To bow them low before thee, Lord,
And glorify thy name.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Truth from the earth, like to a flower,
Shall bud and blossom then,
And Justice, from her heavenly bower,
Look down on mortal men.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thee will I praise, O Lord, my God,
Thee honor and adore
With my whole heart, and blaze abroad
Thy name for evermore.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: For great thou art, and wonders great
By thy strong hand are done:
Thou, in thy everlasting seat,
Remainest God alone.

hymns.title: Jesus his empire shall extend
hymns.author: H. Ballou
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus his empire shall extend;
Beneath his gentle sway
Kings of the earth shall humbly bend,
And his commands obey.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: From sea to sea, from shore to shore,
All nations shall be blest;
We hear the noise of war no more,--
He gives his people rest.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: As clouds descend in gentle showers,
When spring renews her reign;
And call to life the fragrant flowers
O'er forest, hill and plain;--

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: So Jesus, by his heavenly grace,
Descends on man below,
And o'er the millions of our race
His gentle blessings flow.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: All that the reign of sin destroyed,
The Saviour shall restore;
And, from the treasures of the Lord,
Shall give us blessings more.

hymns.title: Come, sing a Saviour's power
hymns.author: E. Turner
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, sing a Saviour's power,
And praise his mighty name;
His wondrous love adore,
And chant his growing fame.
Wide o'er the world a king shall reign,
And righteousness and peace maintain.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The sceptre of his grace
He shall forever wield;
His foes, before his face,
To strength divine shall yield:
The conquest of his truth shall show
What an almighty arm can do.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: His alienated sons,
By sin beguiled, betrayed,
Shall then be born at once,
And willing subjects made:
Such numbers shall his courts adorn
As dew-drops of the vernal morn.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: His realm shall ever stand,
By liberal things upheld:
And from his bounteous hand
All hearts with joy be filled.
A universe with praise shall own
The countless honors of his throne.

hymns.title: Hail to the Lord's Anointed
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Hail to the Lord's Anointed,
Great David's greater Son!
Hail! in the time appointed
His reign on earth begun!
He comes to break oppression,
To set the captive free,
To take away transgression,
And rule in equity.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: He shall descend like showers
Upon the fruitful earth,
And love and joy, like flowers,
Spring in his path to birth;
Before him, on the mountains,
Shall peace, the herald, go;
And righteousness, in fountains,
From hill to valley flow.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: For him shall prayer unceasing
And daily vows ascend,
His kingdom still increasing,--
A kingdom without end:
The tide of time shall never
His covenant remove:
His name shall stand forever;--
That name to us is love.

hymns.title: When God descends with men to dwell
hymns.author: H. Ballou
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When God descends with men to dwell,
And all creation makes anew,
What tongue can half the wonders tell?
What eye the dazzling glories view?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Zion, the desolate, again
Shall see her lands with roses bloom;
And Carmel's mount, and Sharon's plain,
Shall yield their spices and perfume.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Celestial streams shall gently flow;
The wilderness shall joyful be;
Lilies on parchéd ground shall grow;
And gladness spring on every tree;

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The weak be strong, the fearful bold,
The deaf shall hear, the dumb shall sing,
The lame shall walk, the blind behold;
And joy through all the earth shall ring.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Monarchs and slaves shall meet in love;
Old pride shall die, and meekness reign,--
When God descends from worlds above,
To dwell with men on earth again.

hymns.title: On Zion, his most holy mount
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: On Zion, his most holy mount,
God will a feast prepare;
And Israel's sons, and Gentile lands,
Shall in the banquet share.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: See to the vilest of the vile
A free acceptance given!
See rebels, by adopting grace,
Sit with the heirs of heaven!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The pained, the sick, the dying, now
To ease and health restored,
With eager appetites partake
The plenties of the board.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: But, O, what draughts of bliss unknown,
What dainties shall be given,
When, with the myriads round the throne,
We join the feast of heaven!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: There joys immeasurably high
Shall overflow the soul,
And springs of life, that never dry,
In thousand channels roll.

hymns.title: "Give us room, that we may dwell,"
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: "Give us room, that we may dwell,"
Zion's children cry aloud:
See their numbers how they swell!
How they gather like a cloud!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O, how bright the morning seems!
Brighter from so dark a night:
Zion is like one that dreams,
Filled with wonder and delight.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Lo! thy sun goes down no more:
God himself will be thy light:
All that caused thee grief before
Buried lies in endless night.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Zion, now arise and shine;
Lo! thy light from heaven is come
These that crowd from far are thine;
Give thy sons and daughters room.

hymns.title: As showers on meadows newly mown
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: As showers on meadows newly mown,
Jesus shall shed his blessings down;
Crowned with whose life-infusing drops,
Earth shall renew her blissful crops.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The dews and rains, in all their store,
Drenching the pastures o'er and o'er,
Are not so copious as that grace
Which sanctifies and saves our race.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: As, in soft silence, vernal showers
Descend, and cheer the fainting flowers,
So, in the secrecy of love,
Falls the sweet influence from above.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: That heavenly influence let me find
In holy silence of the mind,
While every grace maintains its bloom,
Diffusing wide its rich perfume.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Nor let these blessings be confined
To me, but poured on all mankind,
Till earth's wild wastes in verdure rise,
And a young Eden bless our eyes.

hymns.title: Mark the soft-falling snow
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Mark the soft-falling snow,
And the descending rain!
To heaven, from whence it fell,
It turns not back again;
But waters earth through every pore,
And calls forth all her secret store.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Arrayed in beauteous green
The hills and valleys shine,
And man and beast are fed
By providence divine:
The harvest bows its golden ears,
The copious seed of future years.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: "So," saith the God of grace,
"My Gospel shall descend,
Almighty to effect
The purpose I intend;
Millions of souls shall feel its power,
And bear it down to millions more."

hymns.title: In God's eternity
hymns.author: H. Ballou
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: In God's eternity
There shall a day arise,
When all the race of man shall be
With Jesus in the skies.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: As night before the rays
Of morning flees away,
Sin shall retire before the blaze
Of God's eternal day.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: As music fills the grove
When stormy clouds are past,
Sweet anthems of redeeming love
Shall all employ at last.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Redeemed from death and sin,
Shall Adam's numerous race
A ceaseless song of praise begin,
And shout redeeming grace.

hymns.title: Let all the earth their voices raise
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Let all the earth their voices raise,
To sing the choicest psalm of praise;
To sing and bless Jehovah's name:
His glory let the heathen know,
His wonders to the nations show,
And all his saving works proclaim.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The heathen know thy glory, Lord:
The wondering nations read thy word:
Among us is Jehovah known;
Our worship shall no more be paid
To gods which mortal hands have made;
Our Maker is our God alone.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Come the great day, the glorious hour,
When earth shall feel his saving power,
And barbarous nations fear his name;
Then shall the race of man confess
The beauty of his holiness,
And in his courts his grace proclaim.

hymns.title: Upon the Gospel's sacred page
hymns.author: Bowring
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Upon the Gospel's sacred page
The gathered beams of ages shine;
And, as it hastens, every age
But makes its brightness more divine.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Truth, strengthened by the strength of thought,
Pours inexhaustible supplies,
Whence sagest teachers may be taught,
And Wisdom's self become more wise.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: More glorious still as centuries roll,
New regions blest, new powers unfurled,
Expanding with the expanding soul,
Its waters shall o'erflow the world;

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Flow to restore, but not destroy;
As when the cloudless lamp of day
Pours out its floods of light and joy,
And sweeps each lingering mist away.

hymns.title: Great God, whose universal sway
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Great God, whose universal sway
The known and unknown worlds obey;
Now give the kingdom to thy Son;
Extend his power, exalt his throne.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The heathen lands, that lie beneath
The shades of overspreading death,
Revive at his first dawning light,
And deserts blossom at the sight.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The saints shall flourish in his days,
Dressed in the robes of joy and praise;
Peace, like a river, from his throne
Shall flow to nations yet unknown.

hymns.title: Rise, crowned with light, imperial Salem, rise
hymns.author: Pope
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Rise, crowned with light, imperial Salem, rise!
Exalt thy towering head, and lift thine eyes!
See heaven its sparkling portals wide display,
And break upon thee in a flood of day!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: See a long race thy spacious courts adorn,
See future sons and daughters yet unborn,
In crowding ranks on every side arise,
Demanding life, impatient for the skies!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: See barbarous nations at thy gates attend,
Walk in thy light, and in thy temples bend!
See thy bright altars thronged with prostrate kings,
While every land its joyous tribute brings.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The seas shall waste, the skies to smoke decay,
Rocks fall to dust, and mountains melt away
But fixed his word, his saving power remains,
Thy realm shall last, thy own Messiah reigns.

hymns.title: Yes, we trust the day is breaking
hymns.author: Kelly
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Yes, we trust the day is breaking;
Joyful times are near at hand;
God, the mighty God, is speaking,
By his word, in every land:
When he chooses,
Darkness flies at his command.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: While the foe becomes more daring,
While he enters like a flood,
God, the Saviour, is preparing
Means to spread his truth abroad:
Every language
Soon shall tell the love of God.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: God of Jacob, high and glorious,
Let thy people see thy hand;
Let the gospel be victorious,
Through the world, in every land;
Then shall idols
Perish, Lord, at thy command.

hymns.title: When shall the voice of singing
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When shall the voice of singing
Flow joyfully along?
When hill and valley, ringing
With one triumphant song,
Proclaim the contest ended,
And Him, who once was slain,
Again to earth descended,
In righteousness to reign?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Then from the craggy mountains
The sacred shout shall fly,
And shady vales and fountains
Shall echo the reply:
High tower and lowly dwelling
Shall send the chorus round,
The hallelujah swelling
In one eternal sound.

hymns.title: Lo! what a glorious sight appears
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lo! what a glorious sight appears
To our believing eyes!
The earth and seas are passed away,
And the old rolling skies.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: From the third heaven, where God resides
That holy, happy place,
The new Jerusalem comes down,
Adorned with shining grace.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: "The God of glory down to men
Removes his blessed abode;
Men, the dear objects of his grace,
And he, the loving God.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: "His own soft hand shall wipe the tears
From every weeping eye;
And pains and groans, and griefs and fears,
And death itself shall die."

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: How long, dear Saviour, O how long
Shall this bright hour delay?
Fly swifter round, ye wheels of time,
And bring the welcome day.

hymns.title: Long as the darkening cloud abode
hymns.author: Richards
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Long as the darkening cloud abode,
So long did ancient Israel rest;
Nor moved they, till the guiding Lord
In brighter garments stood confest.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Father of spirits, Light of light,
Lift up the cloud, and rend the veil;
Shine forth in fire, amid that night,
Whose blackness makes the heart to fail.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: 'T is done! to Christ the power is given;
His death has rent the veil away,
Our great Forerunner entered heaven,
And oped the gate of endless day.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Nor shall those mists that brood o'er time,
Forever blind the mental eye;
They backward roll, and light sublime
Beams glory from our God on high.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Adoring nations hail the dawn,
All kingdoms bless the noontide beam,
And light, unfolding life's full morn,
Is vast creation's deathless theme.

hymns.title: Come, kingdom of our God
hymns.author: Johns
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, kingdom of our God,
Sweet reign of light and love!
Shed peace, and hope, and joy abroad,
And wisdom from above.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Over our spirits first
Extend thy healing reign;
There raise and quench the sacred thirst,
That never pains again.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Come, kingdom of our God!
And make the broad earth thine,
Stretch o'er her lands and isles the rod
That flowers with grace divine.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Soon may all tribes be blest
With fruit from life's glad tree;
And in its shade like brothers rest
Sons of one family.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Come, kingdom of our God!
And raise thy glorious throne
In worlds by the undying trod,
Where God shall bless his own.

hymns.title: Pour, blessed Gospel, glorious news for man
hymns.author: Ashworth
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Pour, blessed Gospel, glorious news for man!
Thy stream of life o'er springless deserts roll:
Thy bond of peace the mighty earth can span,
And make one brotherhood from pole to pole.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: On, piercing Gospel, on! of every heart,
In every latitude, thou own'st the key:
From their dull slumbers savage souls shall start,
With all their treasures first unlocked by thee!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Tread, kingly Gospel, through the nations tread!
With all the civil virtues in thy train:
Be all to thy blest freedom captive led;
And Christ, the true emancipator, reign!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Spread, giant Gospel, spread thy growing wings!
Gather thy scattered ones from every land:
Call home the wanderers to the King of kings;
Proclaim them all thine own;--'t is Christ's command!

hymns.title: Hark! the song of jubilee
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Hark! the song of jubilee,
Loud as mighty thunders roar,
Or the fulness of the sea,
When it breaks upon the shore;--
Hallelujah to the Lord!
God omnipotent shall reign;
Hallelujah! let the word
Echo round the earth and main.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Hallelujah!--hark! the sound,
Heard through earth, and through the skies,
Wakes above, beneath, around,
All creation's harmonies:
See Jehovah's banner furled,
Sheathed his sword; he speaks,--'t is done!
And the kingdoms of this world
Are the kingdoms of his Son.

hymns.title: See how great a flame aspires
hymns.author: C. Wesley
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: See how great a flame aspires,
Kindled by a spark of grace!
Jesus' love the nations fires,
Sets the kingdoms on a blaze.
To bring fire on earth he came:
Kindled in some hearts it is:
O that all might catch the flame,
All partake the glorious bliss!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: When he first the work begun,
Small and feeble was his day:
Now the word doth swiftly run,
Now it wins its widening way:
More and more it spreads and grows,
Ever mighty to prevail;
Sin's strong-holds it now o'erthrows,
Shakes the trembling gates of hell.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Saw ye not the cloud arise,
Little as a human hand?
Now it spreads along the skies,
Hangs o'er all the thirsty land!
Lo! the promise of a shower
Drops already from above!
Haste, O Lord, and quickly pour
All the spirit of thy love.

hymns.title: Thou, whose wide extended sway
hymns.author: A. C. Thomas
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Thou, whose wide extended sway
Suns and systems e'er obey!
Thou, our Guardian and our Stay,
Evermore adored:
In prospective, Lord, we see
Jew and Gentile, bond and free,
Reconciled in Christ to thee,
Holy, holy Lord.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thou by all shalt be confessed,
Ever blessing, ever blest,
When to thy eternal rest,
In the courts above,
Thou shall bring the sore oppressed;
Fill each joy-desiring breast;
Make of each a welcome guest,
At the feast of love.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: When destroying death shall die,
Hushed be every rising sigh,
Tears be wiped from every eye,
Never more to fall;
Then shall praises fill the sky,
And angelic hosts shall cry,
Holy, Holy Lord, Most High,
Thou art all in all!

hymns.title: On thy church, O Power Divine
hymns.author: Spirit of the Psalms
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: On thy church, O Power Divine,
Cause thy glorious face to shine;
Till the nations from afar
Hail her as their guiding star;
Till her sons, from zone to zone,
Make thy great salvation known.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Then shall God, with lavish hand,
Scatter blessings o'er the land;
Earth shall yield her rich increase,
Every breeze shall whisper peace,
And the world's remotest bound
With the voice of praise resound.

hymns.title: Sound the full chorus! let praises ascend
hymns.author: Peculiar
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Sound the full chorus! let praises ascend
To God the Creator, our Father and Friend.
Sing, for the light of his truth is before us,
And we will give thanks, and rejoice in his name;
His banner of love in its glory waves o'er us;
That love will continue forever the same.
Sound the full chorus, &c.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Praise to Jehovah! Give praise--let it rise
From earth, in its fulness--and swell to the skies!
Give glory and praise! For a ransomed creation
The gospel of peace in its triumph shall see;
Our God hath redeemed us--and Christ our salvation
Appears, from transgression and death to make free!
Praise to Jehovah, &c.

hymns.title: We long to see that happy time
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: We long to see that happy time,
That long-expected, blissful day,
When men of every name and clime
The glorious gospel shall obey.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The word of God shall firm abide,
Though earth and hell should dare oppose;
The stone cut from the mountain's side,
To universal empire grows.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Afric's emancipated sons
Shall shout to Asia's rapt'rous song,
Europe, with her unnumbered tongues,
And western climes the strain prolong.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: From east to west, from north to south,
Immanuel's kingdom shall extend,
And every man, in every face,
Shall meet a brother and a friend.

hymns.title: The radiant dawn of gospel light
hymns.author: M. Rayner
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The radiant dawn of gospel light,
The prophet saw in vision bright,
And hailed th' auspicious day,
When Christ should all his grace disclose
And cure the world of all its woes,
By truth's triumphant sway.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The blind their eyes shall open wide;
To drink the light's o'erflowing tide,
The deaf sweet music hear;
The lame like bounding hart shall leap;
The dumb no longer silence keep,
But shout redemption near.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And there shall be a holy way,
In which the simple shall not stray--
The path so plain and bright.
Wayfaring men therein shall walk,
And of their home and kindred talk,
With rapture and delight.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: No ravenous beast in quest of prey,
No lion lurking in the way,
Shall ever there be seen.
The place where dragons lay concealed,
Large crops of waving grass shall yield,
With reeds and rushes green.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: And when to Zion's peaceful home
The ransomed of the Lord shall come,
(O haste the blissful day!)
Glad strains shall every tongue employ
In songs of everlasting joy,
And sighing flee away.

hymns.title: Amazing, beauteous change
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Amazing, beauteous change!
A world created new!
My thoughts with transport range,
The lovely scene to view:
In all I trace,
The work is thine;
Saviour divine,
Be thine the praise!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: See crystal fountains play
Amidst the burning sands;
The river's winding way
Shines through the thirsty lands;
New grass is seen,
Its carpet spreads
And o'er the meads
Of living green.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Where pointed brambles grew,
Entwined with horrid thorn,
Gay flowers, forever new,
The painted fields adorn;
The blushing rose,
In union fair,
And lily there,
Their sweets disclose.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The tyrants of the plain
Their savage chase give o'er;
No more they rend the slain.
And thirst for blood no more;
But infant hands
And lions yoke
Fierce tigers stroke,
In flowery bands.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: O, when, Almighty Lord,
Shall these glad scenes arise,
To verify thy word,
And bless our wondering eyes?
That earth may raise,
United songs
With all its tongues,
Of ardent praise.

hymns.title: Still in shades of midnight darkness
hymns.author: J. Taylor
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Still in shades of midnight darkness
Abject sits the Pagan world;
There the banner of salvation
Ne'er hath been by time unfurled;
Nor their idols
From their blood-stained altars hurled.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Yet the promise stands securely,
And Messiah's reign shall spread;
Not in vain his glorious conquest;
Not in vain the Saviour bled.
Chief immortal!
God's own hand hath crowned thy head.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: To this blessed dispensation
Millions yet unborn shall fly;
See the rising splendor beaming
Till it gilds the western sky.
Glorious Gospel!
Still thy triumphs multiply.

hymns.title: Ye subjects of the Lord! proclaim
hymns.author: Pratt's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Ye subjects of the Lord! proclaim
The royal honors of his name:
"Jehovah reigns!" be all our song.
'T is He, thy God, O Zion, reigns!
Prepare thy most harmonious strains
Glad hallelujahs to prolong.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Tremble, ye pageants of a day,
Formed, like your slaves, of brittle clay!
Down to the dust your sceptres bend;
To everlasting years He reigns,
And undiminished state maintains,
When kings, and suns, and time shall end.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: So shall his favored Zion live:
In vain confed'rate nations strive
Her sacred turrets to destroy;
Her Sov'reign sits enthroned above,
And endless power and endless love
Ensure her safety and her joy.

hymns.title: Daughter of Zion, from the dust
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Daughter of Zion, from the dust
Exalt thy fallen head;
Again in thy Redeemer trust:
He calls thee from the dead.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Awake, awake; put on thy strength,
Thy beautiful array;
The day of freedom dawns at length,
The Lord's appointed day.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Rebuild thy walls, thy bounds enlarge,
And send thy heralds forth;
Say to the south, "Give up thy charge,
And keep not back, O north!"

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: They come, they come;--thine exiled bands.
Where'er they rest or roam,
Have heard thy voice in distant lands,
And hasten to their home.

hymns.title: O, who shall see the glorious day
hymns.author: Moore
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O, who shall see the glorious day,
When, throned on Zion's brow,
The Lord shall rend the veil away
That hides the nations now!
When earth no more beneath the fear
Of his rebuke shall lie,
When pain shall cease, and every tear
Be wiped from every eye!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Then, Judah, thou no more shalt mourn
Beneath the heathen's chain;
Thy days of splendor shall return,
And all be new again.
The fount of life shall then be quaffed
In peace by all who come;
And every wind that blows, shall waft
Some long-lost wand'rer home.

hymns.title: Father of faithful Abraham! hear
hymns.author: Pratt's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father of faithful Abraham! hear
Our earnest suit for Abraham's seed:
Justly they claim the fervent prayer
From us, adopted in their stead;
Who mercy, through their fall, obtain,
And Christ, by their rejection, gain.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: But hast thou finally forsook,
Forever cast thine own away?
Wilt thou not bid the murderers look
On Him they pierced, and weep and pray?
Yes! gracious Lord, thy word is past--
"All Israel shall be saved at last."

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Come, then, thou great Deliverer, come!
The veil from Jacob's heart remove:
Receive thine ancient people home,
That, quickened by thy dying love,
In their recovery we may find
Life from the dead for all mankind.

hymns.title: Onward speed thy conquering flight
hymns.author: S. F. Smith
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Onward speed thy conquering flight;
Angel, onward speed;
Cast abroad thy radiant light,
Bid the shades recede;
Tread the idols in the dust,
Heathen fanes destroy,
Spread the gospel's holy trust,
Spread the gospel's joy.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Onward speed thy conquering flight;
Angel, onward haste;
Quickly on each mountain's height
Be thy standard placed;
Let thy blissful tidings float
Far o'er vale and hill,
Till the sweetly-echoing note
Every bosom thrill.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Onward speed thy conquering flight;
Angel, onward fly:
Long has been the reign of night;
Bring the morning nigh:
'Tis to thee the heathen lift
Their imploring wail;
Bear them Heaven's holy gift,
Ere their courage fail.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Onward speed thy conquering flight
Angel, onward speed;
Morning bursts upon our sight--
'Tis the time decreed:
Jesus now his kingdom takes,
Thrones and empires fall,
And the joyous song awakes,
"God is all in all."

hymns.title: O Zion, tune thy voice
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O Zion, tune thy voice,
And raise thy hands on high;
Tell all the earth thy joys,
And boast salvation nigh.
Cheerful in God,
Arise and shine,
While rays divine
Stream all abroad.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: He gilds thy mourning face
With beams that cannot fade;
His all-resplendent grace
He pours around thy head.
The nations round
Thy form shall view,
With lustre new
Divinely crowned.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: In honor to his name,
Reflect that sacred light,
And loud that grace proclaim,
Which makes thy darkness bright;
Pursue his praise,
Till sovereign love
In worlds above
The glory raise.

hymns.title: O thou Sun of glorious splendor
hymns.author: Urwick's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O thou Sun of glorious splendor,
Shine with healing in thy wing;
Chase away these shades of darkness;
Holy light and comfort bring.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Let the heralds of salvation
Round the world with joy proclaim,
"Death and hell are spoiled and vanquished
Through the great Immanuel's name."

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Take thy power, almighty Saviour;
Claim the nations for thine own;
Reign, thou Lord of life and glory,
Till each heart becomes thy throne.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Then the earth, o'erspread with glory,
Decked with heavenly splendor bright
Shall be made Jehovah's dwelling--
As at first, the Lord's delight.

hymns.title: Isles of the south, awake
hymns.author: Brown
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Isles of the south, awake!
The song of triumph sing;
Let mount, and hill, and vale,
With hallelujahs ring:
Shout, for the idol's overthrown,
And Israel's God is God alone.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Wild wastes of Afric, shout!
Your shackled sons are free;
No mother wails her child
'Neath the banana-tree:
No slave-ship dashes on thy shore;
The clank of chains is heard no more.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Shout, vales of India, shout!
No funeral fires blaze high;
No idol song rings loud,
As rolls the death-car by:
The banner of the cross now waves
Where Christian heralds made their graves.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Shout, hills of Palestine!
Have you forgot the groan,
The spear, the thorn, the cross,
The wine-press trod alone,
The dying prayer that rose from thee,
Thou garden of Gethsemane?

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Hail, glad, millennial day!
O, shout, ye heavens above!
To-day ye nations sing
The song, redeeming love:
Redeeming love the song shall be:
Hail, blessed year of jubilee!

hymns.title: To Christ, the Son, the Father spake
hymns.author: H. Ballou
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: To Christ, the Son, the Father spake:
Lo, ask of me, and I will make
The heathen to thy sceptre bend;
The utmost parts of all the earth
Are thine inheritance by birth,
And wide thine empire shall extend.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Now Jesus waves his sceptre high,
Unfurls his banners in the sky,
While loud the gospel trumpets sound:
His enemies with sore dismay,
Retire in haste and yield the day,
While trophies to the Lord abound.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Before him kings and tyrants fall,
Detest their crowns, and on him call,
And he a pardon free doth give:
The world in sin was dead before;
To life the world he will restore,
And in him all the world shall live.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O Lord, thy government shall be
Extended wide from sea to sea,
And long thy sceptre thou shalt hold;
As long as sun or moon shall shine,
Thou King of earth shalt reign divine,
The mysteries of thy grace unfold.

hymns.title: Lord, have mercy when we pray
hymns.author: Milman
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, have mercy when we pray
Strength to seek a better way;
When our wakening thoughts begin
First to loathe their cherished sin;
When our weary spirits fail,
And our aching brows are pale;
When our tears bedew thy word;
Then, O then, have mercy, Lord.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lord, have mercy when we lie
On the restless bed and sigh,--
Sigh for death, yet fear it still;
From the thought of former ill;
When the dim, advancing gloom
Tells us that our hour is come;
When is loosed the silver cord;
Then, O then, have mercy, Lord.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Lord, have mercy, when we know
First how vain this world below:
When its darker thoughts oppress,
Doubts perplex, and fears distress;
When the earliest gleam is given
Of the bright but distant heaven;
Then thy fostering grace afford;
Then, O then, have mercy, Lord.

hymns.title: The long-lost son, with streaming eyes
hymns.author: Village Hymns
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The long-lost son, with streaming eyes,
From folly just awake,
Reviews his wanderings with surprise;
His heart begins to break.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: "I starve," he cries, "nor can I bear
The famine in this land,
While servants of my Father share
The bounty of his hand.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: "With deep repentance I'll return
And seek my Father's face;
Unworthy to be called a son,
I'll ask a servant's place."

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Far off the Father saw him move,
In pensive silence mourn,
And quickly ran with arms of love,
To welcome his return.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: O, let thy boundless mercy shine
On my benighted soul,
Correct my passions, mend my heart,
And all my fears control.

hymns.title: Forgive us for thy mercy's sake
hymns.author: Wesley's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Forgive us for thy mercy's sake;
Our multitude of sins forgive;
And for thy own possession take,
And bid us to thy glory live;
Live in thy sight and gladly prove
Our faith by our obedient love.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The covenant of forgiveness seal,
And all thy mighty wonders show;
Our hidden enemies expel,
And conquering them to conquer go,
Till all of pride and wrath be slain,
And not one evil thought remain.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O, put it in our inward parts
The living law of perfect love:
Write the new precept on our hearts;
We shall not then from thee remove,
Who in thy glorious image shine,
Thy people, and forever thine.

hymns.title: The wandering star and fleeting wind
hymns.author: Beddome
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The wandering star and fleeting wind
Are emblems of the fickle mind;
The morning cloud and early dew
Bring our inconstancy to view.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: But cloud and wind and dew and star,
Only a faint resemblance bear;
Nor can there aught in nature be
So changeable and frail as we.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Our outward walk and inward frame
Are scarcely through an hour the same:
We vow, and straight our vows forget,
And then those very vows repeat.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: With contrite hearts, Lord, we confess
Our folly and unsteadfastness:
When shall these hearts more stable be,
Fixed by thy grace alone on thee?

hymns.title: Sweet is the friendly voice
hymns.author: Jervis
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Sweet is the friendly voice
Which speaks of life and peace;
Which bids the penitent rejoice,
And sin and sorrow cease.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: No balm on earth like this
Can cheer the contrite heart;
No flattering dreams of earthly bliss
Such pure delight impart.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Still merciful and kind,
Thy mercy, Lord, reveal:
The broken heart thy love can bind,
The wounded spirit heal.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thy presence shall restore
Peace to my anxious breast:
Lord, let my steps be drawn no more
From paths which thou hast blessed.

hymns.title: Lord, we have wandered from thy way
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, we have wandered from thy way,
Like foolish sheep have gone astray,
Our pleasant pastures we have left,
And of their guard our souls bereft.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Exposed to want, exposed to harm,
Far from our gentle Shepherd's arm;
Nor will these fatal wanderings cease,
Till thou reveal the paths of peace.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O seek thy thoughtless servants, Lord,
Nor let us quite forget thy word;
Our erring feet do thou restore,
And keep us that we stray no more.

hymns.title: Jesus demands this heart of mine
hymns.author: Steele
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Jesus demands this heart of mine,
Demands my love, my joy, my care,
But ah, how dead to things divine,
How cold my best affections are!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: 'Tis sin, alas! with dreadful power,
Divides my Saviour from my sight;
O, for one happy, shining hour
Of sacred freedom, sweet delight!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Come, gracious Lord; thy love can raise
My captive powers from sin and death,
And fill my heart and life with praise,
And tune my last, expiring breath.

hymns.title: O sinner! bring not tears alone
hymns.author: Breviary
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O sinner! bring not tears alone,
Or outward form of prayer:
But let it in thy heart be known
That penitence is there.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: To beat the breast, the clothes to rend,
God asketh not of thee:
Thy secret soul he bids thee bend
In true humility.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O righteous Judge! if thou wilt deign
To grant us what we need;
We pray for time to turn again,
And grace to turn indeed.

hymns.title: My soul before thee prostrate lies
hymns.author: Richter
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: My soul before thee prostrate lies;
To thee, her source, my spirit flies;
My wants I mourn, my chains I see;
O let thy presence set me free.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: In life's short day, let me yet more
Of thy enlivening power implore;
My mind must deeper sink in thee,
My foot stand firm from wandering free.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Take full possession of my heart;
The lowly mind of Christ impart;
I still will wait, O Lord, on thee,
Till, in thy light, the light I see.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: One only care my soul should know,
Father, all thy commands to do;
Oh! deep engrave it on my breast,
That I in thee alone am blest.

hymns.title: Times without number have I prayed
hymns.author: C. Wesley
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Times without number have I prayed,
"This only once forgive;"
Relapsing when thy hand was stayed,
And suffered me to live.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Yet now the kingdom of thy peace,
Lord, to my heart restore;
Forgive my vain repentances,
And bid me sin no more.

hymns.title: There was joy in heaven
hymns.author: Heber
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: There was joy in heaven!
There was joy in heaven!
When this goodly world to frame,
The Lord of light and mercy came:
Shouts of joy were heard on high,
And the stars sang from the sky,
"Glory to God in heaven!"

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: There was joy in heaven!
There was joy in heaven!
When of love the midnight beam
Dawned on the towers of Bethlehem;
And along the echoing hill
Angels sang--"On earth good will,
And glory in the heaven!"

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: There is joy in heaven!
There is joy in heaven!
When the sheep that went astray
Turns again to virtue's way;
When the soul by grace subdued
Sobs its prayer of gratitude,
Then is there joy in heaven!

hymns.title: Blest Instructor! from thy ways
hymns.author: Merrick
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Blest Instructor! from thy ways
Who can tell how oft he strays?
Save from error's growth our mind,
Leave not, Lord, one root behind.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Purge us from the guilt that lies
Wrapt within our heart's disguise;
Let us thence, by thee renewed,
Each presumptuous sin exclude.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Let our tongues, from error free,
Speak the words approved by thee:
To thine all-observing eyes,
Let our thoughts accepted rise.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: While we thus thy name adore,
And thy healing grace implore,
Blest Instructor! bow thine ear:
God our strength! propitious hear.

hymns.title: O God, thy grace impart
hymns.author: R. Palmer
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O God, thy grace impart!
Revive my fainting heart;
My zeal inspire;
Reveal thyself to me,
And may my love to thee
Pure, warm, and changeless be,--
A living fire.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: While life's dark maze I tread,
And griefs around me spread,
Be thou my guide;
Bid darkness turn to day,
Wipe sorrow's tears away,
Nor let me ever stray
From thee aside.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: When ends life's transient dream,
When death's cold sullen stream
Shall o'er me roll,
O Father, then in love,
Fear and distress remove,
And bear me safe above,--
A ransomed soul.

hymns.title: Soft are the fruitful showers that bring
hymns.author: Henry Moore
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Soft are the fruitful showers that bring
The welcome promise of the spring,
And soft the vernal gale:
Sweet the wild warblings of the grove,
The voice of nature and of love,
That gladden every vale.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: But softer in the mourner's ear
Sounds the mild voice of mercy near,
That whispers sins forgiven;
And sweeter far the music swells,
When to the raptured soul she tells
Of peace and promised heaven.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Fair are the flowers that deck the ground;
And groves and gardens blooming round,
Unnumbered charms unfold:
Bright is the sun's meridian ray,
And bright the beams of setting day,
That robe the clouds in gold.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: But far more fair the pious breast,
In richer robes of goodness dressed,
Where heaven's own graces shine;
And brighter far the prospects rise,
That burst on faith's delighted eyes,
From glories all divine.

hymns.title: When darkness long has veiled my mind
hymns.author: Cowper
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When darkness long has veiled my mind,
And smiling day once more appears,
Then, my Creator! then I find
The folly of my doubts and fears.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Straight I upbraid my wandering heart,
And blush that I should ever be
Thus prone to act so base a part,
Or harbor one hard thought of thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O! let me then at length be taught,
What I am still so slow to learn,--
That God is love, and changes not,
Nor knows the shadow of a turn.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Sweet truth, and easy to repeat!
But when my faith is sharply tried,
I find myself a learner yet,
Unskilful, weak, and apt to slide.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: But, O my God! one look from thee
Subdues the disobedient will,
Drives doubt and discontent away,
And thy rebellious child is still.

hymns.title: O thou, who hast at thy command
hymns.author: Mrs. Cotterill
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O thou, who hast at thy command
The hearts of all men in thy hand!
Our wayward, erring hearts incline
To have no other will but thine.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Our wishes, our desires, control;
Mould every purpose of the soul;
O'er all may we victorious be
That stands between ourselves and thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thrice blest will all our blessings be,
When we can look through them to thee,
When each glad heart its tribute pays
Of love, and gratitude, and praise.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: And while we to thy glory live,
May we to thee all glory give,
Until the final summons come,
That calls thy willing servants home.

hymns.title: So let our lips and lives express
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: So let our lips and lives express
The holy gospel we profess:
So let our works and virtues shine,
To prove the doctrine all divine.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thus shall we best proclaim abroad
The honors of our Saviour, God,
When the salvation reigns within.
And grace subdues the power of sin.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Our flesh and sense must be denied,
Passion and envy, lust and pride,
While justice, temperance, truth and love,
Our inward piety approve.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Religion bears our spirits up,
While we expect that blessed hope,
The bright appearance of the Lord,
And faith stands leaning on his word.

hymns.title: A soldier's course, from battles won
hymns.author: Gisborne
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: A soldier's course, from battles won
To new-commencing strife;
A pilgrim's, restless as the sun--
Behold the Christian's life!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O! let us seek our heavenly home,
Revealed in sacred lore;
The land whence pilgrims never roam,
Where soldiers war no more;

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Where grief shall never wound, nor death,
Beneath the Saviour's reign;
Nor sin, with pestilential breath,
His holy realm profane;

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The land where, suns and moons unknown,
And night's alternate sway,
Jehovah's ever-burning throne
Upholds unbroken day;

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Where they who meet shall never part;
Where grace achieves its plan;
And God, uniting every heart,
Dwells face to face with man.

hymns.title: "Thus shalt thou love the Almighty Lord
hymns.author: E. Taylor
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: "Thus shalt thou love the Almighty Lord,
With all thy heart, and soul, and mind:"
So speaks to man that sacred word,
For counsel and reproof designed.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: "With all thy heart;" no idol thing,
Though close around the heart it twine,
Its interposing shade must fling,
To darken that pure love of thine.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: "With all thy mind;" each varied power,
Creative fancy, musings high,
And thoughts that glance behind, before,
These must religion sanctify.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: "With soul and strength;" thy days of ease,
While vigor nerves each youthful limb,
And hope and joy, and health and peace,
All must be freely brought to him.

hymns.title: Thrice happy souls, who, born from heaven
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Thrice happy souls, who, born from heaven,
While yet they sojourn here,
Do all their days with God begin,
And spend them in his fear.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: 'Midst hourly cares, may love present
Its incense to thy throne;
And while the world our hands employs,
Our hearts be thine alone.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: As sanctified to noblest ends,
Be each refreshment sought;
And by each various providence
Some wise instruction brought.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: When to laborious duties called,
Or by temptations tried,
We'll seek the shelter of thy wings,
And in thy strength confide.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: In solid, pure delights like these,
Let all our days be past;
Nor shall we then impatient wish,
Nor shall we fear, the last.

hymns.title: My soul, be on thy guard
hymns.author: Heath
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: My soul, be on thy guard;
Ten thousand foes arise;
The hosts of sin are pressing hard
To draw thee from the skies.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O, watch, and fight, and pray;
The battle ne'er give o'er;
Renew it boldly every day,
And help divine implore.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Ne'er think the victory won,
Nor lay thine armor down:
Thy arduous work will not be done
Till thou obtain thy crown.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Fight on, my soul, till death
Shall bring thee to thy God;
He'll take thee, at thy parting breath,
To his divine abode.

hymns.title: Blest are the humble souls that see
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Blest are the humble souls that see
Their emptiness and poverty;
Treasures of grace to them are given,
And crowns of joy laid up in heaven.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Blest are the souls that thirst for grace,
Hunger and long for righteousness;
They shall be well supplied and fed
With living streams and living bread.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Blest are the pure, whose hearts are clean
From the defiling power of sin;
With endless pleasure they shall see
A God of spotless purity.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Blest are the men of peaceful life,
Who quench the coals of growing strife;
They shall be called the heirs of bliss,
The sons of God, the God of peace.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Blest are the sufferers who partake
Of pain and shame for Jesus' sake;
Their souls shall triumph in the Lord;
Glory and joy are their reward.

hymns.title: Patience, O, 't is a grace divine
hymns.author: Rippon's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Patience, O, 't is a grace divine,
Sent from the God of peace and love,
That leans upon our Father's arm,
As through the wilds of life we rove.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: By patience, we serenely bear
The troubles of our mortal state,
And wait, contented, our discharge,
Nor think our glory comes too late.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O, for this grace to aid us on,
And arm with fortitude the breast,
Till, life's tumultuous voyage o'er,
We reach the shores of endless rest.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Faith into vision shall resign,
Hope shall in full fruition die,
And patience in possession end,
In the bright worlds of bliss on high.

hymns.title: Who shall towards thy chosen seat
hymns.author: Merrick
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Who shall towards thy chosen seat
Turn, O Lord, his favored feet?
Who shall at thine altar bend?
Who shall Zion's hill ascend?
Who, great God, a welcome guest,
On thy holy mountain rest?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: He whose heart thy love has warmed,
He whose will, to thine conformed,
Bids his life unsullied run;
He whose word and thought are one;
Who, from sin's contagion free,
Lifts his willing soul to thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: He who thus, with heart unstained,
Treads the path by thee ordained,
He shall towards thy chosen seat
Turn, O Lord, his favored feet;
He thy ceaseless care shall prove,
He shall share thy constant love.

hymns.title: Lord, who's the happy man, that may
hymns.author: Tate & Brady
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, who's the happy man, that may
To thy blest courts repair,
Not, stranger-like, to visit them,
But to inhabit there?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: 'Tis he whose every thought and deed
By rules of virtue moves;
Whose generous tongue disdains to speak
The thing his heart disproves;

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Who never did a slander forge,
His neighbor's fame to wound;
Nor hearken to a false report,
By malice whispered round;

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Who to his plighted vows and trust
Has ever firmly stood;
And though he promise to his loss,
He makes his promise good.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: The man who by this steady course
Has happiness ensured,
When earth's foundations shake, shall stand
By Providence secured.

hymns.title: "Blest are the meek," he said
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: "Blest are the meek," he said,
Whose doctrine is divine;
The humble-minded earth possess,
And bright in heaven will shine.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: While here on earth they stay,
Calm peace with them shall dwell,
And cheerful hope and heavenly joy
Beyond what tongue can tell.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The God of peace is theirs;
They own his gracious sway;
And yielding all their wills to him,
His sovereign laws obey.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O gracious Father, grant,
That we this influence feel,
That all we hope, or wish, may be
Subjected to thy will.

hymns.title: Happy the meek, whose gentle breast
hymns.author: Scott
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Happy the meek, whose gentle breast,
Clear as the summer's evening ray,
Calm as the regions of the blest,
Enjoys on earth celestial day.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: His heart no broken friendships sting,
No storms his peaceful tent invade;
He rests beneath th' Almighty wing,
Hostile to none, of none afraid.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Spirit of grace, all meek and mild,
Inspire our breasts, our souls possess;
Repel each passion rude and wild,
And bless us as we aim to bless.

hymns.title: Whatever dims thy sense of truth
hymns.author: M. W. Hale
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Whatever dims thy sense of truth,
Or stains thy purity,
Though light as breath of summer air
Count it as sin to thee.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Preserve the tablet of thy thoughts
From every blemish free,
While the Redeemer's lowly faith
Its temple makes with thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And pray of God, that grace be given
To tread time's narrow way:--
How dark soever it may be,
It leads to cloudless day.

hymns.title: Blest are the pure in heart
hymns.author: Keble
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Blest are the pure in heart
For they shall see our God;
The secret of the Lord is theirs,
Their soul is Christ's abode.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Still to the lowly soul
He doth himself impart,
And for his temple and his throne
Chooseth the pure in heart.

hymns.title: O, 't is a lovely thing to see
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O, 't is a lovely thing to see
A man of prudent heart,
Whose thoughts and lips and life agree
To act a useful part.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: When envy, strife, and wars begin,
In fierce, contentious souls,
Mark how the sons of peace come in,
And quench the kindling coals.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Their minds are humble, mild, and meek,
Nor let their anger rise;
Nor passion moves their lips to speak,
Nor pride exalts their eyes.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Their lives are prudence mixed with love;
Good works employ their day;
They join the serpent with the dove,
But cast the sting away.

hymns.title: The bird that soars on highest wing
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The bird that soars on highest wing
Builds on the ground her lowly nest;
And she that doth most sweetly sing
Sings in the shade when all things rest:--
In lark and nightingale we see
What honor hath humility.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: When Mary chose the better part,
She meekly sat at Jesus' feet;
And Lydia's gently opened heart
Was made for God's own temple meet:--
Fairest and best adorned is she
Whose clothing is humility.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The saint that wears heaven's brightest crown
In deepest adoration bends;
The weight of glory bows him down
Then most when most his soul ascends:--
Nearest the throne itself must be
The footstool of humility.

hymns.title: Is there ambition in my heart
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Is there ambition in my heart?
Search, gracious God, and see;
Or do I act a haughty part?
Lord, I appeal to thee.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: I charge my thoughts, be humble still,
And all my carriage mild;
Content, my Father, with thy will
And quiet as a child.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The patient soul, the lowly mind,
Shall have a large reward:
Let saints in sorrow lie resigned,
And trust a faithful Lord.

hymns.title: Thus saith the first, the great command
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Thus saith the first, the great command,
"Let all thy inward powers unite
To love thy Maker and thy God
With utmost vigor and delight.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: "Then shall thy neighbor next in place
Share thine affections and esteem;
And let thy kindness to thyself
Measure and rule thy love to him."

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: This is the sense that Moses spoke;
This did the prophets preach and prove,
For want of this the law is broke,
And the whole law's fulfilled by love.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: But, O, how base our passions are!
How cold our charity and zeal!
Lord, fill our souls with heavenly fire,
Or we shall ne'er perform thy will.

hymns.title: Servants of Christ, arise
hymns.author: L. H. Sigourney
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Servants of Christ, arise,
And gird you for the toil;
The dew of promise from the skies
Already cheers the soil.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Go where the sick recline,
Where mourning hearts deplore;
And where the sons of sorrow pine,
Dispense your hallowed lore.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Urge, with a tender zeal,
The erring child along,
Where peaceful congregations kneel,
And pious teachers throng.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Be faith, which looks above,
With prayer, your constant guest,
And wrap the Saviour's changeless love
A mantle round your breast.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: So shall you share the wealth,
That earth may ne'er despoil,
And the blest gospel's saving health
Repay your arduous toil.

hymns.title: And is the gospel peace and love
hymns.author: Steele
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: And is the gospel peace and love?
So let our conversation be;
The serpent blended with the dove,
Wisdom and meek simplicity.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Whene'er the angry passions rise,
And tempt our thoughts or tongues to strife,
On Jesus let us fix our eyes,
Bright pattern of the Christian life!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O, how benevolent and kind!
How mild! how ready to forgive!
Be this the temper of our mind,
And his the rules by which we live.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Dispensing good where'er he came,
The labors of his life were love;
