If, then, we love our Saviour's name
Thus let us our relation prove.

hymns.title: Ye servants of the Lord
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Ye servants of the Lord,
Each in his office wait,
Observant of his heavenly word,
And watchful at his gate.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Let all your lamps be bright,
And trim the golden flame;
Gird up your loins, as in his sight,
For awful is his name.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Watch,--'tis your Lord's command;
And while we speak, he's near;
Mark the first signal of his hand,
And ready all appear.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O, happy servant he,
In such a posture found!
He shall his Lord with rapture see
And be with honor crowned.

hymns.title: Children of light, awake
hymns.author: Bulfinch
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Children of light, awake,
At Jesus' call arise,
Forth with your leader to partake
His toils, his victories.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Ye must not idly stand,
His sacred voice who hear;
Arm for the strife the feeble hand,
The holy standard rear.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Nought doth the world afford,
But toil must be the price;
Wilt thou not, servant of the Lord,
Then toil for paradise?

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Awake, ye sons of light,
Strive till the prize be won;
Far spent already is the night;
The day comes brightening on.

hymns.title: Through sorrow's night and danger's way
hymns.author: H. K. White
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Through sorrow's night and danger's way
Amid the deepening gloom,
The soldiers of an injured King
Are marching to the tomb.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Their service done, securely laid
In this their last retreat,
Unheeded o'er their silent dust
The storms of life shall beat.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Yet not thus lifeless in the grave
The vital spark shall lie;
O'er nature's ruins it shall rise,
To reach its kindred sky.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Then heaven's soft dew o'er every eye
Shall shed its mildest rays;
And the long silent dust shall wake
In strains of endless praise.

hymns.title: O, speed thee, Christian, on thy way
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O, speed thee, Christian, on thy way,
And to thy armor cling;
With girded loins the call obey
That grace and mercy bring.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: There is a battle to be fought,
An upward race to run,
A crown of glory to be sought,
A victory to be won.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O, faint not, Christian, for thy sighs
Are heard before His throne;
The race must come before the prize,
The cross before the crown.

hymns.title: The Christian warrior, see him stand
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The Christian warrior, see him stand
In the whole armor of his God;
The spirit's sword is in his hand;
His feet are with the gospel shod.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: In panoply of truth complete,
Salvation's helmet on his head,
With righteousness, a breastplate meet,
And faith's broad shield before him spread.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: With this omnipotence he moves;
From this the alien armies flee;
Till more than conqueror he proves,
Through Christ, who gives him victory.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thus strong in his Redeemer's strength,
Sin, death and hell he tramples down,--
Fights the good fight; and takes at length,
Through mercy, an immortal crown.

hymns.title: Religion! in its blessed ray
hymns.author: G. Rogers
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Religion! in its blessed ray
All thought of hopeless sorrow flies,
Despair and anguish melt away
Where'er its healing beams arise.
How dark our sinful world would be--
A flowerless desert, dry and drear!
Did not this light, O God, from thee
Its gloom dispel, its aspect cheer.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Oh! by it many a heart is soothed,
Which else would be with sorrow crushed,
And many a dying pillow smoothed,
And sob of parting anguish hushed.
Across the troubled sky of time
It doth the bow of promise bend,
A symbol of that cloudless clime
That waits the soul when time shall end.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Religion! may its holy light
Our footsteps guide to paths of peace!
Our solace in deep sorrow's night,
Our stay as mortal powers decrease.
With this our guide, we care not when
Death's signal to depart is given;
Its word shall bring our spirits then
The calm and holy peace of heaven.

hymns.title: Thus saith the high and lofty One
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Thus saith the high and lofty One:
"I sit upon my holy throne;
My name is God, I dwell on high,
Dwell in my own eternity.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: "But I descend to worlds below;
On earth I have a mansion too;
The humble spirit, and contrite,
Is an abode of my delight.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: "The humble soul my words revive;
I bid the mourning sinner live;
Heal all the broken hearts I find,
And ease the sorrows of the mind.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: "The soul that seeks me shall obtain
Immortal wealth and heavenly gain;
Eternal life is his reward,
Life, and the favor of the Lord."

hymns.title: They who seek the throne of grace
hymns.author: Methodist Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: They who seek the throne of grace
Find that throne in every place;
If we love a life of prayer,
God is present everywhere.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: In our sickness, in our health;
In our want or in our wealth,
If we look to God in prayer,
God is present everywhere.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: When our earthly comforts fail,
When the woes of life prevail,
'Tis the time for earnest prayer,
God is present everywhere.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Then, my soul, in every strait,
To thy Father, come and wait;
He will answer every prayer,
God is present everywhere.

hymns.title: Sweet is the prayer whose holy stream
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Sweet is the prayer whose holy stream
In earnest pleading flows!
Devotion dwells upon the theme,
And warm and warmer glows.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Faith grasps the blessing she desires;
Hope points the upward gaze;
And Love, celestial Love, inspires
The eloquence of praise.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: But sweeter far the still, small voice,
Unheard by human ear,
When God has made the heart rejoice,
And dried the bitter tear.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: No accents flow, no words ascend;
All utterance faileth there;
But Christian spirits comprehend,
And God accepts the prayer.

hymns.title: Awake, my soul! lift up thine eyes
hymns.author: Mrs. Barbauld
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Awake, my soul! lift up thine eyes!
See where thy foes against thee rise,
In long array a numerous host;
Awake, my soul! or thou art lost.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: See where rebellious passions rage,
And fierce desires and lusts engage;
The meanest foe of all the train
Has thousands and ten thousands slain.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Come then, my soul! now learn to wield
The weight of thine immortal shield;
Put on the armor from above,
Of heavenly truth and heavenly love.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The terror and the charm repel,
And powers of earth and powers of hell,
The man of Calvary triumphed here;--
Why should his faithful followers fear?

hymns.title: Awake, my soul! stretch every nerve
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Awake, my soul! stretch every nerve,
And press with vigor on;
A heavenly race demands thy zeal,
And an immortal crown.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: A cloud of witnesses around
Hold thee in full survey;
Forget the steps already trod,
And onward urge thy way.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: 'T is God's all-animating voice
That calls thee from on high;
'T is his own hand presents the prize
To thine aspiring eye;--

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: That prize with peerless glories bright,
Which shall new lustre boast,
When victors' wreaths and monarchs' gems
Shall blend in common dust.

hymns.title: Am I a soldier of the cross
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Am I a soldier of the cross,
A follower of the Lamb?
And shall I fear to own his cause,
Or blush to speak his name?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Must I be carried to the skies
On flowery beds of ease,
While others fought to win the prize,
And sailed through bloody seas?

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Sure I must fight, if I would reign;
Increase my courage, Lord!
I'll bear the toil, endure the pain,
Supported by thy word.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thy saints, in all this glorious war,
Shall conquer, though they're slain:
They see the triumph from afar,
And soon with Christ shall reign.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: When that illustrious day shall rise,
And all thy armies shine
In robes of victory through the skies,
The glory shall be thine.

hymns.title: Awake, our souls, away, our fears
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Awake, our souls, away, our fears;
Let every trembling thought be gone;
Awake and run the heavenly race,
And put a cheerful courage on.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: True 'tis a strait and thorny road,
And mortal spirits tire and faint;
But they forget the mighty God,
That feeds the strength of every saint.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: From thee, the overflowing spring,
Our souls shall drink a fresh supply,
While such as trust their native strength,
Shall melt away, and droop, and die.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Swift as an eagle cuts the air,
We'll mount aloft to thine abode;
On wings of love our souls shall fly,
Nor tire amidst the heavenly road.

hymns.title: Prayer is the soul's sincere desire
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Prayer is the soul's sincere desire,
Uttered or unexpressed,
The motion of a hidden fire,
That trembles in the breast.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Prayer is the burden of a sigh,
The falling of a tear,
The upward glancing of an eye,
When none but God is near.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Prayer is the simplest form of speech
That infant lips can try,
Prayer the sublimest strains that reach
The Majesty on high.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Prayer is the Christian's vital breath,
The Christian's native air,
The watchword at the gates of death;
He enters heaven with prayer.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Prayer is the contrite sinner's voice,
Returning from his ways;
While angels in their songs rejoice,
And cry "Behold, he prays!"

hymns.title: Child, amidst the flowers at play
hymns.author: Mrs. Hemans
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Child, amidst the flowers at play,
While the red light fades away;
Mother, with thine earnest eye
Ever following silently;
Father, by the breeze of eve
Called thy daily work to leave;
Pray! ere yet the dark hours be,
Lift the heart and bend the knee!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Traveller, in the stranger's land,
Far from thine own household band;
Mourner, haunted by the tone
Of a voice from this world gone;
Captive, in whose narrow cell
Sunshine hath not leave to dwell;
Sailor, on the darkening sea--
Lift the heart and bend the knee!

hymns.title: Go when the morning shineth
hymns.author: Edin. Lit. Review
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Go when the morning shineth,
Go when the noon is bright,
Go when the eve declineth,
Go in the hush of night;
Go with pure mind and feeling,
Cast earthly thought away,
And, in thy closet kneeling,
Do thou in secret pray.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Remember all who love thee,
All who are loved by thee;
Pray, too, for those who hate thee,
If any such there be;
Then for thyself, in meekness,
A blessing humbly claim,
And blend with each petition
Thy great Redeemer's name.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Or, if 'tis e'er denied thee
In solitude to pray,
Should holy thoughts come o'er thee
When friends are round thy way,
E'en then the silent breathing,
Thy spirit raised above,
Will reach his throne of glory,
Where dwells eternal love.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O, not a joy or blessing
With this can we compare,--
The grace our Father gave us
To pour our souls in prayer:
Whene'er thou pin'st in sadness,
Before his footstool fall;
Remember, in thy gladness,
His love who gave thee all.

hymns.title: 'T is by the faith of joys to come
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: 'T is by the faith of joys to come
We walk through deserts dark as night;
Till we arrive at heaven, our home,
Faith is our guide, and faith our light.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The want of sight she well supplies;
She makes the pearly gates appear;
Far into distant worlds she flies,
And brings eternal glories near.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Cheerful we tread the desert through,
While faith inspires a heavenly ray;
Though lions roar and tempests blow,
And rocks and dangers fill the way.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: So Abraham, by divine command,
Left his own house to walk with God;
His faith beheld the promised land,
And fired his zeal along the road.

hymns.title: Faith adds new charms to earthly bliss
hymns.author: Salisbury Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Faith adds new charms to earthly bliss,
And saves us from its snares;
Its aid in every duty brings,
And softens all our cares.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The wounded conscience knows its power
The healing balm to give;
That balm the saddest heart can cheer,
And make the dying live.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Wide it unveils celestial worlds,
Where deathless pleasures reign,
And bids us seek our portion there,
Nor bids us seek in vain.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: On that bright prospect may we rest,
Till this frail body dies;
And then, on faith's triumphant wings,
To endless glory rise.

hymns.title: Faith is the Christian's prop
hymns.author: Christian Watchman
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Faith is the Christian's prop
Whereon his sorrows lean;
It is the substance of his hope,
His proof of things unseen;
It is the anchor of his soul
When tempests rage and billows roll.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Faith is the polar star
That guides the Christian's way,
Directs his wanderings from afar
To realms of endless day;
It points the course where'er he roam,
And safely leads the pilgrim home.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Faith is the rainbow's form,
Hung on the brow of heaven,
The glory of the passing storm,
The pledge of mercy given;
It is the bright, triumphal arch,
Through which the saints to glory march.

hymns.title: O, for a faith that will not shrink
hymns.author: Bath Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O, for a faith that will not shrink
Though pressed by every foe,
That will not tremble on the brink
Of any earthly woe!--

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: That will not murmur nor complain
Beneath the chastening rod,
But, in the hour of grief or pain,
Will lean upon its God;--

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: A faith that shines more bright and clear
When tempests rage without;
That when in danger knows no fear,
In darkness feels no doubt;--

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Lord, give us such a faith as this,
And then, whate'er may come,
We'll taste, e'en here, the hallowed bliss
Of an eternal home.

hymns.title: Borne o'er the ocean's stormy wave
hymns.author: Sidney
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Borne o'er the ocean's stormy wave,
The beacon's light appears,
When yawns the seaman's watery grave,
And his lone bosom cheers.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Then, should the raging ocean foam,
His heart shall dauntless prove,
To reach, secure, his cherished home,
The haven of his love.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: So, when the soul is wrapt in gloom,
To worldly grief a prey,
Thy beams, blest Hope, beyond the tomb,
Illume the pilgrim's way.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: They point to that serene abode
Where holy faith shall rest,
Protected by the sufferer's God,
And be forever blest.

hymns.title: Children of the Heavenly King
hymns.author: Cennick
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Children of the Heavenly King,
As ye journey, sweetly sing;
Sing your Saviour's worthy praise,
Glorious in his works and ways.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Ye are travelling home to God,
In the way the fathers trod;
They are happy now, and ye
Soon their happiness shall see.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Shout, ye little flock, and blest;
You on Jesus' throne shall rest;
There your seat is now prepared,
There your kingdom and reward.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Lord, submissive make us go,
Ready, leaving all below;
Only thou our Leader be,
And we still will follow thee.

hymns.title: There is a hope, a blesséd hope
hymns.author: H. H. Hawley
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: There is a hope, a blesséd hope,
More precious and more bright
Than all the joyless mockery
The world esteems delight.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: There is a star, a lovely star,
That lights the darkest gloom,
And sheds a peaceful radiance o'er
The prospects of the tomb.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: There is a voice, a cheering voice,
That lifts the soul above,
Dispels the painful, anxious doubt,
And whispers, "God is love."

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: That voice, aloud from Calvary's height,
Proclaims the soul forgiven;
That star is revelation's light;
That hope, the hope of heaven.

hymns.title: All nature feels attractive power
hymns.author: Drennan
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: All nature feels attractive power,
A strong, embracing force;
The drops that sparkle in the shower,
The planets in their course.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thus, in the universe of mind,
Is felt the law of love;
The charity both strong and kind,
For all that live and move.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: In this fine sympathetic chain
All creatures bear a part;
Their every pleasure, every pain,
Linked to the feeling heart.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: More perfect bond, the Christian plan
Attaches soul to soul;
Our neighbor is the suffering man,
Though at the farthest pole.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: To earth below, from heaven above,
The faith in Christ professed,
More clearly shows that God is love,
And whom he loves is blessed.

hymns.title: O, may our sympathizing breasts
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O, may our sympathizing breasts
The generous pleasure know,
Kindly to share in others' joy,
And weep for others' woe!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Where'er the helpless sons of grief
In low distress are laid,
Soft be our hearts their pains to feel,
And swift our hands to aid.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O, be the law of love fulfilled
In every act and thought,
Each angry passion far removed,
Each selfish view forgot!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Be thou, my heart, dilated wide
With this kind, social grace,
And, in one grasp of fervent love,
All earth and heaven embrace.

hymns.title: Happy the heart where graces reign
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Happy the heart where graces reign,
Where love inspires the breast:
Love is the brightest of the train,
And strengthens all the rest.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Knowledge--alas! 'tis all in vain,
And all in vain our fear;
Our stubborn sins will fight and reign,
If love be absent there.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: This is the grace that lives and sings,
When faith and hope shall cease;
'Tis this shall strike our joyful strings
In realms of endless peace.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Before we quite forsake our clay,
Or leave this dark abode,
The wings of love bear us away
To see our gracious God.

hymns.title: O God, my Father, and my King
hymns.author: Browne
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O God, my Father, and my King,
Of all I have, or hope, the spring!
Send down thy spirit from above,
And fill my heart with heavenly love.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: May I from every act abstain,
That hurts or gives another pain:
And bear a sympathizing part,
Whene'er I meet a wounded heart.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And let my neighbor's prosperous state
A mutual joy in me create;
His virtuous triumph let me join;
His peace and happiness be mine.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: And though my neighbor's hate I prove,
Still let me vanquish hate with love;
And every secret wish suppress,
That would abridge his happiness.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Let love through all my conduct shine,
An image fair, though faint, of thine!
Thus let me his disciple prove,
Who came to manifest thy love.

hymns.title: This is the first and great command
hymns.author: Roscoe
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: This is the first and great command--
To love thy God above;
And this the second--as thyself
Thy neighbor thou shalt love.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Who is my neighbor? He who wants
The help which thou canst give;
And both the law and prophets say,
This do, and thou shalt live.

hymns.title: God of our mercy and our praise
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: God of our mercy and our praise,
Thy glory is our song;
We'll speak the honors of thy grace
With a rejoicing tongue.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: When Christ among the sons of men
In humble form was found,
With cruel slanders, false and vain,
They compassed him around.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Their miseries his compassion moved,
Their peace he still pursued;
They rendered hatred for his love,
And evil for his good.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Their malice raged without a cause;
Yet, with his dying breath,
He prayed for murderers on his cross,
And blest his foes in death.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: O, may his conduct, all divine,
To us a model prove:
Like his, O God, our hearts incline
Our enemies to love.

hymns.title: Faith, hope, and love, now dwell on earth
hymns.author: Christian Psalmist
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Faith, hope, and love, now dwell on earth,
And earth by them is blest;
But faith and hope must yield to love,
Of all the graces best.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Hope shall to full fruition rise,
And faith be sight above;
These are the means, but this the end,
For saints forever love.

hymns.title: Faith, hope, and charity, these three
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Faith, hope, and charity, these three,
Yet is the greatest charity;
Father of lights, these gifts impart
To mine and every human heart.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Faith, that in prayer can never fail,
Hope, that o'er doubting must prevail,
And charity, whose name above
Is God's own name, for God is love.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The morning star is lost in light,
Faith vanishes at perfect sight,
The rainbow passes with the storm
And hope with sorrow's fading form.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: But charity, serene, sublime,
Beyond the reach of death and time,
Like the blue sky's all-bounding space,
Holds heaven and earth in its embrace.

hymns.title: Mistaken souls! that dream of heaven
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Mistaken souls! that dream of heaven,
And make their empty boast
Of inward joys, and sins forgiven,
While they are slaves to lust.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Vain are our fancies, airy flights,
If faith be cold and dead;
None but a living power unites
To Christ the living head.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: 'T is faith that purifies the heart;
'T is faith that works by love;
That bids all sinful joys depart,
And lifts the thoughts above.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: This faith shall every fear control
By its celestial power,
With holy triumph fill the soul
In death's approaching hour.

hymns.title: The uplifted eye, and bended knee
hymns.author: Scott
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The uplifted eye, and bended knee,
Are but vain homage, Lord, to thee;
In vain our lips thy praise prolong,
The heart a stranger to the song.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The pure, the humble, contrite mind,
Sincere, and to thy will resigned,
To thee a nobler offering yields,
Than Sheba's groves, or Sharon's fields.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Love God and man--this great command,
Doth on eternal pillars stand;
This did thine ancient prophets teach,
And this thy Well-Belovéd preach.

hymns.title: How beautiful the sight
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How beautiful the sight
Of brethren who agree
In friendship to unite,
And bonds of charity!
'T is like the precious ointment shed
O'er all his robes from Aaron's head.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: 'Tis like the dews that fill
The cups of Hermon's flowers;
Or Zion's fruitful hill,
Bright with the drops of showers;
When mingling odors breathe around,
And glory rests on all the ground.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: For there the Lord commands
Blessings, a boundless store,
From his unsparing hands,
Yea, life for evermore.
Thrice happy they who meet above
To spend eternity in love!

hymns.title: Lord! subdue our selfish will
hymns.author: C. Wesley
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord! subdue our selfish will;
Each to each our tempers suit,
By thy modulating skill,
Heart to heart, as lute to lute.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Sweetly on our spirits move;
Gently touch the trembling strings:
Make the harmony of love,
Music for the King of kings!

hymns.title: Blest are the sons of peace
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Blest are the sons of peace,
Whose hearts and hopes are one;
Whose kind designs to serve and please
Through all their actions run.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Blest is the pious house
Where zeal and friendship meet;
Their songs of praise, their mingled vows,
Make their communion sweet.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thus on the heavenly hills
The saints are blest above,
Where joy like morning dew distils,
And all the air is love.

hymns.title: The glorious universe around
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The glorious universe around,
The heavens with all their train,
Sun, moon, and stars, are firmly bound
In one mysterious chain.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The earth, the ocean, and the sky,
To form one world agree,
Where all that walk, or swim, or fly,
Compose one family.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: In one fraternal bond of love,
One fellowship of mind,
The saints below and saints above
Their bliss and glory find.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Here in their house of pilgrimage,
Thy statutes are their song;
There, through one bright, eternal age,
Thy praises they prolong.

hymns.title: The saints on earth and those above
hymns.author: C. Wesley
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The saints on earth and those above
But one communion make:
Joined to their Lord in bonds of love,
All of his grace partake.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: One family, we dwell in him;
One church above, beneath;
Though now divided by the stream,
The swelling stream of death.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: One army of the living God,--
To his command we bow;
Part of the host have crossed the flood,
And part are crossing now.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O God, be thou our constant guide!
And when the word is given,
Sustain us o'er the fearful tide,
And bring us safe to heaven.

hymns.title: Let party names no more
hymns.author: Beddome
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Let party names no more
The Christian world o'erspread;
Gentile and Jew, and bond and free,
Are one in Christ their head.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Among the saints on earth
Let mutual love be found;
Heirs of the same inheritance,
With mutual blessings crowned.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Let envy and ill-will
Be banished far away;
Those should in holy friendship dwell,
Who the same Lord obey.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thus will the church below
Resemble that above;
Where streams of pleasure always flow,
And every heart is love.

hymns.title: How blest the sacred tie that binds
hymns.author: Barbauld
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How blest the sacred tie that binds
In union sweet according minds!
How swift the heavenly course they run,
Whose hearts, and faith, and hopes are one!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: To each the soul of each how dear!
What jealous love, what holy fear!
How doth the generous flame within
Refine from earth, and cleanse from sin!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Their streaming eyes together flow
For human guilt and mortal woe;
Their ardent prayers together rise
Like mingling flames in sacrifice.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Together shall they seek the place
Where God reveals his awful face:
How high, how strong, their raptures swell
There's none but kindred souls can tell.

hymns.title: Omniscient God, 'tis thine to know
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Omniscient God, 'tis thine to know
The springs whence wrong opinions flow;
To judge from principles within,
When frailty errs, and when we sin.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Who with another's eye can read,
Or worship by another's creed?
Revering thy command alone,
We humbly seek and use our own.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: If wrong, forgive; accept, if right,
Whilst faithful, we obey our light,
And judging none, are zealous still
To follow, as to learn, thy will.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: When shall our happy eyes behold
Thy people, fashioned in thy mould?
And charity our kindred prove
Derived from thee, O God of love?

hymns.title: Not different food, nor different dress
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Not different food, nor different dress,
Compose the kingdom of our Lord;
But peace, and joy, and righteousness,
Faith, and obedience to his word.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: When weaker Christians we despise,
We do the gospel mighty wrong;
For God, the gracious and the wise,
Receives the feeble with the strong.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Let pride and wrath be banished hence,
Meekness and love our souls pursue,
Nor shall our practice give offence
To saints, the Gentile or the Jew.

hymns.title: Imposture shrinks from light
hymns.author: Scott
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Imposture shrinks from light,
And dreads the curious eye;
But sacred truths the test invite,
They bid us search and try.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: With understanding blest,
Created to be free,
Our faith on man we dare not rest,
Subject to none but thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Lord, give the light we need;
With soundest knowledge fill;
From noxious error guard our creed,
From prejudice our will.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The truth thou shalt impart,
May we with firmness own;
Abhorring each evasive art,
And fearing thee alone.

hymns.title: Zeal is that pure and heavenly flame
hymns.author: Newton
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Zeal is that pure and heavenly flame
The fire of love supplies;
Whilst that which often bears the name,
Is self but in disguise.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: True zeal is merciful and mild,
Can pity and forbear;
The false is headstrong, fierce and wild,
And breathes revenge and war.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: While zeal for truth the Christian warms,
He knows the worth of peace;
But self contends for names and forms,
Its party to increase.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Zeal has attained its highest aim,
Its end is satisfied,
If sinners love the Saviour's name,--
Nor seeks it aught beside.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: This idol self, O Lord, dethrone,
And from our hearts remove;
And let no zeal by us be shown
But that which springs from love.

hymns.title: Happy the man whose cautious steps
hymns.author: Needham
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Happy the man whose cautious steps
Still keep the golden mean;
Whose life by wisdom's rules well formed,
Declares a conscience clean.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: To sect or party his large soul
Disdains to be confined;
The good he loves of every name,
And prays for all mankind.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: His business is to keep his heart;
Each passion to control;
Nobly ambitious well to rule
The empire of his soul.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Not on the world his heart is set,
His treasure is above;
Nothing beneath the sovereign good
Can claim his highest love.

hymns.title: How happy is he born or taught
hymns.author: Sir H. Wotton
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How happy is he born or taught,
Who serveth not another's will;
Whose armor is his honest thought,
And simple truth his highest skill;

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Whose passions not his masters are;
Whose soul is still prepared for death;
Not tied unto the world with care
Of prince's ear or vulgar breath;

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Who God doth late and early pray
More of his grace than goods to lend,
And walks with man from day to day,
As with a brother and a friend.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: This man is freed from servile bands
Of hope to rise, or fear to fall;
Lord of himself, though not of lands,
And having nothing, yet hath all.

hymns.title: O happy is the man, who hears
hymns.author: Logan
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O happy is the man, who hears
Instruction's warning voice;
And who celestial wisdom makes
His early, only choice.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Her treasures are of more esteem
Than east or west unfold;
And her rewards more precious are
Than all their mines of gold.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: In her right hand she holds to view
A length of happy days;
Riches with splendid honors joined,
Her left hand full displays.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: She guides the young with innocence
In pleasure's path to tread;
A crown of glory she bestows
Upon the hoary head.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: According as her labors rise,
So her rewards increase;
Her ways are ways of pleasantness,
And all her paths are peace.

hymns.title: Mere human power shall fast decay
hymns.author: Campbell's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Mere human power shall fast decay,
And youthful vigor cease;
But they who wait upon the Lord
In strength shall still increase.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: They with unwearied feet shall tread
The path of life divine,
With growing ardor onward move,
With growing brightness shine.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: On eagles' wings they mount, they soar;
Their wings are faith and love;
Till, past the cloudy regions here,
They rise to heaven above.

hymns.title: O happy soul that lives on high
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O happy soul that lives on high,
While men lie grovelling here!
His hopes are fixed above the sky,
And faith forbids his fear.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: His conscience knows no secret stings,
While grace and joy combine
To form a life whose holy springs
Are hidden and divine.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: He waits in secret on his God;
His God in secret sees;
Let earth be all in arms abroad,
He dwells in heavenly peace.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: His pleasures rise from things unseen,
Beyond this world and time;
Where neither eyes nor ears have been,
Nor thoughts of mortals climb.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: He wants no pomp nor royal throne
To raise his honors here;
Content and pleased to live unknown,
Till Christ, his life, appear.

hymns.title: Lord, from whom all blessings flow
hymns.author: C. Wesley
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord, from whom all blessings flow,
Perfecting the church below!
Steadfast may we cleave to thee;
Love the mystic union be.
Join our faithful spirits, join
Each to each, and all to thine:
Lead us through the paths of peace,
On to perfect holiness.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Sweetly may we all agree,
Touched with softest sympathy:
There is neither bond nor free,
Great nor servile, Lord, in thee;
Love, like death, hath all destroyed
Rendered all distinctions void!
Names, and sects, and parties fall:
Thou, O Christ, art all in all!

hymns.title: Religion can assuage
hymns.author: Steele
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Religion can assuage
The tempest of the soul;
And every fear shall lose its rage
At her divine control.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Through life's bewildered way,
Her hand unerring leads;
And o'er the path her heavenly ray
A cheering lustre sheds.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: When reason, tired and blind,
Sinks helpless and afraid,
Thou blest supporter of the mind,
How powerful is thine aid!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O, let us feel thy power,
And find thy sweet relief,
To brighten every gloomy hour
And soften every grief.

hymns.title: How blest is he, who ne'er consents
hymns.author: Tate & Brady
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How blest is he, who ne'er consents
By ill advice to walk;
Nor stands in sinners' ways, nor sits
Where men profanely talk:

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: But makes the perfect law of God
His business and delight;
Devoutly reads therein by day,
And meditates by night.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Like some fair tree, which, fed by streams,
With timely fruit does bend,
He still shall flourish, and success
All his designs attend.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Ungodly men, and their attempts,
No lasting root shall find;
Untimely blasted, and dispersed
Like chaff before the wind.

hymns.title: Blest is the man who fears the Lord
hymns.author: Exeter Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Blest is the man who fears the Lord!
His well established mind,
In every varying scene of life,
Shall true composure find.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Oft through the deep and stormy sea
The heavenly footsteps lie;
But on a glorious world beyond
His faith can fix its eye.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Though dark his present prospects be,
And sorrows round him dwell,
Yet hope can whisper to his soul,
That all shall issue well.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Full in the presence of his God,
Through every scene he goes;
And, fearing him, no other fear
His steadfast bosom knows.

hymns.title: When true religion gains a place
hymns.author: Proud
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When true religion gains a place,
And lives within the mind,
The sensual life subdued by grace,
And all the soul refined:

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The desert blooms in living green,
Where thorns and briers grew;
The barren waste is fruitful seen,
And all the prospect new.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O happy Christian, richly blessed!
What floods of pleasure roll!
By God and man he stands confessed,
In dignity of soul.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Substantial, pure, his every joy:
His Maker is his friend;
The noblest business his employ,
And happiness his end.

hymns.title: He who walks in virtue's way
hymns.author: Bowring
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: He who walks in virtue's way,
Firm and fearless, walketh surely;
Diligent, while yet 'tis day,
On he speeds, and speeds securely.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Flowers of peace beneath him grow,
Suns of pleasure brighten o'er him;
Memory's joys behind him go,
Hope's sweet angels fly before him.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thus he moves from stage to stage,
Smiles of earth and heaven attending;
Softly sinking down in age,
And at last to death descending.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Cradled in its quiet deep,
Calm as summer's loveliest even,
He shall sleep the hallowed sleep;
Sleep that is o'erwatched by Heaven.

hymns.title: The man, in life wherever placed
hymns.author: Burns
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The man, in life wherever placed,
Hath happiness in store,
Who walks not in the wicked's way,
Nor learns their guilty lore:

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Nor from the seat of scornful pride
Casts forth his eyes abroad,
But with humility and awe,
Still walks before his God.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: That man shall flourish like the trees
Which by the streamlets grow;
The fruitful top is spread on high,
And firm the root below.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: But he whose blossom buds in guilt
Shall to the ground be cast,
And, like the rootless stubble, tossed
Before the sweeping blast.

hymns.title: Sweet is the bliss of souls serene
hymns.author: Keble
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Sweet is the bliss of souls serene,
When they have sworn and steadfast mean,
Counting the cost, in all t' espy
Their God, in all themselves deny.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O could we learn that sacrifice,
What lights would all around us rise!
How would our hearts with wisdom talk,
Along life's dullest, dreariest walk!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: We need not bid, for cloistered cell,
Our neighbor and our work farewell,
Nor strive to wind ourselves too high
For sinful man beneath the sky:

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The trivial round, the common task,
Would furnish all we ought to ask;
Room to deny ourselves; a road
To bring us, daily, nearer God.

hymns.title: Sometimes a light surprises
hymns.author: Cowper
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Sometimes a light surprises
The Christian while he sings:
It is the Lord, who rises
With healing in his wings:
When comforts are declining,
He grants the soul again
A season of clear shining,
To cheer it after rain.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: In holy contemplation,
We sweetly then pursue
The theme of God's salvation,
And find it ever new:
Set free from present sorrow,
We cheerfully can say,
"E'en let the unknown morrow
Bring with it what it may."

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: It can bring with it nothing,
But he will bear us through:
Who gives the lilies clothing,
Will clothe his people too:
Beneath the spreading heavens,
No creature but is fed;
And he who feeds the ravens,
Will give his children bread.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Though vine, nor fig tree neither,
Its wonted fruit should bear;
Though all the field should wither,
Nor flocks, nor herds be there:
Yet God the same abiding,
His praise shall tune my voice;
For while in him confiding,
I cannot but rejoice.

hymns.title: I want a sober mind
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I want a sober mind,
A self-renouncing will,
That tramples down and casts behind
The baits of pleasing ill;
A soul inured to pain,
To hardship, grief and loss,
Bold to take up, firm to sustain
The consecrated cross.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: I want a godly fear,
A quick-discerning eye,
That looks to thee when sin is near,
And sees the tempter fly;
A spirit still prepared,
And armed with jealous care,
Forever standing on its guard,
And watching unto prayer.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: I want a true regard,
A single, steady aim,
Unmoved by threatening or reward,
To thee and thy great name;
A zealous, just concern
For thine immortal praise;
A pure desire that all may learn
And glorify thy grace.

hymns.title: Come, ye that love the Lord
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, ye that love the Lord,
And let your joys be known;
Join in a song with sweet accord,
And thus surround the throne.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The sorrows of the mind
Be banished from the place!
Religion never was designed
To make our pleasures less.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The men of grace have found
Glory begun below;
Celestial fruits on earthly ground
From faith and hope may grow.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The hill of Sion yields
A thousand sacred sweets,
Before we reach the heavenly fields,
Or walk the golden streets.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Then let our songs abound,
And every tear be dry;
We're marching through Immanuel's ground,
To fairer worlds on high.

hymns.title: Our heaven is everywhere
hymns.author: Miss Fletcher
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Our heaven is everywhere,
If we but love the Lord,
Unswerving tread the narrow way,
And ever shun the broad.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: 'T is where the trusting heart
Bows meekly to its grief,
Still looking up with earnest faith
For comfort and relief.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Where guileless infancy
In happiness doth dwell,
And where the aged one can say
"He hath done all things well."

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Wherever truth abides
Sweet peace is ever there;
If we but love and serve the Lord,
Our heaven is everywhere.

hymns.title: Heaven is here; its hymns of gladness
hymns.author: J. G. Adams
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Heaven is here; its hymns of gladness
Cheer the true believer's way,
In this world where sin and sadness
Often change to night our day.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Heaven is here; where misery lightened
Of its heavy load is seen,
Where the face of sorrow brightened
By the deed of love hath been:

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Where the bound, the poor, despairing
Are set free, supplied and blest;
Where, in others' anguish sharing,
We can find our surest rest.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Where we heed the voice of duty
Rather than man's praise, or rod;
This is heaven,--its peace, its beauty,
Radiant with the smile of God.

hymns.title: Oh, where, our Saviour! sweeps the line
hymns.author: Mrs. Case
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Oh, where, our Saviour! sweeps the line
That marks thy kingdom's holy reign?
Is it where northern meteors shine
Or gilds the cross the southern main?
Where breaks the dawn o'er spicy lands?
Or twilight sleeps on desert sands?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Is it where sunny skies grow dim
With smoke of heathen sacrifice?
Or where, in costly domes, the hymn
Is taught on incense clouds to rise?
Nay, nay, thy blessed word has shown
Thy kingdom is the heart alone!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: That solemn world, whose bounds between
Life's mysteries of birth and death,
Are filled with warring hosts unseen,
Beings of power, though not of breath--
The spirit realm, where'er it be,
Is the dominion swayed by thee.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Wild, phantom shapes of gloom and fear,
Roam dimly through the haunted spot,
And earth holds not a land so drear
As the sad heart that owns thee not,
Where sorrows wound and pleasures pall,
And death's dread shadow darkens all.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: But lift thy sceptre there, its bowers
Shall be serene and sweet and fair,
And, as in time's primeval hours,
The holy ones shall gather there,
And heaven's own peace the soul o'erflow,
E'en while it lingers here below.

hymns.title: As earth's pageant passes by
hymns.author: Beaumont
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: As earth's pageant passes by,
Let reflection turn thine eye
Inward, and observe thy breast;
There alone dwells solid rest.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: That's a close immured tower,
Which can mock all hostile power;
To thyself a tenant be,
And inhabit safe and free.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Say not that this house is small,
Girt up in a narrow wall;
In a cleanly, sober mind,
Heaven itself full room doth find.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The infinite Creator can
Dwell in it; and may not man?
Here, content, make thy abode
With thyself and with thy God.

hymns.title: While thee I seek, protecting Power
hymns.author: H. M. Williams
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: While thee I seek, protecting Power!
Be my vain wishes stilled;
And may this consecrated hour
With better hopes be filled.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thy love the power of thought bestowed;
To thee my thoughts would soar;
Thy mercy o'er my life has flowed--
That mercy I adore!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: In each event of life, how clear
Thy ruling hand I see!
Each blessing to my soul more dear,
Because conferred by thee.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: In every joy that crowns my days,
In every pain I bear,
My heart shall find delight in praise,
Or seek relief in prayer.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: When gladness wings my favored hour,
Thy love my thoughts shall fill;
Resigned, when storms of sorrow lower,
My soul shall meet thy will.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: My lifted eye, without a tear,
The gathering storm shall see;
My steadfast heart shall know no fear;--
That heart shall rest on thee!

hymns.title: When, wakened by thy voice of power
hymns.author: Bowring
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When, wakened by thy voice of power,
The hour of morning beams in light,
My voice shall sing that morning hour,
And thee, who mad'st that hour so bright.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The morning strengthens into noon;
Earth's fairest beauties shine more fair;
And noon and morning shall attune
My grateful heart to praise and prayer.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: When 'neath the evening western gate
The sun's retiring rays are hid,
My joy shall be to meditate,
E'en as the pious patriarch did.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: As twilight wears a darker hue,
And gathering night creation dims,
The twilight and the midnight, too,
Shall have their harmonies and hymns.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: So shall sweet thoughts, and thoughts sublime,
My constant inspirations be;
And every shifting scene of time
Reflect, my God, a light from thee.

hymns.title: I love to steal awhile away
hymns.author: Mrs. Brown
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I love to steal awhile away
From every cumbering care,
And spend the hours of setting day
In humble, grateful prayer.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: I love to think on mercies past,
And future good implore,
And all my cares and sorrows cast
On him whom I adore.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: I love by faith to take a view
Of brighter scenes in heaven;
The prospect doth my strength renew,
While here by tempests driven.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thus, when life's toilsome day is o'er,
May its departing ray
Be calm as this impressive hour,
And lead to endless day.

hymns.title: O God, my strength, my hope
hymns.author: C. Wesley
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O God, my strength, my hope,
On thee I cast my care,
With humble confidence look up,
And know thou hearest prayer.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O, for a godly fear,
A quick-discerning eye,
That looks to thee when sin is near,
And sees the tempter fly!--

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: A spirit still prepared,
And armed with jealous care,
Forever standing on its guard,
And watching unto prayer!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Lord, let me still abide,
Nor from my hope remove,
Till thou my patient spirit guide
To better worlds above.

hymns.title: Like morning, when her early breeze
hymns.author: Moore
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Like morning, when her early breeze
Breaks up the surface of the seas,
That, in their furrows, dark with night,
Her hand may sow the seeds of light;

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thy grace can send its breathings o'er
The spirit, dark and lost before;
And freshening all its depths, prepare
For truth divine to enter there!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Till David touched his sacred lyre,
In silence lay the unbreathing wire;
But when he swept its chords along,
E'en angels stooped to hear the song.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: So sleeps the soul, till thou, O Lord,
Shall deign to touch its lifeless chord;
Till, waked by thee, its breath shall rise
In music worthy of the skies.

hymns.title: To keep the lamp alive
hymns.author: Cowper
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: To keep the lamp alive,
With oil we fill the bowl;
'T is water makes the willow thrive,
And grace that feeds the soul.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The Lord's unsparing hand
Supplies the living stream;
It is not at our own command,
But still derived from him.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Man's wisdom is to seek
His strength in God alone;
And e'en an angel would be weak,
Who trusted in his own.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Retreat beneath his wings,
And in his grace confide;
This more exalts the King of kings
Than all your works beside.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: In God is all our store,
Grace issues from his throne;
Whoever says, "I want no more,"
Confesses he has none.

hymns.title: As the hart, with eager looks
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: As the hart, with eager looks,
Panteth for the water-brooks,
So my soul, athirst for thee,
Pants the living God to see;
When, O when, with filial fear,
Lord, shall I to thee draw near?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Why art thou cast down, my soul?
God, thy God, shall make thee whole:
Why art thou disquieted?
God shall lift thy fallen head,
And his countenance benign
Be the saving health of thine.

hymns.title: Supreme and universal Light
hymns.author: Henry Moore
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Supreme and universal Light!
Fountain of reason! Judge of right!
Parent of good! whose blessings flow
On all above, and all below:

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Assist us, Lord, to act, to be,
What nature and thy laws decree;
Worthy that intellectual flame,
Which from thy breathing spirit came!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: May our expanded souls disclaim
The narrow view, the selfish aim;
But with a Christian zeal embrace
Whate'er is friendly to our race.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O Father, grace and virtue grant!
No more we wish, no more we want:
To know, to serve thee, and to love,
Is peace below,--is bliss above.

hymns.title: Author of good, to thee we turn
hymns.author: Merrick
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Author of good, to thee we turn:
Thine ever wakeful eye
Alone can all our wants discern,
Thy hand alone supply.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O, let thy love within us dwell,
Thy fear our footsteps guide;
That love shall vainer loves expel,
That fear all fears beside.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And, O, by error's force subdued,
Since oft, with stubborn will,
We blindly shun the latent good,
And grasp the specious ill,--

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Not what we wish, but what we want,
Let mercy still supply:
The good we ask not; Father, grant;
The ill we ask, deny.

hymns.title: I want the spirit of power within
hymns.author: C. Wesley
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I want the spirit of power within,
Of love, and of a healthful mind;
Of power to conquer every sin,
Of love to God and all mankind;
Of health that pain and death defies,
Most vigorous when the body dies.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O that the Comforter would come,
Nor visit as a transient guest,
But fix in me his constant home,
And keep possession of my breast;
And make my soul his loved abode,
The temple of indwelling God!

hymns.title: While some in folly's pleasures roll
hymns.author: Cotton
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: While some in folly's pleasures roll,
And court the joys that hurt the soul,
Be mine that silent, calm repast,
A conscience peaceful to the last.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: With this companion in the shade,
My soul no more shall be dismayed;
But fearless meet life's dreariest gloom,
And the pale monarch of the tomb.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Amidst the various scenes of ills,
Each blow some kind design fulfils;
And can I murmur at my God,
While love supreme directs the rod?

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: His hand will smooth my rugged way,
And lead me to the realms of day;
To milder skies, and brighter plains,
Where everlasting pleasure reigns.

hymns.title: My God, permit me not to be
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: My God, permit me not to be
A stranger to myself and thee:
Amidst a thousand thoughts I rove,
Forgetful of my highest love.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Why should my passions mix with earth,
And thus debase my heavenly birth?
Why should I cleave to things below,
And let my God, my Saviour go?

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Call me away from flesh and sense;
Thy sovereign word can draw me thence:
I would obey the voice divine,
And all inferior joys resign.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Be earth, with all her scenes, withdrawn,
Let noise and vanity be gone:
In secret silence of the mind,
My heaven, and there my God, I find.

hymns.title: Rise, my soul, and stretch thy wings
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Rise, my soul, and stretch thy wings,
Thy better portion trace;
Rise from transitory things,
Towards heaven, thy native place:
Sun, and moon, and stars decay,
Time shall soon this earth remove;
Rise, my soul, and haste away
To seats prepared above.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Rivers to the ocean run,
Nor stay in all their course;
Fire ascending seeks the sun,--
Both speed them to their source:
So a soul that's born of God
Pants to view his glorious face,
Upward tends to his abode,
To rest in his embrace.

hymns.title: Come, O thou universal good
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, O thou universal good!
Balm of the wounded conscience, come!
The hungry, dying spirit's food;
The weary, wand'ring pilgrim's home;
Haven to take the shipwrecked in,
My everlasting rest from sin!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Come, O my comfort and delight!
My strength and health, and shield, and sun
My boast, my confidence, and might,
My joy, my glory, and my crown;
My gospel-hope, my calling's prize,
My tree of life, my paradise.

hymns.title: How sweet the name of Jesus sounds
hymns.author: Newton
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How sweet the name of Jesus sounds
In a believer's ear!
It soothes his sorrows, heals his wounds,
And drives away his fear.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: It makes the wounded spirit whole,
It calms the troubled breast;
'T is manna to the hungry soul,
And, to the weary, rest.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Weak is the effort of my heart,
And cold my warmest thought,
But when I see thee as thou art,
I'll praise thee as I ought.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Till then I would thy love proclaim,
With every fleeting breath;
And may the music of thy name
Refresh my soul in death.

hymns.title: Our Father in heaven, we hallow thy name
hymns.author: Mrs. Hale
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Our Father in heaven, we hallow thy name!
May thy kingdom holy on earth be the same!
O give to us daily our portion of bread;
It is from thy bounty that all must be fed.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Forgive our transgressions, and teach us to know
That humble compassion which pardons each foe:
Keep us from temptation, from weakness and sin,
And thine be the glory forever--Amen.

hymns.title: Our Father, who in heaven art
hymns.author: T. Whittemore
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Our Father, who in heaven art,
All hallowed be thy name:
Thy kingdom come, thy will be done,
On earth, in heaven the same.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Give us this day our daily bread,
Our debts, O Lord, forgive,
As we forgive our enemies
And thus obedient live.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Into temptation lead us not,
From evil keep us clean;
Thine is the kingdom, glory, power
Forevermore, Amen.

hymns.title: Our heavenly Father, hear
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Our heavenly Father, hear
The prayer we offer now:--
Thy name be hallowed far and near,
To thee all nations bow.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thy kingdom come; thy will
On earth be done in love,
As saints and seraphim fulfil
Thy perfect law above.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Our daily bread supply,
While by thy word we live;
The guilt of our iniquity
Forgive, as we forgive.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: From dark temptation's power
Our feeble hearts defend;
Deliver in the evil hour,
And guide us to the end.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Thine, then, forever be
Glory and power divine;
The sceptre, throne, and majesty
Of heaven and earth are thine.

hymns.title: O Thou, enthroned in worlds above
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O Thou, enthroned in worlds above,
Our Father and our Friend!
Lo, at the footstool of thy love,
Thy children humbly bend.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: All reverence to thy name be given;
Thy kingdom wide displayed;
And, as thy will is done in heaven,
Be it on earth obeyed.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Our table may thy bounty spread,
From thine exhaustless store,
From day to day with daily bread,--
Nor would we ask for more.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: That pardon we to others give,
Do thou to us extend;
From all temptation, Lord, relieve;
From every ill defend.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: And now to thee belong, Most High,
The kingdom, glory, power,
Through the broad earth and spacious sky,
Both now and evermore.

hymns.title: O Thou, whose power o'er moving worlds presides
hymns.author: Dr. Johnson
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O Thou, whose power o'er moving worlds presides,
Whose voice created, and whose wisdom guides,
On darkling man in pure effulgence shine,
And cheer his clouded mind with light divine.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: 'T is thine alone to calm the pious breast
With silent confidence and holy rest;
From thee, great God, we spring, to thee we tend;
Path, Motive, Guide, Original, and End.

hymns.title: Behold, what wondrous grace
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Behold, what wondrous grace
The Father has bestowed
On sinners of a mortal race,
To call them sons of God!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Nor doth it yet appear
How great we must be made;
But when we see our Saviour here,
We shall be like our Head.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: A hope so much divine
May trials well endure;
May purify our souls from sin,
As Christ, the Lord, is pure.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: We would no longer lie
Like slaves beneath the throne;
Our faith shall Abba, Father, cry,
And thou the kindred own.

hymns.title: Ah wretched souls, who strive in vain
hymns.author: Mrs. Steele
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Ah wretched souls, who strive in vain,
Slaves to the world, and slaves to sin!
A nobler toil may I sustain,
A nobler satisfaction win.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: May I resolve, with all my heart,
With all my powers, to serve the Lord;
Nor from his precepts e'er depart,
Whose service is a rich reward.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Be this the purpose of my soul,
My solemn, my determined choice,
To yield to his supreme control,
And in his kind commands rejoice.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O may I never faint nor tire,
Nor, wandering, leave his sacred ways;
Great God! accept my soul's desire,
And give me strength to live thy praise.

hymns.title: Great God! my joyful thanks to thee
hymns.author: Browne
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Great God! my joyful thanks to thee
Shall, like thy gifts, continual be:
In constant streams thy bounty flows,
Nor end nor intermission knows.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thy kindness all my comforts gives;
My numerous wants thy hand relieves;
Nor can I ever, Lord, be poor,
Who live on thy exhaustless store.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: If what I wish thy will denies,
It is that thou art good and wise;
Afflictions, which may make me mourn,
Thou canst, thou dost, to blessings turn.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Deep, Lord, upon my thankful breast,
Let all thy favors be imprest;
And though withdrawn thy gifts should be
In all things I'll give thanks to thee.

hymns.title: How rich thy favors, God of grace
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How rich thy favors, God of grace!
How various, how divine!
Full as the ocean they are poured,
And bright as heaven they shine.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: He to eternal glory calls,
And leads the wondrous way
To his own palace where he reigns
In uncreated day.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Jesus, the herald of his love,
Displays the radiant prize,
A crown of never-ending bliss,
To our admiring eyes.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The songs of everlasting years
That mercy shall attend,
Which leads, through sufferings of an hour,
To joys that never end.

hymns.title: O Help us, Lord! each hour of need
hymns.author: H. H. Milman
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O Help us, Lord! each hour of need
Thy heavenly succor give;
Help us in thought, and word, and deed,
Each hour on earth we live.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O help us, when our spirits bleed,
With contrite anguish sore,
And when our hearts are cold and dead,
O help us, Lord, the more.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O help us through the prayer of faith
More firmly to believe;
For still the more the servant hath,
The more shall he receive.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O help us, Father! from on high;
We know no help but thee;
O! help us so to live and die,
As thine in heaven to be.

hymns.title: Come, let us pray: 'tis sweet to feel
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, let us pray: 'tis sweet to feel
That God himself is near;
That, while we at his footstool kneel,
His mercy deigns to hear:
Though sorrows cloud life's dreary way,
This is our solace--let us pray.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Come, let us pray: the burning brow,
The heart oppressed with care,
And all the woes that throng us now,
Will be relieved by prayer:
Our God will chase our griefs away;
O, glorious thought!--come, let us pray.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Come, let us pray: the mercy-seat
Invites the fervent prayer.
Our Heavenly Father waits to greet
The contrite spirit there:
O, loiter not, nor longer stay
From him who loves us; let us pray.

hymns.title: When Israel of the Lord beloved
hymns.author: Sir Walter Scott
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When Israel of the Lord beloved,
Out from the land of bondage came,
Her father's God before her moved,
An awful guide in smoke and flame.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: By day, along th' astonished lands,
The cloudy pillar glided slow;
By night, Arabia's crimsoned sands
Returned the fiery column's glow.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thus, present still, though now unseen,
When brightly shines the prosperous day,
Be thoughts of thee a cloudy screen,
To temper the deceitful ray!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: And O, when gathers on our path,
In shade and storm, the frequent night,
Be thou, long-suffering, slow to wrath,
A burning and a shining light!

hymns.title: I want a principle within
hymns.author: C. Wesley
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I want a principle within
Of jealous, godly fear;
A sensibility of sin,
A pain to find it near.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: I want the first approach to feel
Of pride, or fond desire;
To catch the wandering of my will,
And quench the kindling fire.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: From thee that I no more may part,
No more thy goodness grieve,
The filial awe, the fleshly heart,
The tender conscience give.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Quick as the apple of the eye,
O God, my conscience make!
Awake my soul when sin is nigh,
And keep it still awake.

hymns.title: Father of light! conduct my feet
hymns.author: Smart
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father of light! conduct my feet
Through life's dark, dangerous road;
Let each advancing step still bring
Me nearer to my God.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Let heaven-eyed prudence be my guide;
And, when I go astray,
Recall my feet from folly's path
To wisdom's better way.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Teach me in every various scene
To keep my end in sight;
And while I tread life's mazy track,
Let wisdom guide me right.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: That heavenly wisdom from above
Abundantly impart;
And let it guard, and guide, and warm,
And penetrate my heart:

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Till it shall lead me to thyself,
Fountain of bliss and love!
And all my darkness be dispersed
In endless light above.

hymns.title: The bird let loose in Eastern skies
hymns.author: Moore
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The bird let loose in Eastern skies,
Returning fondly home,
Ne'er stoops to earth her wing, nor flies
Where idle warblers roam.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: But high she shoots through air and light--
Above all low delay,
Where nothing earthly bounds her flight,
Nor shadow dims her way.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: So grant me, God, from every snare
Of sinful passion free,
Aloft through faith's serener air
To hold my course to thee.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: No sin to cloud, no lure to stay
My soul, as home she springs;
Thy sunshine on her joyful way,
Thy freedom on her wings.

hymns.title: From every stormy wind that blows
hymns.author: Stowell
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: From every stormy wind that blows,
From every swelling tide of woes,
There is a calm, a sure retreat;
'Tis found before the mercy-seat.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: There is a place were Jesus sheds
The oil of gladness on our heads,--
A place of all on earth most sweet;
It is the heavenly mercy-seat.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: There is a scene where spirits blend,
Where friend holds fellowship with friend;
Though sundered far, by faith they meet
Around one common mercy-seat.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: There, there, on eagle wings we soar,
And sin and sense molest no more;
And heaven comes down our souls to greet,
And glory crowns the mercy-seat.

hymns.title: When fainting in the sultry waste
hymns.author: Steele
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When fainting in the sultry waste,
And parched with thirst extreme,
The weary pilgrim longs to taste
The cool, refreshing stream.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: So longs the weary, fainting mind,
Oppressed with sins and woes,
Some soul-reviving spring to find,
Whence heavenly comfort flows.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O, may I thirst for thee, my God,
With ardent, strong desire;
And still, through all this desert road,
To taste thy grace aspire.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Then shall my prayer to thee ascend,
A grateful sacrifice;
My mourning voice thou wilt attend,
And grant me full supplies.

hymns.title: 'T is a point I long to know
hymns.author: Newton
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: 'T is a point I long to know,--
Oft it causes anxious thought,--
Do I love the Lord, or no?
Am I his, or am I not?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: If I love, why am I thus?
Why this dull and lifeless frame?
Hardly, sure, can they be worse,
Who have never heard his name.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: If I pray, or hear, or read,
Sin is mixed with all I do;
You that love the Lord indeed,
Tell me, is it thus with you?

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Yet I mourn my stubborn will,
Find my sin a grief and thrall;
Should I grieve for what I feel,
If I did not love at all?

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Father, let me love thee more,
If I love at all, I pray;
If I have not loved before,
Help me to begin to-day.

hymns.title: Beset with snares on every hand
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Beset with snares on every hand,
In life's uncertain path I stand:
Father divine! diffuse thy light,
To guide my doubtful footsteps right.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Engage this roving, treacherous heart,
Wisely to choose the better part;
To scorn the trifles of a day,
For joys that none can take away.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Then let the wildest storms arise,
Let tempests mingle earth with skies,
No fatal shipwreck shall I fear,
But all my treasures with me bear.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: If thou, my Saviour, still be nigh,
Cheerful I live, and joyful die;
Secure, when mortal comforts flee,
To find ten thousand worlds in thee.

hymns.title: God is a spirit just and wise
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: God is a spirit just and wise,
He sees our inmost mind;
In vain to heaven we raise our cries,
And leave our souls behind.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Nothing but truth before his throne
With honor can appear;
The painted hypocrites are known
Through the disguise they wear.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Their lifted eyes salute the skies,
Their bending knees the ground;
But God abhors the sacrifice
Where not the heart is found.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Lord, search my thoughts, and try my ways,
And make my soul sincere;
Then shall I stand before thy face,
And find acceptance there.

hymns.title: Be it my only wisdom here
hymns.author: Wesley's Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Be it my only wisdom here,
To serve the Lord with filial fear,
With loving gratitude;
Superior sense may I display,
By shunning every evil way,
And walking in the good.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O may I still from sin depart!
A wise and understanding heart,
Father, to me be given!
And let me through thy Spirit know
To glorify my God below,
And find my way to heaven.

hymns.title: While here as wandering sheep we stray
hymns.author: Merrick
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: While here as wandering sheep we stray,
Teach us, O teach us, Lord, thy way!
Dispose our hearts, with willing awe,
To love thy word, to keep thy law;
That, by thy guiding precepts led,
Our feet the paths of truth may tread.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Great Source of light to all below!
Teach us thy holy will to know:
Teach us to read thy word aright,
And make it our supreme delight;
That, purged from vain desires, our mind
In thee its only good may find.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Maker, instructer, judge of all,
O hear us when on thee we call!
To us, all-bounteous Lord, dispense
Thy grace, and guiding influence!
Preserve us in thy holy ways,
And teach our hearts to speak thy praise!

hymns.title: Almighty God! in humble prayer
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Almighty God! in humble prayer
To thee our souls we lift;
Do thou our waiting minds prepare
For thy most needful gift.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: We ask not golden streams of wealth
Along our path to flow;
We ask not undecaying health,
Nor length of years below.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: We ask not honors, which an hour
May bring and take away;
We ask not pleasure, pomp, and power,
Lest we should go astray.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: We ask for wisdom;--Lord, impart
The knowledge how to live;
A wise and understanding heart
To all before thee give.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: The young remember thee in youth,
Before the evil days!
The old be guided by thy truth
In wisdom's pleasant ways!
