lines: Loosed from the ark, a heavenly dove,
The promise-branch of olive bore,--
Pledge of returning peace and love
That beamed more brightly than before:--

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: So when affliction's waters glide
From the enfranchised soul away,
More peaceful, pure, and sanctified,
The soul emerges into day.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: And then, as with the olive bough
The heavenly dove of old drew near,
Some gentle words of truth will flow,
In holy music on the ear.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: O'er all the transient things of time,
The oblivious foot of years hath trod;
But all that's sacred and sublime
Stands steadfast as the truth of God.

hymns.title: In thy courts let peace be found
hymns.author: Bowring
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: In thy courts let peace be found,
Be thy temple full of love;
There we tread on holy ground,
All serene, around, above.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: While the knee in prayer is bent,
While with praise the heart o'erflows,
Tranquillize the turbulent!
Give the weary one repose!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Be the place for worship meet,
Meet the worship for the place;
Contemplation's best retreat,
Shrine of guilelessness and grace!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: As an infant knows its home,
Lord! may we thy temples know;
Thither for instruction come--
Thence by thee instructed go.

hymns.title: Long be our fathers' temple ours
hymns.author: Cunningham
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Long be our fathers' temple ours,
Far hence the time in which it falls;
A thousand spirits watch its bowers,
A cloud of angels guard its walls.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: And be their shield by us possessed;
Lord, rear around the blest abode,
The buttress of a holy breast,
The rampart of a present God.

hymns.title: Though faint and sick, and worn away
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Though faint and sick, and worn away
With poverty and woe,
My widowed feet are doomed to stray
'Mid thorny paths below.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Be thou, O Lord, my Father still,
My confidence and guide:
I know that perfect is thy will,
Whate'er that will decide.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: I know the soul that trusts in thee
Thou never wilt forsake;
And though a bruiséd reed I be,
That reed thou wilt not break.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Then keep me, Lord, where'er I go,
Support me on my way,
Though, worn with poverty and woe,
My widowed footsteps stray.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: To give my weakness strength, O God,
Thy staff shall yet avail;
And though thou chasten with thy rod,
That staff shall never fail.

hymns.title: Where shall the child of sorrow find
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Where shall the child of sorrow find
A place for calm repose?
Thou, Father of the fatherless,
Pity the orphan's woes!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: What friend have I in heaven or earth,
What friend to trust but thee?
My father's dead--my mother's dead;
My God, remember me!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thy gracious promise now fulfil,
And bid my trouble cease;
In thee the fatherless shall find
Pure mercy, grace and peace.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: I've not a secret care or pain,
But he that secret knows;
Thou, Father of the fatherless,
Pity the orphan's woes!

hymns.title: Come the rich and come the poor
hymns.author: Bowring
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come the rich and come the poor,
To the Christian temple door;
Let their mingled prayers ascend
To the universal Friend.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Here the rich and poor may claim
Common ancestry and name;
Claim a common heritage,
In the gospel's promise page.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Of the same materials wrought;
By the same instructor taught;
Walking in life's common way;
Tending to the same decay.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Rich and poor at last shall meet
At the heavenly mercy seat;
Where the name of rich and poor
Never shall be uttered more.

hymns.title: Oh, what a struggle wakes within
hymns.author: Bowring
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Oh, what a struggle wakes within,
When in the spirit's solitude,
The tempting, treacherous thoughts of sin,
In all their luring smiles intrude!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: 'Tis then, my Father! then I feel
My nature's weakness, and, oppressed,
Like a poor trembling child I steal
To thee, for safety, and for rest.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Beneath thy shadow let me live!
Be thou my Friend--my Father be!
I bend in trust--I pray! forgive
The erring child that flies to thee!

hymns.title: "Let there be light!"--When from on high
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: "Let there be light!"--When from on high,
O God, that first commandment came,
Forth leaped the sun; and earth and sky
Lay in his light, and felt his flame.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: "Let there be light!"--The light of grace
And truth, a darkling world to bless,
Came with thy word, when on our race
Broke forth the Sun of Righteousness.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Light of our souls! how strong it grows:
That sun, how wide his beams he flings,
As up the glorious sky he goes,
With light and healing in his wings!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Give us that light! O God, 'tis given!
Hope sees it open heaven's wide halls
To those who for the truth have striven;
And Faith walks firmly where it falls.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Churches no more, in cold eclipse,
Mourn the withholding of its rays;
It gilds their gates, and on the lips
Of every faithful preacher plays.

hymns.title: Fallen is thy throne, O Israel
hymns.author: Moore
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Fallen is thy throne, O Israel!--
Silence is on all thy plains,--
Thy dwellings all lie desolate,--
Thy children weep in chains.
Where are the dews that fed thee
On Ethan's barren shore?
That fire from heaven that led thee
Now lights thy path no more!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lord, thou didst love Jerusalem!
Once she was all thy own!
Her love thy fairest heritage,
Her power thy glory's throne;
Till evil came and blighted
Thy long-loved olive tree,
And Salem's shrines were lighted
For other gods than thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Then sunk the star of Solyma,
Then passed her glory's day,
Like heath that in the wilderness
The wild wind whirls away.
Silent and waste her bowers,
Where once the mighty trod;
And sunk those guilty towers,
Where Baal reigned as God.

hymns.title: Why on the bending willows hung
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Why on the bending willows hung,
Israel! still sleeps thy tuneful string?--
Still mute remains thy sullen tongue,
And Zion's song denies to sing?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Awake! thy sweetest raptures raise;
Let harp and voice unite their strains
Thy promised King his sceptre sways;
Jesus, thine own Messiah, reigns!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: No taunting foes the song require:
No strangers mock thy captive chain:
But friends provoke the silent lyre,
And brethren ask the holy strain.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Nor fear thy Salem's hills to wrong,
If other lands thy triumph share:
A heavenly city claims thy song;
A brighter Salem rises there.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: By foreign streams no longer roam;
Nor, weeping, think of Jordan's flood:
In every clime behold a home,
In every temple see thy God.

hymns.title: Hear what God the Lord hath spoken
hymns.author: Cowper
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Hear what God the Lord hath spoken,
"O my people, faint and few,
Comfortless, afflicted, broken,
Fair abodes I build for you;
Thorns of heart-felt tribulation
Shall no more perplex your ways;
Ye shall name your walls, Salvation,
And your gates shall all be praise.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: "There, like streams that feed the garden,
Pleasures without end shall flow;
For the Lord, your faith rewarding,
All his bounty shall bestow;
Still, in undisturbed possession,
Peace and righteousness shall reign:
Never shall you feel oppression,
Hear the voice of war again.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: "Ye no more your suns descending,
Waning moons no more shall see;
But, your griefs forever ending,
Find eternal noon in me;
God shall rise, and shining o'er you,
Change to day the gloom of night;
He, the Lord, shall be your glory,
God, your everlasting light."

hymns.title: The triumphs of the martyred saints
hymns.author: Ancient Hymns
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The triumphs of the martyred saints
The joyous lay demand;
The heart delights in song to dwell
On that victorious band--
Those whom the senseless world abhorred,
Who cast the world aside,
Deeming it worthless, for the sake
Of Christ, their Lord and Guide.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: For him they braved the tyrant's rage,
The scourge's cruel smart;
The wild beast's fang their bodies tore,
But vanquished not the heart;
Like lambs before the sword they fell,
Nor cry nor plaint expressed;
For patience kept the conscious mind
And armed the fearless breast.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: What tongue can tell the crown prepared
The martyr's brow to grace?
His shining robe, his joys unknown,
Before thy glorious face?
Vouchsafe us, Lord, if such thy will.
Clear skies and seasons calm;
If not, the martyr's cross to bear,
And win the martyr's palm.

hymns.title: Flung to the heedless winds
hymns.author: Luther
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Flung to the heedless winds,
Or on the waters cast,
Their ashes shall be watched,
And gathered at the last:
And from that scattered dust,
Around us and abroad,
Shall spring a plenteous seed
Of witnesses for God.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The Father hath received
Their latest living breath;
Yet vain is Satan's boast
Of victory in their death:
Still, still, though dead, they speak,
And trumpet-tongued proclaim
To many a wakening land
The one availing name.

hymns.title: For all thy saints, O God
hymns.author: Ancient Hymns
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: For all thy saints, O God,
Who strove in Christ to live,
Who followed him, obeyed, adored,
Our grateful hymn receive.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: For all thy saints, O God,
Accept our thankful cry,
Who counted Christ their great reward,
And strove in him to die.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: They all, in life and death,
With him, their Lord in view,
Learned from thy Holy Spirit's breath
To suffer and to do.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: For this thy name we bless,
And humbly beg that we
May follow them in holiness,
And live and die in thee.

hymns.title: Open, Lord, my inward ear
hymns.author: Peculiar
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Open, Lord, my inward ear,
And bid my heart rejoice;
Bid my quiet spirit hear
The comfort of thy voice;
Never in the whirlwind found,
Or where earthquakes rock the place
Still and silent is the sound,
The whisper of thy grace.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: From the world of sin, and noise,
And hurry, I withdraw;
For the small and inward voice
I wait with humble awe;
Silent I am now and still,
Dare not in thy presence move;
To my waiting soul reveal
The secret of thy love.

hymns.title: When dread misfortune's tempests rise
hymns.author: H. Ballou, 2d
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When dread misfortune's tempests rise,
And roar through all the darkened skies,
Where shall the anxious pilgrim gain
A shelter from the wind and rain?
Within the covert of thy grace,
O Lord, there is a hiding-place,
Where, unconcerned, we hear the sound,
Though storm and tempest rage around.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: When, wandering o'er the desert bare
Of burning sands and sultry air,
We've sought the cheerless region through,
But found no stream to meet our view,--
'Tis then, the rivers of thy love,
Descending from thy throne above,
Supply our wants, and soothe our pain,
And raise our fainting souls again.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: When in a weary land we tire,
And our exhausted powers expire,
With toil, and care, and heat oppressed,
Where shall our languid spirits rest?
O, who could bear the blasting ray,
And all the burden of the day,
Did not a Rock in Zion stand,
O'ershading all this weary land!

hymns.title: All nature's works his praise declare
hymns.author: H. Ware
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: All nature's works his praise declare
To whom they all belong;
There is a voice in every star,
In every breeze a song.
Sweet music fills the world abroad
With strains of love and power;
The stormy sea sings praise to God--
The thunder and the shower.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: To God the tribes of ocean cry,
And birds upon the wing;
To God, the powers that dwell on high
Their tuneful tribute bring.
Like them let man the throne surround,
With them loud chorus raise,
While instruments of loftiest sound
Assist his feeble praise.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Great God! to thee we consecrate
Our voices and our skill;
We bid the pealing organ wait
To speak alone thy will.
Oh, teach its rich and swelling notes
To lift our souls on high;
And while the music round us floats,
Let earth-born passion die.

hymns.title: Not for the summer's hour alone
hymns.author: L. H. Sigourney
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Not for the summer's hour alone,
When skies resplendent shine,
And youth and pleasure fill the throne,
Our hearts and hands we join;

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: But for those stern and wintry days
Of sorrow, pain, and fear,
When Heaven's wise discipline doth make
Our earthly journey drear;--

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Not for this span of life alone,
Which like a blast doth fly,
And as the transient flowers of grass,
Just blossom, droop, and die;--

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: But for a being without end
This vow of love we take;
Grant us, O God, one home at last,
For thy great mercy's sake.

hymns.title: When on her Maker's bosom
hymns.author: Heber
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: When on her Maker's bosom
The new-born earth was laid,
And nature's opening blossom
Its fairest bloom displayed;
When all with fruits and flowers,
The laughing soil was dressed,
And Eden's fragrant bowers
Received their human guest,--

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: No sin his face defiling,
The heir of nature stood,
And God, benignly smiling,
Beheld that all was good.
Yet in that hour of blessing
A single want was known,--
A wish the heart distressing,--
For Adam was alone.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O God of pure affection,
By men and saints adored,
O, give us thy protection
Around this nuptial board.
May thy rich bounties ever
To wedded love be shown,
And no rude hand dissever
Whom thou hast linked in one.

hymns.title: O Thou, at whose dread name we bend
hymns.author: C. Sprague
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O Thou, at whose dread name we bend,
To whom our purest vows we pay,
God over all, in love descend,
And bless the labors of this day.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Our fathers here, a pilgrim band,
Fixed the proud empire of the free;
Art moved in gladness o'er the land,
And Faith her altars reared to thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Here, too, to guard, through every age,
The sacred rights their valor won,
They bade instruction spread her page,
And send down truth from sire to son.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Here still, through all succeeding time,
Their stores may truth and learning bring
And still the anthem-note sublime
To thee from children's children sing.

hymns.title: God of our fathers! from whose hand
hymns.author: J. G. Adams
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: God of our fathers! from whose hand
Came all our lights and blessings down,--
Who this devoted, favored land
Dost with thy choicest mercy crown!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: To Learning and to Knowledge reared--
We dedicate with prayer and praise
This edifice, to thee, revered
Above all gods, through endless days!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Accept the offering--deign to dwell
With thy confiding children here;
The shades of Ignorance dispel,--
In Truth's omnipotence appear!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Here through successive years may come
The youthful mind--fair Wisdom's guest;
Long be this house Instruction's home,
When those who reared it sink to rest.

hymns.title: Raise the adoring song
hymns.author: J. G. Adams
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Raise the adoring song!
Praises to God belong,
In this glad hour!
He who from worlds on high,
Spreads over earth and sky
Proofs of his majesty,
Goodness and power!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Praise, that Instruction's voice
Bids the young heart rejoice
In this fair land;
Praise, that the humblest mind
Wisdom's true light may find,
Ground on which all inclined
Freely may stand.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Source of all holiness!
With thy rich favor bless
This house of thine;
Here be true knowledge sought,
Here purest wisdom taught,
Wisdom with Freedom fraught,
Freedom divine!

hymns.title: Let monumental pillars rise
hymns.author: P. H. Sweetser
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Let monumental pillars rise
In majesty sublime--
Their granite columns shall decay
Before the touch of time.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: But mind, enlightened and refined,
Shall live beyond the sky,
And heavenly sciences explore,
When time itself shall die!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: A nobler monument we raise
Than costly marble pile--
A beacon light to lead the way
From ignorance and guile.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: This house, with prayer, O God, we give
To truth's supreme control;
To virtue and progressive thought,
The riches of the soul.

hymns.title: There is a pure and peaceful wave
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: There is a pure and peaceful wave,
That issues from the throne of love,
Whose waters gladden as they lave
The bright and heavenly courts above.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: In living streams behold that tide
Through Christ the rock profusely burst;
And in his word, behold supplied
The fount for which our spirits thirst.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The pilgrim faint, who seems to sink
Beneath the sultry sky of time,
May here repose, and freely drink
The waters of that better clime.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: And every soul may here partake
The blessings of the fount above;
And none who drink will e'er forsake
The crystal stream of boundless love.

hymns.title: Soldier, to the contest pressing
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Soldier, to the contest pressing,
Onward, let thy watchword be;
God upon thee pours his blessing;
What though man derideth thee!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Onward, though the fagot's burning
By thy pathway's only light;
Onward, death and danger spurning;
Onward in the path of right!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: God, for all thy wants providing,
Armor trusty hath for thee;
Gird thyself, in him confiding,
With the goodly panoply:

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Righteousness thy breast defending,
And thy feet with justice shod:
Onward; with the foe contending,
Wield thy sword, the word of God.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Thine the helmet of salvation,
Faith thy mighty shield shall be;
And let prayer and supplication,
Lance and glorious falchion be;

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: Onward then, with bold contending,
In the path the martyrs trod:
God to thee his strength is lending;
Onward, in the strength of God.

hymns.title: Eternal God, our humbled souls
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Eternal God, our humbled souls
Before thy presence bow;
With all thy wasting magazines,
How terrible art thou!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The flames thy messengers become,
And their destruction pour,
And that which we in strength had reared
Lies mouldered in an hour.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Within our pleasant places, Lord,
Destruction rears its head,
And blackened walls and smoking heaps
Along our streets are spread.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Lord, in this hour we come to thee,
With awe adore thy name;
Yet bless the hand of guardian love,
That snatched us from the flame.

hymns.title: Amid surrounding gloom and waste
hymns.author: E. H. Chapin
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Amid surrounding gloom and waste,
From nature's face we flee;
And in our fear and wonder haste
O nature's life, to thee!
Thy ways are in the mighty deep;
In tempests as they blow;
In floods that o'er our treasures sweep;
The lightning; and the snow.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Though earth upon its axis reels,
And heaven is veiled in wrath;
Not one of nature's million wheels
Breaks its appointed path;
Fixed in thy grasp, the sources meet
Of beauty and of awe;
In storm or calm, all pulses beat
True to the central law.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thou art that law, whose will thus done
In seeming wreck and blight,
Sends the calm planets round the sun,
And pours the moon's soft light.
We trust thy love; thou best dost know
The universal peace;
How long the stormy force should blow,
And when the flood should cease.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: And though around our path some form
Of mystery ever lies,
And life is like the calm and storm
That checker earth and skies,
Through all its mingling joy and dread,
Permit us, Holy One,
By faith to see the golden thread
Of thy great purpose run.

hymns.title: How are thy servants blest, O Lord
hymns.author: Addison
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: How are thy servants blest, O Lord!
How sure is their defence!
Eternal wisdom is their guide,
Their help omnipotence.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: In foreign realms, and lands remote,
Supported by thy care,
They pass unhurt through burning climes,
And breathe in tainted air.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thy mercy sweetens every soil,
Makes every region please;
The hoary, frozen hills it warms,
And smooths the boisterous seas.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: In midst of dangers, fears, and death,
Thy goodness I'll adore,
And praise thee for thy mercies past,
And humbly hope for more.

hymns.title: "Where'er my Gospel is proclaimed
hymns.author: H. Bacon
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: "Where'er my Gospel is proclaimed,
Through the long ages yet to be,
There shall this deed of love be named
Which she this hour hath done for me."

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lord, while our eyes on Mary rest,
And see the precious perfume poured,
With thrilling power our thoughts invest
The sacred record of thy word.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: We bring to God, in thy dear name,
The tribute of our grateful praise.
For many a deed, unknown to fame,
Where woman her true homage pays.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The wife, the mother, sister, friend,--
All holy may her influence be!
The sweetness of her kindness blend
With Temperance, Truth, and Charity.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Oh not a work is wrought in vain
Where love like Mary's fills the heart;
Memorials of that love remain,
A sacred influence to impart.

hymns.title: Bless, O bless, Almighty Father
hymns.author: J. G. Adams
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Bless, O bless, Almighty Father,
Woman's mission with our race,--
Her fond strivings here to gather
Fruits of thy redeeming grace.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Though her way be not where honor
Wins the gazing world's acclaim,
Yet we bless thee that upon her
Rests the power of Jesus' name.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: In that name, O Father, strengthen
Her full heart and ready hands;
May her efforts serve to lengthen
Christian love's encircling bands.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Where the mourning and the needy
And the suffering faint and die,
Be her presence sure and speedy,
Mercy's blessings to supply.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Where old error's words are spoken,
Be truth's witness by her given,
Till, the spell of bondage broken,
Earth redeemed resembles heaven.

hymns.title: Walk in the light! so shalt thou know
hymns.author: Barton
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Walk in the light! so shalt thou know
That fellowship of love,
His Spirit only can bestow,
Who reigns in light above.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Walk in the light! and thou shalt own
Thy darkness passed away,
Because that light hath on thee shone
In which is perfect day.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Walk in the light! and e'en the tomb
No fearful shade shall wear;
Glory shall chase away its gloom,
For Christ hath conquered there!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Walk in the light! and thine shall be
A path, though thorny,--bright:
For God, by grace, shall dwell in thee,
And God himself is light!

hymns.title: The past is dark with sin and shame
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The past is dark with sin and shame,
The future dim with doubt and fear;
But, Father, yet we praise thy name,
Whose guardian love is always near!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: For man has striven ages long
With faltering steps to come to thee,
And in each purpose high and strong
The influence of thy grace could see.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: He could not breathe an earnest prayer
But thou wert kinder than he dreamed,
As age by age brought hopes more fair,
And nearer still thy kingdom seemed.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: But never rose within his breast,
A trust so calm and deep as now;
Shall not the weary find a rest?
Father! Preserver! answer thou!

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: 'Tis dark around, 'tis dark above,
But through the shadow streams the sun;
We cannot doubt thy certain love,
And man's great aim shall yet be won!

hymns.title: Know, my soul, thy full salvation
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Know, my soul, thy full salvation;
Rise o'er sin, and fear, and care;
Joy to find, in every station,
Something still to do, or bear.
Think what spirit dwells within thee;
Think what Father's smiles are thine;
Think what Jesus did to win thee;--
Child of heaven! canst thou repine?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Haste thee on from grace to glory,
Armed with faith, and winged with prayer;
Heaven's eternal day's before thee,
God's own hand shall guide thee there:
Soon shall cease thine earthly mission,
Soon shall pass thy pilgrim days;
Hope shall change to glad fruition,
Faith to sight, and prayer to praise.

hymns.title: Great Source of life and light
hymns.author: Wesleyan
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Great Source of life and light,
Thy heavenly grace impart,
And by thy holy spirit write
Thy law upon my heart.
My soul would cleave to thee;
Let nought my purpose move;
O, let my faith more steadfast be,
And more intense my love!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Long as my trials last,
Long as the cross I bear,
O, let my soul on thee be cast
In confidence and prayer!
Conduct me to the shore
Of everlasting peace,
Where storm and tempest rise no more,
Where sin and sorrow cease.

hymns.title: Theories, which thousands cherish
hymns.author: Waterston
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Theories, which thousands cherish,
Pass like clouds that sweep the sky;
Creeds and dogmas all may perish;
Truth herself can never die.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: From the glorious heavens above her,
She has shed her beams abroad,
That the souls who truly love her,
May become the sons of God.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Worldlings blindly may refuse her,
Close their eyes and call it night;
Learned scoffers may abuse her,
But they cannot quench her light!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thrones may totter, empires crumble,
All their glories cease to be;
While she, Christ-like, crowns the humble,
And from bondage sets them free.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: God himself will e'er defend her
From the fury of her foe,
Till she, in her native splendor,
Sits enthroned o'er all below.

hymns.title: In the morning sow thy seed
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: In the morning sow thy seed,
Nor at eve withhold thy hand;
Who can tell which may succeed,
Or if both alike shall stand,
And a glorious harvest bear,
To reward the sower's care?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Sow it 'mid the haunts of vice--
Scenes of infamy and crime;
Suddenly, may Paradise
Burst, as in the northern clime
Spring, with all its verdant race,
Starts from Winter's cold embrace.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Sow it with unsparing hand;
'Tis the kingdom's precious seed,
'Tis the Master's great command,
And his grace shall crown the deed;
He hath said, the precious grain
Never shall be sown in vain.

hymns.title: Death moves with victor's tread
hymns.author: J. G. Adams
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Death moves with victor's tread
In our high places, Lord!
The honorable dead
We mourn with one accord;
Our souls, oppressed, before thee bow,
Heed thou the prayer, accept the vow.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: While thus we feel the rod
Of thine afflictive love,
Teach us, our fathers' God,
Thy justice to approve.
Though all thy ways we cannot trace,
May we not doubt thy guardian grace.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O keep us in thy hand,
A chosen race for thee;
And make our own loved land
The true home of the free;
Where sin shall cease, and righteousness
Forever dwell, forever bless.

hymns.title: The Lord is on his holy throne
hymns.author: Mrs. Sigourney
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The Lord is on his holy throne,
He sits in kingly state;
Let those who for his favors seek,
In humble silence wait.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: True prayer is not th' imposing sound
That clamorous lips repeat;
But the deep silence of a soul
That clasps Jehovah's feet.

hymns.title: Glory to God on high
hymns.author: Unknown
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #20476 - Hymns for Christian Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Glory to God on high!
Forever bless his name;
Let earth, and seas, and sky
His wondrous love proclaim;
To him be praise
And glory given
By all on earth,
And all in heaven.

hymns.title: To prayer, to prayer!—for the morning breaks
hymns.author: H. Ware
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: To prayer, to prayer!—for the morning breaks,
And earth in her Maker’s smile awakes;
His light is on all below and above,
The light of gladness, and life, and love:
O, then, on the breath of this early air,
Send upward the incense of grateful prayer.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: To prayer!—for the day that God has blest
Comes tranquilly on with its solemn rest;
It speaks of creation’s early bloom,—
It speaks of the Prince who burst the tomb:
Then summon the spirit’s exalted powers,
And devote to heaven the hallowed hours.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Awake, awake, and gird up thy strength,
To join Christ’s holy band at length;
To Him who unceasing love displays,
Whom the powers of nature unceasingly praise;
To Him thy heart and thy hours be given,
For a life of prayer is the life of heaven.

hymns.title: Come, ye who love the Lord
hymns.author: *Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Come, ye who love the Lord!
And let your joys be known;
Join in a song with sweet accord,
And thus surround His throne.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The sorrows of the mind
Be banished from this place!
Religion never was designed
To make our pleasures less.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The sons of God have found
That heaven begins below:
Celestial fruits, on earthly ground,
From faith and hope may grow.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Then let our sorrows cease,
And every tear be dry;
We’re travelling through the paths of peace
To fairer worlds on high.

hymns.title: Be still! be still! for all around
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Be still! be still! for all around,
On either hand, is holy ground:
Here in His house, the Lord to-day
Will listen, while His people pray.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thou, tost upon the waves of care,
Ready to sink with deep despair,
Here ask relief, with heart sincere,
And thou shalt find that God is here.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thou who hast laid within the grave
Those whom thou hadst no power to save,
Believe their spirits now are near,
For angels wait while God is here.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thou who hast dear ones far away,
In foreign lands, ’mid ocean’s spray,
Pray for them now, and dry the tear,
And trust the God who listens here.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Thou who art mourning o’er thy sin,
Deploring guilt that reigns within,
The God of peace is ever near;
The troubled spirit meets Him here.

hymns.title: Thirsting for a living spring
hymns.author: Unknown
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Thirsting for a living spring,
Seeking for a higher home,
Resting where our souls must cling,
Trusting, hoping, Lord, we come.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Glorious hopes our spirit fill,
When we feel that Thou art near:
Father! then our fears are still,
Then the soul’s bright end is clear.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Life’s hard conflict we would win,
Read the meaning of life’s frown;
Change the thorn-bound wreath of sin
For the spirit’s starry crown.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Make us beautiful within
By Thy spirit’s holy light:
Guard us when our faith burns dim,
Father of all love and might!

hymns.title: Unto Thy temple, God of love
hymns.author: Gaskell
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Unto Thy temple, God of love,
Once more we come with willing feet,
To raise our thoughts this world above,
And Thy paternal blessing meet.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: May all Thy purest presence feel,
And silent keep each vain desire;
With humble hearts before Thee kneel,
And unto holier strength aspire.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: May all be bound in bonds more true
To Thee, who art our life and light,
That through each path which we pursue,
We still may keep Thy love in sight.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: And may we, when the day shall close,
Review its course without a fear;
And, nearer heaven than when it rose,
Feel it is good to have been here.

hymns.title: Lord! what offering shall we bring
hymns.author: J. Taylor
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Lord! what offering shall we bring,
At Thine altars when we bow?
Hearts, the pure, unsullied spring,
Whence the kind affections flow:

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Willing hands, to lead the blind,
Heal the wounded, feed the poor;
Love, embracing all our kind,
Charity, with liberal store.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Teach us, O thou Heavenly King,
Thus to show our grateful mind,
Thus the accepted offering bring,
Love to Thee and all mankind.

hymns.title: Here, in this place of prayer
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Here, in this place of prayer,
Father! Thy face we seek:
Grant us that blessed peace to share,
Known to the pure and meek.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Come then to holy prayer,
Souls that seem lost in night;
Leave to the Lord your heavy care,
Who giveth life and light.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Come with a trusting prayer,
Though fears press down your souls;
The Saviour’s promise, “I am there,”
Each saddening fear controls.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Here, in this place of prayer,
Let hearts in union meet:
Come, cast the load you cannot bear
Low at the Saviour’s feet.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Then from this house of prayer
Shall mingling praise be given,
And angels, ’mid the holy air,
Shall bear the notes to heaven.

hymns.title: The offerings to Thy throne which rise
hymns.author: Bowring
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The offerings to Thy throne which rise,
Of mingled praise and prayer,
Are but a worthless sacrifice,
Unless the heart be there.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Upon Thine all-discerning ear
Let no vain words intrude;
No tribute but the vow sincere,—
The tribute of the good.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Our offerings will indeed be blest,
If sanctified by Thee;
If Thy pure spirit touch the breast
With its own purity.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O, may that spirit warm each heart
To piety and love,
And to life’s lowly vale impart
Some rays from heaven above.

hymns.title: Our God is a spirit, and they who aright
hymns.author: Barton
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Our God is a spirit, and they who aright
Would offer the worship He loveth,
In the heart’s holy temple will seek with delight
The spirit the Father approveth.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: This, this is the worship the Saviour made known
When she of Samaria found him
By the Patriarch’s well, sitting weary, alone,
With the stillness of noontide around him.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: He having once entered hath shown us the way,
O God! how to worship before Thee,
Not with the vain forms of that earlier day,
But in spirit and truth to adore Thee.

hymns.title: Great God! the followers of Thy Son
hymns.author: H. Ware
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Great God! the followers of Thy Son,
We bow before Thy mercy-seat,
To worship Thee, the Holy One,
And pour our wishes at Thy feet.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O, grant Thy blessing here to-day!
O, give Thy people joy and peace!
The tokens of Thy love display,
And favor, that shall never cease.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: We seek the truth which Jesus brought;
His path of light we long to tread;
Here be his holy doctrines taught,
And here their purest influence shed.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: May faith, and hope, and love abound;
Our sins and errors be forgiven;
And we, from day to day, be found
Children of God, and heirs of heaven.

hymns.title: O Holy Father! ’mid the calm
hymns.author: W. H. Burleigh
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O Holy Father! ’mid the calm
And stillness of this evening hour,
We would lift up our solemn psalm
To praise Thy goodness, and Thy power
For over us, and over all,
Thy tender mercies still extend,
Nor vainly shall Thy children call
On Thee, our Father and our Friend!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Kept by Thy goodness though the day,
Thanksgiving to Thy name we pour;
Night o’er us, with its stars,—we pray
Thy love, to guard us evermore!
In grief, console; in gladness, bless;
In darkness, guide; in sickness, cheer;
Till, in the Saviour’s righteousness,
Before Thy throne our souls appear!

hymns.title: Teach us to pray
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Teach us to pray!
O Father, we look up to Thee,
And this our one request shall be,
Teach us to pray!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Teach us to pray!
A form of words will not suffice,
The heart must bring its sacrifice:
Teach us to pray!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Teach us to pray!
To whom shall we Thy children turn?
Teach Thou the lesson we would learn,
Teach us to pray!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Teach us to pray!
To Thee alone our hearts look up,
Prayer is our only door of hope,
Teach us to pray!

hymns.title: Father and God of all mankind
hymns.author: C. Wesley
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father and God of all mankind,
And all the hosts above,
Let every understanding mind
Unite to praise Thy love.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Thy kingdom come, with power and grace,
To every heart of man;
Thy peace, and joy, and righteousness,
In all our bosoms reign.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The righteousness that never ends,
But makes an end of sin;
The joy that human thought transcends,
Now to our souls bring in:

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The kingdom of established peace,
Which can no more remove;
The perfect powers of godliness,
The omnipotence of Love.

hymns.title: Day by day the manna fell
hymns.author: *Conder
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Day by day the manna fell;
O, to learn this lesson well!
Still by constant mercy fed,
Give us, Lord, our daily bread.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: “Day by day,” the promise reads;
Daily strength for daily needs;
Cast foreboding fears away;
Take the manna of to-day.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Lord, our times are in Thy hand;
All our sanguine hopes have planned
To Thy wisdom we resign,
And would mould our wills to Thine.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Thou our daily task shalt give;
Day by day to Thee we live;
So shall added years fulfil
Not our own, our Father’s will.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: O, to live exempt from care,
By the energy of prayer;
Strong in faith, with mind subdued,
Glowing yet with gratitude!

hymns.title: God of all grace, we come to Thee
hymns.author: *Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: God of all grace, we come to Thee,
With humble, prayerful hearts;
Give what Thine eye delights to see,
Truth in the inward parts:

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Give deep humility; the sense
Of holy sorrow give:
A strong, desiring confidence,
To hear Thy voice and live:

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Patience, to watch, and wait, and weep,
Though mercy long delay;
Courage, our fainting souls to keep,
And trust Thee, though Thou slay.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Give these,—and then Thy will be done!
Thus strengthened with all might,
We, in the spirit of Thy Son,
Shall pray, and pray aright.

hymns.title: What shall we ask of God in prayer
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: What shall we ask of God in prayer?
Whatever good we want;
Whatever man may seek to share,
Or God in wisdom grant.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Father of all our mercies,—Thou
In whom we move and live!
Hear us in heaven, Thy dwelling, now,
And answer, and forgive.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: When harassed by ten thousand foes,
Our helplessness we feel,
O, give the weary soul repose,
The wounded spirit heal!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: When dire temptations gather round,
And threaten or allure,
By storm or calm, in Thee be found
A refuge strong and sure.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: When age advances, may we grow
In faith, in hope, and love;
And walk in holiness below
To holiness above.

hymns.title: Father, in Thy mysterious presence kneeling
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father, in Thy mysterious presence kneeling,
Fain would our souls feel all Thy kindling love;
For we are weak, and need some deep revealing
Of Trust and Strength and Calmness from above.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lord, we have wandered forth through doubt and sorrow,
And Thou hast made each step an onward one;
And we will ever trust each unknown morrow,—
Thou wilt sustain us till its work is done.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: In the heart’s depths a peace serene and holy
Abides, and when pain seems to have her will,
Or we despair,—O, may that peace rise slowly,
Stronger than agony, and we be still!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Now, Father, now, in Thy dear presence kneeling,
Our spirits yearn to feel Thy kindling love;
Now make us strong, we need Thy deep revealing
Of Trust and Strength and Calmness from above.

hymns.title: O God! who knowest how frail we are
hymns.author: Gaskell
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O God! who knowest how frail we are,
How soon the thought of good departs;
We pray that Thou wouldst feed the fount
Of holy yearning in our hearts.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Let not the choking cares of earth
The precious springs of life o’ergrow;
But, ever guarded by Thy love,
Still purer may their waters flow.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: To Thee, with sweeter hope and trust,
Be every day our spirits given;
And may we, while we walk on earth,
Walk more as citizens of heaven.

hymns.title: From every fear and doubt, O Lord
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: From every fear and doubt, O Lord,
In mercy set us free,
While in the confidence of prayer
Our hearts draw near to Thee!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: In all our trials, struggles, joys,
Teach us Thy love to see,
Which by the discipline of life
Would draw us unto Thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Our lives, devoted to Thy will,
Our sacrifice shall be,
And then will death, whene’er it come,
But draw us nearer Thee.

hymns.title: Father of light! conduct our feet
hymns.author: Smart
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father of light! conduct our feet
Through life’s dark, dangerous road;
Let each advancing step still bring
Us nearer to our God.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Let heaven-eyed prudence be our guide;
And when we go astray,
Recall our feet from folly’s paths
To wisdom’s better way.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: That heavenly wisdom from above
Abundantly impart;
And let it guard, and guide, and warm,
And penetrate each heart;

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Till it shall lead us to Thyself,
Fountain of bliss and love!
And all our darkness be dispersed
In endless light above.

hymns.title: O Father! lift our souls above
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O Father! lift our souls above,
Till we find rest in Thy dear love;
And still that Peace divine impart
Which sanctifies the inmost heart,
And makes each morn and setting sun
But bring us nearer to Thy throne.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: May we our daily duties meet,
Tread sin each day beneath our feet,
And win that strength which doth Thy will
And seeth Thee, and so is still;
And fixed on Thy sustaining arm,
Find daily food and know no harm.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Help us with man in peace to live,
Our brother’s wrong in love forgive,
And day and night the tempter flee
Through strength which comes alone from Thee!
Thus will our spirits find their rest,
In Thy deep Peace forever blest.

hymns.title: Father, who art on high
hymns.author: Mrs. Hemans
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father, who art on high!
Weak is the melody
Of harp or song to reach Thy gracious ear,
Unless the heart be there,
Winging the words of prayer
With its own fervent faith, or suppliant tear.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: O, let Thy spirit move
O’er those who bend in love,
Be Thou amidst them as a heavenly guest;
So shall our cry have power
To win from Thee a shower
Of healing gifts for every wounded breast.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O, let Thy breath once more
Within the soul restore
Thine own first image, Holiest and Most High!
As a clear lake is filled
With hues of heaven instilled,
Down to the depths of its calm purity.

hymns.title: Father in heaven, to whom our hearts
hymns.author: H. Ware, Jr
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father in heaven, to whom our hearts
Would lift themselves in prayer,
Drive from our souls each earthly thought,
And show Thy presence there.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Each moment of our lives renews
The mercies of the Lord;
Each moment is itself a gift
To bear us on to God.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Help us to break the galling chains
This world has round us thrown;
Each passion of our hearts subdue,
Each cherished sin disown.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: O Father! kindle in our souls
A never-dying flame
Of holy love, of grateful trust,
In Thine almighty name.

hymns.title: Father, to us Thy children, humbly kneeling
hymns.author: *J. F. Clarke
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father, to us Thy children, humbly kneeling,
Conscious of weakness, ignorance, sin and shame,
Give such a force of holy thought and feeling,
That we may live to glorify Thy name:

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: That we may conquer base desire and passion
That we may rise from selfish thought and will,
O’ercome the world’s allurement, threat, and fashion,
Walk humbly, gently, leaning on Thee still.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Let all Thy goodness by our minds be seen,
Let all Thy mercy on our souls be sealed,
Lord, if Thou wilt, Thy power can make us clean,
O, speak the word! Thy servants shall be healed.

hymns.title: “Lord, Thy glory fills the heaven
hymns.author: Ancient Hymns
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: “Lord, Thy glory fills the heaven;
Earth is with its fulness stored;
Unto Thee be glory given,
Holy, holy, holy Lord!”
Heaven is still with anthems ringing;
Earth takes up the angels’ cry,
“Holy, holy, holy,” singing,
“Lord of hosts, the Lord most High!”

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Ever thus in God’s high praises,
Brethren, let our tongues unite,
Whilst our thoughts His greatness raises,
And our love His gifts excite.
With His seraph train before Him,
With His holy church below,
Thus unite we to adore Him,
Bid we thus our anthem flow:—

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: “Lord, Thy glory fills the heaven;
Earth is with its fulness stored;
Unto Thee be glory given,
Holy, holy, holy Lord!
Thus, Thy glorious name confessing,
We adopt the angels’ cry,
‘Holy, holy, holy,’—blessing
Thee, the Lord our God most High!”

hymns.title: Songs of praise the angels sang
hymns.author: *Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Songs of praise the angels sang,
Heaven with hallelujahs rang,
When, Creation’s work begun,
Spake the Lord, and it was done.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Songs of praise awoke the morn,
When the Prince of Peace was born;
Songs of praise arose, when he
Captive led captivity.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Heaven and earth must pass away,
Songs of praise shall crown that day;
God will make new heavens and earth,
Songs of praise shall hail their birth.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: And shall man alone be dumb,
Till that glorious kingdom come?
No; his heart delights to raise
Psalms and hymns and songs of praise.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Saints below, with heart and voice,
Still in songs of praise rejoice,
Learning here, by faith and love,
Songs of praise to sing above.

hymns.title: Holy, holy, holy Lord God Almighty
hymns.author: Heber
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Holy, holy, holy Lord God Almighty!
Early in the morning our song shall rise to Thee;
Holy, holy, holy! merciful and mighty!
All Thy works shall praise Thy name in earth, sky, and sea.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Holy, holy, holy! all the saints adore Thee,
Casting down their golden crowns around the glassy sea;
Cherubim and seraphim falling down before Thee,
Thou who wast, and art, and evermore shalt be!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Holy, holy, holy! Though the darkness hide Thee,
Though the eye of sinful man Thy glory may not see,
Only Thou art holy, there is none beside Thee,
Infinite in power, in love, and purity!

hymns.title: Father of Spirits! humbly bent before Thee
hymns.author: *Bowring
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father of Spirits! humbly bent before Thee,
Songs of glad homage unto Thee we bring;
Touched by Thy spirit, O, teach us to adore Thee;
Let Thy light attend us,
Let Thy love befriend us,
Father of our spirits, Everlasting King!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Send forth Thy mandate, gather in the nations,
Through the wide universe Thy name be known,
Millions of voices shall join in adorations,
Every soul invited,
Every voice united,
Joining to adore Thee, Everlasting One!

hymns.title: My God! in morning’s radiant hour
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: My God! in morning’s radiant hour
To Thee will I lift up my heart;
The shades of night obey Thy power,
And at Thy sun’s bright beams depart.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Father and Guardian! to Thy shrine
The life Thou shieldest will I bring;
All, great Creator! all is Thine;
The heart my noblest offering!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The morning light shall see my prayer,
The noonday calm shall know my praise;
And evening’s still and fragrant air
My grateful hymn to Thee shall raise.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: So shall sweet thoughts and hopes sublime
My constant inspirations be;
And every shifting scene of time
Reflect, my God, a light from Thee!

hymns.title: Father! glory be to Thee
hymns.author: Gaskell
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father! glory be to Thee,
Source of all the good we see!
Glory for the blessed Light
Rising on the ancient night!
Glory for the hopes that come
Streaming through the silent tomb!
Glory for Thy spirit given,
Guiding us in peace to heaven!

hymns.title: Israel’s Shepherd, guide us, feed us
hymns.author: Bickersteth
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Israel’s Shepherd, guide us, feed us,
Through our pilgrimage below,
And beside the waters lead us,
Where Thy flock rejoicing go.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Lord, Thy guardian, presence ever,
Meekly kneeling, we implore;
We have found Thee, and would never,
Never wander from Thee more.

hymns.title: As every day Thy mercy spares
hymns.author: Chr. Psalmist
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: As every day Thy mercy spares
Will bring its trials or its cares,
O Father, till my life shall end,
Be Thou my counsellor and friend!
Teach me Thy statutes all divine,
And let Thy will be always mine!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: When each day’s scenes and labors close,
And wearied nature seeks repose,
With pardoning mercy, richly blest,
Guard me, my Father, while I rest;
And as each morning sun shall rise,
O, lead me onward to the skies!

hymns.title: Peace of God, which knows no measure
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Peace of God, which knows no measure,
Heavenly sunlight of the soul,
Peace beyond all earthly treasure,
Come and all our hearts control!
Come, almighty to deliver!
Naught shall make us then afraid;
We will trust in Thee forever,
Thou on whom our hope is stayed!

hymns.title: Guide us, Lord! while, hand in hand
hymns.author: Beard’s Coll
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Guide us, Lord! while, hand in hand,
Journeying toward the better land;
Foes we know are to be met,
Snares the pilgrim’s path beset;
Clouds upon the valley rest,
Rough and dark the mountain’s breast;
And our home may not be gained,
Save through trials well sustained.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Guide us, while we onward move,
Linked in closest bonds of love,
Striving for the holy mind,
And the soul from sense refined;
That when life no longer burns,
And the dust to dust returns,
With the strength which Thou hast given
We may rise to Thee and heaven.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: God of mercy! on Thee, all
Humbly for Thy guidance call;
Save us from the evil tongue,
From the heart that thinketh wrong,
From the sins, whate’er they be,
That divide the soul from Thee.
God of grace! on Thee we rest;
Bless us, and we shall be blest.

hymns.title: From all that dwell below the skies
hymns.author: Watts
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: From all that dwell below the skies
Let the Creator’s praise arise!
Let the Redeemer’s name be sung,
Through every land, by every tongue!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Eternal are Thy mercies, Lord!
Eternal truth attends Thy word;
Thy praise shall sound from shore to shore,
Till suns shall rise and set no more.

hymns.title: Go in peace!—serene dismission
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Go in peace!—serene dismission
To the loving heart made known,
When it pours in deep contrition
Prayer before the eternal throne.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Go in peace! thy sins forgiven,
Christ hath healed thee, set thee free
Every spirit-fetter riven,
Go in peace, and liberty!

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Saviour! breathe this benediction
O’er our spirits while we pray;
Let us part in sweet conviction
Thou hast blessed our souls to-day.

hymns.title: Through all this life’s eventful road
hymns.author: Gaskell
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Through all this life’s eventful road,
Fain would I walk with Thee, my God,
And make Thy presence light around,
And every step on holy ground.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Each blessing would I trace to Thee,
In every grief Thy mercy see;
And through the paths of duty move,
Conscious of Thine encircling love.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And when the angel Death stands by,
Be this my strength, that Thou art nigh;
And this my joy, that I shall be
With those who dwell in light with Thee.

hymns.title: Thy presence, ever living God
hymns.author: Doddridge
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Thy presence, ever living God!
Wide through all nature spreads abroad;
Thy watchful eyes, which never sleep,
In every place Thy children keep.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: While near each other we remain,
Thou dost our lives and powers sustain;
When separate, we rejoice to share
Thy counsels, and Thy gracious care.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: To Thee we now commit our ways,
And still implore Thy heavenly grace;
Still cause Thy face on us to shine,
And guard and guide us still as Thine.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Give us within Thy house to raise
Again united songs of praise;
Or, if that joy no more be known,
Give us to meet around Thy throne.

hymns.title: God of our fathers! by whose hand
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: God of our fathers! by whose hand
Thy people still are blest,
Be with us through our pilgrimage,
Conduct us to our rest.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Through each perplexing path of life
Our wandering footsteps guide;
Give us each day our daily bread,
And raiment fit provide.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: O, spread Thy sheltering wings around,
Till all our wanderings cease,
And at our Fathers loved abode
Our souls arrive in peace.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: To Thee, our Father and our God,
We our whole souls resign;
And thankful own, that all we are
And all we have is Thine.

hymns.title: As the sun’s enlivening eye
hymns.author: *Newton
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: As the sun’s enlivening eye
Shines on every place the same;
So the Lord is always nigh
To the souls that love His name.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: When they move at duty’s call,
He is with them by the way;
He is ever with them all,
Those who go and those who stay.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: From His holy mercy-seat
Nothing can their souls confine;
Still in spirit they may meet,
And in sweet communion join.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Father, hear our humble prayer!
Tender Shepherd of Thy sheep,
Let Thy mercy and Thy care
All our souls in safety keep.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: In Thy strength may we be strong;
Sanctify each cross and pain;
Give us, if Thou wilt, ere long
Here to meet in peace again.

hymns.title: Unchangeable, all-perfect Lord
hymns.author: Father!
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Unchangeable, all-perfect Lord!
Essential life’s unbounded sea!
What lives and moves, lives by Thy word;
It lives and moves and is, from Thee!
Whate’er in earth, or sea, or sky,
Or shuns, or meets, the wandering thought,
Escapes, or strikes, the searching eye,
By Thee was to existence brought.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: High is Thy power above all height,
Whate’er Thy will decrees is done;
Thy wisdom, holiness and might
Can by no finite mind be known.
What our dim eyes could never see,
Is plain and naked in Thy sight;
What thickest darkness veils, to Thee
Shines clearly as the morning light;

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Thine, Lord, is holiness, alone:
Justice and Truth before Thee stand:
Yet, nearer to Thy sacred throne,
Love ever dwells at Thy right hand.
And to Thy love and ceaseless care,
Father! this light, this breath, we owe;
And all we have, and all we are
From Thee, great Source of Life! doth flow.

hymns.title: Mighty God! the first, the last
hymns.author: M. Gaskell
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Mighty God! the first, the last!
What are ages, in Thy sight,
But as yesterday when past,
Or a watch within the night?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: All that being ever knew,
Far, far back, ere time had birth,
Stands as clear within Thy view
As the present things of earth.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: All that being e’er shall know
On, still on, through farthest years,
All eternity can show,
Bright before Thee now appears.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: In Thine all-embracing sight,
Every change its purpose meets,
Every cloud floats into light,
Every woe its glory greets.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Whatsoe’er our lot may be,
Calmly in this thought we’ll rest,—
Could we see as Thou dost see,
We should choose it as the best.

hymns.title: I cannot always trace the way
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I cannot always trace the way
Where Thou, almighty One, dost move,
But I can always, always say
That God is love.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: When fear her chilling mantle throws
O’er earth, my soul to heaven above,
As to her native home, upsprings,
For God is love.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: When mystery clouds my darkened path,
I’ll check my dread, my doubts reprove;
In this my soul sweet comfort hath,
That God is love.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Yes, God is love;—a thought like this
Can every gloomy thought remove,
And turn all tears, all woes, to bliss,
For God is love.

hymns.title: O Source divine, and Life of all
hymns.author: Sterling
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: O Source divine, and Life of all,
The Fount of being’s wondrous sea!
Thy depth would every heart appall,
That saw not Love supreme in Thee.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: We shrink before Thy vast abyss,
Where worlds on worlds eternal brood;
We know Thee truly but in this,
That Thou bestowest all our good.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And so, ’mid boundless time and space,
O, grant us still in Thee to dwell,
And through the ceaseless web to trace
Thy presence working all things well!

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Nor let Thou life’s delightful play
Thy truth’s transcendent vision hide;
Nor strength and gladness lead astray
From Thee, our nature’s only guide.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Bestow on every joyous thrill
Thy deeper tone of reverent awe;
Make pure Thy children’s erring will,
And teach their hearts to love Thy law!

hymns.title: No human eyes Thy face may see
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: No human eyes Thy face may see;
No human thought Thy form may know;
But all creation dwells in Thee,
And Thy great life through all doth flow!

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: And yet, O strange and wondrous thought!
Thou art a God who hearest prayer,
And every heart with sorrow fraught
To seek Thy present aid may dare.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And though most weak our efforts seem
Into one creed these thoughts to bind,
And vain the intellectual dream,
To see and know the Eternal Mind,—

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Yet Thou wilt turn them not aside,
Who cannot solve Thy life divine,
But would give up all reason’s pride
To know their hearts approved by Thine.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: And Thine unceasing love gave birth
To our dear Lord, Thy holy Son,
Who left a perfect proof on earth,
That Duty, Love, and Truth are one.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: So, though we faint on life’s dark hill,
And Thought grow weak, and Knowledge flee,
Yet Faith shall teach us courage still,
And Love shall guide us on to Thee!

hymns.title: Father! there is no change to live with Thee
hymns.author: Jones Very
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father! there is no change to live with Thee;
Save that in Christ I grow from day to day;
In each new word I hear, each thing I see,
I but rejoicing hasten on my way.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: The morning comes, with blushes overspread,
And I, new-wakened, find a morn within;
And in its modest dawn around me shed,
Thou hear’st the prayer and the ascending hymn.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Hour follows hour, the lengthening shades descend;
Yet they could never reach as far as me.
Did not Thy love its kind protection lend,
That I, Thy child, might sleep in peace with Thee.

hymns.title: Father! Thy wonders do not singly stand
hymns.author: Jones Very
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Father! Thy wonders do not singly stand,
Nor far removed where feet have seldom strayed;
Around us ever lies the enchanted land,
In marvels rich to Thine own sons displayed.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: In finding Thee are all things round us found!
In losing Thee are all things lost beside!
Ears have we, but in vain sweet voices sound,
And to our eyes the vision is denied.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Open our eyes that we that world may see!
Open our ears that we Thy voice may hear!
And in the spirit-land may ever be,
And feel Thy presence with us always near;

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: No more to wander ’mid the things of time,
No more to suffer death or earthly change;
But-with the Christian’s joy and faith sublime,
Through all Thy vast, eternal scenes to range.

hymns.title: God, in the high and holy place
hymns.author: Montgomery
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: God, in the high and holy place,
Looks down upon the spheres;
Yet in His providence and grace
To every eye appears.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: He bows the heavens; the mountains stand,
A highway for our God:
He walks amidst the desert-land;
’Tis Eden where He trod.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The forests in His strength rejoice;
Hark! on the evening breeze,
As once of old, the Lord God’s voice
Is heard among the trees.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: In every stream His bounty flows,
Diffusing joy and wealth;
In every breeze His Spirit blows,—
The breath of life and health.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: His blessings fall in plenteous showers
Upon the lap of earth,
That teems with foliage, fruits, and flowers,
And rings with infant mirth.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: If God hath made this world so fair,
Where sin and death abound,
How beautiful beyond compare
Will Paradise be found!

hymns.title: There’s nothing bright, above, below
hymns.author: Moore
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: There’s nothing bright, above, below,
From flowers that bloom to stars that glow,
But in its light my soul can see
Some feature of the Deity.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: There’s nothing dark, below, above,
But in its gloom I trace Thy love,
And meekly wait the moment when
Thy touch shall make all bright again.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The heavens, the earth, where’er I look,
Shall be one pure and shining book,
Where I may read, in words of flame,
The glories of Thy wondrous name.

hymns.title: The ocean looketh up to heaven
hymns.author: Whittier
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: The ocean looketh up to heaven,
As ’twere a living thing;
The homage of its waves is given,
In ceaseless worshipping.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: They kneel upon the sloping sand
As bends the human knee;
A beautiful and tireless band,
The priesthood of the sea.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: The mists are lifted from the rills,
Like the white wing of prayer;
They kneel above the ancient hills,
As doing homage there.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: The forest-tops are lowly cast
O’er breezy hill and glen,
As if a prayerful spirit passed
On nature as on men.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: The sky is as a temple’s arch:
The blue and wavy air
Is glorious with the spirit march
Of messengers at prayer.

hymns.title: My God! all nature owns Thy sway
hymns.author: H. M. Williams
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: My God! all nature owns Thy sway;
Thou giv’st the night and Thou the day:
When all Thy loved creation wakes,
When morning, rich in lustre, breaks,
And bathes in dew the opening flower,
To Thee we owe her fragrant hour;
And when she pours her choral song,
Her melodies to Thee belong.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Or when, in paler tints arrayed,
The evening slowly spreads her shade,
That soothing shade, that grateful gloom,
Can, more than day’s enlivening bloom,
Still every fond and vain desire,
And calmer, purer thoughts inspire;
From earth the longing spirit free,
And lead the softened heart to Thee.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: As o’er Thy work the seasons roll,
And soothe, with change of bliss, the soul,
O, never may their smiling train
Pass o’er the human sense in vain!
But, oft as on their charms we gaze,
Attune the wondering soul to praise;
And be the joys that most we prize
The joys that from Thy favor rise!

hymns.title: God of the ocean, earth, and sky
hymns.author: Wreford
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: God of the ocean, earth, and sky,
In Thy bright presence we rejoice;
We feel Thee, see Thee ever nigh,
And gladly hear Thy gracious voice.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: We feel Thee in the sunny beam;
We see Thee walk the mountain waves;
We hear Thee in the murmuring stream,
And when the tempest wildly raves.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: God on the lonely hills we meet,
God, in the vale and fragrant grove,
While birds and whispering winds repeat,
That God is there,—the God of love.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: We meet Thee in the pensive hour
When wearied nature sinks to rest;
When dies the breeze, and sleeps the flower
And peace is given to every breast.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: We see Thee when, at eve, afar
We upward lift our wondering sight,
We see Thee in each silent star
That glorifies the gloom of night.

verses.label: Verse 6
lines: But better still, and still more clear,
Thee in Thy holy Son we see;
There Thy own glorious words we hear,
And learn the way to heaven and Thee.

hymns.title: Though wandering in a stranger-land
hymns.author: Fox’s Hymns
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Though wandering in a stranger-land,
Though on the waste no altar stand,
Take comfort! thou art not alone,
While Faith hath marked thee for her own.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Wouldst thou a temple? look above,—
The heavens stretch over all in love;
A book? for thine evangel scan
The wondrous history of man.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And though no organ-peal be heard,
In harmony the winds are stirred;
And there the morning stars upraise
Their ancient songs of deathless praise.

hymns.title: Not only doth the voiceful day
hymns.author: W. H. Burleigh
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Not only doth the voiceful day
Thy loving kindness, Lord! proclaim,
But night, with its sublime array
Of worlds, doth magnify Thy name.
Yea, while adoring seraphim
Before Thee bend the willing knee,
From every star a choral hymn
Goes up unceasingly to Thee.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Day unto day doth utter speech,
And night to night Thy voice makes known;
Through all the earth, where thought may reach,
Is heard the glad and solemn tone;
And worlds, beyond the farthest star
Whose light hath reached a human eye,
Catch the high anthem from afar,
That rolls along immensity.

hymns.title: Hath not thy heart within thee burned
hymns.author: Bulfinch
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Hath not thy heart within thee burned
At evening’s calm and holy hour,
As if its inmost depths discerned
The presence of a loftier power?

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Hast thou not heard ’mid forest glades,
While ancient rivers murmured by,
A voice from forth the eternal shades,
That spake a present Deity?

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And as, upon the sacred page,
Thine eye in rapt attention turned
O’er records of a holier age,
Hath not thy heart within thee burned?

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: It was the voice of God that spake
In silence to thy silent heart;
And bade each worthier thought awake,
And every dream of earth depart.

verses.label: Verse 5
lines: Voice of our God, O, yet be near!
In low, sweet accents, whisper peace;
Direct us on our pathway here,
Then bid in heaven our wanderings cease.

hymns.title: I saw on earth another light
hymns.author: Jones Very
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: I saw on earth another light
Than that which lit my eye
Come forth, as from my soul within,
And from a higher sky.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Its beams still shone unclouded on,
When in the distant west
The sun I once had known had sunk
Forever to his rest.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: And on I walked, though dark the night,
Nor rose his orb by day;
As one to whom a surer guide
Was pointing out the way.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: ’Twas brighter far than noonday’s beam,
It shone from God within;
And lit, as by a lamp from heaven,
The world’s dark track of sin.

hymns.title: Wouldst thou in thy lonely hour
hymns.author: Anonymous
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Wouldst thou in thy lonely hour
Praises to the Eternal pour?
I will teach thy soul to be
Temple, hymn, and harmony.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: Sweeter songs than poets sing
Thou shalt for thine offering bring;
Softly murmured hymns, that dwell
In devotion’s deepest cell.

verses.label: Verse 3
lines: Know that music’s holiest strain
Loves to linger, loves to reign,
In that calm of quiet thought
Which the passions trouble not.

verses.label: Verse 4
lines: Wouldst thou in thy lonely hour
Praises to the Eternal pour?
Thus thy soul may learn to be
Temple, hymn, and harmony.

hymns.title: Open, Lord, mine inward ear
hymns.author: Methodist
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: Open, Lord, mine inward ear,
And bid my heart rejoice;
Bid my quiet spirit hear
The comfort of Thy voice;
Never in the whirlwind found,
Or where earthquakes rock the place,
Still and silent is the sound,
The whisper of Thy grace.

verses.label: Verse 2
lines: From the world of sin, and noise,
And hurry, I withdraw;
For the small and inward voice
I wait with humble awe;
Silent am I now and still,
Will not in Thy presence move;
To my waiting soul reveal
The secret of Thy love!

hymns.title: As, down in the sunless retreats of the ocean
hymns.author: Moore
hymns.source: Project Gutenberg #33180 - A Book of Hymns for Public and Private Devotion

verses.label: Verse 1
lines: As, down in the sunless retreats of the ocean,
